https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Universal Genève SAS Presentation Polerouter

I’m thrilled to be offering an incredibly cool Universal Genève Polerouter which was presented to an SAS Pilot. The Polerouter is one of Gerald Genta’s earliest and most iconic designs. This watch comes from the original owner who lived in Sweeden and flew for the SAS for 20 years. The dial is well preserved and the 18K yellow gold case is unpolished and in spectacular condition.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1018 With Original Papers

I am excited to offer this awesome Rolex reference 1018. The reference 1018 is a rare and oversized Oyster Perpetual model which measures 36mm (43mm lug-to-lug). The 1018 was a reference that was not mass-produced and as a result of its narrow font has become an exciting OP for collectors who appreciate minimal and balanced dials. This example features one of the nicest grey soleil dials which remains in a fantastic state. Oftentimes, the reference 1018 will show signs of water ingress or feature service dials. This example dates to 1966 and was sold at S. Brunati in 1969 The case is lightly polished and the watch is paired on a period correct 72086 bracelet with 58 end links.

To learn more about the 1018, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘ Collector's Guide: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1018 ’ which I contributed towards.

The watch comes with the original papers.

Rolex Precision Reference 8895

This Rolex Precision reference 8845 is a very rare model which I have only seen on two occasions. This particular example features a beautiful non-luminous dial which has taken on a slight discoloration over the years. The case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). It features drilled lugs.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 14060 Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 14060 dates to 1999 with an A serial. The case has been lightly polished, but is in strong condition. It features a “Swiss Only” dial.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 126613LB Brand New & Full Set

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most versatile and adorned wristwatches. This example is “brand new” had never been worn except for this wrist shot. The watch was purchased in February of 2022.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes from the original owner, and comes “full set” with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Greenleaf & Crosby Pocket Watch in 18K Gold Box & Extract

This Patek Philippe pocket watch is a fantastic timepiece dating to 1913. It features a 17 ligne movement described in the Extract from the Archives to be “super thin” and features “a lever escapement”. On the movement, the retailer Greenleaf & Crosby is emphasized. The watch comes from the original owner’s family, which was a prominent political family in Florida. It features a bassine style case with an open-face. The dial features a beautiful "natté" guilloche pattern and Roman numerals. The case back features the initials of the original owner in black enamel.

The watch comes with its original box and an Extract from The Archives.

Patek Philippe Reference 5226G Brand New & Full Set

This Patek Philippe reference 5226G is an awesome modern wristwatch from the highly acclaimed watchmaker. The dial features a gradient texture and the lume gives off a vintage inspired aesthetic. The case is unpolished. The watch dates to August of 2022.

The watch comes from the original owner and is “full set” with box and papers.

Heuer Dato Carrera First Execution Reference 3147N Unpolished

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s, the present watch is notably a “first execution” and features an unpolished case. Finding Carreras from the 1960s with in great condition is extremely difficult. The dial is also highly rare and features “Fab. Suisse” indicating it was for the French market. It is paired on a rare Gay Frères bracelet for Heuer.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote for HODINKEE back in 2013:

https://www.hodinkee.com/articles/understanding-the-earliest-heuer-carreras

PRICE: $19,000 on Bracelet ($14,000 on leather strap).

Heuer Autavia Tropical Reference 1163V Unpolished

In my opinion, the Heuer Autavias are some of the best buys in vintage watches. They feature very large cases which are very enjoyable on the wrist. This example features an unpolished case which measures 43mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The dial has developed a beautiful tropical brown color, which is accented very nice by the colorful hands and registers. The watch is paired on a Heuer bracelet made by Gay Frères.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here 11/29/2022

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 in 14k Gold

The Daytona reference 6263 in gold with a black dial and black acrylic bezel is one of the most desirable vintage Rolex models, made even more special when it is in the rare and rosy 14k gold for the American market. This watch is in outstanding condition and the gold has developed a bit of oxidation making it even more of a rich rose gold color. The tritium luminous plots are in excellent condition and match the hands perfectly. The black acrylic bezel appears to be original and is in outstanding condition while the crown and pushers appear to be the original 14k gold and the pushers are the correct Mark 2 pushers for this serial range. The bracelet is also a Rolex reference 7205 and made of 14k gold.

The watch measures approximately 37mm in diameter. The serial number is 5.4 million, dating to circa 1978-1979. It does not have the original box and papers, but does have excellent provenance.

This is a grail watch and any collector would be thrilled to own and wear this example.

Patek Philippe Reference 2552

This Patek Philippe reference 2552 is a spectacular example in 18K yellow gold. The watch dates to 1954 and was sold in 1955. The case is unpolished and has crisp hallmarks. The case dimensions are 36mm in diameter and 41mm lug-to-lug. It is paired on an 18mm Camile Fournet leather strap and features gold Patek Philippe buckle made by Wenger.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Reference 5402ST

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is among the most iconic wristwatches and I’m thrilled to offer this exceptional vintage example. This example is the original Royal Oak reference 5402ST. 2,000 examples were made in each A ,B, and C series.

This example is a C series and dates to the mid 1970s. The dial shows slight development of patina, and remains very beautiful with a subtle warmth. The hands and crown appear to have been replaced at some point over the years. I strongly believe the case and bracelet remain unpolished. The watch should fit a wrist up to 7.5 inches and features the original flip buckle clasp. The case measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug to lug).

This watch does not come with box and papers.

Audemars Piguet Reference 4010ST

This Audemars Piguet reference 4010ST is a fantastic steel timepiece from 1976. It is a rare, octagonal model from the Le Brassus manufacturer. During the previous owner’s correspondence with Michael Friedman of AP, the Historian and Head of Complications cited only seeing a handful of examples in person throughout his career.

The steel case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 2120 (the same movement within the Royal Oak, with exception to the date feature).

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives.

Patek Philippe Pocket Watch

I’m thrilled to offer this spectacular Patek Philippe pocket watch. The case features a beautiful monogram on the caseback, and commemorative engraving to Ancel J Brown from Blanchard Press Inc. dating to July 30, 1924.

The watch does not include box and papers. The Extract from The Archives is currently on order from Patek Philippe.

Patek Philippe Reference 1543 Retailed by Haussmann

This Patek Philippe reference 1543 is an awesome timepiece with a double signed dial by Haussmann. The watch dates to 1949, and was sold in the same year. The 18K yellow gold case has been lightly polished, but is in strong condition. The dial is well preserved, along with the beautifully hand-engraved caseback which was dedicated to a professor in Rome.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives.

Rolex "Pre-Daytona" Chronograph Reference 6238

The Rolex “Pre-Daytona” reference 6238 is a great chronograph for Rolex enthusiasts. This particular model dates to circa 1966. The case is 36mm in diameter and has been lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

J. N. Shapiro "Infinity Series" In Steel

Joshua Shapiro has earned his reputation amongst watch collectors as one of the rising American independent watchmakers based in California. This beautiful J.N. Shapiro features the "infinity weave" engine-turned palladium dial, which is synonymous with the brand. The steel case measures just over 40mm in diameter (and 47mm lug-to-lug). It is in “like new” condition.

The watch is paired on a supple, butterscotch strap, giving a more casual aesthetic. It also comes with its original leather strap. This watch dates to 2022 and to the best of our knowledge is the first example to hit the secondary market.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner.

Boucheron Reflet With Black Dial

The Boucheron Reflet remains foreign to many American watch collectors and enthusiasts, yet enjoys a fantastic reputation amongst European collectors that are more familiar with the retailer Boucheron. Just as the Reverso and Cartier Basculante are admired for their interactive case designs, the Reflet has its unique design which they patented in the mid 1940s. Early examples are often powered by the OMEGA Calibre 302, however we are not certain this example features this calibre. It is a manual wind movement, and features a beautiful Clous de Paris case which was made in Paris (as seen with the eagle hallmarks below). Interesting to note, Andy Warhol enjoyed the stylish model in his collection. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm lug-to-lug and 22mm in width. The black dial is extremely attractive and features slight discoloration on the exterior.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Oyster Precision Reference 6425

This Rolex Oyster Precision reference 6425 is a fantastic, time-only model. The watch has great presence on the wrist at 36mm in diameter and 42mm lug-to-lug. The watch is a manual wind, with a very elegant feel on the wrist with a 10.5mm height (including crystal).

The case is spectacular and has not been polished. The engine-turned bezel adds a bit of style alongside the awesome hour markers. The watch is paired on a “Honey Wind” strap by Nick Gabarro.

It does not include box and papers.

Vacheron & Constantin Reference 6415

This Vacheron & Constantin reference 6415 is an incredibly well-preserved timepiece dating to the 1950s. The watch is ultra-thin featuring the calibre 1003 manual wind movement. It comes on its original leather strap made by G.Klötzl Maroquinerie Créations. It also comes with the original gold buckle made by Wenger. The caseback features a remarkable perlage.

The watch comes with the original box.

Zenith Hexagon Wristwatch “As New”

This Zenith wristwatch is a very interesting watch from the 1970s with a hexagonal case. The case is unpolished and is in “as new” condition. It measures 41mm wide and 37mm in length. It features a very striking grey dial and comes with the original strap and buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Autavia GMT Reference 2446C

This Heuer Autavia GMT reference 2446C is really fantastic condition. It dates to circa 1968. The case is unpolished and it has just recently been serviced. It features and insanely nice dial. The luminous hour markers and hands have developed a light discoloration which is very attractive.

The watch comes from the original owner. It does not come with box and papers.

Longines "American Motors Corporation" in 14K Gold

This Longines wristwatch features an awesome corporate dial with the American Motors Corporation branding. The watch measures 33mm in diameter (37mm lug-to-lug). It is a manual wind movement. The case is made of 14k gold and remains unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Zenith Respirator

This Zenith Respirator is a very interesting and rare vintage model. It features a beautiful blue dial with a grooved center section, giving off a dynamic look. The case measures 30mm in diameter and just under 40mm lug-to-lug. It is paired on its original NSA bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Breitling "Raquel Welch" Reference 765 For LIP

This Breitling "Raquel Welch" reference 765 is an awesome and rare example that was retailed for the French market to LIP. The case measures 41mm in diameter (49mm lug-to-lug). It has been lightly polished, but has strong form.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Chronograph Pocket Watch

This Heuer chronograph pocket watch features what can only be described as a jaw-dropping two-tone dial. The fact it has remained so beautiful over the years is really remarkable. The watch dates to circa 1940s, and features a US import code in the movement. The case is unpolished. It comes with a plastic stand, which makes it a perfect accessory for one’s desk.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Ikepod Megapode "New Old Stock & Full Set"

The Ikepod Megapode is one of the many great timepieces designed by Marc Newsom. This particular example is in “new old stock” condition. The case is 46mm in diameter and is 18mm in height.

The watch is powered by the Valjoux calibre 7750 chronograph which is inverted. The crown at 3 o’clock operates the under-glass slide rule bezel.

This watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

Ikepod "Red" Manatee "New Old Stock & Full Set"

This watch is a very interesting Marc Newson design which incorporates a world time complication. They were made to help raise awareness of the threatened species in the early 2000s. The watch is in “new old stock” condition and is “full set” with box and papers.

Rolex Submariner "Cermit" Reference 126610LV “Unworn Full Set”

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most versatile and adorned wristwatches. This particular model is has never been worn outside of the photo in this listing. The watch was purchase in June of 2022.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes from the original owner, and comes “full set” with box and papers.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 216570 "Full Set Unpolished"

This Rolex Explorer II reference 216570 is an awesome example. The case measures 43mm in diameter (and 50.5mm lug-to-lug). This example remains unpolished and dates to circa 2017.

This watch comes from the original owner and is “full set” with box or papers.

Breitling Navitimer "Gold Plated" Reference 806

The Breitling Navitimer reference 806 is among the iconic mid-century chronographs. This particular example dates to circa 1950, and features a beautiful dial. The case was replated with gold at some point in the past. The case measures 40mm in diameter (and 48mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447T

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s. You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

This example is a later Carrera from circa 1968. The watch is powered by the manual-wind Valjoux calibre 72. The case appears to be unpolished and the dial has minor wear. Overall, it is a Fantastic sports watch at a great value.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Montreal Chronograph Reference 750.503N

This Heuer Montreal chronograph reference 750.503N dates to the late 1970s. The case is unpolished and very mint. It measures 41mm wide and 43.5mm in length. There are slight marks on the dial. It features an automatic movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Zodiac Automatic SST 36000 “As New”

This Zodiac Automatic SST 36000 is a very cool and funky timepiece from the 1970s. The watch features a high beat movement. It has a spectacular quadrant dial with altering shades of blue.

The case is unpolished and is in “as new” condition. It measures 39mm wide and 35mm in length.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Vulcain Cricket Reference 305002

This Vulcain Cricket is an oversized model known as the reference 305002. The case is gold plated and unpolished. It measures 37mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). The crown has been replaced. The watch is powered by the calibre 120, and the alarm is quite loud.

The watch does not come with box and papers.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here 11/9/22

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Rolex Daytona Reference 6239 Big Daytona Dial

I’m thrilled to be offering this Rolex Daytona reference 6239 featuring a “Big Daytona” dial. The dial is in remarkable condition and leaves such an impression upon you when it is on the wrist. I believe the case has may have been lightly polished, but is in very strong condition with great form.

The watch features the early “300 Units Per Hour” variant bezel which is very crisp. The case features a 1.59M serial number dating it to circa 1967.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Daytona Reference 6262 Unpolished Full Set

I’m thrilled to offer this spectacular Rolex Daytona reference 6262. The case and bezel remains unpolished. The dial is in beautiful shape with very clean subdials.

The watch measures approximately 36mm in diameter (39mm including the crown). It features a 2.39M serial number dating to circa 1970. It was sold at the famous U.S. retailer Linz.

It came from the original owner and is full set with the original box and papers. It also comes with both the rivet bracelet featured in the photos and the original Jubilee bracelet not pictured in the listing.

Rolex Double Red Sea-Dweller Reference 1665 Thin Case & Tropical

This Rolex Sea-Dweller reference 1665 features the highly desired and vibrant "Double Red" dial. The case is lightly polished, but in strong condition overall. The watch measures 40mm in diameter. It previously came from the original owner. The dial has the original tritium lume on the dial and hands.

The watch features a 1.7M serial dating to circa 1967.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016

The Rolex Explorer I reference 1016 is the watch I am asked more than any other watch and for good reason. The Explorer reference 1016 was introduced in 1960 and stayed in production until 1989. It is a beloved sports watch that can be not only recognized but appreciated by almost everyone.

This example features a beautiful, gradient black dial with pale luminous hour markers. The case is lightly polished, but very strong. The watch features a 9.06M serial dating to circa 1986. It is paired on a Rolex 78360 bracelet with 580 end links.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 5513 Maxi Mark IV Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is an awesome example. The case and original bracelet are unpolished. It features a spectacular warm lume on the dial which is a “Maxi Mark IV”. All in all, this is a dream-like vintage Sub!

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 3970EP

The Patek Philippe reference 3970 is part of the legendary family of Patek Philippe perpetual calendar chronographs. It is the successor to the cherished reference 2499 with its origin beginning in 1986. It would be produced over the course of the following two decades and remains one of the finest timepieces within the recent chapters of Patek Philippe’s history.

The platinum case is unpolished and the stepped lugs remain in strong condition. The lug width is 19mm.

The movement is operated through the calibre 27-70 Q (which originates through the Lemania 2310). The pump pushers are incredibly satisfying when engaging the chronograph. The watch is accompanied with its original platinum deployant buckle.

Tudor Oyster Prince Reference 7995/0 Unpolished

This Tudor Oyster Prince is a very awesome wristwatch with a beautiful black dial. The hour markers are very appealing on this example as they stand very tall and are distinguished against the black background. The watch is an automatic, and the case is in fabulous shape appearing to be unpolished. This example dates to circa 1968.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 5513 Pre-Comex Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 features an unpolished case, as well as a rare “Pre-Comex” dial. The watch has been fitted with a True Dome D19 crystal. The caseback has some corrosion and the bracelet is original to the watch. The watch is 40mm.

Seaboard-Yacht Big Eye Chronograph Unpolished

I am extremely excited to offer this Seaboard-Yacht “Big Eye” chronograph. These are quite rare and very much a watch that collectors go crazy for. The 36mm case is unpolished and has twisted lugs that chronograph lovers will recognize within models like the Speedmaster. The dial on this watch is phenomenal, resembles vintage denim, and would be an incredible watch to wear with denim buttondown shirts or coats. It is paired on a very supple tan strap.

The watch was just recently serviced and does not include box and papers.

Rolex Coke GMT-Master II Reference 16710

This Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16710 with “Coke” bezel is unpolished. The watch has an E serial, indicating production circa 1990. The watch is 40mm. This is a beautiful example in honest condition.

Zenith El Primero G 381 18K Gold Unpolished & Tropical

The Zenith El Primero is one of the iconic chronographs from the 20th century. This particular example has a beautiful and tropical dial. The 18K gold case is unpolished. The case back features the engraving “1972 Chicago Sweepstakes Sales Captain Award”.

This watch does not include box or papers.

Heuer Complete Calendar Chronograph with Moonphase

This vintage Heuer is a really remarkable timepiece. It features a Valjoux 88 with complete calendar chronograph with a moonphase indication. The dial is incredible with a warm tone developed over the years and beautifully well preserved. The steel case is very sharp and unpolished. The watch is 35mm in diameter and 42.5mm lug-to-lug. Hopefully, this watch will end up in the collection of a passionate Heuer enthusiast, because it is really something special.

The watch does not include box and papers.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Reference 42042 1st Generation with Military Blue Dial

This Vacheron Constantin Overseas reference 42042 is an awesome a 1st generation model with the rare military blue dial. The watch dates to circa 2001 and is in fantastic condition. It is unpolished.

The watch comes with its original papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1603

This Datejust Reference 1603 is a very beautiful example with an lightly steel case and the engine-turned bezel is unpolished. The watch features a super cool engraving reading “To La Vaughn Davis From The Fillingims”. The watch features a 1.21M serial number dating to circa 1966.

The watch does not include box and papers.

Universal Genève Reference 10372-2 Retailed by Tiffany & Co. In 18K Gold

This Universal Genève is an awesome timepiece with a chronometer-grade microrotor movement with an HON import code. It features a double-signed dial retailed by Tiffany & Co. The lugs are very stylish and the 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. It features a fantastic case back engraving to the former owner from his mother and father.

The watch does not include box and papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16078 with Bark Bezel & Central Links

The Rolex reference 16078 is a rare variant in the Datejust line that features a “bark” finish on both the bezel and bracelet center links. The case is strong, possibly lightly polished. The watch has a 5.3 million serial number, indicating production in 1978-1979. It has a sapphire crystal, which is original for the reference. The watch is 36mm in diameter.

Rolex Daytona Reference 116523 Two-Tone

This Rolex Daytona reference 116523 features a two-tone stainless steel and yellow gold case and bracelet, as well as an unusual blue dial with Arabic numerals. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch is 40mm and includes its original box and papers.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 216570 Brand New Unworn Full Set

This Rolex Explorer II reference 216570 is an awesome modern watch. The watch dates to 2019. Outside of this photo, it is unworn.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

Rolex Sky-Dweller Reference 326939 in White Gold Like New Full Set

This Sky-Dweller has a special dial for the steel models. Only a few came from the factory like this. It is like new. The watch was purchased in 2019. The watch has only been worn a handful of times.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5065A Jumbo

This Patek Philippe reference 5065A features a 38mm steel case that remains unpolished. The watch comes with a black strap, as well as the popular “khaki” green. Both straps are uncut. The watch comes with its original papers.

Universal Genève Uni-Compax Reference 12445

This Universal Genève Uni-Compax is a really fantastic timepiece and I am thrilled to be offering it. The reference is a 12445 and the 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. The strap requires a female spring bar. The watch features a 1.1M serial number dating to circa 1945. The non-luminous dial is very gorgeous and has amazing Art Deco numerals.

The watch was just recently serviced, and does not include box and papers.

Heuer Carrera Reference 36475 Eggshell Dial

This Heuer Carrera reference 36475 is a very cool example with a rare and early “eggshell” dial. The case is strong and probably unpolished. It is a first series, with a 54,XXX serial number. The dial’s luminous material is the original radium.

Rolex Air-King Reference 116900 Full Set

This Rolex Air-King reference 116900 is a great modern watch. The case is unpolished an measures 40mm in diameter. The dial features pronounced numerals at 3, 6 and 9 and features the highly attractive green “Rolex” print.

The watch comes with with box and papers.

Ikepod Hemipode Chronometer Grande Date Full Set As New

This Ikepod Hemipode Chronometer Grande Date is a very cool and rare watch. It is in new old stock condition. The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 16570 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Explorer II reference 216570 is an awesome modern watch. The watch dates to 2019. Outside of this photo, it is unworn.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

Rolex Submariner Reference 116613LB Brand New Unworn Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 116613LB is an awesome sports watch. Outside of this photo, it is unworn. The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Rolex Day-Date 41 in White Gold Full Set

This Rolex Day-Date 41 is very bold watch with a beautiful blue dial and radial numerals. The case is white gold and appears to be lightly polished.

The watch comes full set (box and papers).

Rolex Daytona Reference 116520 Full Set

This Rolex Daytona reference 116520 is a great daily driver. It features a lightly polished case and Z serial number dating to circa 2006.

The watch comes full set (box and papers) from the original owner.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. This is a beautiful example with a case that has been lightly polished, although it is very strong. The serial number is a 5.53M dating to 1978.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Vacheron & Constantin Reference 7397 in Steel

This Vacheron & Constantin reference 7397 is such a cool timepiece. Steel Vacheron & Constantin models are quite uncommon and this tonneau-shaped case is unpolished with a beautiful finish on the surfaces.

This watch does not come with box and papers, however an Extract from the Archives has been ordered.

Vintage Ulysse Nardin 14K Yellow Gold Wristwatch

It is not often that I find myself discussing Ulysse Nardin timepieces, however when I came across this wristwatch I was compelled to offer it for sale. The yellow-gold case which remains unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter and 42mm lug-to-lug. The dial is very beautiful and features drilled minute markers that resemble many Patek Philippe dials from the same era. The watch is a manual wind, and extremely elegant on the wrist.

This watch does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Ultraman Reference 145.012-67

The OMEGA Speedmaster reference 145.012 is regarded as one of the most cherished iterations of the iconic Speedmaster model. Nicknamed after its cameo within the Japanese television series "Ultraman" (or Ultra Series), its distinctive orange hand is the perfect example of how collectors can become enthralled by the slightest nuances.

This example will make for a great addition to any Moonwatch lover’s collection. The Speedmaster comes with its Extract from OMEGA confirming the orange hand. The case remains unpolished. The dial is in pristine condition and is taking on a slightly tropical appearance. The watch is paired on a tan strap. It came from original owner and also includes OMEGA 1039 bracelet with 516 end links (not pictured).

The watch does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA Seamaster 300 Reference CK 2913

This is a really spectacular OMEGA Seamaster 300 reference CK 2913. It is a rare US Navy watch with movement number engraved on case back. The case is lightly polished. According to the Extract, the watch dates to August 13, 1959 and was delivered to the US Sixth Fleet and US Military Sea Transportation Service in Europe.

The watch does not come with box and papers, but it does come with Extract from the Archives

IWC Aquatimer Reference 3536-001 in Titanium

This IWC Aquatimer is an awesome choice for those who are looking for a durable and great looking sports watch. The titanium case is in great shape overall. The watch is powered by a 37524 calibre, which is a modified ETA 2892. The watch was last serviced in 2013.

The watch comes with box and service cards

Rolex Submariner Reference 116610 Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 116610 is an awesome sports watch. The case is well worn but it remains unpolished. The watch dates to circa 2012.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Baume & Mercier Calendar Chronograph in 18K Gold

I don’t often source Baume & Mercer timepieces, however there are some examples that stand out in the vintage catalog as undeniably cool watches. This calendar chronograph is certainly one of those watches! The 18k gold case is unpolished and appears to be paired on its original strap with the original buckle. The moon is decorated beautifully and watch features a spectacular Singer dial. For those looking to dip their toes into complicated vintage watches, this is provides awesome value!

The watch does not include box and papers.

Tudor Snowflake Submariner Reference 7021

This Tudor Submariner reference 7021 (or 7021/0) has a remarkable dial with a unique patina. The case is lightly polished but in good form. The case back features the engraving “Jim Fogus”. The watch features its original grey insert in attractive condition and a roulette date wheel alternating from black (odd dates) to red (even dates). It has a 760,XXX serial dating to circa 1971.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Tudor Oyster-Prince Reference 7995/0 Unpolished

This Tudor Oyster-Prince reference 7995/0 is a really great vintage watch. The case is unpolished and the dial has developed a beautiful patina making the white font stand out brilliantly. The thick hour markers are very pronounced and complement the font and steel case.

The watch does not include box and papers. A bracelet can be added for an additional cost.

Rolex Sea-Dweller Reference 126603 Brand New Unworn Full Set

This Sea-Dweller reference 126603 is an awesome modern watch. The watch was purchased within the last two years. Outside of this photo, it is unworn.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

Seiko True Pogue Automatic Chronograph reference 6139-6005

This Seiko reference 6139-6005 is a “True” Pogue Chronograph in that is the same reference and dial worn by Colonel Pogue on Skylab H1. The dial/bezel combination on this watch is iconic and this example is in exemplary condition. The watch is approximately 40mm.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here 12/19/2022

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Perpetual Calendar Reference 25654BA.00.0944BA.01

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak reference 25654BA.00.0944BA.01 is an exceptional perpetual calendar sports watch. The 18K gold case has a beautiful matching gold / champagne dial. The dial is very beautiful with a gold surface and sunken registers. The watch should fit a wrist up to 7.3 inches. The case appears to have been previously polished, but is in outstanding condition. We believe it dates to the late 1980s

The watch does not come with box and papers. The previous owner had obtained an Extract from the Archives of AP through the NYC boutique, but has misplaced it.

Rolex Small Crown Submariner Reference 6536/1 Unpolished

I’m very excited to offer this Rolex “Small Crown” Submariner reference 6536/1 for sale. The case is in great overall shape and remains unpolished. It measures 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). It features the original bezel, which could be as much as $30-$40k by itself.

The luminous-hour markers have a spectacular warm patina which is much more beautiful in person. Complementing the orange lume is the awesome gilt font and chapter ring. The watch dates to circa 1959. It is paired on a period correct Swiss-made Oyster rivet bracelet dated to circa 1957 (with 65 end links). The watch comes from the grandson of the original owner who served in Vietnam. It was just serviced for the first time.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 6090 Black Diamond Dial

I’m thrilled to offer this Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 6090 for sale. It is one of the most stunning Rolex Oyster Perpetual watches I have ever seen. The dial is a phenomenal non-luminous gilt example with applied dagger and six diamond hour markers starting at 12:00 (a style that many collectors adore). It is a very rare example with a black and diamond dial similar to the historic “Bao Dai” reference 6062.

The watch dates to circa 1952. The case is 18K gold and has been lightly polished and measures 33.5mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). It comes from the widow of the second owner (who was a famous car dealer in New York). It accompanies the guarantee and chronometer certificate from 1952, strap and buckle. I have paired it with a stretch Rivet bracelet made by Gay Frères which dates to circa 1953. For those who love elegant sports Rolex models.

Universal Genève Tri-Compax Reference 22539 Non-Luminous Unpolished

I’m very excited to offer this outstanding Universal Genève Tri-Compax reference 22539. The watch is in fantastic condition and the case is unpolished. Furthermore, it features a rare non-luminous dial with incredible Art-Deco numerals which has developed a nice warm tone. The watch is powered by UG’s column-wheel caliber 287. For those that love vintage Universal Genève timepieces, this one is a real sweetie.

Heuer "Viceroy" Autavia Reference 1163V Unpolished

In my opinion, Heuer "Viceroy" Autavias are some of the best buys in vintage watches. This Autavia reference 1163V is nicknamed the "Viceroy" as it was made for a promotion with Viceroy cigarette company of the United States of America in the 1970s. You can read more about the history of these watches in this article by Jeff Stein. This example is unpolished and comes on a rally strap with a signed Heuer buckle.

Heuer Mareographe Reference 2444

The Heuer Mareographe reference 2444 is essentially the Heuer version of the Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer (also made by Heuer). This example has a spectacular warm cream colored dial with excellent blue accents in the registers. The minute register is designated for timing yacht races. The case is lightly polished and measures roughly 35mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1960s.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447S Eggshell Dial Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 2447S is a very great example with a rare and early “eggshell” white dial. Later examples had silver dials The case is unpolished. The watch is powered by the Valjoux calibre 72. It is a first series, with a 56,4XX serial number. The dial and hands have the original radium luminous material.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

Rolex Oyster Reference 6426 "Like New"

This Rolex Oyster reference 6426 is an absolutely stunning vintage Rolex. The dial features incredible 3, 6, 9 applied numerals which resemble the numerals on the Rolex “Padellone” dials. This luminous dial was made by Fabrique de Cadran Stern Frères and remains pristine. The case is in “like new” condition, and recently had the case back sticker intact. It was removed since, but is preserved with the watch. It is paired on a later added stretch Rivet Bracelet with 57 end links which is period correct.

The case is unpolished and 34mm in diameter, but wears larger with a 42mm lug-to-lug length. It features a manual wind movement.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 5980/1A Blue Dial Unpolished Full Set

This Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 5980/1A is a fantastic example. This model is currently discontinued in steel with the blue dial. The case is unpolished.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 124270 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Explorer reference 124270 is a fantastic modern sports watch with great proportions. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug) and 11.5mm thick. This particular example has a very crisp and unpolished case. The watch comes from the original owner who wore the watch only a handful of times.

It comes “full set” with box and papers.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced.

Vulcain Cricket 2nd Series Reference 304001

This Vulcain Cricket is incredible with luminous “watermelon” hour markers and applied 3, 6, 9, and 12 numerals. The 34mm chrome case is in mint condition and is extremely well preserved. Often, chrome cases will show signs of degradation and chipping. The case back is stainless steel and lug width is 18mm. It is paired on its original leather strap and buckle. It also comes with its original hardshell box and the catalog it was sold with. The watch comes from a collector who purchased it from the original owner, an American who was vacationing in Lugano at the time of purchase. It is truly a connoisseur’s Cricket.

You can read more about this Vulcain Cricket in Charlie Dunne’s article ‘A “Full Set” Vulcain Cricket Reference 304001 with Original Strap, Buckle, Presentation Box & Papers’.

Vulcain Cricket "Waterprotected" in Steel

This Vulcain Cricket is a reference S2329A and dates to pre-1964 as seen by the radium indication on both sides of the 6 o’clock hour marker.

The dial features the thin “Vulcain” font which is also less compact compared to the earliest models. It features the beautifully applied-faceted hour markers, with luminous stick hands.

The watch dates to the early to mid-1950s and is powered by the Calibre 120’s successor the MSR S2. The watch is paired with a tan Wind Vintage strap by Nick Gabarro.

Longines Chronograph Reference 7412

The dial features a tremendous sunburst finish, complemented by a blue pulsation and asthmometer scale. The bi-registers are slightly sunken, giving a bit of dimension to the dial. The dial furniture is comprised solely of tall-faceted baton markers, along with baton hands.

It is a very traditional style, with straight lugs. The case is 36mm in diameter (excluding the crown), or 41mm lug-to-lug.

The watch houses the calibre 30CH manual-wound chronograph movement. It accompanies a Lognines Extract from the Archives from 2020 in which it details that the watch was invoiced on April 28, 1964 to Société Francaise Longines (SFL), which was the French subsidiary for the manufacturer.

Patek Philippe Two-Tone 18k White Gold and 18k Yellow Gold Pocket Watch

This Patek Philippe pocket watch is a very attractive two tone model in 18K while and yellow gold. It features an amazing sector dial with applied yellow gold hour markers. The interior case back is engraved “Presented To George W. Bowers in recognition of his valued services as president of S. George Company Dec. 28. 1942. George W. Bowers was born In 1897 in Wheeling, WV. The S. George Company was located In Wellsburg, WV producing printed paper sacks of flour and meal.

It does not come with box and papers. An Extract from The Archives is currently being ordered for it.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-66 CB Case

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding Omega Speedmaster reference 105.012-66 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

The reference 105.012 was the first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied Omega logo. It is in phenomenal condition, with a slightly warm tone developing throughout the perimeter, and is beginning to show signs of becoming tropical.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to the excitement of many Speedy lovers. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line from 1963 to 1966.

The case was made by CB, and remains very crisp, showing its true form. The watch appears to be unpolished and has its original crown, which is uncommon as most were replaced during service. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. The reference 105.012-66 is highly prized and collected because it was the exact same model worn by Neil Armstrong to the Moon. The watch was just serviced and I am thrilled to offer this great watch to the next lucky owner.

Vintage Rolex Prince Reference 1343

Absent of any external branding or coronet, this Rolex Prince could easily be mistaken for a “regular” watch from the 1930s to the untrained eye. However, to those that understand and appreciate the storied history of the Genevan watchmaker this timepiece is a testament to the earlier, more refined chapter of the brand’s catalogue.

Unfortunately, Rolex Princes are almost always found in a compromised state due to a common practice in the 1980s and 1990s where collectors would have the dials redone. So when a beautifully preserved example like this appears, it is truly a special occasion. The dial remains unrestored and had developed a gorgeous patina over the years.

I am extremely proud to offer this exceptional Art Deco timepiece for sale, as they rarely surface in such a state.

Vulcain Cricket Reference 303002

This reference 303002 is in the family of oversized Vulcain Crickets. These models are extremely loud and very exciting when they come up for sale. This example features the bold “Vulcain” font as seen in the OG models. The watch wears very well at 38mm in diameter. The case has been lightly polished.

It does not come with box and papers.

Movado M95 Chronograph Unpolished

The Movado M95 chronographs are incredible timepieces not only for their movements, but also the cases and dials. This particular wristwatch features a luminous dial made by Fabrique de Cadran Stern Frères. It does have some scratches and patina, but it is a beautiful and unrestored example. The numerals are very cool and similar to those found in various Rolex references from the same period. The case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It was also produced by Taubert Frères (formerly François Borgel.

It does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 130J Chronograph with Breguet Numerals w/ Extract

I’m thrilled to offer this very exciting Patek Philippe chronograph reference 130 in yellow gold. This is a very refined timepiece which dates to 1946. The dial features tremendous Breguet numerals which is confirmed in the Extract notes. The watch is powered by the prestigious calibre 13-130 which was featured in many of the most important chronographs from Patek Philippe in this period. The case is lightly polished, but in strong condition compared to most reference 130 chronographs I have seen. The case measures 33mm in diameter, but wears quite nice at 41mm lug-to-lug.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 is one of the coolest 1675s I’ve come across. It is a beautiful example in honest condition with an unpolished case. The watch has an 3.11M serial, indicating production circa 1972. The bezel has an insanely cool blue and faded red. The watch is 38mm in diameter (46.5mm lug-to-lug). It is photographed on a C+I bracelet, but I am selling it on a 7836 folded bracelet on correct 280 end links.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 600 Pocket Watch with Black Dial

The Patek Philippe reference 600 is one of the most beautiful time-only pocket watches from the manufacturer and has become one of favorite references. Introduced in 1933, the reference 600 began a legacy which lasted over three decades becoming one of the most iconic Patek Philippe pocket watches. It is important to note the context of 1933, as at this point Patek Philippe had pivoted towards producing more wristwatches than pocket watches (a decision that served to keep their manufacturer afloat under the new Stern leadership).

Going back in time we have this spectacular and early version of the reference 600. It is 44.5mm in diameter (1mm wider than our example from 1952). While easily overlooked, the mid case in fact sits flat below the bezel and does not intersect to profile. Nicknamed “Moderne” for it’s large and timeless design, it is very slender at 8mm in height. More subtle details on the watch are quite interesting. For instance, the bow is 15mm wide (over a mm wider than in later ref. 600s). Also, worth noting is the crown is comparatively large at 6mm (over 1.5mm wider than on the white dial variant).

The black dial or “noir cadran” is confirmed in the Extract from the Archives, and is produced by Fabrique de Cadran Stern Frères. It features a exquisite enamel track which complements the subsidiary dial, signature and 18K gold case. Notably, the dial features the “long signature” (Patek, Philippe & Cie) which would be truncated to “Patek Philippe'“ around 1949. The “long signature” is extremely charming and when well preserved like the above dial makes the watch a real trophy for collectors.

The ref. 600 features applied “Heures Lapidées” hour markers is outstanding. The large dial and dauphine hands make for one of the most distinguished designs. The bow would be a dramatic transition from the more common “Bassine” and “Murat” style pocket watches and serve to be a much more modern design over into the 1950s and 60s.

The present example features an unpolished 18K gold case with beautiful patina developing on the surfaces. The case back features the initials engraving “BCS ERS”, leaving one to infer the timepiece was a gift, potentially from a father to son, or perhaps wife to husband. Unlike the previous example, the interior case back on this timepiece does not designate the model as a reference 600, as this is a much earlier example dating to 1939.

The Poinçon de Maître (case maker’s hallmark) bears the number 9 for Emile Vichet. There are also reference 600s with cases produced by Eggly & Cie which are much less common. The watch is powered by the calibre 17-21/12 and is stamped twice with the Geneva Seal.

The watch does not include box and papers. It comes with the Extract from The Archives.

Piaget w/ Johnny Cash Provenance Reference 9131 C4

This vintage Piaget is just a marvelous timepiece. It originally belonged to Saul Holiff who was the personal manager of singer-songwriter, Johnny Cash. It is a reference 9131 C4. the C4 designated for the Clous de Paris case. The case back is beautifully engraved “S.I.H. Nov. 61”.

The case measures 23mm in diameter and the bracelet is very long (suitable for a wrist slightly above 7 inches). For those looking for a sophisticated and distinct timepiece, this is an absolute treasure.

The watch comes with its original box and a page from a catalog featuring the reference.

Benrus Golden Voice Cricket by Vulcain From President Lyndon B. Johnson

This is a very special timepiece with incredible provenance. At first glance, it may appear to be a normal Golden Voice Cricket. When you look closer, it begins to get more interesting. First, the watch is signed Benrus. This is extremely uncommon, as Benrus had their own alarm watches using the A. Schilds movement. Then when you notice the arched motto “Do Unto Others As You Would Have Them Do Unto You” it begins to get more interesting. The Golden Rule can be found on several rare Patek Philippe Reference 2526 dials, which common theory suggests they were special orders from President Lyndon B. Johnson. He would often give these watches out as gifts to work colleagues and friends. Additionally, this dial bears the President’s initials L.B.J. which is also seen on his personal Vulcain Cricket.

This watch was given to the wife of Lawrence J Klein, who managed President Johnson’s ranch. As for the watch which was given to Klein, he had received a Rolex Day-Date reference 1803! The watch comes from the family of the original owner.

The watch is gold plated, and remains unpolished. It is a rare model which I have not seen before. It does not come with box and papers. The watch has just been serviced.

Rolex Red Submariner Reference 1680 Unpolished Mark 4 Dial

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 dates and features the highly desired red Submariner text. The lume on this example is a very nice warm tone and even more attractive in person. The dial is a “Mark IV” with the white font under the red Submariner text.

The case is fantastic and is unpolished. It features the original bezel insert. The Sub is paired with a 9315 Rolex bracelet with 380 end links. The watch features a 2.95M serial number dating to circa 1972.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Gondolo & Labouriau Pocket Watch in Silver

This Patek Philippe pocket watch is an awesome timepiece with a very large silver case. The watch was sold at Gondolo & Labouriau (one of the most prestigious Brazilian retailers based in Rio de Janeiro). The Gondolo pocket watches remain one of the best value buys for collectors passionate about Patek Philippe.

It does not come with box and papers. We ordered An Extract from The Archives and are currently waiting for it.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 Full Set

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. It features an uncommon Mark 2 dial. The lume on the dial is a nice pale yellow, and the hour and minute hand has a darker tone. The case is 36mm in diameter. It has been lightly polished, but is in is in strong condition. This example has a 2.11M serial number dating the watch to circa 1969. The watch is paired on a 7836 folded link bracelet by Rolex with 258 end links.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I that I produced in 2022.

The watch comes from the original owner, who bought the watch while in service in Vietnam. It includes include box and papers.

Rolex Thunderbird Datejust Reference 1625

The Rolex Thunderbird Datejust is a very cool watch. They are classic, but also a bit sporty with their robust signature bezels in white gold. The case measures 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug).

The case features a 784,XXX serial number dating the watch to circa 1962. It is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet which dates to 1965.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Zenith Covergirl El Primero Reference A3818 Unpolished

This Zenith “Covergirl” El Primero reference A3818 is such a sweetie! Collectors can attest to the fact that the Zenith El Primero reference A3818 is one of the most desirable vintage Zenith models. Its nickname "Covergirl" derives from its feature on the cover of the Zenith book by Manfred Rössler.

Most examples that I see have overpolished cases. This example is unpolished and features its original latter bracelet by Gay Frères.

The watch was just recently serviced. It does not come with box and papers. I received this watch comes from the original owner’s grandson who was a Zenith TV executive.

Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520 with “inverted 6”. It has a serial dating it to circa 1991. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. The case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).This watch does not include the original box and papers.

Piaget 12PCI Gold Automatic Reference 13406

This vintage Piaget watch is a very attractive timepiece with painted Roman numerals. The 18k yellow gold case measures 32mm in diameter (36mm lug-to-lug). It appears to be unpolished. It is paired on what appears to be the original strap and buckle.This watch does not include the original box and papers.

Vacheron & Constantin 18K Yellow Gold Dress Watch

This vintage Vacheron & Constantin is a very attractive dress watch. The case is made by Antoine Gerlach and has stylish “Cornes de Vache lugs. It is powered by the manual wind calibre 453. The watch measures just over 32mm in diameter, but wears a bit larger with a 41mm lug-to-lug length.

This watch does not include the original box and papers.

Heuer Camaro Reference 7220T

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive raving, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera.

The reference 7220T is powered by the manual wind calibre Valjoux 72. The T in the reference designates the tachymeter scale. The case is unpolished and the watch is paired on a nice tan strap that matches well with the lume. The case measures 37mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

Mido Powerwind Rainbow Diver Reference 5907 Full Set Unpolished

I have always thought the Mido Powerwind “Rainbow Diver” reference 5907 is the quintessential Easter watch. It has one of the most distinctive colorways across all vintage watches and it is immediately recognized across the room. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 1137OC. The case is unpolished and measures 36.5mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on its original mesh bracelet signed Mido.

In Tomas Rosputinsky’s 2020 article on Fratello, he highlights these as a “fine quality Mido 5907 piece sells north of €10,000 easily”. Furthermore, Monaco Legends auctioned one for €16,250 in 2021. I’ve extremely excited to offer this collector’s grade example for a great price!

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

OMEGA Seamaster Reference 166.022 Unpolished

This OMEGA Seamaster is a very cool variant in the vintage Seamaster line. The dial has a similar style to the Speedmaster “Companion dial”, and is also similar to the Alaska Project Speedmaster. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 562. The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). Overall, this is a super fun watch that proves you don’t have to break the bank to enjoy vintage watches!

The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Wide Boy Datejust Reference 1601 Non-Luminous Unpolished

This is a super cool Rolex “Wide Boy” Datejust reference 1601 with a the uncommon non-luminous dial! The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The watch has a 3.11M serial number dating the watch to circa 1972.

It was just serviced and is running great. The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Autavia Reference 2446C GMT Unpolished

The Heuer Autavia reference 2446C GMT is such a cool sports watch. These EPSA cases are very large, and wear incredible for those who prefer modern proportions. The case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter and 47mm lug-to-lug). The lugs are compact and angled downward, so this watch does become isolating for those who also appreciate traditional sizes. It features an awesome dial with warm luminous hour markers which match the hands.

It is powered by the Valjoux calibre 724. The serial number is 129967 dating the watch to circa 1968.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe La Flamme Collection Reference 4815/1 Full Set

This Patek Philippe Reference reference 4815/1 is a very interesting timepiece from the “La Flamme” collection. Very little has been written about this reference and the collection as a whole. This watch is powered by a quartz movement which was just serviced. The 18k yellow gold case measures 21mm by 26mm. The bracelet is incredibly elegant with a weaved link pattern. The dial features beautiful diamond hour marker at 3, 6, 9 and 12 which are complemented by the ornate ribbon hands.

The watch comes with with box, papers and the Certificate of Origin.

Tudor Submariner Reference 79090 Like New Box and Materials

This Tudor Submariner reference 79090 is an exceptional example. The watch is in “like new” condition with minimal scratches. It still retains the original sticker on the case back. The watch dates to circa 1993.

The case is unpolished and measures 38mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes with box and materials.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 Straight Writing Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 is spectacular example. The dial is turning to a slightly dark brown. It features the straight writing or “scritte dritte” case back. The case is unpolished and measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 861 made by Lemania) and it features its original crown and crystal.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was purchased from the family of the original owner.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-68 Transitional Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-68 is an awesome “transitional” example with the applied OMEGA logo. The dial has an extremely attractive lume which matches great with the hands. The case is unpolished and measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 861 made by Lemania) and it features its original crown and crystal.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

We ordered an Extract from the Archives for it and are awaiting its arrival.

Rolex Oysterdate Reference 6694 Unpolished

The Rolex Oysterdate reference 6694 is an awesome reference that is a great model to enter into the vintage Rolex scene with. Not to say your first Rolex shouldn’t be a GMT or Sub, but if you are hoping to find out what the craze in vintage watches is all about, an Oysterdate is a great and affordable model. This example has an unpolished case which measures 34mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on an Oyster bracelet with correct 60 end links. The dial is beautiful with extremely cool faceted hour markers at 6 and 9 o’clock. It features a 1.001M serial number dating to circa 1963.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great.

Rolex Pocket Watch 25 World's Records Reference 2199 Unpolished

This is a very cool Rolex pocket watch with the achievement of “25 World's Records” signed on the dial. The case designates the watch to reference 2199 and the metal as Snowite. It has a very cool silvered dial with the Greek Key (Meander). The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Hamilton Mil-W - 46374B

This Hamilton Mil-W - 46374B is a cool US Army watch issued in June 1983. The case measures 33mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug) and is in great shape. It is a manual wind movement. Whether you are interested in getting their first vintage military watch, or have a diverse collection, this Hamilton is a great affordable watch.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-6

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This fantastic reference 2998-6 has great luminous hour markers and matching hands. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a tan leather strap. The crown is a later example which has been replaced. It features the original dot over 90 bezel.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter. We ordered an Extract from the Archives for it and are awaiting its arrival.

Enicar Ultrasonic Reference 1000/24 a S Like New

This Enicar Ultrasonic is an awesome time only dress watch. It is in “like new” condition and has a super cool compressor case. The watch is 35mm in diameter and just a great watch for under $1,000.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Universal Genève Polerouter Date Reference 204610 Unpolished

The Polerouter is an iconic watch designed by Gérald Genta and remains one of the great affordable vintage watches. This Universal Genève Polerouter Date is a super cool reference 204610. The dial has developed a tropical brown and it retains the painted white font and cross hair.

The case is unpolished and was made by Huguenin Frères. The case measures 34mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the automatic calibre 218-2 microtor movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012-67 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional reference 145.012-67 is a very cool and unpolished example. The case measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement calibre 321. It features a replacement crown.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Small Crown Submariner Reference 6536/1

The Rolex “Small Crown” Submariner reference 6536/1 is a classic reference. The dial on this example has an extremely cool look with intense color. The case has been lightly polished and measures 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). It features the original bezel.

The luminous-hour markers have a spectacular warm patina which is much more beautiful in person. Complementing the orange lume is the awesome gilt font and chapter ring. The watch dates to the 3rd quarter of 1957 and has a 306,XXX serial. It is paired on a period correct Swiss-made Oyster Rivet bracelet dated to circa 1957 (with 65 end links).

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 Darth Reference 101.035 with Black Dial

This A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 reference 101.035 is a fantastic timepiece dating to 1999. It has a fantastic black dial which looks so great with the white font. The platinum case appears to be lightly polished and measures 38.5mm in diameter. One thing that is worth highlighting in these Lange watches are the very nice numerals and hands. The watch is powered by the calibre L 901.0. The applied-Roman numerals are very refined and something to admire while looking down at your wrist.

The watch comes with an extract. It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Daytona Reference 6239 Double Swiss Underline

ThisRolex Daytona reference 6239 is a very cool example with a rare “Double Swiss” and “Underline” dial. It has a rare and early 275 bezel. The dial has some wear, and the hour and minute hands were replaced by Rolex decades back. The case has been lightly polished, but is in strong shape. It comes from the family of the original owner in Florida. It is paired on a C+I stretch Rivet bracelet dating to 1961. The case has a 923,XXX serial and dates to circa 1963.

The watch was just recently serviced and is running great.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Calatrava Reference 2482

This Patek Philippe reference 2482 is an amazing timepiece in 18K yellow gold. The case is unpolished and has a great size at 36mm in diameter and 44mm lug-to-lug. The lug design on this model are very attractive from both straight forward and the profile. The dial is made by Fabrique de Cadran Stern Frères and is untouched. This example has my personal favorite dial configuration with similar numerals found in the Rolex "Padellone", Rolex Oyster reference 6426, Rolex Oyster reference 6082, Rolex Precision reference 4583, certain Movado M95 chronographs and St. Christopher coin watches.

The case back features an amazing engraving “To Frank From Dave 1958”. The watch is powered by the manual wind calibre 27 SC, and features the HOX import code indicating it was sold in the United States. For those searching for a rare and a remarkable Patek Philippe Calatrava, look no further!

The watch does not come with box and papers. An Extract from The Archives has been ordered for this watch.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 345.0802 18K Gold

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This fantastic reference 345.0802 is most certainly one of the great iterations within the model. It has an awesome dial and hands which give a very clean look to this gold case and black bezel. These models are very rare. According to WATCHFID there were produced between 1980 and 1988 and approximately 300 examples made, and it was the second Speedmaster in 18K yellow gold.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The case is likely unpolished. The watch does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 861 L), and is 42mm in diameter.

Heuer Autavia Exotic Reference 1563 Unpolished

This Heuer Autavia reference 1563 is one of the coolest vintage Heuer models which features the rare “Exotic” dial. It features the same case as the reference 1163V. Looking at the dial, you will notice the smaller subsidiary seconds at 10 o’clock, and the 30 minute register at 3 o’clock giving it a very cool asymmetrical dial. With orange accents, it is just a great look. The watch is powered by the calibre 15. It is paired on Gay Frères bracelet. It dates to circa 1975.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Eberhard Scafograf 200

The Eberhard Scafograf 200 is an insanely cool and rare sports watch. It features a distinct dial with large luminous markers for divers. The watch is paired on the original bracelet. The case appears to be lightly polished and measures 39mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Camaro Exotic Racing Reference 7743 NT Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive raving, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera. This particular reference 7743 NT has an awesome dark brown “exotic racing” dial. The radial finished bezel is always a highlight within unpolished exampled such as this. The watch is powered by the Valjoux 7730, a formidable bi-register chronograph that has stood the test of time.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

Technos Sky Diver Unpolished

This Technos Sky Diver is a super cool watch with a beautiful tropical dial. I really enjoy this case style. It has a 38mm diameter, and awesome long lugs measuring 48mm lug-to-lug. The case is unpolished and the watch is powered by an automatic 30 jewel movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Camaro Reference 73345 CHT Gold Plated As New

This Heuer Camaro reference 73345 is such a cool watch. I am thrilled to be offering this example in “as new” condition with the intact case back sticker still on. The watch is gold plated and has a really fantastic dial with brown sundials. The watch is powered by the Valjoux 7733 manual wind chronograph movement. For those who want an affordable museum-grade watch, this is a real sweetie!

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Eterna-Matic Kontiki Super Sector Dial 130 FTP

This Eterna-Matic Kontiki Super 130 FTP is an awesome timepiece with a sick sector dial with orange accents. The case is lightly polished. It measures 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

The watch is paired on a Gay Freres Eterna-Matic bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA Flightmaster Reference 145.013

This OMEGA Flightmaster reference 145.013 is a very funky watch with a nice tropical dial. The dial and hands add a great bit of character with the green blue and yellow. The case has been lightly polished. It is powered by the calibre 910, and is paired on the original bracelet.

The watch does not come with an Extract, box or papers.

Favre-Leuba Bathy 160

This Favre-Leuba Bathy 160 is a super cool and rare dive watch. It is quite interesting in that it incorporates a depth gauge into the watch itself. It has a nice case which has been lightly polished. It measures 39mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on a very cool NSA (Novatit SA), Favre-Leuba signed bracelet.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Eberhard & Co. Pre Extra-Fort Pink/Salmon Dial

This Eberhard Pre Extra-Fort chronograph is a very cool chronograph from the 1940s. It features a pink/salmon dial which is very uncommon for the period. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 39mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Ikepod Hemipode Weekplanner NOS Full Set

The Ikepod Weekplanner one of the coolest Ikepod models. It is a rare model and in new old stock condition. It is powered by the ETA 2892.A2 giving it a very interesting dial layout on this complicated Ikepod. The orange accents on the calendar hand and weeks gives off a great look. It measures 43mm in diameter. The watch

Vulcain Cricket with Non-Luminous Roman Numeral Dial Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket is among the earliest styles produced just over 100 years after the La-Chaux-de-Fonds manufacturer’s inception. Like the “LBJ” dial Crickets, it features the original or “OG” style case. It measures 34mm in diameter (just under 43mm lug-to-lug). The steel case features drilled lugs and is paired with an 18mm strap made by Nick Gabarro.

The dial is non-luminous and features applied-Roman numerals. The case remains unpolished and features some wear on the left edges of the bezel from removal. The watch is powered by the calibre 120, and has an exceptional voice when the alarm is engaged.

Heuer Yacht-Timer As New

I've always had a penchant for vintage Heuer. I love to both hunt and collect the stop watches and other ephemera from the brand. This particular Heuer Yacht Timer wristwatch is one of those timepieces I obsess over and have several examples in my personal collection. The stopwatch is powered by the calibre 7710 and it is in “as new” condition with the case back sticker still on it! It is such a fun timepiece to play with in the office, or wear on the wrist with a nylon strap! The case is quite a big one at 52mm in diameter! It is paired on a Phoenix grey 22mm nylon. If you are interested in entering the world of vintage Heuer, this is certainly a great place to start!

The watch does not come with box or papers.
