https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Rolex Paul Newman Daytona Reference 6239

This is a rare Rolex “Paul Newman” Daytona Reference 6239 with a spectacular white dial. This watch has resided in a private collection for over 25 years. I’ve been hunting it for years and am excited to finally be able to offer it for sale.

The first “Paul Newman” exotic dials we see on the Daytona reference 6239 are in the 1.6M range. From my experience, those are all black. Starting at the 1.7M serial range, we see white-dial “Paul Newmans” like the one Paul himself wore which was slightly later 2.005M serial that went for $17.8M at auction in October 2019.

These earliest white dial reference 6239s have an extremely vibrant white and red and pop. They also have a thin seconds register hand and flared hour and minute hand that the later ones in 1.9M-2M range and beyond do not. Those have thicker and straight hands with a pointed tip. These early ones are extremely rare and I really like the vintage aspect of the thin subsidiary hand and flared hour/minute hands like on the earliest reference 6239 and reference 6240 examples. The other notable aspect of the earliest Paul Newman Daytona dials is the large T SWISS T below 6:00. Later examples have more of a “sing-a-song” T SWISS T that is smaller and goes up and down like a rollercoaster.

Overall, you could dedicate years looking for a beautiful early “Paul Newman'“ dial like the present example. It is just not something you can find or source. The case is lightly polished, but very strong overall and I consider this one of the most beautiful “Paul Newman” Daytona examples I’ve held. The serial number is 1.8 million.

The watch does not come with box and papers. This is a rare opportunity to find an outstanding example of an early Rolex “Paul Newman” Daytona with white dial

Patek Philippe Reference 3940R Box & Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in rose gold. It features an exhibition case back showing the self-winding calibre 240Q. This example dates to circa 2004. The case is 36mm and the case is in strong, possibly unpolished condition.

The watch comes with a deployant buckle in 18k rose gold, an Extract from The Archives confirming production and sale in 2004, an additional green leather strap with Patek Philippe pin buckle in 18k rose gold, and a Patek Philippe bo

Longines Sei Tacche Reference 5697 Retailed by Türler

This Longines reference 5697 is an incredibly cool Sei Tacche watch. The steel case measures 33.5mm in diameter, but straight lugs give off a bit more of a bold look on the wrist at 42mm lug-to-lug.

It features even luminous numerals. In addition, the dial features subtle circular-luminous hour markers which are also seen in various dials throughout the 1940s and 1950. On the outskirts of the dial is a great painted track indicating the 60 seconds/minutes and numerals for every 5-minute demarcation. The design is often found in 1940s chronographs and models like the Vulcain Crickets, but when they are featured in a time-only model it just gives the watch an extra measure of confidence. Not to mention, it also has a double-signed dial with the Türler signature. The prestigious retailer’s origin began in 1883 when the founders César Alexander Türler and Jean Henri Türler opened up shop in Biel, Switzerland.

If the watch itself was not cool enough, it also features a case back engraving! On the case back, an engraving reads: “IN DANKBARER ANERKENNUNG. TREUER DIENSTLEISTUNG 25 JAHRE. GERBER & CO. A.G.” (“In grateful recognition of loyal service 25 years of Gerber & Co. A.G.“). This watch was given to an employee of a Zurich based tobacco company commemorating 25 years of service.

Learn more about this watch within this extensive article.

The watch does not come with box or papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives.

Patek Philippe Reference 2555

This Patek Philippe reference 2555 is a very interesting Calatrava model dating to 1955. The watch features dauphine hands and incredible textured hour markers. The very strong and possibly unpolished. It measures 32mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). The lug width is 19mm. According to Phillips, only 39 yellow gold examples have appeared publicly making the watch particularly rare.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from the Archives which can be seen below.

Blancpain Tribute To Fifty Fathoms MIL-SPEC Full Set

The Blancpain Fifty Fathoms is an iconic dive watch adored by collectors. This particular example is the Tribute To Fifty Fathoms MIL-SPEC, a limited edition watch of 500 examples. The case is unpolished and is powered by the calibre 1151.

This watch comes “full set” with box and papers. It comes from original owner who wore it only a few times, and is in mint condition!

Universal Genève Tri-Compax Reference 22279 1 Non-Luminous Unpolished

Universal Genève is one of the most fantastic watchmakers with a great past catalog. The case appears to be unpolished and very crisp. The diameter measures 35mm and is 42.5mm lug-to-lug. It is powered by the manual-wind calibre 481. The dial is in a great state and has wonderful dagger hour markers. For those who are in search for an awesome triple calendar moonphase watch, this is a great contender!

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Glashütte Original Senator Perpetual Calendar w/ Box and Service Papers

This Glashütte Original Senator Perpetual Calendar is a spectacular perpetual calendar. The dial configuration has a very clean, yet imaginative layout with great apertures for the moonphase and date. The case measures 38mm in diameter and 46mm lug-to-lug. The watch was recently serviced in 2022. We’re excited to offer this incredible timepiece for a fraction of what most luxury perpetual calendars are priced at.

It comes with box and service papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012-67 SP Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional reference 145.012-67 SP is a very cool and unpolished example paired on a grey canvas strap. The case measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement calibre 321. It features the original crown which is rarely seen on these. The crystal has the OMEGA logo in the center. Overall, this is a spectacular Speedy which would be a joy on any true watch lover's wrist!

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch does not come with an Extract.

Heuer Monaco Reference 1133B Unpolished Full Set

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement, the calibre 11. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic. This example is in phenomenal condition. The case is unpolished, and the dial is stunning. In addition, it is paired on the Heuer bracelet made by NSA. In my view, this watch is much more favorable on the wrist paired on the super comfortable bracelet.

The watch comes with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 3086 Retailed By Gübelin

Along with pocket watches, vintage Patek Philippe ladies dress watches provide exceptional value for watch collectors and connoisseurs. This particular timepiece is no exception. It is a brilliant reference 3086 with a double-signed dial by Gübelin. These "Joallerie" references are certainly some of the most creative designs.

The case measures 14.5mm in diameter by 15mm in length. The 18K yellow gold bracelet has a fantastic texture that is very rich and evocative of the 1950s-1960s Patek Philippe catalog. The bracelet is relatively long and should fit a wrist up to 6.25 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers or Extract from The Archives.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: Eric Wind
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Rolex Oyster Perpetual Date "1985 Honda Bowl" Reference 15000

This Rolex Perpetual Date reference 15000 is quite the timepiece. The engraving on the back - Honda Bowl Winner ‘85 - was initially somewhat a mystery. Upon further investigation, rather than for a college football game, we believe it may have been for the first-place prize for the winner of the AMA Supercross 250SX race at Daytona International Speedway in March of 1985. It is the only known example and was possibly won by Bob “Hurricane” Hannah who finished first in the race while riding a Honda bike.

The watch is 34mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 42mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The watch is powered by the calibre 3035, and features a quickset (indicative of the 5 digit reference).

It is preserved in like-new condition with the original Jubilee bracelet. The bracelet clasp is so tight, that it appears it was only unfastened a few times. The case serial number is 7.3 million. The bold red double signed “Honda” is incredibly distinct and demands one’s attention.

Rolex Date Reference 1500

This Rolex Date reference 1500 is remarkable. The dial features a vertically brushed texture that gives an extremely refined appearance to the watch. The case back is engraved with the former owner’s name, ‘Robert L. May”. The watch is 14k gold and is on its original 14k rivet Oyster bracelet. I’m thrilled to offer this timepiece “full set” with box and papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-1

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This museum-grade example reference 2998-1 features a gorgeous tropical dial. The case remains unpolished in my view. The original black “Base 1000” aluminum bezel is the best condition one I have ever seen. It includes an Extract of Archives from OMEGA dating to February 16, 1960, and delivered to a US Army base in Europe. This watch does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter. The watch was just serviced by Alkis of Swiss Watch Repair.

This is the best of the best and a bargain!

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster ’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

An “OG” Vulcain Cricket

Perhaps one of the most underrated-entry level wristwatches in the vintage market, the Vulcain Cricket is a truly important wristwatch that was at one point the most sought-after timepiece in the world. This Vulcain Cricket is among the earliest styles produced just over 100 years after the La-Chaux-de-Fonds manufacturer’s inception. Like the “LBJ” dial Crickets, it features the original or “OG” style case. It measures 34mm in diameter (just under 43mm lug-to-lug). The steel case features drilled lugs and is paired with an 18mm “honey” Wind Vintage strap made by Nick Gabarro.

The dial is non-luminous and features applied-Arabic numerals. As seen in the photos, the dial has blemishes (most notably at 11 o’clock) and the decimal track on the exterior of the dial’s print has faded. Nevertheless, it is a fantastic-vintage representation of the iconic Cricket.

The case remains unpolished and features some wear on the left edges of the bezel from removal. The watch is powered by the calibre 120 and has an exceptional voice when the alarm is engaged.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16233 Ivory Pyramid Dial Retailed by Tiffany & Co.

This Rolex Datejust reference 16233 is a particularly special timepiece in that the ivory-pyramid dial is also signed by Tiffany & Co. The watch was originally sold in the early 1990s with a serial number dating to 1991. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 3135.

The case is unworn, with the exception of this photograph. The case back sticker remains on the watch, and it is accompanied by its original box and papers. The original owner was a dance instructor in Virginia and it is a museum grade watch.

Breitling Navitimer Early Reference 806

I’m excited to offer this early Breitling Navitimer reference 806. This watch was awarded to Matthew Rosenhaus by The Eddie Jacobson Memorial Foundation on April 29, 1956 in Kansas City, Missouri. Rosenhaus was “an honorary vice-chairman of the Anti-Defamation League of B’nai B’rith and vice president of the ADE Foundation, who died yesterday of the age of 68. A noted civic leader and philanthropist, he was president and chairman of the board of the J. B. Williams Company, Inc., vice chairman of the board and member of the executive committee of Nabisco, Inc., and chairman of the executive committee and a member of the board of Columbia Pictures Industries, Inc.” Jacobson was “an American businessman best known as an Army comrade, business partner, and close friend of President Harry S. Truman.”

It is powered by a calibre 178 movement by Venus. The case is unpolished and it is paired with a tan Wind Vintage strap by Nick Gabarro.

Cartier “Tank Asymètrique” Limited Edition Reference 2488 in Platinum

Released in 1996 alongside the thematic ‘Magical Art of Cartier’, this Cartier “Tank Asymètrique” reference 2488 Limited Edition in Platinum was produced in a total of 100 examples. The watch is numbered 92/100 on the caseback. Its more common sibling was was produced in yellow gold with a total run of 300 timepieces.

The case remains unpolished and measures 34mm lug-to-lug, and 23mm in diameter (excluding the crown). The watch is powered by the calibre 9P2, and the timepiece features the iconic sapphire cabochon crown with a beaded exterior.

Rolex Datejust Turn-O-Graph Reference 16013

The Datejust Turn-O-Graph is a very cool model that blends the elegance of the Datejust with the sportiness of a rotating bezel. This particular reference 16013 is a breathtaking example. The case and bracelet are in exceptional and unpolished condition. It has a 9.87M serial number dating to circa 1986. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 3035. It does not have its original box and papers, but was worn only a handful of times.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: Eric Wind
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Patek Philippe Reference 570 in White Gold

The Patek Philippe reference 570 is one of the most refined and sought-after dress watches, and an iconic model within the Calatrava family. This specific watch is a reference 570G, the G indicating a case produced in white gold. It features a non-luminous dial with applied-dauphine hour markers. According to the Patek Philippe Extract from the Archives that accompanies the watch, it was produced and sold during the first quarter of 1964.

This watch does not include the original box and paper. It is powered by the Caliber 27 SC manual-wind movement made by Patek Philippe.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-66 CB Case

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding Omega Speedmaster reference 105.012-66 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

The reference 105.012 was the first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied Omega logo. It is in phenomenal condition, with a slightly warm tone developing throughout the perimeter, and is beginning to show signs of becoming tropical.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to the excitement of many Speedy lovers. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line from 1963 to 1966.

The case was made by Huguenin Frères, and remains very crisp, showing its true form. The watch appears to be unpolished and has its original crown, which is uncommon as most were replaced during service. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. The reference 105.012-66 is highly prized and collected because it was the exact same model worn by Neil Armstrong to the Moon. The watch was just serviced and I am thrilled to offer this great watch to the next lucky owner.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can watch HODINKEE’s ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The Omega Speedmaster ’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article )

Rolex Precision Reference 4583

The reference 4583 is one of my favorite time-only dress models from Rolex. In fact, it was one of my first vintage Rolex watches which I shared in a video interview with Crown & Caliber in 2017 .

This watch dates to the 1950s. Its dial features a small crown and applied numeral at 12 (as well as at 6 o’clock), which is reminiscent of the Rolex “Padellone” reference 8171. The remaining hours feature applied dagger and pyramid markers also found on the more complicated Rolex. A very distinct attribute is the leaf or “feuille” hands which are seldomly seen in Rolex models beyond this era.

The steel case is very elegant and remains unpolished. It features down-turned lugs with crisp bevels. It is powered by the manual-wind calibre 700. The watch just recently had a service and is keeping excellent time.

Rolex Air-King Reference 5500 with Black Dial

This Rolex Air-King reference 5500 is very interesting as it features a black dial that not has begun to take on a tropical aesthetic, but it also features very interesting faceted-hour markers. The luminous material at the edge of the markers and alpha hands has a warm-yellow patina.

Introduced in the mid-1940s, the Air-King is one of the great timepieces that has stood the test of time. This example has a serial number of 642XXX dating to circa 1949. The watch is powered by the calibre 1530.

Rolex Submariner Maxi Mark IV Dial Reference 5513

This Rolex Submariner Maxi Mark IV Dial Reference 5513 is a great watch and appears to be unpolished. It features the original Rolex 9310 Oyster folded link bracelet. The lume is somewhat of an unusual darker color. The watch dates to circa 1979 with a serial number 6.19M. This timepiece was formerly owned by a gentleman whose name is engraved on the case back.

Gallet “Snow White” MultiChron 12

Gallet has made some of the greatest chronographs of all time and the MultiChron 12 is among the most special watches from the manufacturer. This particular example is referred to by collectors as the "Snow White". The steel case is unpolished and measures 37mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). It is paired with a 20mm “Honey” Wind Vintage strap made by Nick Gabarro. The watch is powered by the Excelsior Park 40.

Rolex “White” Submariner Reference 1680

This Rolex “White” Submariner reference 1680 dates to circa 1978. The case is unpolished and in insanely nice condition. The watch features a True Dome D19 on it for a clean look without the cyclops. It does not include the original box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 1575 made by Rolex), and is 40mm in diameter.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675/3

The present watch is a GMT-Master reference 1675/3 in two-tone steel and yellow gold. The GMT-Master originally was designed by Rolex in association with Pan Am for its pilots and remains one of the most iconic watches ever made by the company. This watch is a 5.6 million serial dating to circa 1978. The case is unpolished which is extremely hard to find in these models.

The watch comes on a two-tone Oyster solid-link bracelet. It does not come with box and papers. It has a True Dome D38 crystal without cyclops for a cleaner look.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and the corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE.

Cartier Tank Normale

The Tank Normale is one of the iconic models within the Cartier Tank family and the original design based on a French tank. It has been particularly special in my career as I was privileged to sell a smaller Tank Normale example that belonged to Jackie Kennedy Onassis during my time as Vice President, Senior Specialist for Christie's. This particular example is in great condition and dates to the late 1970s. The “Paris” dial features slight crazing, however it remains unrestored and has the “secret signature” at 7:00. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures 23mm wide by 30mm lug to lug. The octagonal sapphire cabochon crown features the flattened surface and appears to be original. The watch is accompanied by what is likely its original deployant clasp in 18k gold, but it does not include the original box and papers. It has a Cartier International Service sticker on the back from its last service. This is a wonderful watch for a discerning individual.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: Eric Wind
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Rolex Daytona Reference 6265

This is a museum-grade example Rolex Daytona reference 6265. The luminous plots on the dial’s peripheral remain intact, which is oftentimes not the case as these would be susceptible to damage during service. This watch is from circa 1979, with a serial number in the 4.01 million range. The case is in remarkable condition and remains unpolished. It measures 37mm in diameter. The movement is a manual wind calibre 727 made by Valjoux.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1002 "Full Set"

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 1002 is a spectacular watch. The luminous hands and dial have turned to a pale yellow color which looks brilliant on the white dial. The watch is paired with a Jubilee bracelet. The stainless-steel case measures 34mm in diameter (or 39.5mm lug-to-lug). The case is crisp and unpolished. This models dates to 1969 with its 2.07M serial number. The papers indicated the watch was sold to a gentleman in New York City in December of 1970. For those looking for a homerun Rolex without breaking the bank, this watch is a great option!

Rolex Lapis Lazuli Day-Date Reference 18038

This vintage Rolex 18038 is absolutely incredible featuring a rare dial made of lapis lazuli. This dial is an incredibly vibrant and deep blue tone depending upon the light. The movement features a “single quickset” calendar function to set the date quickly. The 18K gold case has been lightly polished but remains very strong. The dial is uncompromised and remains completely intact.

Rolex "IBM" Datejust Reference 16030 "Full Set"

This Rolex Datejust Reference 16030 is in fantastic condition. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). The lume is an extraordinarily beautiful warm cream color. The watch dates to circa 1980 with a serial number of 6.41M. The caseback features the former owner’s name engraved with his employer’s (IBM) logo. It was received by the employee on August 15, 1981.

The watch comes "full set" with box and papers from the original owner’s widow. However, it is worth mentioning that the serial number is mismatched on the papers due to a mistake by IBM at presentation. It does have a hang-tag with correct serial number.

Cartier Santos

This Cartier Santos is a beautiful timepiece with an unpolished case. It measures 27mm in diameter and 36mm lug-to-lug. The case is very elegant at 5mm thick. The watch features two owl hallmarks indicating was likely sold in the French market. It dates to the 1970s and is one of the most attractive examples I’ve seen in years. With so many vintage Cartier models achieving record-breaking amounts at auction, this timepiece represents fantastic value.

Rolex Winn-Dixie “Truck Logo” Air-King Reference 5500 “10 Year Safe Driver”

Some of the most interesting Rolex watches are the special “Winn-Dixie” models which were earned by long-term employees at the supermarket franchise. The “10 Year Safe Driver” watches were given to Winn-Dixie employees who had performed for a decade without any incidents while behind the wheel. The logo resembles the shape of a truck, and this has resulted in many mistakenly referring to it as the “Truck Logo”.

Introduced in circa 1957, the reference 5500 remained in production for over 30 years. The case measures 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 40mm). The lug width is 19 mm and the height is 12 mm including the crystal. Each reference 5500 will feature drilled lugs. This reference 5500 was produced circa 1984 with an 8.0M serial number. The caseback features faint initials of the original owner’s engraving. The watch has been lightly polished.

Rolex Winn-Dixie Air-King Reference 14000 “10 Year Safe Maintenance”

This example is for a driver employee that had an impressive 10-year period in which they avoided any accidents on the job.

The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The Winn-Dixie stamp has developed a slight patina in the lower half of the dial. The case is unpolished and features a Z serial number circa 2006-2007.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here 5/16/2022

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: Eric Wind
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

The Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 3700/11AJ is part of the family of the iconic Nautilus designs from Gérald Charles Genta. This particular example dates to 1987. The two-tone case has been lightly polishe, but remains very crisp.

The watch comes with the original Certificate of Origin indicating sale at Altier Jewelers in Boca Raton, FL in 1987 and wooden Patek Philippe box.

The Patek Philippe reference 3970 is part of the legendary family of Patek Philippe perpetual calendar chronographs. It is the successor to the cherished reference 2499 with its origin beginning in 1986. It would be produced over the course of the following two decades and remains one of the finest timepieces within the recent chapters of Patek Philippe’s history.

The platinum case is unpolished and the stepped lugs remain in strong condition. The lug width is 19mm. It is very uncommon to feature the silver-white dial in a platinum case (which is confirmed by the original documents it came with).

The movement is operated through the calibre 27-70 Q (which originates through the Lemania 2310). The pump pushers are incredibly satisfying when engaging the chronograph. This watch dates to 2004 and comes with its original box and papers.

This AB Svenska Urdepoten (ASU) chronograph is a fantastic entry-level 1930s chronograph. The stainless steel case measures at 32mm in diameter (38mm lug-to-lug). The flat-surfaced bezel adds to the vintage charm. Although the case back does have a slight curve, it wears quite close to the wrist due to the tapered lugs. Great development of patina has altered the dial, and many of the painted numerals have faded slightly. Luckily it is faint enough to appreciate the fact several numeral styles and sizes can be seen throughout the complicated dial. Arabic-painted numerals are featured at the 9 o’clock subdial which measures 60 seconds. At 3 o’clock, one can see painted-Breguet numerals which indicate the 45-minute measurement take place upon activating the chronograph.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

This square Audemars Piguet wristwatch is a remarkable timepiece with a very rare-blue dial. The 18K yellow gold case watch measures 26mm by 26mm and is paired on an elegant 14K bracelet. The watch is unpolished and has great provenance. It was originally owned by the first Latina clerk of court in New York and was likely a gift from a mobster/mafia boyfriend.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

This Rolex Daytona reference 116520 is a fantastic automatic sports watch. The dial has a light creamy appearance, and it features the earlier skinny/thin hands. The case has been lightly polished.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

This A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 Daymatic reference 320.025 is a remarkable timepiece from the German manufacturer. The robust case is very satisfying to wear on the wrist at 39.5mm in diameter. The platinum case is unpolished.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

This Rolex Air-King reference 5500 is very special in that the dial is decorated with the Calvert Distillers Company logo. The watch was awarded to the recipient in 1977. The case is lightly polished and the dimensions are slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm. It features a T serial number dating to 1996. The watch is paired with a C + I Oyster Rivet bracelet

The watch does not come with box and papers.

This Concord is a beautiful timepiece at an affordable price. The 18K yellow-gold case is 34mm in diameter and is unpolished. It is paired with an 18mm “Honey” Wind Vintage strap made by Nick Gabarro. The watch is powered by a quartz movement which just had a replaced battery. The case back is engraved with the former owner’s name from 2002.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

This Certina is a beautiful timepiece at an affordable price. The 18K yellow-gold case is 34mm in diameter and is unpolished. It is paired with an 18mm “Honey” Wind Vintage strap made by Nick Gabarro. The watch is powered by a quartz movement which just had a replaced battery. The case back is engraved with the former owner’s name from 2001.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Rolex Daytona Reference 6263

This Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 is a great example. The early pre-Sigma Mark 1 dial is in great condition with white printing and the lume plots remain fully intact, which is rare for vintage Daytonas. It also features the original rare Mark 1 screw-down pushers. It has a period correct U.S. C+I dated 1973. The case has been lightly polished, but remains strong and maintains its original form. It features its original rare “Mark 1” acrylic bezel, which is in strong condition, but has a crack between 110 and 100 on the tachymeter bezel.

This watch has a 2.8 million case serial number dating to circa 1971-1972. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 3700/11AJ

The Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 3700/11AJ is part of the family of the iconic Nautilus designs from Gérald Charles Genta. This particular example dates to 1987. The two-tone case has been lightly polished, but remains very crisp.

The watch comes with the original Certificate of Origin indicating sale at Altier Jewelers in Boca Raton, FL in 1987 and wooden Patek Philippe box. The price has been lowered to $99,000.

Rolex Air-King Reference 14000

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is an awesome example with a rare and beautiful grey dial. The watch features a E serial number and dates to circa 1990. The case is appears to be lightly polished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

This watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16238 “Full Set”

The Rolex Datejust is one of the brand's most iconic models. This reference 16238 in 18K yellow gold is a rare reference as full precious metal Datejusts are no longer produced. It is paired on a gold rivet bracelet with solid end links and the watch and bracelet appear to be unpolished. The serial is from circa 2002

The watch comes “full set” with a box and papers.

Tiffany & Co. Bracelet Watch

This yellow gold Tiffany & Co. ladies watch is a delicate and gorgeous timepiece. The design of the bracelet is extremely stylish and stunning. The gold hour markers and hands compliment the white dial exquisitely. This watch is powered by a C.H. Meylan movement. The engraving on the case back reads R.L.M. We understand this watch was originally owned by Bunny Mellon.

This watch does not come with box or papers. The watch should fit a 5.9 inch, and it comes with an additional link.

Patek Philippe Reference 3970EP

The Patek Philippe reference 3970 is part of the legendary family of Patek Philippe perpetual calendar chronographs. It is the successor to the cherished reference 2499 with its origin beginning in 1986. It would be produced over the course of the following two decades and remains one of the finest timepieces within the recent chapters of Patek Philippe’s history.

The platinum case is unpolished and the stepped lugs remain in strong condition. The lug width is 19mm. It is very uncommon to feature the silver-white dial in a platinum case (which is confirmed by the original documents it came with).

The movement is operated through the calibre 27-70 Q (which originates through the Lemania 2310). The pump pushers are incredibly satisfying when engaging the chronograph. This watch dates to 2004 and comes with its original box and papers.

The price has been lowered to $190,000

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
Rolex "Padellone" Reference 8171 in Steel

This Rolex "Padellone" reference 8171 is a very remarkable timepiece. It features a non-luminous dial made by Fabrique de Cadran Stern Frères and feuille hands with an English calendar. The dial has amazing patina and is extremely compelling and very hard to take one's eyes off of it which I would classify as a "Rolex by God" example.

Phillips recently sold a similar example with a luminous dial and hands for the equivalent of $352,000 in May which I found far less attractive. It is paired on an 18mm Gay Freres stretch rivet bracelet, but also includes its original greyish-blue strap with Rolex buckle.

The watch came from the grandson of the original owner who was a Polish-American pilot and the watch sat for decades unused. It does not come with box and papers.

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. Mr. Journe is one of the most significant watchmakers of the past century and the Chronomètre à Résonance is arguably his most iconic model.

This F.P.Journe Chronomètre à Résonance in platinum with brass movement is one of the first 70 produced. This white gold dial has developed a sensational and shimmery patina, which is the charm of these early Journe watches. We believe the case to be unpolished. The two-tone sub-dials are also original, which is important as these are sometimes replaced and ruins the look. The engravings are not shallow. It also has a thinner font on the power reserve.

This watch was originally purchased new in 2000 for $33,600 USD. It comes from the original owner and it is full set with the original box and papers. It includes a copy of original sales receipt.

For more information on the early Journe watches, please see this guide by A Collected Man:

https://www.acollectedman.com/blogs/journal/a-collectors-guide-to-early-f-p-journe

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 6614

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 6614 is a very rare reference and the predecessor to the Oyster Perpetual reference 1018 . I am extremely proud to offer this “like new” 6614 as I have to imagine it will be years before I find a better example. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug) and wears very slim at 11mm in height. Notably on the case back, the very rare stamping of “Stainless Steel” is added.

The dauphine hands have developed a beautiful patina. Not only does the dial feature an applied coronet, but it also features the applied “ROLEX” letters. As an early OP, it is absent of the “Superlative” language. The dial features bold hour markers and warm luminous plots which are complementary of the 20mm “Honey Wind” strap made by Nick Gabarro.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Submariner "King Maxi" Reference 5512

This Rolex Submariner "King Maxi" Reference 5512 is an awesome example with highly attractive lume. The watch has a 5M serial dating to circa 1978/1979. It features the original bezel insert and a TrueDome D19 crystal. The case has been lightly polished but is very strong. It is paired on a period correct 93150 bracelet with the correct 580 endlinks and has been tested to be water resistant.

This watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Datejust "Black Dial" Reference 16014

This Rolex Datejust reference 16014 is a great timepiece with an uncommon black dial. It is paired with a period correct Jubilee bracelet. The steel case is very crisp and possibly unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 5712R “Full Set”

This Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 5712R is a very attractive complicated model. The 18K yellow gold case is very sharp and I believe it remains unpolished. The watch comes with two additional straps, one being the original leather it was sold with.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 3800 in Yellow Gold "Full Set and Unpolished"

This Patek Philippe Nautilus is remarkable reference 3800 in 18K yellow gold. The watch dates to circa 1985 and remains unpolished. The dial is absolutely gorgeous, and features original lume in the hour markers and hands. It is powered by an automatic movement. It is an amazing timepiece for either a man or lady.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

Rolex Daytona "John Mayer" Reference Number 116508

Nicknamed for the 2nd appearance on HODINKEE Talking Watches, the Rolex Daytona reference number 116508 would take on the rockstar nickname “ "John Mayer". Despite being a departure from the Daytona’s utility-based nature, this model has become admired amongst a range of Rolex collectors.

The watch comes from the original owner and is offered full set with box and papers.

Ulysse Nardin

It is not often that I find myself discussing Ulysse Nardin timepieces, however when I came across this wristwatch I was compelled to buy it. This is example is in “like new” condition and appears to be accompanied with the original strap and buckle. The dial is very beautiful with a vertically brushed silvery-white with an awesome depiction of the anchor. The case still features the original sticker on the back.

This watch does not come with box and papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

A. Lange & Söhne Meter Dial Datograph Ref 403.035 in Platinum Full Set

This A. Lange & Söhne Datograph Reference 403.035 is one of the most exceptional chronograph watches ever produced and is an icon from the German manufacturer. This is an early example with a “Meter dial”. The dial reads “Base 1000 Meter" by three o’clock, whereas later examples say “Meters” instead of “Meter”. The case is made of platinum. It appears to be lightly polished. It measures 39mm in diameter, approximately 47.5mm lug-to-lug.

We believe the watch dates to circa 1999, but it is certainly an early example.

The watch comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera 1153N Blue/Grey Dial Retailed by Abercrombie & Fitch Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 1153N is an incredible chronograph with a very special double-signed dial by Abercrombie & Fitch. It features the calibre 11 movement. It dates to circa 1969 The case appears to be unpolished. The dial is a grey tachymeter dial that has a slightly blue appearance. These are some of the most attractive dials in vintage Carreras.

This watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running great.

To learn more about Abercrombie & Fitch watches made by Heuer see our article and corresponding video Collector's Guide: Vintage Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer Watches!

Patek Philippe Cloisonné Enamel World Time Ref 5231J-001 in 18k YG Unpolished Full Set Like New

This Patek Philippe World Time reference 5231J-001 is an incredibly special timepiece. The dial is cloisonné Grand Feu enamel depicting the Americas, Europe, and Africa. The colors are tremendous in these dials and a beautiful scene each time you look at the time. The watch is powered by the calibre 240 HU - an ultra-thin self-winding movement with a 24-hour disc to set the time for any major city you travel to. The 18k yellow gold case is in like new condition and has only been worn a few times over the years. It features the classic World Time hour hand. The watch debuted in 2019 and is one of the most sophisticated Patek Philippe world time models. It measures 38.5mm in diameter, 47mm lug-to-lug and 10.25mm thick.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. It was originally sold in December 2021.

A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia Jubiläums Langematik Ref. 302.025 in Platinum Full Set

This A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia Jubiläums Langematik-Reference 302.025 is a stunning time only watch. The case is made of platinum, and measures approximately 37mm in diameter, 44mm lug to lug and is 8.5mm thick. It features an exhibition caseback displaying the caliber L921.7 movement. The watch was made in an anniversary series of 500 pieces.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. The watch dates to 2009. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer "Panda" Camaro Reference 7843SN Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive raving, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings, such as the Autavia and Carrera. The case appears to be unpolished.

This Camaro reference 7843SN is a great stainless steel chronograph and is a lesser-seen model. The case measures 37mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Monaco Reference 1533G on Bracelet Unpolished

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic.

This example is an awesome reference 1533G. The watch is in fantastic condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It features a rare solid metallic grey dial grey dial variant. It is paired on a “Mark 1” Heuer NSA (Novavit SA) bracelet. It is powered by the automatic calibre 15 with running seconds. For those looking for an outstanding vintage Monaco, this is a great option!

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. It comes with a rally strap and Heuer signed buckle.

Rolex Land-Dweller 40mm Ref 127334 in Stainless Steel Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Land-Dweller is a monumental new release from the manufacturer with design cues from the Oysterquartz Day-Date and Datejust. The reference 127334 is beautiful 40mm case with one of the most comfortable bracelets. The watch is powered by the Rolex automatic calibre 7135 which is seen in the exhibition caseback. This example is unpolished. It features the beautiful honeycomb dial motif, and the 6 and 9 numerals inspired by the 1950s Rolex dials.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does comes full set with box and papers from the original owner. It was sold in December 2025.

OMEGA Speedmaster Moonshine Ref 310.60.42.50.01.002 In 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished W/ Box And Papers

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era.

This OMEGA Speedmaster is a great modern chronograph. The 42mm case and bracelet are both in Moonshine gold, OMEGA’s 18k yellow gold alloy. The watch features a black dial with contrasting white subdials. The watch features an exhibition caseback displaying the 3861 movement. The watch shows some signs of wear, but we believe it to be unpolished.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article)

The watch comes with a box and papers, it dates to 2026. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The retail price for this watch is $49,300.

OMEGA Speedmaster “SILVER SNOOPY AWARD” 50TH Anniversary Unpolished

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era.

The OMEGA Speedmaster “SILVER SNOOPY AWARD” 50TH Anniversary model is one of the most cherished OMEGA modern Speedmaster models. We believe the steel case is unpolished and measures 42mm in diameter and 47.5mm lug-to-lug. It comes with a stainless steel tapered bracelet and is powered by the OMEGA Co‑Axial Master Chronometer Calibre 3861. We believe the case is unpolished. The bracelet was added. It does not have a nylon or buckle. It is sick.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article)

This watch does not come with a box or papers. It comes with a service box. We do not know the full service history but it is running well.

Breitling B01 Adare Manor Chronograph Ref. AB01185A1L1X1 w/ Box and Papers Brand New Stickered

This stainless steel Breitling chronograph reference AB01185A1L1X1 is a great chronograph made in stainless steel. The case measures approximately 42mm in diameter. It features a green dial and green leather strap.

This was $10,398.38 at retail.

This watch has special logo on the caseback of Adare Manor (Ireland's Most Spellbinding 5 Star Castle Hotel And Spa).

The watch comes in a box and papers, it dates to 2020. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Breitling Shamrock Green Diamond Set Galactic 36 Ref. A37330 Full Set Brand New Stickered

This stainless steel Breitling Galactic 36 chronograph is an awesome watch in stainless steel. The case measures approximately 36mm in diameter. It features a green dial. The bracelet is extremely comfortable. It features a beautiful diamond set bezel.

The watch is brand new and stickered.

The watch comes in a box and papers, it dates to 2020. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 on Folded Oyster Bracelet

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example dates to circa late 1974 with a 3.6M serial. It features a tritium matte dial. The case has been polished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on Rolex folded Oyster 7838 bracelet with 358 end links without date code dating to 1973-1975. The hands have been relumed.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Heuer Carrera Blue Dial Ref. 73353

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013. This Carrera, reference 73353, features an interesting cushion case and blue dial with a tachymeter on the inner bezel. The stainless steel case measures approximately 38mm in diameter and 44mm lug-to-lug. It is paired with a blue leather strap and anonymous stainless steel buckle. The case appears to be unpolished.

This watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the full service history but it is running well.

A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia Thin Ref. 201.027 in 18k White Gold Full Set

This A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia reference 201.027 is a sophisticated modern dress watch. The case is made of 18k white gold, and measures approximately 37mm in diameter, 43mm lug to lug and is 5.9mm thick. The watch is time only and features a silver dial and an exhibition caseback displaying the L093.1 movement. This model is a great entry into the brand.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. The watch dates to 2020. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The retail price for this watch is $27,000.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 1803 in 18k YG Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is an outstanding example. The 18K yellow gold case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter. It features a 4.01M serial dating to 1975. The champagne dial is incredibly attractive. It is paired on a beautiful tan leather strap.

The watch does not come box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Coral Dial Midas Reference 4314 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Midas reference 4314 is an incredible and stylish timepiece. It features a rare coral dial. These dials are extremely beautiful and very special. The case is 18K yellow gold and appears to be unpolished. The bracelet fits a wrist up slightly over 6 inches.

Additional links would need to be purchased and sourced by the next owner.

The case measures 23mm in diameter. The bracelet and deployant are firm.

The watch features a 4.2M serial dating to circa 1976.

For those looking for an amazing Rolex Midas with integrated bracelet, look no further.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Rolex Lapis Lazuli Dial Midas Reference 4314 in 18K White Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Midas reference 4314 is an incredible and stylish timepiece. It features an extremely rare lapis lazuli dial. These dials are very special and highly admired by collectors. The case is 18K white gold and appears to be unpolished. The bracelet fits a wrist up slightly over 6 inches. Additional links would need to be purchased and sourced by the next owner.

The case measures 23mm in diameter. The bracelet and deployant are firm.

The watch features a 4.2M serial dating to circa 1976.

For those looking for an amazing white gold Rolex Midas with integrated bracelet, this is a brilliant watch.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Rolex Tiger Eye Dial Midas Reference 4314 in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Papers

This Rolex Midas reference 4314 is an incredible and stylish timepiece. It features an extremely rare tiger eye stone dial. These dials are highly admired by collectors and have an unrivaled impression on the wrist. The case is 18K yellow gold. It has been lightly polished. The bracelet fits a wrist up slightly over 6 inches. Additional links would need to be purchased and sourced by the next owner.

The case measures 23mm in diameter. The bracelet and deployant are firm.

The watch features a 4.2M serial dating to circa 1976.

For those looking for an amazing stone dial Rolex Midas, look no further!

It does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 1803 in Rose Gold Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a phenomenal example and it is a very rare 18K rose gold. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter. It features a 3.59M serial dating to 1974. The watch was sold at Bucherer. The dial is in amazing condition.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

Rolex Wood Dial Florentine Finish Ladies Datejust Reference 6917 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Box

This Rolex Ladies Datejust reference 6917 an incredible example. It has an 18k yellow gold case and beautiful wood dial. The case appears to be unpolished. It features a Florentine finish on the case and bracelet links. The watch features an 6.48M serial number dating to circa 1980. It measures 26mm in diameter. It is paired on a very cool Jubilee bracelet made by Rolex with G date code that is likely original to the watch.

It comes with a box.

It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar,

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference 3151 Unpolished w/ Extract

Introduced in 1950 at the Basel Fair, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s alarm wristwatch was one of the most significant releases within the watch industry. The later defined “Memovox” would take on several adaptations in the form of and entire product line of pocket watches, as well as stationary and travel clocks. While perhaps not spoken about as frequently as other models, there is no doubt that the Memovox is an icon.

This Memovox is a special example as it was briefly in my personal collection. Both the reference 3150 and 3151 were the first Memovoxes to be introduced commercially. The dial is very nice on this example with blue accents. The case is unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter and is quire long with a length of 42mm lug-to-lug. The lugs flair out and have a lug width of approximately 18mm. It is a two-body case design, with the lugs attached to the bezel and the case back being a snap on. The crowns are unsigned on these models.

Learn more about the Memovox reference 3151 in Charlie Dunne’s Collector’s Guide: The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference 3151.

The watch does not come with box or papers. It was just serviced and is running great. The watch comes with an extract from the archives.

Blancpain Villeret Automatic Ref 1195 on Bracelet in 18k Yellow Gold

This Blancpain Villeret automatic watch is an incredibly cool model. It seems to be the reference 1195. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter. It is paired on an extremely nice bracelet that can fit a wrist approximately 7 inches in circumference.

The watch dates to the 1990s.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Blancpain Villeret Skeleton Reference 0211418S in 18k YG Unpolished

This Blancpain Villeret skeleton model is an incredibly cool model. It seems to be the reference 0211418S. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 33.5mm in diameter and 37mm lug to lug. It is paired on a Blancpain strap and buckle.

The watch dates to the 1990s.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Blancpain Villeret Automatic Complete Calendar Ref 6595 on Bracelet in 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Blancpain Villeret automatic watch is an incredibly cool complicated model. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter and 37mm lug to lug. It is paired on an extremely nice bracelet made by Gay Freres that can fit a wrist approximately 7 inches in circumference.

The watch dates to the 1990s.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Blancpain Villeret Perpetual Calendar Ref 5395 on Bracelet in 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Blancpain Villeret automatic watch is an incredibly cool perpetual calendar model. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter and 37mm lug to lug. It is paired on an extremely nice bracelet that can fit a wrist approximately 6.9 inches in circumference.

The watch dates to the 1990s.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 3800 in Yellow Gold

This Patek Philippe Nautilus is remarkable reference 3800 in 18K yellow gold. The case is very strong and probably has been lightly polished in the past. The dial is absolutely gorgeous and has a bluish/black color. It is powered by an automatic movement. It is an amazing timepiece for either a man or lady. For those looking for an elegant yellow gold Nautilus, this is an outstanding example!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. We do not know the date of production.

Blancpain Villeret Minute Repeater Ref 0033 on Bracelet in 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Box

This Blancpain Villeret is a spectacular model with a minute repeater. The minute repeater is very sophisticated and sounds incredible. These are some of the highest quality chiming watches of the era. This Blancpain minute repeater features a case made by the late Jean-Pierre Hagmann, a master case maker who also crafted the cases for Patek Philippe’s minute repeaters. According to Blancpain, developing the caliber 33 for its fourth masterpiece took more than 10,000 hours. Introduced in 1988, it was one of the smallest minute repeaters yet, measuring just 3.3mm thick. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter and 37mm lug to lug. It is paired on an extremely nice bracelet made by Gay Freres that can fit a wrist approximately 7 inches in circumference.

The watch dates to the 1990s.

It does not come with papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master II Reference 16713 Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master II Reference 16713 is a very beautiful sports watch. The watch dates to circa 2002 with a Y serial. The bracelet code features an AD code dating to circa 2003 and is likely original to the watch. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master II Reference 16710 Unpolished w/ Papers

This Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16710 with “Pepsi” bezel insert is a super attractive watch. The red has faded to a nice salmon/pink!

The case appears to be unpolished. The watch has a P serial, indicating production circa 2000. The watch is 40mm in diameter. The bracelet features a DE date code dating to circa 2001 and is likely original to the watch.

It does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex GMT-Master II Pepsi Reference 16710 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master reference 16710 is a very fun watch. This example dates to circa 2000 with a P serial. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). It features a DE date code on the bracelet dating to circa 2001 and is likely original to the watch.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!

Blancpain Flying Tourbillon Ref. 0023 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished Full Set

This Blancpain Flying Tourbillon reference 0023 is an incredible timepiece. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter and 37mm lug to lug. These watches were made with the help of independent watchmaker Vincent Calabrese.

The watch dates to 1989.

It does come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Cartier Santos Dumont Salmon Dial Reference 1575 in Platinum Unpolished

This Cartier Santos is a classic timepiece that represents one of the iconic designs dating back to 1904. This example is a limited edition with salmon dial and Breguet hands. It is a limited example of 90. The platinum case appears to be unpolished. The watch is a manual wind movement. It measures 27mm in diameter by 36mm in length.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Compax Reference 22703 On GF Bracelet

The Universal Genève Compax reference 22703 is a very cool watch. The dial is extremely attractive. The bezel insert has some wear. The case has been lightly polished. It is a massive chronograph and measures 40mm in diameter. It dates to the mid 1960s. It is paired on a Gay Freres bracelet that is very comfortable.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!

Patek Philippe Retailed by Gübelin Ref 3520 in 18k Clous De Paris Unpolished

This is a beautiful and elegant Patek Philippe Calatrava reference 3520 in 18k yellow gold. It is retailed by Gübelin. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 32mm in diameter by 37mm in length. The case features a beautiful Clous De Paris texture. It features a gold plated buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!

Audemars Piguet Blue Dial Clous de Paris in 18K White Gold Unpolished

This is a beautiful and elegant Audemars Piguet dress watch in 18k white gold. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 33mm in diameter by 38mm in length. The case features a beautiful Clous De Paris textured dial in blue which is extremely uncommon. It features a steel buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!

Cartier Santos Dumont Extra Plat in 18K Yellow Gold with Papers & Later Box

This Cartier Santos is a classic timepiece that represents one of the iconic designs dating back to 1904. This example is a great watch dating to the early 1980s and sold in 1989. The 18k yellow gold case appears to be unpolished. The watch is a manual wind movement. It measures 25mm in diameter by 35mm in length. It features a fantastic octagonal crown. The crystal features some scratches but the dial is not cracked.

The watch comes with papers and a later box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Piaget Polo Lapis Dial Reference 15562 C 701 In 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Piaget Polo reference 7661 C 701 is an incredible timepiece with a stunning lapis lazuli dial. The lapis dial models are incredibly luxurious and rare. The watch is powered by a quartz movement. The case and bracelet is made of 18K yellow gold. It appears to be unpolished. The watch measures 32mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fit a wrist as big as 7.3 inches.

The Piaget Polo was the watch for stylish members of high society as well as car dealers in the 1980s. Famous actress and Bond girl Ursula Andress reportedly helped put the watch on the map when she wore it during the World Polo Cup in the 1980s outside Palm Beach in Wellington, Florida. It’s often a watch seen on the wrists of moneyed Palm Beach residents who bought them new in the 1980s or who inherited them from their parents.

Learn more about the exciting designs and movements of vintage Piaget in the article ‘Undervalued: Vintage Piaget Watches’.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3493 in 18k White Gold Unpolished

This is a very special and refined timepiece by Patek Philippe reference 3493. The case and bracelet are made of 18k white gold. The bracelet is very intricately made and special. It was produced by Jean-Pierre Ecoffey who produced many of these high-end bracelets for Patek, Audemars Piguet, and others. The case measures 26mm by 26mm. It dates to the mid-1960s-1970s. It appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Constellation Lapis Dial Diamond Bezel In 18k Yellow Gold

This OMEGA Constellation is a very beautiful and elegant model from the 1980s. It measures 34mm in diameter by 37mm in length. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It has been lightly polished. The bezel features a beautiful diamond set arrangement. The dial features a rare lapis lazuli configuration. The bracelet should fit a wrist approximately 6.6 inches in circumference. It is powered by a quartz movement.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Oval Shaped Model In 18k Yellow Gold

This is a very beautiful Cartier Tank in 18k yellow gold. The watch measures 28mm in diameter by 32mm lug to lug. The case has been polished in the past. The dial features painted Breguet numerals. It features a Bueche Girod manual wind movement. The case features British import codes and the watch dates to the 1960s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Tiffany & Co Ladies Datejust Reference 69173 w/ Tapestry Dial Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Ladies Datejust reference 69173 is a very special example with a tapestry dial. Additionally, it is double signed and features a Tiffany & Co. stamp. It has a two-tone case. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch features an R serial number dating to circa 1987. It measures 26mm in diameter.

The bracelet features an M date code dating to 1988 and is likely original to the watch.

It comes with box and papers. The watch was just serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Datejust Linen Dial 16014 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 16014 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to circa 1981 with a 6.5M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The watch is in very nice condition. The case appears to be unpolished.

The bracelet code features a G date code dating to circa 1982 and is likely original to the watch.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

OMEGA Constellation 168.004 /14 SC-62 In 18k W/ Box

This OMEGA Constellation is an amazing timepiece. It measures 36mm in diameter by 41mm in length. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It has been lightly polished. The dial features a fantastic champagne color.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3854 Roman Dial In 18k Yellow Gold Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 3854 is an elegant timepiece in fantastic condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on the original strap and buckle. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures approximately 29mm by 29mm. It was sold in 1992.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Ref 3854/3 In 18k Gold Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 3854/3 is an amazing dress watch in fantastic condition. The case appears to be unpolished. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures approximately 29mm by 29mm. It was sold in 1992.

The bracelet is very intricately made and one of the highest quality bracelets on any Patek Philippe. It can fit a wrist slightly over 7.3 inches.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3860/3 In 18k Gold Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 3860/3 is an incredible dress watch in fantastic condition. The case appears to be unpolished. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures approximately 29mm by 31mm. It was sold in 1992.

The dial texture has one of the most captivating patterns we have seen on a Patek Philippe dial. It is really intricate and impressive to look at.

The bracelet is very intricately made and one of the highest quality bracelets on any Patek Philippe. It can fit a wrist slightly over 6.8 inches.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3571-1 In 18k White Gold

This Patek Philippe reference 3571-1 is an incredible dress watch in fantastic condition. The case is lightly polished. The bracelet is unpolished. The case is made of 18k white gold. It measures approximately 26mm by 26mm.

The blue dial has an intricate and impressive texture that is out of this world. The cases were also made in an intensive process with numbers on the frame corresponding to the movement’s plate.

The bracelet is very intricately made and one of the highest quality bracelets on any Patek Philippe. It can fit a wrist slightly over 7.3 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Paris Cristallor Reference 78096 in 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished

This an incredible Cartier model - the Cristallor reference 78096. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. The Cristallor is highly admired for the large dimensions and design. It measures 28mm in diameter by 35mm in length. The case appears to be unpolished. It dates to the 1970s.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 4290 Roman Dial In 18k Yellow Gold Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 4290 is an elegant timepiece in fantastic condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on the original strap and buckle. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures approximately 31mm by 25mm. It was sold in 1992.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Red Dial Tank Must WSTA0054 Unpolished

The Cartier Tank Must is part of the legendary Tank family of watches from the famed jeweler. The watch is an attractive, deep red color. It is paired on the original Cartier strap and Cartier buckle. The watch is unpolished and in excellent condition. The watch measures approximately 22mm is diameter and and 31mm lug to lug.

It does not come with box and papers.

Cartier Blue Dial Tank Must WSTA0055 Unpolished

The Cartier Tank Must is part of the legendary Tank family of watches from the famed jeweler. The dial and strap are a lovely dark blue color. It is paired on the original Cartier strap and Cartier buckle. The watch measures approximately 22mm is diameter and and 31mm lug to lug.

It does not come with box and papers.

We do not know the production date.

Cartier Green Dial Tank Must WSTA0056 Unpolished

The Cartier Tank Must is part of the legendary Tank family of watches from the famed jeweler. This watch features a beautiful green dial. It is paired on the original Cartier strap and Cartier buckle. The watch is unpolished and in excellent condition. The watch measures approximately 22mm is diameter and and 31mm lug to lug.

Corum Coin Watch 1894 Unpolished

This is a very cool Corum coin watch. It is made of 18K gold. It is manual wind movement. it measures approximately 28mm in diameter.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Corum Coin Watch 1896 Unpolished

This is a very cool Corum coin watch. It is made of 18K gold. It is an automatic movement. It measures approximately 35mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

Corum Coin Watch 1926 Unpolished

Certina Argonaut 280 on Bracelet Unpolished

The Certina Argonaut 280 is an incredibly cool and stylish vintage watch. It presents great value for everyone and has a fantastic case design. It measures 37mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). The watch dates to the 1970s. It features a very rare red dial and is paired on an NSA bracelet signed Certina.

This watch was in Eric’s personal collection for many years.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Frank Muller Ref 3801 S6Q in 18k Yellow Gold Full Set

This is a very special Frank Muller reference 3801 S6Q. Frank Muller is one of the many watchmakers who is seeing a resurgence of interest from collectors. This model measures approximately 35mm in diameter. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It features a platinum rotor.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Fortis Rally Diver on Bracelet Unpolished

The Fortis Rally Diver is an incredibly cool and stylish vintage watch. It presents great value for everyone and has a fantastic case design. It measures 37mm in diameter. OMEGA Speedmaster Professional 145.012-67 Unpolished

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was recently serviced and it is running well.

Blancpain Villeret Automatic on Bracelet in Steel Unpolished

This Blancpain Villeret automatic watch is an incredibly cool model. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter. It is paired on an extremely nice bracelet made by Gay Freres.

The watch dates to the 1990s.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Reference S2326B Unpolished

This is a super cool Vulcain Cricket. The massive case measures 41mm lug-to-lug, with a diameter of 38mm (excluding the crown). To learn more about these awesome watches, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Undervalued: Oversized Vulcain Crickets’!

TUDOR Marine National Submariner 9401/0 Unpolished

This TUDOR Submariner reference 9401/0 is a very cool watch which was issued to the Marine Nationale. These MN TUDORs are highly admired and the most desired of the Marine Nationale watches. The watch dates to circa 1976-1977 with a 866,XXX serial. The case back has a 76 engraving.

The hands have been relumed. The case appears to be unpolished.

Cartier Alarm Travel Clock in Sterling Silver

Universal Genève Pink-on-Pink Tri-Compax Reference 12268 in Rose Gold Unpolished

For those looking for an absolutely incredible complicated UG, this is one of the best examples.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
