https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Big Red Daytona Reference 6263 Black Dial Unpolished w/ Box

This Rolex Daytona reference 6263 is an incredible example with a very nice black “Big Red” dial. The dial is spectacular and the lume plots are fully intact, which is rare for vintage Daytonas. The hands and dial have a nice warm lume. The registers are a nice cream color.

It features the correct Mark III screw-down pushers. It is paired on an Oyster 78350 solid link bracelet with 571 end links that appears to be original. The case appears to be unpolished and is extremely crisp with some general wear This watch has a 8.8M case serial number dating to circa 1985. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter. The pushers appear to be the original Mark 3 pushers with ridges and Rolex switched to these pushers in the circa 1981/1982 timeframe for steel Daytonas with screw-down pushers.

The comes with a vintage Rolex box that is period correct and probably original to the watch. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and passed a water resistant check of 5 bar.

This is one of the nicest “Big Red” Daytonas we have ever had at a great price, so we don’t recommend waiting as I have no idea when we will find another like it at this price point.

Rolex Black Dial Date Reference 15037 in 14K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Date reference 15037 is an outstanding example with a black dial. The case is a rare version in 14K yellow gold. It appears to be un unpolished. The case is 34mm in diameter. It is paired on its original Jubilee bracelet with G date code dating to 1982 and appears to be original to the watch. The watch features a 6.7M serial dating to circa 1981. The gold font on the dial is very sharp and resembles the gilt dial Rolex watches of the 1960s. The hands have developed a very attractive patina that appears somewhat green. For those looking for an incredible Rolex Date in yellow gold, this watch is a great option.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Serpico Y Laino Oyster Perpetual Bubbleback Reference 5050 on Gay Frères Bracelet

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual “Bubbleback” reference 5050 is an incredible watch with a non-luminous dial retailed by Serpico Y Laino. Serpico Y Laino is one of the most prestigious names in double-signed dials. It is the Venezuelan retailer known for selling some of the most incredible Rolex and Patek Philippe wristwatches in the 20th century. The watch dates to the late 1940s. The case measures 32mm in diameter by 39mm lug to lug. The dial has developed a beautiful warm tone. It is paired on a beads of rive bracelet made by Gay Frères dating to 1951. It will fit a wrist approximately 7 inches. The case is possibly unpolished.

For those looking for an incredible double-signed Rolex, this is such a special timepiece.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 6702 in 18K Unpolished

We are thrilled to offer this vintage Rolex ladies Datejust! It is a very rare reference 6702 with brick trextured bezel and bracelet similar to the Florentine finish. The watch dates to 1967 and features an 1.74M serial. The case measures 26mm in diameter by 31mm lug to lug. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on the original Rolex bracelet made by Gay Freres dating to 1967. Both the bracelet and bezel feature an incredible textured layout which is so uncommon in vintage Rolex models.

This is one of the most interesting and beautiful watches on the site, so we are very excited for the next owner. The bracelet measures approximately 7 inches.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Explorer I Ref. 1016

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example dates to circa late 1966 with a 1.61M serial. It features a tritium matte dial. The watch may have originally been born with a gilt dial. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on a later service Oyster bracelet dated to circa 2000. The case has been polished in the past.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Rolex “Red Sub” Submariner Ref. 1680 Ghost Bezel

This Rolex reference 1680 “Red Sub” is a great ‘date’ Submariner in stainless steel. The watch features a 2.4M serial number and dates to circa 1970. It is paired with a reference 9315 Rolex folded link Oyster bracelet dating to 1970. It comes with the correct 280 end-links. We believe it to be original to the watch. The dial is attractive and features Submariner in red writing. The bezel insert has faded to a grey “Ghost” color. The case has been polished in the past.

The lug-to-lug length is approximately 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown.

The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Datejust Ref. 1601 “Ghost Dial”

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 1601 is no exception.

The watch dates to circa 1978 and features a 5.0M serial. It features a stunning grey “Ghost” pie-pan dial, with white Rolex text. It is also a “Sigma” dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a reference 62510H Jubilee bracelet, the clasp is a service replacement. The case is very nice and appears to be unpolished. The bracelet has been lightly polished. The dial has been re-lumed. The hands have not.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. It recently passed a water resistance test. We do not know the full service history but the watch is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Exotic Dial C Case Ref S2325B Unpolished on Bracelet

This Vulcain Cricket reference S2325B is a very rare example with exotic dial with blue accents and blue demarcations on the outer track. The stainless steel case is unpolished and the diameter is 34.5mm and 39mm lug-to-lug. The cases on these models are among my favorites of all Vulcain Crickets. These hour markers are incredible and super interesting to admire under a loupe. They are very uncommon and I imagine it would have been a very difficult design to produce. It is paired on the original bracelet which is very rare. The watch has been in Eric’s collection for several years and is one of the coolest Vulcain Crickets we have ever seen.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not come with a box or papers.

Behrens Inventor BHR030 Full Set Like New Stickered

This Behrens Inventor reference BHR030 is an ultra light 20g watch with an openworked dial and power reserve. The oblique style case measures roughly 38 x 42.5mm and is made of titanium. The watch features a sapphire crystal and BM02 base movement. It comes with an integrated rubber strap and titanium pin buckle. It is in like new condition with the original caseback sticker.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. It was originally sold in 2026. We do not know the full service history, but the watch is running well.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Black Dial Ref. 4500V/110A-B483 Full Set Unpolished + Straps

This Vacheron Constantin Overseas reference X45AV483 is a great modern sports watch from one of the most renowned and historically significant manufacturers. The case and bracelet are made of stainless steel. The watch is in excellent condition and appears to be unpolished with minor signs of wear. The case measures 41mm in diameter. It is paired on the stainless steel integrated bracelet and it comes with a leather and rubber strap from Vacheron Constantin.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers, and is from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. The watch was originally sold in November of 2021.

Mulco Non-Luminous Two-Tone Dial Chronograph in Stainless Steel

This Mulco chronograph is a great vintage watch in stainless steel. The case measures approximately 35.5mm in diameter and 42mm lug-to-lug. It features a gorgeous non luminous two-tone silver dial. The watch is manually wound. It is paired with the Private Eye beads of rice bracelet. It dates to the 1950s. For those looking for an interesting chronograph this is a great timepiece.

The watch does not come with a box or papers.We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date United States Merchant Marine Reference 18038 Unpolished

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week.

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 is an incredibly attractive watch with an unbelievably rare United States Merchant Marine dial. This particularly with this original non-Rolex gold bracelet makes for a seriously impressive watch. We would rather have this logo on our watch than the signature of a despot who allegedly committed patricide, honestly! These Merchant Marine Day-Dates are extremely rare and only a few are known. The 18K-gold case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This particular example dates to circa 1978 with a 5.4M serial.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex "Pre-Daytona" Chronograph Ref. 6238

The Rolex “Pre-Daytona” reference 6238 is a great chronograph for Rolex enthusiasts. This example features a silver dial with a printed tachymeter scale. This particular example dates to circa 1965 with a 1.1M serial number. The case is 36mm in diameter and has been polished in the past. It is paired with a C+I bracelet dating to 1969.

This watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished on Oval Link Bracelet

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This is a very special Explorer reference 1016. The case back features an incredible Spanish engraving “Trabajador Distinguido 1975” (Distinguished Worker). It was given to a Mexican field worker by his boss who owned a farm after being a loyal employee for a number of years. The font is hand engraved and particularly attractive.

This example dates to circa late 1972 with a 3.00M serial. It features a tritium matte dial. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on Rolex Oval link bracelet made by Joske's in Mexico.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Rolex Wide Boy Rolex Cup Guards Polo Club Windsor Datejust Ref 1601 Non-Luminous

This is a special Rolex “Wide Boy” Datejust reference 1601 that was likely presented by the then Prince of Wales and now King Charles to the winner at the 1978 Guards Polo Club. It features an attractive and uncommon non-luminous dial which was probably chosen due to the rough nature of the sport. The case has been lightly polished but is attractive. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The watch has a 3.3M serial number dating the watch to circa 1973. It is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet with 55 end links dating to 1970.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph 26331ST.OO.1220ST.02 w/ Service Box

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak reference 26331ST.OO.1220ST.02 is one of the epic Royal Oak chronograph models. The watch measures 41mm in diameter and has a stainless steel case and bracelet. It will fit a wrist slightly above 7 inches. This example features a black “Grande Tapisserie" dial with silver subregisters.

This watch does not come with a box or papers. It comes with a service box. We do not know the full service history but it is running well. We do not know the date of production.

Rolex Submariner Ref. 16610LV “Kermit” Unpolished w/ Card and Bracelet

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This 16610LV features an M serial number and is paired with a “super” Jubilee bracelet with hidden clasp. This reference features a green bezel, a black dial and a date window at the 3 o'clock position. The watch is in incredible condition and appears to be unpolished. The watch comes with an additional oyster bracelet that is fully stickered.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes with the Rolex card and dates to 2008. It does not come with a box.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - April 3, 2026

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Heuer Skipperera Reference 7754 Unpolished

I love the Heuer Skipperera reference 7754. It is one of my favorite chronographs and is a very special and rare timepiece. There are only a handful of Skippereras known, making them a very desired and seldom seen watch in the wild. This example dates to the circa 1968.

We believe the case is unpolished. The dial has some patina on the edge and near the 2 hour marker, but it is extremely beautiful and paired with this strap it is well complemented.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it running well. This example is fresh to market and comes from the family of the original owner.

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1R-001 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is perhaps the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated stainless steel watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today. This particular model is a reference5990/1R-001 dating to 2022. It features the blue dial variant.

It is powered by the CH 28-520 C FUS.

The watch comes from the original owner. The rose gold case appears to be unpolished. It features very minimal signs of wear. For those looking for an incredible complicated Nautilus, this is a fantastic choice! We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Green "50th Anniversary" 15510ST.OO.1320ST.04 Full Set Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the most iconic and sought after sports watches on the planet. Designed by Gerald Genta, collectors have become enamored with this important timepiece from AP.

This Audemers Piguet Royal Oak reference 15510ST.OO.1320ST.04 is an incredible stainless steel sports watch. The case measures 41mm in diameter. The dial is a very special with a khaki green “Grande Tapisserie” pattern. These models were produced to celebrate the 50th Anniversary of the iconic model and are among the most desired Audemars Piguet models.

The watch was sold in late 2022. It comes from the original owner. The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

IWC Portuguese Minute Repeater Reference 5240 in Platinum w/ Box

This is a very sophisticated IWC Portuguese Minute Repeater in platinum. These platinum models were produced in 50 examples. It measures 42mm in diameter. The case is possibly unpolished. The minute repeater is very nice on these examples and these are considered one of the best IWC models of the period.

The watch dates to the mid 1990s. The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. We understand it was serviced in circa 2022 by IWC, but we do not have the paperwork. It is running well.

Rolex Daytona Black Dial Reference 116520 Full Set Unpolished

The reference 116520 was produced from the course of 2000-2016. This specific example was sold in 2014. The watch measures 40mm in diameter. Finding an unpolished reference 116520 is very difficult. We sold the watch a few years ago in NOS condition. It has some wear, but it is still very nice.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Vacheron Constantin Historiques Cornes de Vache 1955 in Platinum 5000H/000P-B058 Full Set

The Vacheron Constantin Historiques Cornes de Vache 1955 is among the most elegant contemporary chronograph models. These have fantastic multi-tone dials that are better than most of the Holy Trinity dials. This example is very attractive and dates to circa 2019.

We believe the platinum case is unpolished. The case measures 38.5mm in diameter. It is paired on an FPJ strap with the platinum Vacheron buckle.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well..

Seiko Credor Eichi II In Rose Gold Full Set

Sitting at the top of the Credor line, the Eichi II is nothing short of a work of art. Powered by the manually finished, black-polished spring drive caliber 7R14, the Eichi II is entirely crafted at Seiko’s storied Micro Artist Studio (MAS) in Shiojiri, led by master Yoshifusa Nakazawa-San. The porcelain dial is hand painted (yes, including the logo and the letters and numbers at 6 o’clock!). The MAS designed rose-gold case is unpolished and measures 39.5mm x 10.3mm. Original crocodile strap has never been worn. The watch is made in Japan, but the deployant is made in Italy!

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it running well. It was purchased in 2019. This example comes from the original owner. The watch retailed for $42,000.

Cartier Trinity Ring Ladies Watch in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Cartier timepiece is extremely elegant and chic. The bezel features the Trinity Ring or “trois anneaux” design associated with the brand. The watch is powered by a quartz movement. It measures 27mm in diameter and is made of 18k yellow gold. The bezel features white, rose, and yellow gold rings. The bracelet is extremely comfortable. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it running well.

OMEGA Art-Deco Wristwatch in 18K Yellow Gold

This OMEGA wristwatch is a very unique and interesting timepiece. The watch dates to the late 1910s. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures 25mm in diameter by 40mm in length. The dial features very interesting painted numerals.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch.

TUDOR Hodinkee Black Bay 58 Unpolished w/ Papers & Hang Tag

The TUDOR Black Bay 58 is one of the coolest modern watches. This particular example is one of only 100 examples produced for HODINKEE’s Friends & Family. I love the model and have it within my personal collection! It is particularly interesting to vintage watches enthusiasts, as the design is very much inspired by the iconic Rolex “Big Crown” Submariner references 6538, 6200, 5510, and the TUDOR Submariner reference 7924. The dials have a similar texture to the matte dials with gold accents inspired by gilt dials. For those who love HODINKEE and appreciate the greatness of the TUDOR Black Bay 58, this is an incredible timepiece to wear in any setting.

The watch has some light wear and scratches, but it is unpolished. It comes from an important and, more importantly, SUPER COOL individual within the HODINKEE universe. I absolutely love my HODINKEE Black Bay 58 and believe that the dual-signed examples, like this one, are very special.

I will never forget seeing my first TUDOR Black Bay 58 on the wrist of Jason Fried in Chicago many years ago and I was floored by the watch. I will never forget seeing the special HODINKEE version on the wrist of Brandon Frazin around 2019 and I was also floored by how awesome the watch was. I was honored to receive one shortly thereafter as a gift from Ben Clymer and it is a watch that really is special to me, particularly since I poured so many hours into writing for the site in its formative years and learned so much in the process. I am excited to be able to offer one for sale now and think whoever gets it will be extremely happy to own it. Phillips recently sold one for around $22,000 through its Perpetual program and this one is less than half the price.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with papers & hang tag.

Piaget Diamond Set 4354 in 18K White Gold Unpolished w/ Box

This Piaget diamond-set wristwatch is a brilliant timepiece with incredible craftsmanship. It appears to be a reference 4354. The case is white gold and appears to be unpolished. The case measures 20mm in diameter.

This would be an incredible jewelry piece for a Gala event - particularly when considering the diamond watch is under $30,000. It makes an incredible impression.

It does not come with papers. It does come with box. We do not know the service history of this watch.

Heuer Viceroy Autavia Reference 1163V on GF Bracelet Unpolished

In my opinion, Heuer "Viceroy" Autavias are some of the best buys in vintage watches. This Autavia reference 1163V is nicknamed the "Viceroy" as it was made for a promotion with Viceroy cigarette company of the United States of America in the 1970s. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 42mm in diameter.

The watch is in outstanding condition. It was just serviced and comes from the original owner.

It is one of the nicest examples we have seen!

It is paired on a Gay Freres bracelet dating to circa 1972 which is likely original to the watch.

You can read more about the history of these watches in this article by Jeff Stein.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex White Dial Daytona Reference 116520 w/ Papers Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona reference 116520 is a fantastic automatic sports watch. This example is an attractive watch. The case appears to be unpolished. It was originally purchased in 2010.

The watch comes with papers. It does not come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Cartier Paris Tank Normale Petite 78093 in 18K Gold Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer this Cartier Tank Normale. The watch is in outstanding condition and features the original Paris dial. The case measures 20mm in diameter and 27.5mm lug-to-lug. It is the same size as Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis’s Cartier, which I sold in 2017 at auction.

It comes on a Cartier deployant.

The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It dates to late 1970s. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Daniel Roth Extra Plat 2517 Diamond Set in Yellow Gold Unpolished

Daniel Roth has seen a great deal of admiration from watch collectors over recent years, and for good reason! This Extra Plat reference 2517 is a spectacular model with an impressive guilloche dial. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It dates to late 1990s. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 26mm in diameter by 32mm lug to lug. The diamond setting on this watch is extremely impressive and amongst the best from the watchmaking industry. The watch is powered by a an automatic calibre.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - April 4, 2025

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 FAP Peruvian Air Force Unpolished

Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 FAP Peruvian Air Force Unpolished

This Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 is one of the best examples I have ever seen. The dial is spectacular and the lume plots remain fully intact, which is rare for vintage Daytonas. The hands and dial have a nice warm lume. It features correct Mark II screw-down pushers. It is paired on what appears to be the original 78350 Oyster bracelet with 571 end links. The case is ridiculously crisp and unpolished. It features its original rare “Mark 1” acrylic bezel, which is in strong condition.

The Peruvian Air Force ordered a small number of Daytonas in the 1970s and they say “FUERZA AREA DEL PERÚ” around the edge and opposite of that on the other edge of the case back is a number assigned to the watch, which in this case is 488.

This watch has a 4.01 million case serial number dating to circa 1975. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 5508 Glossy Dial

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic sports watches that was introduced in 1953. It is a model that represents the greatest attributes of the manufacturer. The reference 5508 began production in circa 1958 and lasted to 1962. It is also the last of the no-crown-guard Submariners. This is an insanely attractive example that dates to the tail end of the reference’s production in circa 1962. The glossy dial is very beautiful with warm-yellow lume. It also features the "exclamation point" at six o'clock. The case measures 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). It has been lightly polished, but is strong.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex Submariner Gilt Dial Reference 5513

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic sports watches and a model that represents the greatest attributes of the manufacturer. I am very excited about this example, as it has an outstanding gilt dial! The puffy yellow lume and gilt font make for an outstanding impression each time you look down at the watch on your wrist. The case is lightly polished, but it is very strong.

This example has a 1.3M serial number dating the watch to circa 1966. The watch is paired on a tight and crisp 7205 Rivet bracelet with 80 end links - which is in phenomenal condition!

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Tropical Datejust Reference 1600

The Datejust is an iconic watch introduced in 1945. This Datejust reference 1600 is defined by the smooth bezel. It has a beautiful tropical dial with a warm tone that reminds me of bark on a tree. The watch has a crystal without cyclops that was added by the previous owner, giving it an Explorer-like look. The case is lightly polished but strong. It dates to circa 1967.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 6305 in 18K Gold

The Datejust is one of the greatest watches introduced in 1945. While we’ve highlighted the great value in steel Datejusts in our past article ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’, models such as this 1953 18K yellow gold example are just divine. This example has the distinct red numerals in the date window complementing the faded “Officially” text on the dial which has developed a beautiful patina. Additionally, it has remarkable “dagger” markers and “alpha” hands.

The 18K gold case is lightly polished, but has a great impression on the wrist. It measures 36mm in diameter and 44mm lug-to-lug. The case back in engraved with the name C.V. WYLIE. The watch is paired on a very crisp Jubilee bracelet dating to the 2nd quarter of 1953.

I’m thrilled to be offering such an awesome watch that will surely be a favorite in the next owner’s collection!

The watch does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA Seamaster Professional 300 Reference 165.024

While much of the conversation of OMEGA surrounds the iconic Speedmaster, the Seamaster truly deserves merit as the longest continually produced watch model from the manufacturer. The OMEGA Seamaster was introduced in 1948, and in circa 1957 the model would take on the more robust style in the Seamaster Professional.

This example is very attractive with a beautifully warm patina in the sword hands and dial. The case is lightly polished but strong. It measures 42mm in diameter, and 47mm lug-to-lug. This example has a 27M movement number dating it to circa 1968.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Tiffany & Co. Reference 6119G-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe Calatrava is one of the most refined timepieces. This particular reference 6119G-001 has a very beautiful design with applied hour markers and sleek grey dial that is vertically brushed. On top of the beautiful look, it features the most prestigious American retailer’s name on the double-signed dial - Tiffany & Co.

The white gold case is unpolished and was only worn a handful of times. The Clous de Paris bezel adds a touch of flair.

The watch comes with box and papers and comes from the original owner and was purchased in 2021.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-65

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding OMEGA Speedmaster reference 105.012-65 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

The reference 105.012 was the first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied OMEGA logo.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to the excitement of many Speedy lovers. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line from 1963 to 1966. The hour and minute hands have been relumed.

The case was made by Huguenin Frères, and is lightly polished but strong. The crown has been replaced during service. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. The reference 105.012-65 is highly prized and collected because it was the exact same model worn by Neil Armstrong to the Moon.

The watch does not come with box, papers or an Extract.

It was just recently serviced and is running great!

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video and read the corresponding article.

Heuer Autavia Reference 2446 First Generation Big Subs

The Heuer Autavia is recognized by collectors as one of the great sports chronographs of the 1960s. Along with many of its contemporaries of the era, it is powered by the Valjoux 72. This example is the first generation case of the Autavia with larger sundials. The dimensions are 38mm (48mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Tiffany & Co. Pocket Watch

This is a very impressive Tiffany & Co. pocket watch dating to the early 20th century. The dial is in fantastic condition with painted-Roman numerals and Arabic numerals. It features very ornate decoration on the 18K gold case with characters and floral motifs. It measures 28mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Junghans Max Bill Unpolished

The Junghans Max Bill is one of the great design watches from the 1960s. Various collectors adore this model, and it represents tremendous value for the price. This example features a nice warm patina, with blemishes in the 6-7 o’clock section. The case measures 34mm in diameter and is unpolished.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Zenith Covergirl El Primero Reference A3818 Unpolished

This Zenith “Covergirl” El Primero reference A3818 is such a sweetie! Collectors can attest to the fact that the Zenith El Primero reference A3818 is one of the most desirable vintage Zenith models. Its nickname "Covergirl" derives from its feature on the cover of the Zenith book by Manfred Rössler.

Most examples that I see have overpolished cases. This example is unpolished and in great condition..

The watch came from the family of the original owner who was employed by Zenith Electronics. We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

Zenith El Primero Reference A386 Unpolished

Introduced in 1969, the Zenith El Primero Reference A386 sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. Powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019, the highly emphasized red chronograph hand glides over the dial in the smoothest manner.

The model’s hands and hour markers feature a pale yellow tritium that immediately catches the attention of the wearer. Competing with the tri-colored subdials, the attributes of this watch are really a beautiful testament to the sports watches of yesteryear.

The watch is 38mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 45.5mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The case remains unpolished showing its original finishing and bevels.

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 16013 w/ Roman Numerals Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Datejust is very attractive timepiece with a beautiful white dial and applied-Roman numerals. The watch dates to 1986 with a 9.2M serial number. The steel case and gold bezel are unpolished and measure 36mm in diameter. The watch is paired on 62523H bracelet. The bracelet is in good condition and has a little play. It is not very stretched.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box or papers. It recently passed a water-resistance test to 3 bar.

Rolex Precision Reference 9708 Unpolished

This Rolex Precision is a very elegant timepiece in 18K yellow gold. The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The dial is in spectacular condition. It features very bold dauphine hands. The watch is paired on a Rolex strap and buckle. The watch dates to circa 1961.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is a super cool example with warm tones of discoloration that give a stunning appearance to the dial. It has an an 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. The bracelet may be unpolished and is well worn. It has a little play, but is not overly stretched. The watch features an T serial number dating to circa 1996.

The watch comes from the widow of the original owner. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master II Reference 126719BLRO in White Gold Full Set

The Rolex GMT-Master II Reference 126719BLRO is a fantastic modern sports watch in white gold. It is a 40mm case. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, the GMT remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch remains unpolished and is like new - maybe worn a few times. It comes from the original owner, and is “full set” with its original box and papers. It was sold in January 2023. It has stickers added on from the owner (not from the factory).

This Vulcain Grand Prix is a super cool time-only model from the manufacurer dating to the late 1950s. The watch features a super cool honeycomb dial with beautiful patina. The case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The crown is a replacement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

Rolex Zenith Daytona Black Dial Reference 16520 Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a black dial Rolex Daytona reference 16520. It has a U serial dating it to circa 1997. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. The case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18038 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 is a super cool example with a beautiful dial. It has an an 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. The bracelet has a little play, but is not overly stretched. The watch features an 5.3 serial number dating to circa 1978.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Reference 15300R w/ Service Papers + Service Box

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is a super clean example paired on a rubber strap. The rose gold case measures 39mm. It is paired on a brand new rubber strap. It comes with an original leather strap. The case was lightly polished.

The watch comes with service papers and a small service box.

This Breguet wristwatch is a very cool and interesting timepiece. The case measures 31mm in diameter (36mm lug-to-lug). The white-gold case is unpolished. It is paired on the original strap and buckle.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

Heuer Tropical Autavia Reference 73363 Unpolished

This Heuer Autavia reference 73363 is a super cool example with a bold unpolished case. The dial has developed a tropical brown look. The watch is powered by the calibre 7733.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. The watch was just serviced. The central seconds hand appears to be replaced.

Heuer Carrera Reference 7753NST w/ Service Box + Papers

This Heuer Carrera reference 7753NST is a very cool and attractive example. It dates to circa 1968. The case is very nice. It has possibly been lightly polished.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

It comes with a serve box and papers.

TUDOR Glossy Submariner Reference 7928 Unpolished

This TUDOR Submariner reference 7928 has a very attractive glossy dial with warm lume. The case production dates to circa 1967. It features the rounded crown guards, has the correct “Long 5” bezel insert and crown. The case is unpolished. It is paired on a period correct stretch C&I bracelet made for Rolex.

The watch does not have box or papers. It is powered by the automatic caliber 390 and is 40mm in diameter. It was recently serviced and passed a 5 bar water test.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex GMT-Master Ref. 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a very awesome 1675 featuring a 2.99M serial number, dating to circa 1972. The case is unpolished and in fantastic condition. It is paired on a Rolex Oyster folded link 7836 bracelet with 380 end links which are correct.

The aluminum bezel insert is extremely attractive with a petrol blue and faded red. The MK I dial has super attractive lume which has developed into a warm yellow.

We do not know the service history. It was recently regulated and is running great. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 3710/1A-001 w/ Certificate of Origin & Papers

The Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 3710/1A-001 very special and interesting Nautilus reference. Released in 1998, the reference 3710 reintroduced the jumbo case within the model. The stainless steel case appears to be unpolished. It measures 42mm in diameter. The watch dates to circa 1998 and was sold in circa 2000.

The watch comes with the original Certificate of Origin and a Patek Philippe warranty papers and leather folder. It does not come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Wide Boy Day-Date 1803 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a phenomenal gold watch. The case is unpolished and looks outstanding as you can see in the photos below. It has a very dressy look with distinctly broad hour markers and hands, giving it the name “Wide Boy”. The case appears to be unpolished. It features a 2.95M serial dating to circa 1972.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Red Submariner 1680 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa late 1972 with a 2.701M serial. The case appears to be unpolished.. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is a nice attractive color. It is paired on a folded link bracelet dating to circa 1971 which is likely original. The bezel insert has a nice faded grey which looks incredible.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Luce Reference 5261R-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe Aquanaut is one of the most desired sports watches on the planet, and this 5261R-001 is a spectacular complicated iteration on the model. The combination of the 18K rose gold case and dial/strap color create a vibrant and youthful look and is part of the “Luce” line.

This example is in great condition. The case appears to be unpolished. The rubber is uncut!

It comes with box and papers. It was sold in 2024.

Rolex Sprite Destro GMT-Master II Reference 126720VTNR Full Set Unpolished Brand New

The Rolex “Destro” or “Sprite” GMT-Master reference 126720VTNR is a watch that took the world by storm. These are incredible timepieces. The watch is brand new.

Please do not make offers. The price is firm.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE.

It comes full set with box and papers. It dates to 2024.

Rolex Submariner Reference 16618 in 18K Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 16618 is an outstanding Submariner dating to circa 1989 with an L serial. The 18k yellow fold case is in fantastic condition and appears to be unpolished. The case has developed beautiful patina. This is a Charlie Dunne style watch. According to the Rolex Submariner book by Nicholas Foulkes, 20,568 examples of the reference 16618 were produced! The hands appear to be replaced service hands.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Universal Genève Compax Pocket Watch Reference 22548 Unpolished

This Universal Genève Compax reference 22548 is an incredibly cool pocket watch. The case design is very angular and features a disco volante bezel style. The rectangular pushers are very attractive from the profile of this case. The bow is also extremely cool.

The watch measures 47mm in diameter. The stainless steel case appears to be unpolished and in great shape. The watch is powered by the calibre 292. It was delivered to the United States.

A similar example was shown within a scene of the 2023 Ferrari film, and we nearly had a heart attack. Perhaps one of the best vintage watches in a film in recent years! Also, one of our clients has a similar example, and he is one of the coolest individuals Charlie Dunne has ever met. You could join this elite group by buying this one. Charlie Dunne will worship you!

The watch does not come with box. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Ref. 855 Unpolished

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 855 is one of my favorite watches of all time. This example dates to circa mid 1960s. The case measures 37mm in diameter and wears excellent. The case appears to be unpolished. The luminous material on the dial and hands are super attractive and it has a nice bold font.

The crown at 2 o’clock is replaced.

The watch is powered by the calibre 825, the first automatic alarm wristwatch to incorporate the calendar complication. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement.

Along with the Atmos clock and Reversos, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. Perhaps the quintessential Memovox is the reference E 855 from the 1960s. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

The watch does not come with box or papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

To learn more about this model, read Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855.

TUDOR Submariner Reference 7016/0 Unpolished

This TUDOR Submariner is a fantastic sports watch. The watch was produced in circa 1974. The watch is in excellent condition. The case appears to be unpolished. The bracelet is period correct Rolex C&I bracelet dating to 1975. The hands have been color matched recently.

It does not come with box and papers. It has just been serviced. It features a TrueDome crystal. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Day-Date Black Honeycomb 228238 in 18K YG Full Set Brand New

The Rolex Day-Date is a very luxurious watch. The black honeycomb dial makes for a very sophisticated and rich look. The watch is a reference 228238. The 18k yellow gold case appears to be unpolished. It is stickered and brand new. It measures 40mm in diameter.

Please do not make offers. The price is firm. The owner wrote in pen on the white cardboard sleeve. See photos below.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold in 2022.

Rolex Cosmograph Daytona 116519LN White Gold Oysterflex Full Set

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116519LN with grey dial is a fantastic iteration of the iconic sports watch. Paired on its original Oysterflex band, it is truly suited to be worn in all active situations.

The watch is well worn and has scratches. The strap is quite short and will fit approximately 6 inches. A new strap would need to be purchased for the next owner if larger. This is your responsibility.

The watch features a grey dial, black Cerachrom bezel, black registers and the beautiful red-arched “Daytona” font. The case is in 18K white gold and is powered by the calibre 4130.

It comes “full set” with original box and papers from the original owner and was sold in 2019.

The owner wrote in pen on the white cardboard sleeve. See photos below.

Rolex Explorer II Ref. 1655

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due to its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

This example has been polished, but it is very attractive. It dates to circa 1979 (6.3 million serial number). It features the automatic calibre 1575 made by Rolex.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It just passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

Heuer Camaro Exotic Dial Reference 73643NT

This Heuer Camaro reference 73643NT is a very cool watch with Exotic racing dial. I always excited to offer very cool and interesting chronographs like this. The watch stainless steel. The watch is powered by the Valjoux 7736 manual wind chronograph movement. It comes on an annoynmous strap and buckle. The case has been polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5968G-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5968G-001 is one of the great contemporary sports watches. At 42,2mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions. The case is white gold. It appears to be unpolished. It has some wear. The watch is powered by the CH 28-520 C and has a flyback chronograph.

It comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was purchased in 2024.

Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer Ref 2443 By Heuer

Before Abercrombie & Fitch was the “middle of the mall store” as Jeff Stein coined it, the company was the preeminent destination for outdoor sporting apparel. Among the high-quality goods you would expect to find were the incredible sports watches by Heuer. The most desirable models from the retailer would be the Seafarer.

This reference 2443 is the first of two variants with the “psychedelic” tidal register in graduated colors. variant. It is much more similar to the Seafarer reference 346. The later version had more of a Tiffany blue color in the minute register and the tidal register was both that Tiffany blue color and yellow rather than graduated colors. All of these early Seafarer models are extremely rare.

The watch features a Valjoux 72 movement which was modified to incorporate a tide complication. The case is polished. The crown is replaced. This Seafarer has a steel case and measures in at 38mm wide. It has a Heuer buckle from later. The hands were replaced by the legendary Heuer watchmaker Hans Schrag and they were vintage Heuer hands.

The watch does not come with box and papers.We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore 25770ST.OO.D050BU.02

The Audemars Piguet 25770ST.OO.D050BU.02.01 is one of the epic Royal Oak Offshore models. The yellow dial and strap are very attractive colors with a pop! We do not know the date of production. The case has been polished in the past. It measures 42mm in diameter.

This watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Ladies Royal Oak Offshore 77151ST Green

The Audemars Piguet ladies Royal Oak Offshore 77151ST is one of the epic watches from AP. The green dial and strap are very attractive colors with a pop! We do not know the date of production. The case has been polished in the past. It measures 28mm in diameter.

This watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

LeCoultre Polaris II Ref E 870 Unpolished

Along with the Atmos clock and Reversos, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. This Polaris II E 870 is a super funky and cool alarm-dive watch. It dates to the 1970s and you can tell this off first impression. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model. The pastel blue bezel insert and dial make the Polaris II one of the most unique looking watches. It is such a futuristic design and fun watch to admire on the wrist.

It is paired on an NSA bracelet from the period. This is a similar style bracelet it would have been sold on, but instead signed JLC.

The case measures 43mm in diameter and 49mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished. The luminous material on the dial and hands are super attractive with a slight amount of loss in the hour markers. The watch is powered by the calibre 916, a high-beat automatic alarm wristwatch to incorporate the calendar complication.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Benrus Type I - Class A Unpolished

The Benrus Type I Class A wristwatch is one of the iconic US Military issued watches and is beginning to develop a cult following over the most recent years. The watch features fixed spring bars and is paired on a black nylon strap. The asymmetrical case appears to be unpolished. It measures at 43mm in diameter and 15mm in thickness.

The watch dates to November 1979. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

By Charlie Dunne

F.P.Journe Pre-Souscription Chronomètre à Résonance in Platinum with Brass Movement w/ Box & Papers

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. Mr. Journe is one of the most significant watchmakers of the past century and the Chronomètre à Résonance is arguably his most iconic model.

This 38mm F.P.Journe Chronomètre à Résonance in platinum with brass movement is one of the first 25 examples produced. This yellow gold dial has developed a sensational and shimmery patina, which is the charm of these early Journe watches. It is considered the most beautiful by collectors. The two-tone sub-dials are also original, which is important as these are sometimes replaced and ruins the look. The engravings are shallow.

It comes full set with the correct early original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Meters First Dial Reference 5513 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is an awesome example with attractive orange lume. The watch has a 1.6M serial dating to circa 1967. It features the original bezel insert. We believe it is unpolished. It is paired on a C&I bracelet.

Learn more about these watches within Charlie Dunne’s article “ Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512 ’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

OMEGA Speedmaster "Tintin" Reference 311.30.42.30.01.004 Full Set Unpolished

The OMEGA Speedmaster is, of course, one of the most iconic and important watches ever made. While it was originally introduced in 1957 for drivers in automotive racing, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of both exploration and sport.

This model is the cult classic "Tintin" reference 311.30.42.30.01.004. The rally-like red and white checkered minute track could be seen as a racing feature. According to OMEGA, it is in fact an homage to the rocket featured in the Belgian magazine Tintin’s cover of “Explorers on the Moon”.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 w/ Papers

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’ .

This example features a 1.72M serial number, dating to circa 1967. The case has been polished, but it is strong with good form. The aluminum bezel insert has taken on a very nice fade and complements the puffy lume. The watch comes a stamped booklet and chronometer papers (which are dated to February of 1968. It is paired on a 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 80 end links (dated to the first quarter of 1968). It was recently serviced by Dr. Greg Petronzi (@truepatina) and is running great.

Rolex Submariner Feet First Reference 5512

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is an awesome example with attractive orange lume. The watch has a 3.3M serial dating to circa 1974. It features the original bezel insert. The case has been lightly polished, but is strong. It is paired on a folded link Oyster bracelet.

Learn more about these watches within Charlie Dunne’s article “ Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512 ’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. It features a TrueDome D 19 crystal. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar. We have blurred out the case back - as it has the original owner’s social security number and name.

Rolex Blue Dial Datejust Reference 1601 w/ Box Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 1601 is an awesome timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). We believe the case is unpolished. It features a beautiful and uncommon blue dial. The dates to circa 1972 with a 2.89M serial.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Two-Tone Daytona 116523 Full Set w/ Service Card

This Rolex Daytona is a very cool reference 116523 in two tone. The watch dates to circa 2005 with a D serial. It was sold in 2006. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). The case has been polished in the past.

It comes with a box or papers and a service card dating to 2015. The service history is unknown beyond 2015, but it is running well. It has just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Submariner Reference 14060 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 14060 dates to circa 1995 with a W serial. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It has just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Date Reference 15010 Unpolished

This Rolex Date reference 15010 is a fantastic watch with a beautiful silver dial. The case 34mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. It features an R serial dating to circa 1998.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history.

Patek Philippe 5207P-001 Minute Repeating Tourbillon Service Sealed Full Set

Patek Philippe 5207P-001 is an incredible timepiece and one of the models that the manufacturer is most proud of. This model is a perpetual calendar tourbillon with minute repeating complication. The platinum case measures 41mm in diameter. It features a beautiful salmon dial.

The watch comes with the original box and papers. It was sent in for service in 2022, and just came back from Patek Philippe. It remains in the service wrap from Patek Philippe.

Rolex Explorer Reference 1016

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989.

This example features an 9M serial dating it to circa 1986. The case is has been polished in the past. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive.

The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 37mm Ref 15450ST.OO.1256ST.01 W/ Box

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the most iconic and sought after sports watches on the planet. This 15450ST reference is a sharp and crisp 37mm example with its striking white and silver gradient dial. We believe the watch is unpolished and is the midsize model.

This watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the date of production. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Rainbow Daytona Reference 116595RBOW In Everose Full Set Like New

Gem-set Daytonas are significantly important watches within Rolex catalog and cherished by collectors. The “Rainbow” Daytona reference 116595RBOW is a very rare timepiece. It is one of the most exceptional gem-set watches ever created. It features 36 baguette-set sapphires in the bezel, 56 diamonds set into the lugs and case. For those that appreciate the craftsmanship in intricate gem-setting, this is a grail watch. It is powered by the calibre 4130.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. This example dates to 2021. It is in like new condition. It comes from the original owner who wore it seldomly.

Rolex Myriad Day-Date Reference 18238 Diamond Dial Unpolished

Gem-set watches are significantly important watches within Rolex catalog and cherished by collectors. This particular example is known as the “myriad” dial Day-Date. These watches were produced in 90s. This example dates to circa 1999. It features an attractive set of non-luminous hands with a black center. The dial is in brilliant condition and the case is unpolished.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Big Red Daytona Reference 6263 Original Owner Full Set

This Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 is an exceptional watch. The dial beautiful and the lume plots remain fully intact, which is rare for vintage Daytonas. The hands and dial have a nice warm lume. It features the correct Mark III screw-down pushers. It is paired on what appears to be the original 78350 Oyster bracelet with 571 end links. The case has been lightly polished but is very strong. It features what appears to be the original acrylic bezel, which is in strong condition.

This watch has a 9.06M case serial number dating to circa 1986. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter.

The watch comes from the original owner with box and papers. It has just recently been serviced. It is water resistant up to 3 bar.

Abercrombie & Fitch Reference 2447SF Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer exceptional vintage watches that I personally would love to own. This Abercrombie & Fitch reference 2447SF is just out of this world. These watches are extremely rare, and only around 20 are known. It is a very early example dating to circa 1965 with a non T dial. It is paired on a later Gay Frères bracelet for Heuer which looks brilliant. The case is unpolished and in incredible condition.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Heuer Rowing Stopwatch

Heuer has a fantastic history that not only involves racing on the track, but also on the water! The brand was the preeminent maker of stopwatches for track and field, as well as nautical sporting events like rowing.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Compax Reference 22209 Unpolished

This Universal Genève is a very nice chronograph reference 22209. The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter and 40mm in length. The dial is very attractive. It is powered by the calibre 281. The case back features an awesome engraving “Company Basketball Champions 1946 USFET”.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Rolex Silver Dial Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 1601 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to circa 1972. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 179163 w/ Roman Numerals Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 179163 is a very attractive timepiece. The two-tone and Roman numerals are a very nice vibe. The watch dates to circa 2003-2005. The case measures 27mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Patek Philippe Ellipse Reference 3546 in White Gold Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 3546 is an incredible timepiece. The case is unpolished. The dial is absolutely stunning. It is powered by the calibre 23 300. The case measures 27mm in diameter and 35mm in length. It is paired on the original strap and buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Cartier Pasha 150th Anniversary Limited Edition W3102255 Unpolished

This Cartier Pasha is a very cool timepiece. The watch features the classic cage over the dial. It features Roman numerals. We do not know the year of production. The case is stainless steel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference ST 145.022 Dot-Over-90 Dial Unpolished w/ Extract

This Speedmaster Professional reference ST 145.022 is a very gorgeous timepiece with a correct dot-over-90 bezel. The case is super attractive and unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 861. The production dates to March 12, 1970 as mentioned in the Extract.

The Speedmaster is, of course, one of the most iconic and important watches ever made. To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great. It does come with an Extract from the Archives.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1601 w/ Silver Dial

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 1601 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to circa 1972. The case measures 36mm in diameter. It is paired on a nice Jubilee 6251H bracelet from 1970 which is in good shape. The watch is very nice with a lightly polished case.

It does not come with box and papers. The watch was just recently serviced and it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16750

The Rolex GMT-Master reference 16750 was introduced in circa 1979 with a production run until circa 1988. These models feature the calibre 3075 which improved upon the ref. 1675’s movements with a higher vibrations per hour at 28,800 and quickset date function. This example dates to circa 1983 and the case is lightly polished. The bezel has a striking blue and a very cool faded red. The luminous hands and dial have a nice patina.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

This watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Heuer Jochen Rindt Autavia Reference 2446 Unpolished

The Heuer Autavia is recognized by collectors as one of the great sports chronographs of the 1960s. Along with many of its contemporaries of the era, it is powered by the Valjoux 72. Nicknamed the “Jochen Rindt” for its association with the German Formula One driver, the reference 2446 is regarded as one of the great Heuer racing chronographs.

This example is the second generation case of the Autavia which was introduced from 1967-1969. The lugs are a very fluid design, tapering down toward the wrist with thinner, beveled lugs (as opposed to the beefier non-beveled lugs found on the 1st generation). It is unpolished and maintains its true structure. The dimensions are 38mm with a height of 13mm. The crown is unsigned, but we believe it is original. We purchased the watch from a small estate auction years ago where it was consigned by the family of the original owner. The tritium lume is original in the hands and on the dial and a beautiful color.

The dial is in beautiful condition and is breathtaking to witness in the sunlight. The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Heuer Autavia Jochen Rindt Reference 2446

The Heuer Autavia is recognized by collectors as one of the great sports chronographs of the 1960s. Along with many of its contemporaries of the era, it is powered by the Valjoux 72. Nicknamed the “Jochen Rindt” for its association with the German Formula One driver, the reference 2446 is regarded as one of the great Heuer racing chronographs.

The lugs are a very fluid design, tapering down toward the wrist. It is lightly polished. The dimensions are 38mm with a height of 13mm.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Heuer USS Whale Carrera Reference 2447S Eggshell Dial

We are thrilled to offer this “Whale” of a Heuer Carrera Reference 2447S with significant military provenance. Acquired from Ambassador Linton Forrestal Brooks (U.S. Navy Retired), the caseback is engraved “U.S. Naval Submarine School / LT. L.F. Brooks / USN / June 17, 1966 / SPEAR FOUNDATION PRIZE.”

Until I knew its history, this was a watch I wouldn’t think existed – an early “eggshell” white dial in a later series case with a 91,8xx serial number. Brooks received this remarkable watch in 1966 as a prize for graduating first in his submarine school class at the U.S. Naval War College. This annual watch prize tradition started in 1938 and continues to this day. L.Y. Spear, President of the Electric Boat Company (which became General Dynamics), started the Spear Foundation in 1942 with an endowment to fund the watch prize.

The Spear Foundation allowed the shipmate to pick out his own watch from Mallove’s Jewelers in Groton, Connecticut. Brooks wisely selected a Carrera chronograph, but there was a catch. Navy regulations restricted external radioactive sources aboard nuclear-powered boats, so as to ensure accurate monitoring of radiation levels from propulsion systems. This watch had its tritium luminous material removed from dial and hands. An early dial was evidently selected for this purpose. This was possibly done on special order from Heuer, or by the retailer at the time of original sale.

Brooks served a distinguished Navy career, including as Commander of the Sturgeon-class nuclear-powered attack submarine USS Whale (SSN-638) from 1975 to 1978, retiring with the rank of Captain. As a civilian, Brooks held important Senate-confirmed positions in government. He was appointed Ambassador to lead negotiation of two strategic arms reduction treaties between the U.S. and Russia, and served as U.S. Undersecretary of Energy for Nuclear Security, responsible for managing the National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA).

The watch is powered by the Valjoux calibre 72. The case is lightly polished but very strong. The dial and hands had the original tritium luminous material removed. The signed crown is original. The watch is fitted on a black leather rally strap from Abel Court with a rare signed vintage “Sun” buckle. The watch does not come with box and papers.

The watch will be accompanied by military ephemera personally owned by Ambassador Brooks – a framed photograph of USS Whale (SSN-638) during her sea trials, a heavy bronze insignia removed from the Whale and mounted on a wood plaque for presentation to Brooks, and selected books from Brooks’ library bearing personal inscriptions and his writings regarding the subject of submarine warfare.

The watch was just serviced and it is running great.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855 W/ Papers

Along with the Atmos clocks, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. Perhaps the quintessential Memovox is the reference E 855 from the 1960s. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

The watch is powered by the calibre 825, the first automatic alarm wristwatch to incorporate the calendar complication. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement.

This example dates to circa 1967. The dial and hands feature tritium lume. The case is lightly polished, but very nice. The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Casio Yacht Timer TRW-31

This Casio Yacht Timer TRW-31 is a fantastic multi-purpose timepiece. In addtion to the chronograph, the top of the dial incorporates a yacht timer. It is an awesome throwback to those who grew up wearing the Casio wristwatches in middle school and perfect if you have a desire to revisit the past nostalgia of the brand.

IWC Automatic In Steel Unpolished

This IWC is a very cool example in stainless steel. The vertically brushed dial is very attractive with prominent hour markers. The case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.
