By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Great White Tiffany & Co. Sea-Dweller Reference 1665

This Rolex “Great White” Sea-Dweller reference 1665 is an incredibly special watch with a Tiffany & Co. signed dial. The watch comes from the original owner who purchased it while he was an undergraduate economics student at Princeton University. He decided to travel into New York City on fateful day in 1979 and purchased the watch. It has been a faithful companion to him for all these years, but the original owner had some unexpected expenses come up and he decided to give us the opportunity to sell it.

It features the “Great White” Sea-Dweller dial with beautiful yellow lume. It is a “Maxi” Sea-Dweller dial and was among the last examples of the legendary reference 1655 made. These examples with 6 million serial numbers have the extremely large hour markers that give them the “Maxi” moniker. I find them to be the most beautiful Great White Sea-Dweller dials. The reference 1655 models with all-white text are known as “Great White” models and the early examples are known as “Double Red” Sea-Dwellers with two red lines of text and the earliest, essentially prototype and not-for-sale, Sea-Dwellers are known as “Single Red” Sea-Dwellers with a single line of red text.

The hour markers match the hands, which have developed patina on the surfaces and are very attractive. The seconds hand was relumed as the lume had fallen out of it. The case has been polished. It dates to 1979 with a 6.22M serial. The bezel insert was replaced during a Rolex service and we have placed a period correct Mark 3 Fuzzy 40 insert on the watch to complete the look.

The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. It is paired on an Oyster bracelet with VE date code dating to circa 1980 and is original to the watch. The inside clasp has the serial engraved from during a Rolex service.

The watch comes does not come box and papers. It was serviced by Rolex this month and comes with a RSC Rolex Service Center card with QR code. The original owner stated he only had it serviced one other time. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It features a TrueDome D39 crystal.

I have to say this is the most beautiful Sea-Dweller dials ever seen, even without the Tiffany & Co. signature.

Patek Philippe "Dorè" Dial Reference 3940J Unpolished & Extract

This Patek Philippe reference 3940 in yellow gold is a rare and uncommon model from the early period of the amazing reference 3940. Whereas the earliest examples of the Patek Philippe reference 3940 only had a solid case back. This example for sale dates to 1985. Starting in the early 1990s, Patek Philippe made the 3940 models with both exhibition and solid case backs except for the platinum model, which only had a solid case back for the entirety of its run.

The references 3940 is arguably the most elegant Patek Philippe watches made over the last 40 years. The watch is powered by the self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor, which when released was the thinnest automatic perpetual calendar movement available. The case is 36mm, and it comes on tan strap. We believe the case remains unpolished. The watch shows little wear and features crisp hallmarks, which is extremely rare for reference 3940s. The dial is a very beautiful "dorè" dial.

The watch comes with an Extract from the Archives indicating a production of 1985. It is believed this watch was in the first ten 3940s made after the original Beyer series.

It includes a beautiful 18k gold mesh bracelet to make the watch even more elegant.

There is no question that the special 3940s and 3970s are some of the most in-demand models these days. The holy grail 3940s are the first 25 3940s made for Beyer with the beautiful doré dials. Of those, the first 15 have German dials (yuck!) and number 16-25 have English dials (hooray!). In 2022, number 19 sold for over $386,000 USD at auction. I heard of another example selling for over $450,000 around that time. Just last month, number 12 with a German dial (yuck!) sold at auction in France for over $300,000 USD, although the auction house requires you to pay to see the results and we could not figure out how to pay them to get the exact number. Number 1 was presented to the Beyer family and is seen in the late Rene Beyer’s Talking Watches episode. Number 2 was originally owned by the legendary super collector Dr. Eugene Gschwind (1921-1991) of Basel, Switzerland and is now owned by the Stern family who owns Patek Philippe. The watch is on display at the Patek Philippe Museum in Geneva on the fifth floor in a glass case on the wall near the library. Eric quoted Dr. Gschwind in his talk to the Horological Society of New York in 2019: “One must know how to purchase exceptional objects today, at tomorrow’s prices.” Unfortunately, Dr. Gschwind’s old watch is quite overpolished (like so many watches in the Patek Philippe Museum), although it will presumably never be resold so it doesn’t matter anyways!

This early example with the early dial featuring the more vintage PATEK PHILIPPE font is probably rarer than those 25 examples with Beyer dials. And for some collectors, having the cleaner dial without a retail signature is preferred. These early doré dials are truly sensational and a bit more metallic than later doré dials we have sold, although we would not turn down any of them!

If I was a betting man, I would probably place a bet that this watch is going to go up in value significantly in the coming years. And even if it doesn't, it is so difficult to find one of these early examples, let alone in this unpolished and beautiful condition. Maybe we should all take the advice of the original owner of No. 2 and buyer this exceptional watch today!

This is a firm price, lowball scumbags and tire kickers need not try!

Rolex Cartier Oyster Perpetual Reference 1005 in 14K Yellow Gold

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 1005 is an incredibly cool example with the very rare, double-signed dial from Cartier New York. The case is 14K yellow gold and it is paired on a period correct 14K gold oval link Rolex bracelet. The case is lightly polished. The watch has a 3.81M serial dating to circa 1974. For those who love Rolex and Cartier, this is a tremendous opportunity to own an elegant timepiece.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It came from the original family based in the Palm Beach area.

Rolex Red Submariner Reference 1680 w/ Meters First Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa late 1969 with a 2.15M serial. It is an incredibly attractive Red Sub and has been one I have tracked for a number of years. We believe the case is unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is a superb yellow. It is paired on a 7206 rivet bracelet from a few years earlier (1967). For those looking for an outstanding

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Bombé Oyster Perpetual Reference 6590 Unpolished on Bracelet

These Rolex Bombé Oyster Perpetual watches are very special models with the nickname deriving from the cambered lugs. This particular reference 6590 is in fantastic condition and features the uncommon Explorer dial with applied 3, 6, and 9 numerals. The 18K yellow gold case is appears to be unpolished. It measures 33.5mm in diameter and 40mm lug-to-lug. The watch dates to circa 1955 with a 120,xxx serial. It is paired on a rare Gay Freres 18k stretch Rivet bracelet with 19mm straight end links dating to 1956. The bracelet could be $20,000 alone if paired a Pre-Daytona!

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This is a superb example that Eric was considering keeping. The case is 36mm in diameter and is super sharp. The case appears to be unpolished. It has an 9.06 million serial dates to circa 1986. It is paired on a period correct Rolex 78360 bracelet with 558 end links. The lume is fantastic with a nice warm color.

For those looking for a world-class 1016, look no further.

The watch is full set and includes box and papers and has an automatic movement (Caliber 1570 made by Rolex).

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5065J-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5065J-001 is one of the great contemporary sports watches. At 38mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions that can appeal to both vintage and modern enthusiasts. This example has a fantastic case which remains unpolished. The hands and dial feature tritium lume which has a nice warm color. According to the Certificate of Origin, it was sold in 2001.

It is powered by the calibre 315 SC and is on view through its exhibition caseback.

The watch comes full set with box, the Certificate of Origin, and corresponding papers. For those looking for an outstanding Aquanaut, you cannot go wrong with this one!

Heuer Bundeswehr Flyback Chronograph Unpolished

This Heuer flyback chronograph is a super cool example for the Bundeswehr (West German Air Force). We believe the watch is unpolished. The case is measures 42mm in diameter. The watch dates to the 1970s.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Tropical Dial Reference 145.022-69 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 is absolutely incredible and I am tempted to keep it for myself. It is one of the nicest and most attractive examples in the world, so who ever purchases it can die happy!

The dial has developed a tropical brown color which is absolutely stunning. The case is unpolished and measures at 42mm with the Hippocampus case back. It is powered by a manual wind movement (an early Caliber 861 made by Lemania) and it features its original crown and crystal. The watch comes from the original owner’s family. It came head only, and I have added a period correct bracelet (clasp code 1039) with the correct 516 end links. When we bought the watch, the edge of the case featured a red paint mark which would indicate the watch leaving the factory.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does not come with an extract.

TUDOR Submariner Reference 94010 Unpolished

This TUDOR Submariner reference 94010 is an insanely attractive example. The case appears to be unpolished. It features an attractive black bezel insert. The watch dates to circa 1979 with a 903,XXX serial. It is paired on a period correct solid link bracelet we added to the watch.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Universal Genève Polerouter Black Gilt Dial Reference S20217-1 Unpolished

This is a very nice Universal Genève Polerouter Reference S20217-2. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter (44mm lug to lug). It features a beautiful non-luminous gilt dial which is in great condition and very attractive.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers.

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711/1R-001 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is perhaps the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated stainless steel watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today. This particular Nautilus reference 5711 dates to 2020. The case is 18k rose gold. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes with the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona Black Dial Ref 116500LN Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a black dial and black Cerachrom bezel. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch was sold in 2020. It comes “full set” with original box and papers from the original owner.

It comes with hang tags. It was worn sparingly and has very little signs of wear.

Rolex Daytona White Dial Ref 116500LN Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a whtie dial and black Cerachrom bezel. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch was sold in 2020. It comes “full set” with original box and papers from the original owner.

It comes with hang tags.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

OMEGA Constellation Grand Luxe 16331 Diamond Dial in Platinum on Bracelet w/ Extract Unpolished

For those that appreciate OMEGA and chronometer watches, the Constellation is a very special timepiece. OMEGA’s Constellation was one of the most important chronometers being produced in the 1950s and 1960s - and was a cornerstone for the manufacturer’s success. While Speedmasters certainly overshadow the Constellation, the Constellations were among the most historically important model across all brands. We are very excited to be able to offer such an important and special timepiece.

This is a very rare and important OMEGA Constellation Grand Luxe reference 16331. This example is one of two known. The watches were purportedly a special order made for a royal family member in Kuwait in the late 1950s. The other example was sold at the Omegamania auction in 2007 for 413,700 CHF. This was one of the most expensive OMEGA watches ever sold until this point. This example has a serial and case number just one above the example that sold for over 413k CHF and to the best of our knowledge only two were made in this configuration.

It features a platinum case and bracelet. The case measures 35mm in diameter, but wears much larger. The platinum bracelet is very long and can fit approximately a 9 inch wrist. The dial is to die for, in that it features diamond hour markers and has a beautiful patina! We believe the case and bracelet are unpolished.

This example dates to 1958. It is easily one the most exciting and elusive OMEGA timepieces on the planet. Furthermore, it is one of the most elegant and beautiful time-only watches I have ever held - from all manufacturers! This was the highest offering of OMEGA during the period and is exquisitely preserved. There are very few opportunities to own important wristwatches like this.

It comes from a very important and distinguished vintage OMEGA collector. The watch does not come with box or papers. It does come with an Extract from OMEGA. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Compax Reference 22499 Chronograph Unpolished

Universal Genève chronographs with an oversized 38mm water-resistant case made by Spillmann are some of the most desirable pieces ever made by the company, particularly those rare few made with three-register movements as the majority of those you see have two-register movements. This particular example has a case serial number that dates it to circa 1945 production and it has wonderful provenance. It was originally owned by David S. Newcombe, M.D. and came to Eric Wind directly from a relative of his who inherited it. Dr. Newcombe served as a Captain in the U.S. Army in Korea and he likely wore this watch during his service there. He later became an expert on the subject of gout. He passed away in 2010. Dr. Newcombe wrote a book entitled Gout: Basic Science and Clinical Practice, a copy of which will be included with the purchase of this watch.

According to the relative, It is possible that Newcombe received this watch as a high school graduation gift and it features “D.S.N” engraved on the case back. You can read more about him in his obituary here: http://archive.boston.com/bostonglobe/obituaries/articles/2010/07/10/dr_david_newcombe_80_renowned_rheumatologist/https://www.legacy.com/obituaries/wickedlocal-wayland/obituary.aspx?n=david-s-newcombe&pid=143587656

The luminous material on the dial is original and we believe the pushers and crown are original. The luminous material in the minute hand was patched at some point presumably decades ago. The case measures 38mm in diameter and approximately 44mm lug to lug.

This watch is from circa 1945, does not include the original box and papers, has a manual wind movement (Caliber 285 made by Universal Genève).

Rolex "Great White" Sea-Dweller Reference 1665

This Rolex "Great White" Sea-Dweller Reference 1665 . The dial is very nice with a hint of yellow in the hands and luminous hours. The watch dates to circa 1981. The case has been polished. The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. The height of the watch is 17mm including the crown. It has a service bezel insert. It has a TrueDome D39 superdome.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Black Dial Chronograph Pocket Watch Reference 5798 Unpolished

This Universal Genève is an extremely cool and uncommon pocket watch chronograph. I am thrilled to offer it because these are such special timepieces. It features a very attractive gilt dial with leaf hands. The case is massive at 55mm in diameter and has a very sharp channelled profile. The watch is powered by the calibre 292.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Railmaster Reference 2914-4 SC Unpolished

This OMEGA Ralimaster reference 2914-4 SC is an awesome watch. It has very beautiful lume. We believe the case is unpolished but heavily worn. The case measures 38mm by 47mm lug to lug. The watch dates to circa 1959 with a 17.3M movement number. The crown is a replacement.

This watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Gallet MultiChron 12 Gilt Luminous Dial Unpolished

This Gallet Multichron 12 chronograph is a super attractive example. The lume is very warm and the gilt dial is in fantastic condition. The case is 37mm in diameter by 43mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished. For those looking for an epic gilt Gallet chronograph, this is a really great choice.

The watch was recently services and it is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

Gallet MultiChron 12 Silver Luminous Dial Unpolished

This Gallet Multichron 12 chronograph is a fantastic example. The lume is very attractive and the dial is in spectacular condition. The case is 37mm in diameter by 43mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished. For those looking for a brilliant Gallet chronograph, this is a really great choice.

The watch was recently services and it is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex White Dial Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 Full Set Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520. It has a U serial dating it to circa 1998. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. We believe the case is unpolished. It is in outstanding condition and looks hardly worn. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Grey Fume Waterprotected Reference S2326B Unpolished

The 1960s and 1970s ushered in a fantastic new style for the Vulcain Cricket. Particularly in the bulkier case references such as the S2326B. The massive steel case measures 41mm lug-to-lug, with a diameter of 38mm (excluding the crown). The case is unpolished. The dial features a beautiful variant in grey fume.

The watch is powered by the calibre MSR S2. It also includes the thin-white “Vulcain” font which is less compact compared to the earliest models.

Read Charlie Dunne’s Collector Guide on: Oversized Vulcain Crickets.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Non-Luminous Oyster Perpetual Reference 6332 on WAB Rivet Bracelet

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual 6332 is a unique timepiece while being a rare and uncommon reference.

This watch is an early post Bubbleback design. The profile of the case is brushed similar to the early Submariner reference 6204. The case is has been lightly polished, but is strong. The dial features an elongated coronet at 12 o’clock. Some other interesting aspects of this watch is the seconds track is shorter than the minute track and the dauphine hands. This watch dates to 1953 with a 0.979M Serial. It measures 34.5mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug. It is paired on a period correct Oyster bracelet, dated to the 4th quarter 1952.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Non-Luminous Precision Reference 9022 Unpolished

This Rolex reference 9022 is a rare and uncommon reference which I love. The dial is a very cool non-luminous example with great hour markers. The case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter. The crown is a service replacement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Reference 6001 Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket reference 6001 is a super cool and funky model from the 1970s. It features an awesome multitone blue dial. It is powered by the MSR calibre. The case is chrome plated and is unpolished. It is approximately 40mm in diameter.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vulcain Alarm Blue Dial Unpolished

This Vulcain Alarm model is a super cool and funky model from the 1970s. It features an awesome blue dial. It is powered by the AS calibre. The case is chrome plated and is unpolished. It is approximately 34.5mm in diameter by 41mm in length.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Radial Dial

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a beautiful Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 with the special “Radial Dial”. It features a 5.02M serial number dating to circa 1978. The case appears to be polished. The bezel insert is super nice and has a beautiful faded blue with sharp red. The bracelet features a VC date code for 1978 and is likely original to the watch. The bracelet is polished.

It was just serviced in 2024 by Dr. Greg Petronzi of True Patina and is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and the corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Sea-Dweller Ref 1665 Full Set

This Rolex “Great White” Sea-Dweller reference 1665 is a very attractive watch and wonderful it has the box and papers. It features the “Great White” Sea-Dweller dial.

It is a “Maxi” Sea-Dweller dial and was among the last examples of the legendary reference 1655 made. These examples with 6 million serial numbers have the extremely large hour markers that give them the “Maxi” moniker. I find them to be the most beautiful Great White Sea-Dweller dials. The reference 1655 models with all-white text are known as “Great White” models and the early examples are known as “Double Red” Sea-Dwellers with two red lines of text and the earliest, essentially prototype and not-for-sale, Sea-Dwellers are known as “Single Red” Sea-Dwellers with a single line of red text.

The case and bracelet have been lightly polished. It dates to 1981 with a 6.6M serial.

The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. It is paired on an Oyster bracelet with F date code dating to circa 1981 and is original to the watch.

The watch comes box and papers. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It features a TrueDome D39 crystal.

TAG Heuer Carrera 'Dato' Limited Edition For HODINKEE Unpolished Full Set Brand New

This TAG Heuer Carrera 'Dato' is a very attractive limited edition release for HODINKEE. The watch is super clean with the black and white dial. It is 39mm in diameter and unpolished. It features the Calibre Heuer 02.

It is Brand Spanking New!

The watch comes "full set" with box and papers. It comes from the original owner.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 14270 Mint Unpolished Full Set Swiss Only

This Rolex Explorer reference 14270 is a great example. It is in mint condition and unpolished. It features the “Swiss Only” dial and was worn very little.

It is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This example features a U serial and dates to circa 1998.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from the original owner. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points: A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

Breguet Classique Ref 5177/5178 with Certificate of Origin & Instruction Booklet

People are saying Breguet is next hot brand. We love hearing that and we hope Abraham-Louis is smiling in heaven hearing the praise. This is a sophisticated Breguet Classique with 18k white gold case. It measures approximately 38mm in diameter. It features an automatic movement which is as beatifully decorated as the dial!

The watch comes with a certificate of origin and instruction papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It dates to 2018.

Rolex Submariner Ref. 1680

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a fantastic sports watch dating to circa 1978 with a 5.26M serial. The bracelet is period correct. It has a serial that is different and was likely added later. The case has been polished in the past.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Heuer Autavia PVD Reference 113.603 w/ Bracelet

This Heuer Autavia reference 113.603 is an awesome watch dating to the 1970s. The case is in great shape and fully PVD coated. It appears to be unpolished. It comes with the Heuer PVD bracelet which is very rare.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox E 855 Unpolished

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 855 is one of my favorite watches of all time. This example dates to circa mid 1960s. The case measures 37mm in diameter and wears excellent. The case appears to be unpolished. The luminous material on the dial and hands are super attractive and it has a nice bold font.

The watch is powered by the calibre 825, the first automatic alarm wristwatch to incorporate the calendar complication. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement.

Along with the Atmos clock and Reversos, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. Perhaps the quintessential Memovox is the reference E 855 from the 1960s. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

The watch does not come with box or papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

To learn more about this model, read Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855.

Abercrombie & Fitch Movado Ermeto Purse Watch

The Movado Ermeto “purse watch” is one of the coolest horological accessories. These watches are super interesting and have some great variations in both material on the sterling silver cases, as well as dial variants and complications. This example is a fantastic example from Abercrombie & Fitch. The watch dates to the 1940s.

It does not come with papers. It does come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 Straight Writing Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 is spectacular example. It features the straight writing or “scritte dritte” case back. The case measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 861 made by Lemania) and it features its original crown. The watch is paired with a more recent 1171 stainless steel bracelet with 633 end links. The case appears to be unpolished.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Autavia Reference 73363 Unpolished

This Heuer Autavia reference 73363 is a super cool example with orange accents and hands. The watch is powered by the calibre 7733. It is paired on an anonymous strap and buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Air-King Reference 14010M with Pink Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished and has a Y serial dating to circa 2002.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Floating Big Red Daytona Reference 6263

I’m excited to offer this amazing Rolex Daytona reference 6263. This particular example features the earlier floating “Big Red” dial which commands attention upon first glance. This watch is from circa 1978, with a serial number in the 5.5 million range.

The case is lightly polished, but is strong. The case measures 37mm in diameter. It is paired on a C+I bracelet from circa 1978 that was probably sold on the watch. The movement is a manual wind calibre 727 made by Valjoux. The watch comes from the family of the original owner. He received it as a retirement gift as a teacher.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar. It has the original crystal. We can replace it with a TrueDome if the buyer would like.

Patek Philippe Amagnetic Reference 3417 Unpolished on Gay Freres Bracelet

The Patek Philippe reference 3417 is an iconic model with a very interesting role in the manufacturer’s history. The 3417 was introduced in 1958 and remained in production into the late 1960s. While many antimagnetic timepieces were being produced - the likes of the Railmaster, Milgauss, Ingenieur - Patek Philippe created a refined offering for the professional. This example features a very attractive dial which we believe to be untouched. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch measures 35mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug).

This watch does not include the original box and papers. It is powered by the calibre 27-AM 400.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is paired on a beautiful stretch Gay Freres bracelet from the era.

Rolex Red Submariner Reference 1680

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa late 1972 with a 3.0M serial. The case has been lightly polished, but is attractive. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is a nice pale color. It is paired on a slightly earlier C+I bracelet dating to circa 1971. It is possible the bracelet was added at point of sale and is original The bezel insert has a nice dark fade which has some grayish tones to it.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. The watch features a True Dome crystal.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Patek Philippe Annual Calendar 5056P Power Reserve Grey Dial in Platinum Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 5056 annual calendar is a very special and complicated timepiece. The platinum case measures approximately 37mm in diameter. We believe it is unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 315 S IRM QA LU. It features the very attractive grey dial with luminous Roman numerals and leaf hands.

The watch comes with box and Extract from the Archives. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It was produced in 2003. It comes with its original platinum tang buckle.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1601 in 18k Yellow Gold

I absolutely love vintage Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 1601 in gold is no exception. The watch dates to circa 1966 and features a 1.26M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter.

The watch is in good condition. The case has been lightly polished, but is attractive. The hands are later replaced with SuperLuminova. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box or papers. It just passed a 3 bar water test.

Chopard L.U.C 1860 In Lucent Steel Full Set Like New

The Chopard L.U.C 1860 In Lucent Steel took the world by storm in 2023, and for good reason. It is probably among the best releases from the manufacturer. It has a beautiful salmon guilloche dial, and the steel case is great. The watch measures 36.5mm case that measures 8.2mm in thickness. It is in like new condition, only worn 3 times.

It comes from the original owner and includes box and papers. This is a great value and not high above the MSRP!

A. Lange & Söhne Datograph Flyback Reference 405.035 Up/Down in Platinum Unpolished Full Set

This is a fantastic and elegant timepiece by A. Lange & Söhne. The watch is powered by the calibre L951.6 comprised of 451 components. The platinum case is 41mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 2021. It features a rare platinum deployant buckle which is very hard to find.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Blue Dial Reference 6718 Mint Condition

This ladies Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 6718 is a superb timepiece with blue dial. The watch is in outstanding condition. The case is unpolished. The case measures 26mm in diameter. It features a 3.57M serial dating to circa 1974.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box or papers. It just passed a 3 bar water test.

Rolex Cellini Prince 5441/9 Full Set Brand New

This Rolex Cellini Prince 5441/9 is a very cool timepiece. The watch is in outstanding condition and brand new. The case is unpolished. The case measures 28mm in diameter. The watch was sold in 2024, but likely sat in the retailer for a few years..

It is in brand new condition. It comes with box and papers from the original owner.

OMEGA Seamaster Reference 168.024 On Bracelet Unpolished w/ Box

This OMEGA Seamaster reference 168.024 is a super nice example. We believe the case is unpolished. The dial is non luminous, and extremely attractive. It measures 35mm in diameter. It is paired on the original beads of rice bracelet. The watch dates to circa late 1960s - early 1970.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers. It does come with a box.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-71 Unpolished

The Speedmaster is, of course, one of the most iconic and important watches ever made. This particular example has the original crystal with the OMEGA symbol in the center, has the original crown and pushers, and has an unpolished case. The tritium on the dial and in the hands is a warm cream color.

We do not have an extract, but Eric received information on this watch several years ago. This watch has a case production of circa 1971 and according to the Archives of OMEGA was produced on June 28, 1973 and originally sold to the United States, does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (caliber 861 made by Lemania), and is 42mm in diameter.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Small Crown Red Depth Rating Submariner Reference 6536/1 Unpolished

I’m very excited to offer this Rolex “Small Crown” Submariner reference 6536/1 with “red depth rating” for sale. This is a very exceptional example and reference 6536s are extremely rare with the red depth rating. The case is in excellent condition and remains unpolished. It measures 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug) and wears amazing on the wrist. It features the original bezel, which could be as much as $30-$40k by itself.

The luminous-hour markers have a spectacular warm patina which is much more beautiful in person. Complementing the orange lume is the awesome gilt font and chapter ring. The watch dates to circa 1955. It is paired on a later Swiss-made Oyster stretch rivet bracelet dated to the first quarter of 1966 (with 80 end links).

The watch does not come with box or papers. The watch comes from the family of the original owner who discovered it in his grandparents possession. We do not know the service history, but it is keeping good time.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Linen Dial Reference 16013 Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a very rare champagne-colored linen dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1985.

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

Rolex Submariner Reference 5513

This is a nice Rolex Submariner reference 5513 dating to circa 1972. The case is lightly polished, but extremelyy strong. It is paired on a 9315 folded link Rolex Oyster bracelet. The dial is very attractive with warm patina and what is likely the original tritium pearl. The bezel assembly appears to be unpolished and the original Mark 3 aluminum bezel insert has a nice fade to it.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

Overall, this is an excellent example of a gorgeous vintage Submariner.

Rolex Black Dial Datejust Reference 16220 Unpolished

I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this black-dial reference 16220 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1991 and features a X serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555B end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well

Rolex Black Dial Oyster Perpetual Reference 1002 Unpolished w/ Box & Catalog

This Oyster Perpetual is a very clean example with a black dial. The watch dates to 1989 and features a L serial. The case measures 34mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case.

It comes with a box and catalog. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 2551 Retailed by Gübelin Unpolished w/ Box

I’m thrilled to offer this exceptional Patek Philippe reference 2551. The condition is incredible and most certainly for a Patek Philippe connoisseur. The dial is double-signed by Gübelin and untouched. The case is in fantastic condition and remains unpolished. It is powered by the automatic calibre 12-600AT. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42.5 lug-to-lug).

It comes with the original Gübelin box. An Extract has been ordered. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera Reference 3647T Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 3647T is a very cool example dating to circa 1966. The watch features an unpolished case, and is powered by the Valjoux calibre 92.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

Waltham Pocket Watch 14K Gold Unpolished

This is a very cool and ornately decorated Waltham pocket watch dating to circa 1907. The 14K yellow gold case has some great floral patterns. The dial is in very nice shape. It measures 34mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

Heuer Carrera Reference 3648N Gold Plated Retailed by Meister Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 3648N features a spectacular double-signed black dial with the retailer Meister. The case is gold plated and has some wear, but remains unpolished. It features a replacement crown. The pushers are possibly replaced. It dates to circa 1965.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

It does not come box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

Rolex Oyster Precision Reference 6422

This Oyster Precision reference 6422 features a spectacular honeycomb dial. The watch dates to circa 1955. The case measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). It has been lightly polished, but is a strong timepiece. It is a manual-wind movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447N Unpolished

The only time better than now to buy a vintage Heuer was back in the 1960s! The Carrera is an iconic sports chronograph which is one of the most versatile watches to have in a collection. This is a very attractive example with the black dial and unpolished case. It measures 36 millimeters in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. The watch is powered by the Valjoux manual-winding calibre 72. It is paired on a nice rally strap and Heuer buckle. The watch dates to circa 1968.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

Heuer Autavia Reference 2446C Unpolished

This Heuer Autavia reference 2446C is in fantastic condition. It dates to circa 1971. It is paired on a Gay Freres bracelet for Heuer dating to 1972. The case is unpolished. It features a rare red seconds hand. The luminous hour markers and hands have developed a light patina that is very attractive.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Cosmic Reference 2471-1 Complete Calendar in Steel

This OMEGA “Cosmic” reference 2471-1 complete calendar is a very interesting and important model within the manufacturer’s history. Not many manufacturers were predicting complicated wristwatches during this period. The case measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The steel case has been polished. The dial is very attractive and features applied-Arabic numerals with a red moonphase which is particularly cool.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Date Reference 15238 In 18K Gold Unpolished W/ Papers

This Rolex Date Reference 15238 is an amazing example in 18 karat gold. This is one of the last Rolex models to feature the gold rivet bracelet which is original to the watch. It features an N serial number circa 1991. The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter

The watch comes papers. It does not come with a box. The watch recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18038 Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week. This reference 18038 is an awesome watch with a very attractive gold dial. The 18K-gold case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The bracelet is very nice with very little stretch. This particular example dates to circa 1982. The case is unpolished.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Pocket Watch with Breguet Numerals

This Patek Philippe pocket watch is a beautiful timepiece. It features the highly desirable applied-Breguet numerals. The case is produced by Eggy & Cie. The case measures 45mm in diameter. It features two remarkable engravings on the case back and interior commemorating the gift from “A.K. to E.P. June 5 1942”.

The watch does not include box and papers. An Extract from The Archives has been ordered.

OMEGA De Ville Pocket Watch w/ Box

This OMEGA pocket watch is a very terrific timepiece. It dates to the 1960s and is in fantastic condition. The steel case measures 45mm in diameter. It is from the De Ville line (before the George Clooney era).

The watch does not include papers. It comes with the original box, chain and fob.

Vacheron & Constantin Linen Dial Reference 6782 Unpolished

This Vacheron & Constantin reference 6782 is a very cool example in 18k yellow gold. It features a special linen dial and remarkable bezel. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!

Heuer Jochen Rindt Autavia Reference 2446 W/ Papers

The Heuer Autavia is recognized by collectors as one of the great sports chronographs of the 1960s. Along with many of its contemporaries of the era, it is powered by the Valjoux 72. Nicknamed the “Jochen Rindt” for its association with the German Formula One driver, the reference 2446 is regarded as one of the great Heuer racing chronographs.

This example dates to circa 1967-1968. The lugs are a very fluid design, tapering down toward the wrist with thinner, beveled lugs (as opposed to the beefier non-beveled lugs found on the 1st generation). It is unpolished and maintains its true structure. The dimensions are 38mm with a height of 13mm. The crown is unsigned, but we believe it is original. .

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Heuer Camaro Reference 7220N Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive raving, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera.

The reference 7220N is powered by the manual wind calibre Valjoux 72. The N in the reference designates the black dial, however it is a nice brown color. The case is unpolished and the watch is paired on a nice brown strap. The case measures 37mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16014 Silver Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 16014 is a phenomenal timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is unpolished. It features a very nice silver dial. The dates to circa 1979.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-66 CB Case Unpolished Full Set

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding OMEGA Speedmaster reference 105.012-65 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

The reference 105.012 were first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied OMEGA logo.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to excitement of many Speedy lovers. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line throughout 1963-1966.

The case was made by BC, and it is unpolished with its original crown, which is uncommon as most were replaced during service. The watch phenomenal condition and remains unpolished. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article)

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

OMEGA Equinoxe Reversible Reference 186.0013 Unpolished

We love the rich and extensive catalog of OMEGA. This particular watch is a fascinating and different offering from the manufacturer. The OMEGA Equinoxe reference 186.0013 is an incredibly cool reversible model. The watch is powered by the calibre 1655. It features a super cool integrated bracelet. This watch is certainly going to start a conversation with the OMEGA obsessed collector, any passionate watch enthusiasts, or anyone with an eye for good design.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Reference 145.005-67 Gold Plated On Bracelet Unpolished

This OMEGA Seamaster chronograph reference 145.005 is an awesome watch from 1967. The dial is in great shape. The case is gold plated and measures 35mm in diameter (41.5mm lug-to-lug). It is unpolished. The watch is paired on a very rare and original stretch bracelet signed OMEGA. It is powered by the venerable calibre 321.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Reference 176005 Gold Plated Unpolished

This OMEGA Seamaster chronograph reference 176005 is large and in charge! The gold plated case measures 42mm in diameter (49mm lug-to-lug). It has developed a crazy patina! The watch is paired on a very rare and original OMEGA strap with buckle. It is powered by the automatic calibre 1040.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Reference 176005

This OMEGA Seamaster chronograph reference 176005 is large and in charge! The stainless steel case measures 42mm in diameter (49mm lug-to-lug). However, it does have a softer side with nice light blue accents via the subdial and hands. It has been lightly polished in the past. The watch is paired on a very long and original OMEGA bracelet. It is powered by the automatic calibre 1040.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 114300 Unpolished Full Set

This Oyster Perpetual is a great modern watch. It is 39 millimeters which is perfect for those who find 36 millimeters too small and 41 millimeters too large. Interestingly, this size was discontinued. This watch is excellent for a “daily driver” or everyday watch. The case is unpolished, which we love! There is some scratching on the bracelet clasp.

We believe the watch was sold in 2019 because the date of sale handwriting was done by someone who is not literate in English. It is “full set” with box and papers.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Big Daytona Reference 6239 Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona reference 6239 is the original and earliest reference of the Daytona. This example features a “Big Daytona” logo where DAYTONA is large and underneath COSMOGRAPH on the dial. I really love the way the ROLEX / COSMOGRAPH / DAYTONA text lines up in descending order like a pyramid in shape.

This example also has the original early “300” Mark 2 reference 6239 bezel, which differs from later examples by having 300 on the tachymeter bezel and “UNITS PER HOUR” between 12 and 2 o’clock, whereas later bezels have it by 3 o’clock. This is the second version of the steel bezel found on the reference 6239 and the earliest Mark 1 examples have a 275 and dashes rather than dots next to the numbers.

The case features a 1.62 million serial number dating it to circa 1967. The case appears to be unpolished. This example was previously owned by a fighter pilot. He had great vision and purchased an amazing Rolex. It is paired on an extremely rare and original bracelet with 71N end links dating to 1967. Most dealers would sell this on a solid link bracelet from the 1980s (YUCK!).

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch features a new TrueDome D21 crystal. It was just serviced and it is running well.

Universal Genève Exotic Compax Reference 885107 Unpolished on Bracelet

This Universal Genève Exotic Compax reference 885107 is a spectacular timepiece in pristine condition. The case appears to be unpolished and the dial is extremely nice condition. The case measures 36mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on a rare UG bracelet.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J 2nd Series w/ Extract & Box Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold featuring the highly desirable 2nd series dial. The dial is English and has beautiful serifs for the month and day abbreviati∂ons in the registers.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. This example dates to circa 1988. The case is 36mm. The case appears to be unpolished. It never had a display back, as seen in the early examples of the reference 3940.

The watch an Extract from the Archives confirming production and sale in 1988, its original Patek Philippe pin buckle in 18k yellow gold, and a Patek Philippe stylus with accents in yellow gold. It comes with a period correct box and stylus.

It was just serviced by Patek Philippe and it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940G w/ Extract Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. The calendar is in English. The Patek Philippe reference 3940 is one of the ultimate in discreet complications.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm and is made of white gold. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch dates to 1999.

It comes with an Extract from the Archives of Patek Philippe confirming production and sale in 1999. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It does not come with box.

Heuer Carrera 2447N Unpolished

The Carrera is an iconic sports chronograph which is one of the most versatile watches to have in a collection. This is a very attractive example with the black dial. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36 millimeters in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. The watch is powered by the Valjoux manual-winding calibre 72. It is paired on a black strap and anonymous buckle. The watch dates to circa 1966.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

Patek Philippe Reference 570 in Yellow Gold w/ Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 570 is one of the most refined and sought-after dress watches, and an iconic model within the Calatrava family. This specific watch is a reference 570 in yellow gold. It features a non-luminous dial with applied-dauphine hour markers. The case has been lightly polished. According to the Patek Philippe Extract from the Archives that accompanies the watch, it was produced in 1958 and sold during 1959. The buckle is Patek Philippe but the small pin portion is steel and can be seen in the photos below.

This watch does not include the original box and paper. It is powered by the Caliber 27 SC manual-wind movement made by Patek Philippe. It comes with an Extract from The Archives.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Tourbillon 6000V/110A-B544 Full Set Unpolished

Vacheron Constantin is 1/3 of The Holy Trinity, and renowned as one of the best complicated watch specialists. This VC Overseas Tourbillon Reference 6000V/110A-B544 is the top of the line models within their current production. The dial is outstanding with a piercing blue. The steel case is unpolished and measures 42.5mm in diameter. It comes with an leather strap and rubber strap!. The bracelet is in excellent condition. For those looking for a grail Vacheron Constantin, look no further!

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was sold in 2022.

Audemars Piguet Kasparov Chronograph Ref 25860ST w/ Box

The Audemars Piguet Chronograph reference 25860ST is an awesome wristwatch. It was famously seen on the wrist of iconic chess grandmaster Garry Kasparov. These watches were released in the late 1990s. The case measures 39mm in diameter. The dial has a beautiful blue with warm tones in the Tapisserie dial.

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the date of production. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 5320G-011 Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 5320G-011 is a great modern complicated Patek Philippe. It is powered by the calibre 324 S Q. The white gold case has beautiful stepped lugs. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

The watch dates to 2022. It comes with box and papers. It comes from the original owner.

Patek Philippe Reference 5235/50R-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5235/50R-001 is an annual calendar regulator. It is powered by the calibre 31-260 REG QA - a high-tech regulating movement which incorporates a Pulsomax® escapement and a Spiromax® balance-spring in Silinvar®. These are two developments from the Patek Philippe Advanced Research department. People will worship you when you inform them about this. The rose gold case has appears to be unpolished. It measures 40.5mm in diameter.

The watch dates to 2022. It comes with box and papers. It comes from the original owner.

Zenith El Primero Ref A384 Tropical Dial on Gay Freres Bracelet

The Zenith El Primero Reference A386 sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. It is powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019.

The watch is 38mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 45.5mm) and comes on the Gay Frères steel ladder bracelet with ZKM end links. The case has been polished. The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The dial has taken on a very attractive patina.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Zenith El Primero Reference A386 Mk I on Gay Freres Bracelet Unpolished

Introduced in 1969, the Zenith El Primero Reference A386 sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. It is powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019. It features beautiful tri-colored subdials.

This is a MK I case. The watch is 38mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 45.5mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. It is paired on a Gay Freres bracelet. The case appears to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 126234 with Wimbledon Dial Full Set Like New

This Rolex Datejust reference 126234 is a great example from the modern catalog. The steel case is 36mm and unpolished. It features a beautiful “Wimbledon” dial which looks great in any setting. The case is unpolished and like new.

This watch comes from original owner and is “full set” with box and papers. It was purchased in 2025.

Patek Philippe Reference 5396G-017 Full Set Unpolished Like New

This is a very special Patek Philippe reference 5396G. The watch is powered by the self-winding calibre 26-330 S QA LU 24H., an automatic annual calendar. This dial variant is among my personal favorites featuring a Sunburst blue with black-gradient and beautiful baguette diamond set dial.

The watch In fantastic condition and is unpolished. The white-gold case wears incredible on the wrist. It measures 38.5mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug).

This watch retails at $68,232 before taxes.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner. It was sold in 2024 and is in like new condition and worn a handful of times.

Vacheron Constantin Patrimony 4115U/000R-B907 Full Set Like New

This Vacheron Constantin Patrimony 4115U/000R-B907 is an incredible timepiece in 18K rose gold. It measures 36.5mm in diameter. It features a beautiful 72 diamond set bezel. It is powered by the automatic calibre 2450 Q6/3. For those looking for an elegant ladies dress watch, this is perfect.

It was sold in 2025 and is like new. It comes from the original owner. The watch comes with box and papers.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference. 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an attractive 1675 featuring a 5.1M serial number, dating to circa 1978. The aluminum bezel insert features a beautiful blue and red.

The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a solid-link Oyster bracelet.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

OMEGA Seamaster 300 Ref. 234.30.41.21.01.001 Full Set

This OMEGA Seamaster 300 Ref. 234.30.41.21.01.001 is a very attractive timepiece. It is a must have for Seamaster enthusiasts and OMEGA collectors! The case measures 41mm in diameter. It was sold in 2023.

It is a very good price. You will sleep well at night knowing you got a fantastic deal.

The watch is in great condition. It comes with box and papers.

DROZ Royal Australian Navy Dive Watch

This DROZ dive watch is a very cool example issued for the Royal Australian Navy. The watch features a very cool dial variant similar to those found on vintage Breguet models. These cases were produced by EPSA. It measures approximately 39mm in diameter.

The case appears to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Heuer Split Seconds Pocket Watch Unpolished

Heuer has had a long history of being one of the leading chronograph manufacturers and even has served as an official timer for the Olympic Games. This is a split-second pocket watch. It counts up to 30 minutes, in addition to a standard time display. This split-second or “rattrapante” can track two different runners crossing a finish line. The first second hand, which is closer to the dial, has a half-moon-shaped counterweight, whereas the other second hand has a teardrop shape as its counterweight. It measures 59mm in diameter. The case is in outstanding condition and unpolished.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Pink Dial Day-Date Reference 18239 in 18K WG with Box

The Day-Date is an iconic model. The reference 18239 is a 36mm watch in white gold model with a sapphire crystal and a “double-quick” day and date setting function.

The watch is paired with a stunning pink dial and a very rare Basque date wheel. The watch features the original 8385 white gold president bracelet. Both the bracelet and the serial number date to circa 1999. The original sticker is present on the caseback. The watch has been polished in the past.

The watch has recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The watch comes with its original box. It does not come with papers.

Rolex Coke GMT-Master II Reference 16710 Extra Insert Full Set

This Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16710 with “Coke” bezel is a super attractive watch. The black and red bezel variety is nicknamed Coke after the famous Coca-Cola soft drink! It also comes with an extra Pepsi insert.

The case has been polished in the past. The watch has an N serial, indicating production circa 1991. The watch is 40mm in diameter.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex Air-King Ref 14000 Silver Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is a superb example. The watch features features a E serial dating to circa 1990. The case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Black Dial Date Ref. 15200 Unpolished

I absolutely love this Rolex Date reference 15200 The watch dates to circa 1997 with a U serial. The case measures 34mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent condition. The case appears to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box or papers. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master Ref. 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an attractive 1675 featuring a 1.72M serial number, dating to circa 1967. The aluminum bezel insert features a beautiful blue and faded pink.

The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a 7206 Rivet Oyster bracelet dating to circa 1968.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

OMEGA Cosmic Complete Calendar in Steel On Bracelet

This OMEGA “Cosmic” reference 2471-1 complete calendar is a very interesting and important model within the manufacturer’s history. Not many manufacturers were predicting complicated wristwatches during this period. The case measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The steel case has been polished. The dial is very attractive and features applied-Arabic numerals with a red moonphase which is particularly cool. It is paired on a contemporary beads-of-rice bracelet.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

A. Lange & Söhne 1815 Reference 234.032 Up/Down in WG Full Set

This is a fantastic and elegant timepiece by A. Lange & Söhne. The watch is powered by the calibre L051.2. The 18k white gold case is 39mm in diameter (45.5mm lug-to-lug) and is unpolished. The watch was sold in June 2022.

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Geneva Sport Gilt Dial Chronograph Unpolished

This Geneva Sport chronograph is a fantastic timepiece. The gilt dial is very attractive. It dates to the 1940s. The watch is very small and cute. We believe it is powered by the GSW 23.7 calibre.

The case measures approximately in 31mm diameter (36mm lug to lug)..

The watch does not come with its original box and papers. The watch was just serviced and it is running well.

Rolex Winn-Dixie Air-King Ref 14000 10 Year Safe Driver Unpolished w/ Papers

The reference 14000 was introduced in circa 1989 and would be produced until 1999. At 6 o’clock, the dial features the printed “T SWISS MADE T”, indicating the luminous material is tritium. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. This is due to the thinner automatic calibre 3135.

Some of the most interesting Rolex watches are the special “Winn-Dixie” models which were earned by long-term employees at the supermarket franchise. This example is for a driving employee that had an impressive 10 year period in which they avoided any accidents on the job. This example features an U6 serial and dates to 1997. The case appears unpolished.

The watch comes with papers. It does not come with box. It was just serviced and passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Champagne Roman Dial Datejust 16013 Unpolished w/ Box

This Rolex Datejust reference 16013 watch. It features a very cool champagne Roman dial (also referred to as Buckley dial). The case appears to be unpolished. The watch features a 4.4M serial service replacement case. You can’t really date these - as Rolex still offers 4.4M serial service cases to this day for a variety of references. The watch is likely from the early 1980s.

It is paired on a Honey Wind strap.

The watch does not include papers. It comes with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance of 5 bar.

Chopard Mille Miglia 8311 Unpolished Full Set

Chopard is a brand that many people love and some even say it is on the level of Patek Philippe. Seriously, there are people at Watches & Wonders making these comparisons! This Chopard Mille Miglia reference 8311 is an exquisite chronograph. It also looks like the 8388 online.

The stainless steel case measures approximately 40 in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. It features a beautiful luminous dial. Tony Traina loves Chopard and that is great! Max Braun also loves the Chopard movements, which is also great! It is paired on the original bracelet. We believe it dates to circa 2018.

This is the best price in this galaxy. Perhaps the best price in the entire universe!

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TAG Heuer Autavia Calibre Heuer 02 For HODINKEE Full Set Unpolished

Did you know HODINKEE collaborated with TAG Heuer on an Orange Boy Autavia homage? I had no clue until right now! We were shocked and investigated the backstory to this model. 🕵️‍♂️

It was released in May of 2018. It retailed at $6,050. The bracelet is quite nice and the dial/accents are very attractive. Kudos!

According to a confidential internal source, less than 50 examples were sold making it a crazy rare watch. We would die before giving up our sources!

The watch is in great condition. The case appears to be unpolished.

This watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Autavia 1563T Unpolished

This Heuer Autavia reference 1563T is an incredibly cool sports chronograph. It features the same case as the reference 1163V. Looking at the dial, you will notice the smaller subsidiary seconds at 10 o’clock, and the 30 minute register at 3 o’clock giving it a very cool asymmetrical dial. With orange accents, it is just a great look. The watch is powered by the calibre 15. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Autavia Reference 2446C SN on Gay Freres Bracelet

This Heuer Autavia reference 2446C SN is an incredible example and in fantastic condition. The case has been lightly polished. It dates to circa 1968-1969. It is paired on a Gay Freres bracelet for Heuer dating to 1971. We understand the dial was previously put in the watch - although it is a very rare dial variant and collectors love it.

These models are very exciting and the dial variant is similar to Daytona models with a much larger impression on the wrist. The case measures approximately 40.5mm. It is powered by the Valjoux 72.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master II 126713GRNR “Zombie” Unpolished Box & Papers

Rolex Rolex GMT-Master II 126713GRNR “Zombie” is a fantastic modern sports watch. The reference number 126713GRNR denotes a steel and 18k yellow gold case and bracelet, and a black and gray rotating bezel. It captures the iconic DNA from the two-tone vintage models. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, the GMT remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

We believe the watch is unpolished. It comes “full set” with its original box and papers. The papers date the watch to 2023. It comes from the original owner who is extremely cool!

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Chopard L.U.C. QUATTRO MARK IV in Platinum Unpolished Full Set As New

This Chopard L.U.C. Quattro is the fourth iteration of the series launched in 2000. The Mark IV debuted at Watches and Wonders 2025. This watch retails for $50,300 (before taxes and fees)

The watch measures at 39mm (approximately 46mm lug to lug) and features a stunning light blue “frosted” Guilloché dial. There is a sub-register that displays small seconds as well as the date. The watch features a four-barrel nine day power reserve COSC-certified movement. The platinum case bears a small bee hallmark between the bottom lugs.

This is the best price in the United States. It is in as new condition and worn once or twice.

This watch comes with the original box and papers

Heuer Autavia Reference 2446C Unpolished with Service Booklet

This Heuer Autavia reference 2446C is very cool example. The case appears to be unpolished. It dates to circa 1968-1969. We understand the bezel insert was replaced with a NOS example. The case measures approximately 40.5mm. It is powered by the Valjoux 72.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It comes with a Heuer service booklet.

Vacheron & Constantin Reference 4217 in 18k RG Unpolished

This Vacheron & Constantin is a very attractive time-only dress watch. The reference is a 4217 and appears to be 18K rose gold. The case appears to be unpolished. The case measures approximately 34mm in diameter. It features a non-luminous dial with even applied-Arabic numerals.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Reference Reference 2444 Unpolished

This Heuer reference 2444 has a spectacular warm cream colored dial. The case appears to be unpolished and measures roughly 35mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1960s.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

IWC Big Pilot's Watch IW500201 Full Set

I am not going to lie to you, I like the IWC Big Pilot. It is a watch that tells everyone “I know about the Submariner and Patek Philippe watches, but I am going full IWC”. Your enemies will be forded to bow down and in deference. The case has been polished. It measures 46mm in diameter. Big Sucker! The watch dates to circa 2003.

If you are looking for a fun throwback to 2009 IYKYK watch collecting, this watch rules.

The watch comes with box or papers.

Arnold & Son GMT Timekeeper Ref 1G2AS Full Set

Hey Arnold! That was my favorite television show as a kid! I would have loved to see Arnold Shortman wear an Arnold & Son. The GMT Timekeeper reference 1G2AS would have been a great choice! The case appears to be unpolished and measures roughly 41mm in diameter. These cases are super cool and similar to Speake Marin models! The GMT function is very nice and the luminous hand set and markers are very attractive.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
