By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Explorer II Reference 1655 Unpolished

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

This example dates to circa 1984 with an 8.2M serial number. It features the automatic calibre 1575 made by Rolex. It comes from the family of the original owner. It is paired on its original Jubilee bracelet. We believe this example is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced for the first time.

Rolex King Midas Reference 4315 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

The King Midas was the most expensive Rolex by far at retail in the 1960s and 1970s. Starting in the 1970s, Rolex changed the dial to have Cellini instead of Midas in Greek on the dial and also introduced the smaller Queen Midas model. The King Midas has been closely associated with Elvis Presley, who is photographed holding one, and more recently with Rihanna, who wore a customized model for her pregnancy announcement.

It is said that Gérald Genta designed these watches and they certainly seemed to be part of the evolution of integrated watch design that led to the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, IWC Ingenieur 1832, and Patek Philippe Nautilus.

This example is 18k yellow gold and we believe it to be unpolished. It is a fantastic timepiece.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 Stealth Reference 101.025 in Platinum

This A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 Stealth reference 101.025 is a super attractive timepiece. The platinum case is very nice and appears to be unpolished. It measures 38.5mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

We do not know the date of production. An Extract can be ordered for an additional charge.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 69178 Diamond/Ruby Bezel in 18K Gold Unpolished w/ Box

We are thrilled to offer this very exceptional Rolex Datejust reference 69178. It features a stunning diamond dial, as well as a diamond and ruby set bezel by Rolex. For those that appreciate gem-set watches, these are such beautiful examples.

This example is 18k yellow gold and we believe it to be unpolished. The case is 26mm in diameter and 33mm lug-to-lug. It is an absolutely stunning timepiece. It features an L serial dating to circa 1989.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a briefcase jewelry box, which is absolutely incredible and a dream piece of ephemera from Rolex. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18038 in 18K Yellow Gold Like New Full Set

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 is a spectacular watch in pristine condition. It was rarely worn. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an 8.9M serial number dating to circa 1986.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Ladies Yacht-Master Reference 69628 Unpolished w/ Box

This Ladies Yacht-Master Reference 69628 is a beautiful watch. It has an 18K gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an W serial number dating to circa 1995.

It comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Yacht-Master II Reference 116688 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Yacht-Master II reference 116688. It was rarely worn. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an Z serial dating to circa 2006-2007.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Box

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is a super nice watch in pristine condition. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an L serial number dating to circa 1989.

It comes with box. It does not come with papers. It comes with a notepad and booklet. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona Reference 116520 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Daytona 116520 is a super nice sports watch. It has an an stainless steel case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an K serial number dating to circa 2000-2001.

It comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Diamond/Baguette Dial Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is an exceptional example. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an E serial number dating to circa 1990.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Diamond Dial and Diamond Bezel Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Box

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is an exceptional example. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an E serial number dating to circa 1990.

It comes with box. It does not come with papers. It comes with a notebook and catalog. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5165A-001 w/ Service Papers

This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5165A-001 is a spectacular sports watch and one of the most beloved models from PP. The stainless steel case has been polished in the past service from Patek Philppe. It measures 38mm in diameter.

This watch comes with service papers. It does not come with the original box or papers. We do not know the date of production. It was serviced by Patek Philippe in 2021.

Gallet MultiChron 12 w/ Luminous Dial Unpolished

Gallet has made some of the greatest chronographs of all time and the Multichron 12 is among the most special watches from the manufacturer. The steel case is unpolished and measures 38mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the Excelsior Park 40. It features a luminous dial and luminous hands.

It does not come with a box or papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Chronoswiss Cabrio Unpolished Full Set

This Chronoswiss Cabrio is a super cool interactive case deisgn from the 1990s. We believe the case is unpolished.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Blancpain in Steel Full Set Unpolished

This is a super attractive Blancpain watch from the 1990s. The case is steel and measures 34mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. It dates to circa 1993.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Reference 14060M Unpolished W/ Box

This Rolex Submariner reference 14060 dates to 2003-2005 with an F serial. The case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter. It is in mint condition.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 w/ Papers and Booklet

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This example features a 1.72M serial number, dating to circa 1967. The case has been polished, but it is strong with good form. The aluminum bezel insert has taken on a very nice fade and complements the puffy lume. The watch comes a stamped booklet and chronometer papers (which are dated to February of 1968. It is paired on a 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 80 end links (dated to the first quarter of 1968). It was just serviced by Dr. Greg Petronzi (@truepatina) and is running great.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Cartier Tank Louis Jumbo Automatique in 18K YG Unpolished

The Cartier Tank Louis is one of the most sophisticated dress watches and these models have grown to become highly collectible over recent years. It is a great contrast to many sports Rolex models within a collection and can be worn in both a casual and dressed up state.

This example dates to circa 1970s. It features an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. These are the largest vintage Tanks. The case measures 28mm in diameter and 35mm in length. The dial is very nice and without any signs of wear or crazing. Often, these have crazing.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

To learn more about the spectacular watches from Cartier, read our article My Dream Cartier Collection.

Patek Philippe Reference 5270P-001 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 5270 is a remarkable grand complication reference introduced by Patek Philippe in 2011. The perpetual-calendar chronograph is powered by the calibre CH 29-535 PS Q. We believe the watch is in unpolished. The platinum case wears incredible on the wrist. It features the highly attractive salmon dial. It has an exhibition case back and a platinum case back.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. It was sold in 2021.

Movado M95 Chronograph Reference 95-224-568 in 14K YG

This is a very cool vintage Movado M95 chronograph. The watch is in fantastic condition. The case has been lightly polished. The 14K yellow gold case measures 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is FB (Taubert/Borgel) case and these are incredibly well made chronograph cases. It features a beautiful vertically silver dial.

This watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running very well.

Cartier Baignoire 7743-1 on Cuban Link Bracelet

This Cartier Baignoire is a very elegant timepiece produced in the 1970s. The watch features a very cool and uncommon Cuban link bracelet. The 18k yellow gold case measures 22mm in diameter by 30mm in length. We believe the case is unpolished. It features a manual wind Bueche Girod movement.

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

Piaget Polo W/ Diamonds Reference 350234 Unpolished

This Piaget Polo Date reference 350234 is an incredible timepiece with a diamond set case and bracelet.

The watch is powered by a Quartz battery and it was just replaced. The case is made of 18K gold and is in a remarkable, unpolished state. It measures 30mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fir a wrist as big as 7.5 inches.

Learn more about the exciting designs and movements of vintage Piaget in the article ‘Undervalued: Vintage Piaget Watches’.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 5320G-011 Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 5320G-001 is a great modern complicated Patek Philippe. It is powered by the calibre 324 S Q. The white gold case has beautiful stepped lugs. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

The watch dates to 2022. It comes with box and papers.

Rolex John Bull Purple Dial Submariner Reference 16803 Two Tone

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This reference 16803 features a 9.4M serial number dating to circa 1987. The case was hand finished / carved at time of purchase at the Bahamian official Rolex retailer John Bull by their master watchmaker Günter Miethe. This was a popular request by the Bahamian retailer throughout the 1980s and these watches are highly prized by collectors for their unique look and island vibes. Our understanding is that Rolex gave their blessing to their official retailer John Bull to customize these watches for the local Bahamian market at the time. While most of the John Bull watches with a bark carved finish were all-steel Submariners and a few were all-steel GMT-Masters. This example features a very attractive purple dial which we see in examples from the 1980s into circa 1990.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.. The watch and bracelet have not been polished since new beyond the John Bull finish, so it has its full proportions.

I fell in love with this watch when I first saw it and I think any buyer that gets this can have a watch they will never see on another person’s wrist. I can’t imagine a more fun watch to wear on a trip to The Bahamas!

Rolex Gilt Dial Explorer I Reference 1016 w/ Papers

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example dates to circa 1963 with a 901,XXX serial. The gilt dial is very attractive and the lume is original. The case is 36mm in diameter. The case has been polished.

It does not include box but it does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Vulcain Cricket with Luminous LBJ Dial Unpolished

This is a spectacular cricket with a beautiful patina to the dial and hands. It features the original or “OG” style case. It measures 34mm in diameter (just under 43mm lug-to-lug). The steel case features drilled lugs and the dial has the distinct diamond hour marker at 12:00 o'clock (the earliest dial variant). The crown and pusher are original.

The early crickets had serial numbers on the case backs before they switched to reference numbers on the case backs. The case remains unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 120, and has an exceptional and loud alarm.

Overall, this is an extremely beautiful example

Vulcain Black Dial Cricket Reference 303000 Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket is an awesome example with a rare black gilt dial. The reference 303000 is an angular 33.5mm diameter case (41mm lug-to-lug). The case is unpolished and features the original signed crown. The watch is powered by the calibre 120.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Honeycomb Waterproof Dial in Steel Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket is an early waterproof model from the brand. The dial features a very rare honeycomb texture with the earlier “waterproof” font. The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter. The watch is powered by the Calibre 120’s successor the MSR S2. For those looking to add a rare Vulcain Cricket to their collection, this is an awesome example.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Inverted 6 White Dial Zenith Daytona Reference 16520

The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520. It is an early example with the inverted 6. It has a n S serial dating it to circa 1993-1994. The case has been polished. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona 116518LN in 18K Yellow Gold on Oysterflex Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Daytona is a very cool example on Oysterflex rubber strap. We believe the case is unpolished. It is made of 18k yellow gold and was sold in 2020. It features the attractive yellow and black dial with red accents.

The watch was sold in 2020. It comes with a box or papers. It is partially stickered and that is awesome!

Charlie Dunne met a vintage watch dealer who wears this watch on a bracelet. He refused to allow Charlie to shoot a photo of it for a photo report because “It was too rare” and Charlie thought this was a very weird interaction.

Rolex Turn-O-Graph Reference 6202 w/ Waffle Dial

This Rolex Turn-O-Graph reference 6202 is a super cool Rolex sports watch from the 1950s. The Turn-O-Graph is the precursor to the mighty Rolex Submariner which was introduced in circa 1953-1954. They are uncommon and a bit mysterious to some, but for those who appreciate Rolex’s historic models the Turn-O-Graph is certainly one to admire.

This example dates to circa 1953 with a 950,XXX serial. It features a beautiful gilt dial with “waffle” texture. It measures 36mm in diameter. The case is probably unpolished. It is paired on a Gay Freres Rivet bracelet with straight end links dating to 1969. It also comes with a Jubilee bracelet from the 1960s.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Universal Genève Golden Shadow in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Universal Genève Golden Shadow is a very cool and uncommon model in 18K yellow gold. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 32mm in diameter (37mm lug to lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Solunar Unpolished

This Heuer Solunar is a super nice example has a spectacular colors in the dial. The minute register is designated for tide changes. The chrome plated case is unpolished and measures roughly 36mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1950s. It features a service crown.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Coin Watch in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

Coin watches are remarkable creations which are both extremely uncommon and very difficult to create. Made from two coins, they are an interesting blend of horology, numismatics, and history. More often than not, these coins would begin as legal tender, then would require a skilled coin maker to dissect two sides, carve an interior within each to house the timepiece, and finally reconstruct the two sides in a hinged fashion. The most sophisticated watchmakers and case makers were involved in creating these timepieces and this Audemars Piguet is an exceptional example of this art.

The movement is signed Andemars Piguet. It is crafted from the 1904 $20 “Liberty Head” designed by James Barton. The case and dial are both very well preserved. The numerals and hour makers have a beautiful gold foil. It dates to circa 1949.

These are the ultimate horological accessories and way cooler than any Audemars Piguet that you could purchase today. We are elated to offer this timepiece.

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

To learn more about vintage coin watches read Charlie Dunne’s authoritative article 'An introduction to coin watches: Where craftsmanship meets currency'!

Rolex Air-King Black Dial Reference 14000 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000M is an awesome example with a rare and beautiful black dial. The watch features a P serial number and dates to circa 2000. We believe the case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex by Tom Sachs GMT-Master II Reference 16718 Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. This particular example a reference 16718 in spectacular condition. The watch features an A serial dating to 1999. The 18K yellow gold case is unpolished and measures 42.5mm in diameter including crown (47.5mm lug-to-lug).

Tom Sachs made two of the most desirable Nike shoes ever: the Mars Yard and Mars Yard 2.0 and his art commands a strong following. Tom is known for deconstructing regular items and in circa 2002 he decided to take a crack at deconstructing a gold Rolex GMT-Master II that he purchased new at retail. He painted the watch black and the black paint wears off with wear. He made 5 examples and this is number 3 of 5. It has been in a private collection of a prominent art dealer since new. A standard Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16718 with box and papers would likely be around the $30,000 price point - adding $19,000 for the provenance.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Saudi Arabian Datejust 16220 w/ Letter of Provenance & Box Unpolished

A steel Datejust is an essential for anyone who loves Rolex. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This reference 16220 is an awesome Datejust with the rare Saudi Arabian crest dial with a very special palm tree and swords. These crests can be seen on models such as Universal Geneve, Rolex and others. These watches were often gifts from a member of the royal family to diplomats.

This Rolex features an E serial and dates to circa 1990. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The condition is excellent. It features the original case back sticker. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch comes from a collector who purchased it from the original owner. It with the box and letter of provenance. It comes with papers - but they do not correspond to the serial number on the watch which occasionally happens. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Big Red Daytona Reference 6263 Full Set

This Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 is one of the best examples I have ever seen. The dial is spectacular and the lume plots are also beautiful. The hands and dial have a nice warm lume - probably the most attractive lume plots in terms of color and size I have ever seen on a Daytona. It features the correct Mark II screw-down pushers. It is paired on what appears to be the original 78350 Oyster bracelet with 571 end links. The case is very nice. It was lightly polished in the past. It features what appears to be the original acrylic bezel, which is in strong condition.

This watch has a 6.3M case serial number dating to circa 1979. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter.

The watch comes with box or papers. I purchased it from the original owner in the past. It was a graduation gift to the original owner.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J 2nd Series Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold featuring the highly desirable 2nd series dial. The dial is English and has beautiful serifs for the month and day abbreviations in the registers.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. This example dates to circa 1988. The case is 36mm. We believe it has never been polished, and is an exceptional example. It never had a display back, as seen in the early examples of the reference 3940.

The watch comes with box and certificate of origin. It was produced in circa August 1989. We can add a gold bracelet for an additional charge.

The adorned “Zenith Daytonas” are certainly among the highly desired neo-vintage Rolex models. It is powered by an automatic Calibre 4030 made by Zenith.

The case is very strong and measures in at is 40mm in diameter. The outer ring of the registers have developed a very attractive beige color. Rolex register rings on Daytonas with black dials from the early through middle 1990s would often develop some darkening to the rings. This effect is often given the nickname “Patrizzi” after the legendary auctioneer Osvaldo Patrizzi, who reportedly first identified this patina.

This example is lightly polished but strong. It dates to circa 1996 and was sold in 1997.

This watch comes papers. It does not come with a box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Retailed by Tiffany & Co. Datejust Reference 16220

This Rolex Datejust reference 16220 is a beautiful example with warm lume plots. However, the more special attribute is that it is among the last Rolex watches with Tiffany & Co. stamped on the dial and with a rare and beautiful black dial.

This watch is an E Serial and dates to 1990, does not include the original box and papers. The case is in strong condition and has been lightly polished in the past. It has an automatic movement (Caliber 3035 made by Rolex), and is 36mm in diameter.

Patek Philippe World Time Reference 5230R-001 Unpolished with Certificate of Origin

This Patek Philippe Reference 5230R-001 is a spectacular world time model in 18k rose gold. The watch was sold in 2017. It is powered by the calibre 240 HU. We believe this example is unpolished.

It does not come with box. It does come with a Certificate of Origin.

Breguet Perpetual Calendar 5327ba/1e/9v6 with Box and Papers Unpolished

This Breguet is a fantastic perpetual calendar timepiece. The dial is beautifully decorated. The 18K gold case is very attractive and we believe it is unpolished.

It comes with box and papers. It was originally sold in 2013. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

A. Lange & Sohne Zeitwerk Striking Time 145.029 Unpolished Full Set

The ZEITWERK STRIKING TIME is the world’s first mechanical wristwatch with a jumping numerals display and a visible striking mechanism.

This example was sold in 2012. It features a white gold case that we believe is unpolished.

It comes with box and papers.. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA 14k Gold Watch with Integrated Bracelet Unpolished

This is a very attractive OMEGA watch with an integrated bracelet.

This example has a 14K yellow gold case and bracelet that we believe is unpolished. It measures 27mm in diameter.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Longines Military Style Watch Unpolished

This Longines is a very attractive watch. It appears to date to the 1940s and was possibly oriented for military purposes because of the fixed lugs. The case is in great shape. It measures 35mm and we believe it to be unpolished. The dial is very interesting with radial painted numerals. It appears to have the original mineral crystal which is very cool.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reference 11012 Travel Clock Retailed By Hermes w/ Pouch

We absolutely love Jaeger-LeCoultre’s clocks. Their catalog is spectacular and this reference 11012 travel clock is one of the coolest styles from the era. It is particularly special because it was sold by Hermès - a retailer with a wonderful relationship with JLC. The case is gold plated, and features a tortoise shell style surface on the body. The dial is spectacular and has an asymmetrical date window which is very attractive. It has a manual-wind alarm calendar.

For those that enjoy decorating their desk or office with incredible timepieces, this is a seriously cool option.

This watch does not come with box or papers. It comes with a pouch. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Precision Ladies with Box Unpolished

This Rolex Precision is a very special timepiece. It is in remarkable condition and features the case back sticker. For those looking for a very interesting and beautiful timepiece from Rolex, this is a great option!

It comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is keeping good time.

OMEGA Constellation †300Hz in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This OMEGA Constellation is an incredible and humongous 18K yellow gold timepiece with a very long bracelet. The watch is in spectacular condition. We believe the case is unpolished. It is powered by the Beta 21 calibre!

The watch does not come with box and papers. A new battery was just added and it is running great!

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Two-Tone Daytona Reference 6239 w/ Racing Provenance

Sometimes we get an e-mail with a watch that blows our socks off, and that was the case when we recently opened an e-mail that came to us from Gary Cummings.

This two-tone vintage Rolex Daytona watch came to us from Gary Cummings, a legend in racing circles as a championship-winning crew chief and race engineer who still is involved in directing important teams and drivers today. This watch has been Gary’s faithful timekeeper since he received it as a gift from Hal Sahlman. Hal was a legendary car and boat racer. Hal had held the World Speed record on water back in 1975 and we believe he was wearing this watch when he set the record. There is an epic photo of him in the boat and it appears this might be on his wrist in the photo.

Gary wore this watch while overseeing legendary drivers including Derek Bell, Bob Wollek, Thierry Boutsen, Oscar Larrauri, Jochen Mass, Roland Ratzenberger, Hurley Haywood, Rick Mears, Jan Lammers, Andy Wallace and AJ Foyt, to mention just a few. Gary wore this watch at Le Mans on 12 occasions and wore it across the Sahara as team manager for the KIA factory team at the 1993 Paris/Dakar Rally. He wore it at every important race track in the United States from serving as Rolex Series Manager for Grand-Am/ NASCAR and the Tudor Series Manager. For more on Gary’s exploits, please listen to the SlipAngle Podcast Episode 329.

Hal likely requested the watch to converted into a two-tone example upon receiving it from Rolex back in the 1960s. It has been in this configuration since then. Gary had it serviced at Rolex on two occasions in the 1980s in this exact configuration. My understanding is that Rolex Geneva probably has photographs of this watch from the 1980s in their service records, but those are generally not accessible to the public.

The bezel is a period correct Mark 1 bezel with gold outer ring that would have been correct for the reference 6241 in gold, 6262 in gold, and 6263 in gold. It is a rare and valuable bezel by itself, probably worth approximately $20,000.

The case has been lightly polished in the past, but is in strong shape.

The dial is very attractive and the luminous tritium material complements the gold accents of the bezel/bracelet very well.

The bracelet is a two-tone oval link that has been on the watch during its entire ownership under Gary. The bracelet is a bit stretched and could perhaps use some tightening restoration, but we leave that for the next owner to decide.

The case has a 1.65M serial and dates to circa 1967. The case has been lightly polished in the past, but is in strong shape.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

We are happy to connect the purchaser of the watch with Gary directly for any conversation and meeting and we look forward to going to a race with Gary soon!

Rolex Explorer I Reference. 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This is one of my favorite Explorers I have ever seen. This example features an 8.5M serial dating it to circa 1984. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is attractive and has a warmer yellow tone. The watch comes with a reference 78360 Oyster bracelet with 580 end links with the bracelet clasp code indicating it was produced in circa 1984 and likely born on the watch.

This is a truly fantastic Explorer I. For those in search of a vintage Rolex Explorer reference 1016, this is your chance!

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history and it is running great. It has just passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Explorer II Ref. 1655 MKII Dial

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

It features a MK II dial and bezel.

This is an awesome Explorer II reference 1655. It dates to circa 1974 (3.77 million serial number). The case has been lightly polished. It has attractive lume.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Movado M95 Chronograph Reference 19068 on Bracelet Unpolished

The Movado M95 chronographs are incredible timepieces not only for their movements, but also the cases and dials. This is very attractive and nice example. It is paired on a period correct Movado bracelet. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter. The case was produced by Taubert Frères (formerly François Borgel). The dial and hands have been relumed.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Solunar Unpolished

This Heuer Solunar is a super cool watch. The minute register is designated for tide changes. The chrome plated case is unpolished and measures roughly 36mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1950s.z the hands have lume loss.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Gallet 24 Hour Dial Chronograph Unpolished

This Gallet chronograph is an awesome vintage watch from the 1960s. These are super attractive with the 24-hour dial. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (44mm lug to lug). It is powered by a Valjoux movement. The hands are a different color, but we believe they contain the original lume. For those looking for an awesome Gallet chronograph, this is a great option.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Ref. 145.012-67 Unpolished

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era.

This example has turned to a tropical dark brown dial. The watch dates to circa 1967 and it is paired with an Omega reference 1039 bracelet with 516 end-links dating to 1967. The case measures 42mm. The case appears to be unpolished.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The Omega Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference. 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960, an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. It was produced until circa 1989. This example has a 8.15M serial number, dating to circa 1984. It is paired on a 78360 Oyster bracelet with H date code dating to circa 1983 with 580 endlinks.

It measures 36mm in diameter and approximately 43mm lug-to-lug. The case appears to be unpolished.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with box or papers.

Rolex GMT-Master 1675 on Presidential in 18K Yellow Gold

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The present watch is an exceptionally preserved GMT-Master reference 1675 in 18k gold.

The case has been lightly polished. It features an incredibly attractive insert. watch features a 3.9M serial dating to circa 1975. The bracelet was made in Venezuela by D’Agosto for Rolex.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE. You can also read about the iconic watch in Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex CERN Dial Milgauss Ref 1019

When it comes to the antimagnetic wristwatches of the 1950s-1960s, several manufacturers produced what would go on to become iconic timepieces. Among those are the IWC Ingenieur, the Eberhard Scientigraf, the Omega Railmaster, as well as Patek Philippe’s reference 2570 / 2570-1 and 3417. However, one of the most beloved models of orientated for the physicist or engineer of the day was none other than the Rolex Milgauss.

The Rolex Milgauss reference 1019 was the successor for the reference 6541. Like many of the Milgauss models over the years, the dial features a distinctive look that deviates from the consistent aesthetics from Rolex models. In this instance, it is the vertically brushed silver dial, with the broad red font.

This example is a CERN dial - a rare non-luminous variant that was created at the request of scientists at CERN for their scientific instruments which could be affected by proximate radioactive materials.

The case has been polished. The watch features a 1.63M serial dating to circa 1969. It features a later Rolex Rivet bracelet dated to 1967. The 38mm Antimagnetic case is designed to withstand 1,000 gauss.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Breguet Classique Ref. 3290 White Gold w/ Box

This Breguet Classique reference 3290 is an elegant white gold dress watch. The case appears unpolished, the watch features a white Roman Numerals dial with a sub seconds register at 6 o’clock. The watch has classic Breguet hands. The watch features a sapphire crystal and manually wound movement. The watch comes from the original owner. It measures approximately 32.5mm in diameter.

We do not know the full service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with papers. It comes with a box.

Heuer “Big Eyes” Black Gilt Dial Reference 407 Unpolished

This Heuer chronograph reference 407 is an amazing example with beautiful gilt dial. It is one of the earliest water resistant chronographs from the 1940s. The chronograph case is incredibly nice and a fantastic design similar to the Gallet chronographs from the era. It measures 35mm in diameter. I have not seen one of these in this nice of condition.

The case is unpolished.

We do not know the full service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with papers. It comes with a box.

Rolex Root Beer GMT-Master Reference 16753

This Rolex “Root Beer” GMT-Master reference 16753 is an awesome GMT. This example dates to circa 1986 with a 9.3 million serial. The watch features a quickset date wheel. The case is lightly polished, but strong. The bezel insert may have been replaced at some point in the past.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Universal Genève Polerouter Black Gilt Dial Reference S20217-1 Unpolished

This is a very nice Universal Genève Polerouter Reference S20217-2. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter (44mm lug to lug). It features a beautiful non-luminous gilt dial which is in great condition and very attractive. It is paired on a contemporary Private Eyes bracelet.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Ford Blue Oyster Perpetual Reference 67198 Full Set Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer this vintage Rolex watch! This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 67198 is a very special example that was sold to Ford Motor Co in 1985. It features a very special “Ford” blue dial with diamond hour markers. It is unclear whether it was sold as a promotional award, or perhaps was given to the wife of a Ford executive.

The watch dates to 1985 and features an 8.77M serial. The case measures 26mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. It is paired on the original Rolex Rivet bracelet which are incredibly hard to find. It features an amazing case back engraving with the Ford motto “Once in A Lifetime” which is used in both past and present marketing material.

The watch comes with the coolest powder blue “Pearl” box with papers indicating the sale to Ford Motor Co. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. This is one of the coolest watches on the site, and I love being able to offer timepieces like this.

Rolex Big Red Daytona Reference 6263 Full Set

This Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 is a very attractive vintage Daytona. The dial is very beautiful and has a warm tone. It is paired on a 78350 Oyster bracelet with 571 end links. The case has been lightly polished. This watch has a 8.7M case serial number dating to circa 1985. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter. It features a service replacement bezel.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Big Red Daytona Reference 6263

This Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 is a very attractive vintage Daytona with a black matte dial. It is paired on a 78350 Oyster bracelet with 571 end links. The case has been lightly polished. This watch has a 6.15M case serial number dating to circa 1979. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter. It features a service replacement bezel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well

Rolex Blue Datejust Reference 16234 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

The watch dates to 1994 and features an S serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555B end links. It features a beautiful blue soilel dial.

The watch is in excellent condition. We believe the case is unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

MB&F Legacy Machine Sequential EVO Full Set

“Maximilian Büsser never said that MB&F would never make a chronograph. What he did say is that MB&F would never make a chronograph like all the other chronographs out there. Those who spoke the language of MB&F always knew what this meant. All good things come with time; better things come with more time and after many years of creating horological art, MB&F brings us something better: the Legacy Machine Sequential EVO, the brand's 20th movement and the first chronograph wristwatch of its kind.”

The MB&F Legacy Machine Sequential EVO is one of the best watches in the MB&F catalog, and for good reason! It is a fully integrated dual chronograph developed for MB&F by Stephen McDonnell with the signature Legacy Machine flying balance wheel. It is paired on a very comfortable rubber strap. The watch dates to 2024. The case measures 44mm with a height of 18.2mm. The case is made of zirconium, which is awesome. For those looking for a fun independent watch that will make everyone bow down, this is a perfect choice.

The watch is in excellent condition. We believe the case is unpolished. It comes with box and papers from the original owner.

Patek Philippe Reference 6007G-010 Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 6007G-010 is one of the recent Calatrava models with a touch of flair via the vibrant colors. This example features a homage to vintage examples with radial numerals. The center of the dial incorporates a beautiful crosshatch pattern complementing the strap. The white gold case is 40mm in diameter. The watch is powered by the calibre 26‑330 S C.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was purchased in 2023.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 6900 Unpolished w/ Box

I’m thrilled to offer this vintage Rolex Ladies Datejust! It is a very rare reference 6900 with Florentine bezel and bracelet. I’ve never held an example as nice as this one.

The watch dates to 1981 and features an 6.78M serial. The case measures 26mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. It is paired on the original Rolex bracelet made by Gay Freres which are incredibly hard to find.

The watch comes with the coolest powder blue “Pearl” box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

This is one of the coolest watches on the site, and I love being able to offer timepieces like this.

Rolex Polar Explorer II Reference 16570

The Rolex Explorer II reference 16570 is a fantastic sports watch. The case is polished. The case measures 40mm. The watch features an A serial and dates to circa 1999.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe 5905/1A-001 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 5905/1A-001 is a very special self-winding annual calendar flyback chronograph. The steel case measures 42mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. It is paired on a beautiful bracelet and has an amazing olive green dial.

The white gold case is 40mm in diameter. The watch is powered by the calibre CH 28‑520 QA 24H.

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was purchased in 2023.

Rolex Datejust Reference 126200 Green Dial Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 126200 is a great example from the modern catalog. The steel case is 36mm. We believe the case is unpolished. It features a beautiful green dial which looks great in any setting. It is paired on an Oyster bracelet and features a smooth bezel.

This watch comes from original owner and is “full set” with box and papers. It was purchased in 2023.

Rolex Pink Dial Air-King Reference 14010 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece with a pink dial. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. We believe the case is unpolished. It features an A serial and dates to circa 1999.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

Hermès H08 Graphene Edition Watch Full Set

The Hermès H08 watch and all watches from Hermès are interesting timepieces. They are one of the best fashion brands to create watches. This is likely because they have sold many incredible watches over the years as one of the most prestigious retailers. The watch is made of graphene composite and has a sunburst satin-brushed ceramic bezel. It measures 42mm x 39mm.

The watch comes with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great. It comes on an orange strap and does not include a black strap.

Kurono Tokyo by Hajime Asaoka 2024 Anniversary Reiwa Full Set Unpolished

Kurono watches are some of the most popular watches coming out of Japan - and for good reason! They have a great story, and are very distinct. As a very popular emerging brand, it seems as though everyone who loves watches loves Kurono watches! Kurono is a Grand Prix d'Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) winner in the years 2020, 2022, and 2023. WOW!

It measures 37mm in diameter (42.75mm lug to lug). The case is unpolished. For those looking for a great conversation timepiece that flies a bit under the radar, this watch is awesome!

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. TheIt was delivered in 2024.

Rolex Air-King 126900 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King 126900 is an amazing modern sports watch. The case is 40mm in diameter and remains unpolished. The watch dates to 2022.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

Heuer Camaro Reference 7743 N on Gay Frères Bracelet Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive racing, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera. This particular reference 7743 N represents why the Camaro is a watch that warrants more discussion. The charcoal dial has aged beautifully turning light brown. The crisp branding is emphasized in white, with highly legible subdials at 6 and 9 o’clock. We believe the case is unpolished. It is powered by the Valjoux 7730, a formidable bi-register chronograph that has stood the test of time.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The bracelet is worth approximately $5,000 alone.

Breitling Black Dial Chronomat Reference 769 Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer this super cool Breitling watch. The Chronomat is one of the ingenious timepieces deveveped by Breitling in 1941. These watches were advertised as the toolwatch for "mathematicians, businessmen, and engineers" with a logarithmic circular slide-rule. The patent was registered in 1940 by the manufacturer’s visionary Willy Breitling. This reference 769 is Venus calibre 175. The case is in outstanding condition and remains unpolished. The case measures 35mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug).

For those who are not familiar with the incredible heritage of Breiting, we encourage you to watch the linked video featuring Breitling’s Historian Fred Mandelbaum. We have become very enthusiastic about vintage Breitling watches over the past two years, and feel their catalog deserves more attention from discerning collectors.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Shreve, Crump & Low Pocket Watch in 18K Yellow Gold

This Patek Philippe pocket watch is an extremely beautiful timepiece dating to the early 20th Century. The enamel dial features a very bold Roman numeral set with blue Breguet hands. The case measures 45mm in diameter. It features “Wolf’s Teeth” gearing. The caseback features a stylized engraving of initials and the interior is engraved “M.E.M. - J.E.F. October 7, 1916”. The movement is signed Patek Philippe Shreve, Crump & Low - a prestigious Boston retailer of Patek Philippe.

To learn more about Patek Philippe pocket watches, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Why Your Perspective Should Change About Patek Philippe Pocket Watches’.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Geneve Compax Evil Nina Reference 885103/01

This Universal Geneve “Evil Nina” Compax reference 885103/01 is a super cool and collectible chronograph. We believe the case/lugs are unpolished, but the caseback has been polished. The case measures 36mm in diameter. This watch dates to the mid 1960s. It features luminous hands within the subsidiary dials.

It does not include the original box or papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux). We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust 6605 Honeycomb Dial w/ Box

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This Datejust reference 6605 is a very special example with a silver honeycomb dial. The case has been lightly polished but is attractive. The watch dates to 1956 and features an 19X,XXX serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet dated to the 4th quarter of 1955.

It does not come with papers, but it does come with a box. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Zenith Defy On Bracelet

The Zenith had some very cool and unusual designs back in the day. This Zenith Defy is one of them with a fun angular case design and integrated bracelet. The case has been lightly polished and is 34mm in diameter. For those looking for a funky vintage watch, this is a cool option.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 214270 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Explorer I reference 214270 is an amazing modern sports watch. The case is 39mm in diameter and remains unpolished. The watch dates to 2019.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Big Daytona Reference 6239 Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona reference 6239 is the original and earliest reference of the Daytona. This example features a “Big Daytona” logo where DAYTONA is large and underneath COSMOGRAPH on the dial. I really love the way the ROLEX / COSMOGRAPH / DAYTONA text lines up in descending order like a pyramid in shape.

This example also has the original early “300” Mark 2 reference 6239 bezel, which differs from later examples by having 300 on the tachymeter bezel and “UNITS PER HOUR” between 12 and 2 o’clock, whereas later bezels have it by 3 o’clock. This is the second version of the steel bezel found on the reference 6239 and the earliest Mark 1 examples have a 275 and dashes rather than dots next to the numbers.

The case features a 1.62 million serial number dating it to circa 1967. The case appears to be unpolished. This example was previously owned by a fighter pilot. He had great vision and purchased an amazing Rolex. It is paired on an extremely rare and original bracelet with 71N end links dating to 1967. Most dealers would sell this on a solid link bracelet from the 1980s (YUCK!).

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch features a new TrueDome D21 crystal. It was just serviced and it is running well.

Rolex Honeycomb Datejust Reference 6305 in Stainless Steel

The Rolex Datejust is one of the greatest watches introduced in 1945. This Rolex Datejust reference 6305 is a very special watch with the rare silver honeycomb dial. The dial features a slight bit of patina and is extremely attractive. The serial is 94,XXX dating to circa 1953.

It has the roulette date wheel which is very cool for the red numerals in the date window complementing the faded “Officially” text on the dial. Additionally, it has remarkable “dagger” markers and “alpha” hands.

The stainless steel case has been lightly polished in the past. It measures 36mm in diameter and 44mm lug-to-lug.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

For those looking for a special 1950s Rolex Datejust, this is a fantastic choice!

Patek Philippe Two-Tone Nautilus Reference 3800/001 w/ Certificate of Origin

This Patek Philippe Nautilus is an outstanding vintage-sports watch in 18K yellow gold and stainless steel. The watch dates to 1996 and has been lightly polished. It measures 37mm in diameter. The dial is absolutely gorgeous, and features original lume in the hour markers and hands. It is powered by an automatic movement.

The watch does not come with box. It comes with the Certificate of Origin dating to 1996. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex "No Date" GMT-Master Ref. 16750

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16750 is a very cool example with the transitional "No Date" Oyster Perpetual font as opposed to the standard “Oyster Perpetual Date”. These are quite rare and incredibly interesting. It dates to circa 1985 with a 8.63M serial. The case has been polished in the past. It features an I date code on the bracelet dating to circa 1984.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the previous reference ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Sky-Dweller Two-Tone Ref. 326933 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Sky-Dweller was introduced in 2012, bringing “The Crown” back into the conversation of complicated timepieces. While the Sky-Dweller is the most complicated Rolex, the beauty lies in Rolex’s ability to tone down the complication into a simple and clear dial layout. The first impression is a highly legible calendar watch, which is an annual calendar with GMT functionality.

The watch features a rotating 18k gold bezel that changes the crown function. It features a two-tone stainless and 18k yellow gold Jubilee bracelet and black dial. The watch measures 42mm in diameter.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers, and was originally sold in 2021. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master II 126711CHNR Unpolished Full Set

Rolex “Root Beer” GMT-Master II reference 126711CHNR is a great modern sports watch. It captures the iconic DNA from the two-tone vintage models. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, the GMT remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch remains in great shape, with minor scratches and is unpolished. It comes from the original owner, and is “full set” with its original box and papers. The papers date the watch to 2020.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex Air-King 116900 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 116900 is a great modern watch. The case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter. The dial features pronounced numerals at 3, 6 and 9 and features the highly attractive green “Rolex” print. It was originally sold in 2020.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Submariner Ref 116613LB Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex “Bluesy” Submariner reference 116613LB is an awesome two-tone sports watch. The watch measures 40mm in diameter and was one of the last 40mm Submariners made by Rolex before they switched to the current 41mm model. This example is unpolished. The watch was sold in August 2019.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers from the original owner.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Rolex Explorer I Ref 14270 w/ Box & Service Papers

The Rolex Explorer reference 14270 is a very fun Explorer. It is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This example features an E serial and dates to circa 1990.

It is one of the earliest 14270s!

The watch comes with box and service papers from 2021. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from the original owner. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points: A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

Vulcain Cricket Retailed by Favre Leuba Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket is among the earliest styles produced just over 100 years after the La-Chaux-de-Fonds manufacturer’s inception. Like the “LBJ” dial Crickets, it features the original or “OG” style case. However, it features the incredibly rare Favre Leuba signed dial. There are less than 10 examples known with the Favre Leuba dial.

It measures 34mm in diameter (just under 43mm lug-to-lug). The steel case features drilled lugs and is paired with an 18mm “honey” Wind strap.

The case remains unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 120, and has an exceptional voice when the alarm is engaged.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Rolex Datejust Ref. 16234 Silver Dial Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This watch features a silver dial and a quickest date movement. The watch is paired with a 62510h Jubilee bracelet. Both the bracelet and the serial number date to circa 1996. The case and bracelet are in great condition and appear to be unpolished.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.. We do not know the service history but the watch is running well.

Rolex Black Dial Air-King Ref. 14000

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is a superb example. The watch features an E serial dating to circa 1990, which is the earlier run of the reference 14000. The case has been lightly polished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire. It feature an uncommon and beautiful black matte dial with all the lume plots in excellent condition. It features the early “Precision” font. It also is an early example with a “holes case” where each of the four lugs has a hole to access the spring bars for holding the bracelet.. A few years later, the reference 14000 moved to have a solid “no-holes case” where the spring bars could only be accessed from the end links.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test. The bracelet appears to be original with the correct 557 B end links.

PRICE: $3,900 (Item SBUXYT4661)

Rolex Datejust Ref. 126234 Black Dial Full Set

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This reference 126234 features a black dial. The watch dates to 2022. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent condition. The watch comes with a white gold fluted bezel and its original Jubilee bracelet.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with a “full set” including box and papers

Rolex Air-King Ref. 14000 Silver Dial w/ Papers Unpolished

This Rolex Air King is a highly attractive example and has a silver dial. The watch has an E serial number dating to circa 1990. It is paired with steel oyster bracelet ref. 78350 with an 03 clasp code dating to circa 1990 as well. The case and bracelet are in great shape and appear to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with a box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed an 5-bar water resistance test.

It’s a super fun piece in a timeless and versatile 34mm case size.

Rolex Ladies Oyster Perpetual Reference 76080 Unpolished

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual is a super attractive ladies model. The case appears to be unpolished and has a very attractive dial. It measures 24mm in diameter. The watch features a K8 serial dating to circa 2000-2001. It feautes a DE11 date code on the bracelet (2001) and is likely original to the watch.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Ladies Oyster Perpetual Ref. 6618 Unpolished

This Oyster Perpetual is a great stainless steel ladies watch. It measures approximately 25mm in diameter. The serial number dates the watch to circa 1970. It is paired on a folded link bracelet and features a silver dial. The watch is in great shape and appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Red Submariner Ref. 1680 w/ Box

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a fantastic sports watch dating to circa 1974 with a 3.71M serial. The bracelet is period correct. The case has been polished in the past. There is no date code on the bracelet. It is likely original to the watch.

It does not come with papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16700 w/ Box & Papers

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16700 is a great watch. It dates to circa 1991 with an N serial. The case appears has been polished. It features an attractive bezel insert. The bracelet dates to circa 1994.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the earlier reference ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex MilSub Submariner Reference 5513 Royal Navy

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches ever produced and they have a cult like following. Many collectors cherish the earliest designs of the 1950s, while some prefer the quintessential design to be Submariners with crown guards which was introduced with the reference 5512/5513 case references. Of the most desired Submariners, models such as the “James Bond” or “Big Crown” come to mind, alongside the elusive “MilSub” models.

We are thrilled to offer this Rolex MilSub reference 5513 issued to the Royal Navy. The case has been lightly polished, but is very attractive. The watch features a 3.9M serial dating to circa 1975 and features an issued engraving on the case back dating to 1975. It is paired on a nylon strap, with fixed springbars. It features the original rare “sword” hands for military specifications. There are approximately 1,110 MilSub examples produced across three references (5513, the double reference 5513/5517, and 5517) of which there are approximately 300 known.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

F.P.Journe Tourbillon Souverain in Steel 38mm Unpolished

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers.

This Tourbillon Souverain in stainless steel measures 38mm. It was the final tourbillon made in a 38mm diameter case. It is one of only 38 examples - which were produced and sold in a set of five watches in circa 2015. We believe the case is unpolished. It is powered by the manual-winding calibre 1498. These five steel models were the only steel mechanical watches ever made by F.P.Journe making them extremely collectible. These models had a specific matte yellow dial only made within this set. The Tourbillon Souverain is easily the most desirable and valuable of the 5 models from the set.

The watch does not come with box and papers (as it was sold alongisde a set of set of 5 watches). We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Green Enamel Oyster Perpetual Reference 6101 In 18K Yellow Gold

We are thrilled to offer this Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 6101 with the incredibly rare and beautiful green enamel dial. These are extremely uncommon to find, and it is very special to witness dials such as these. The dial was made by Stern Freres. 6101 models with the engine turned bezels often appear to correspond with these incredible enamel dials. It features non-luminous dauphine hands.

The case has been lightly polished, but is very attractive. It measures 34mm in diameter. The watch features a 746,XXX serial dating to circa 1952. The crown is a replacement. It is paired on a Rivet bracelet. For those looking for an incredible 1950s Oyster Perpetual, this is a very exciting option!

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Retailed By Beyer Stopwatch Full Set

This Heuer stopwatch is an insanely cool stopwatch with the uncommon retailer dial from Beyer. Even cooler, the watch comes with papers and box which is so uncommon to find. I love collecting stopwatches, and this is a very special example

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Gallet Regulator Chronograph Unpolished

This Gallet Regulator chronograph is a super cool and very rare watch. The multi-tone dial is very cool and features Breguet numerals. The case is unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter and 38mm lug to lug.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 570 in Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 570 is one of the most refined and sought-after dress watches, and an iconic model within the Calatrava family. This specific watch is a reference 570 in yellow gold. It features a non-luminous dial with applied-dauphine hour markers. We believe the case remains unpolished. According to the Patek Philippe Extract from the Archives that accompanies the watch, it was produced and sold during 1962.

This watch does not include the original box and paper. It is powered by the Caliber 27 SC manual-wind movement made by Patek Philippe. It comes with an Extract from The Archives.
