By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Bombé Oyster Perpetual Reference 6590 Unpolished on Bracelet

These Rolex Bombé Oyster Perpetual watches are very special models with the nickname deriving from the cambered lugs. This particular reference 6590 is in fantastic condition and features the uncommon Explorer dial with applied 3, 6, and 9 numerals. The 18K yellow gold case is appears to be unpolished. It measures 33.5mm in diameter and 40mm lug-to-lug. The watch dates to circa 1955 with a 120,xxx serial. It is paired on a rare Gay Freres 18k stretch Rivet bracelet with 19mm straight end links dating to 1956. The bracelet could be $20,000 alone if paired a Pre-Daytona!

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 1 bar.

Rolex Sigma Dial Floating Big Red Daytona Reference 6263

This Rolex “Floating Big Red” Daytona reference 6263 is an extremely rare variant with the “Sigma” dial. This is one of the earliest Big Reds made and it is the first silver Floating Big Red Sigma Daytona I have offered for sale. The Greek Sigma letters next to T SWISS T below 6:00 refers to the hour markers being made of white gold.

The dial is in great condition with bold red “Daytona” font and original tritium lume on the dial and in the hands. The white registers are extremely clean and beautiful.

The case has been lightly polished, but remains strong. This Daytona features its original rare “Mark 1” acrylic bezel (with “hooked 2” in 200). This watch has a 4.12 million case serial number dating to circa 1976. It measures 37mm in diameter. It features a new TrueDome D21 crystal that is gorgeous. The Rolex Oyster solid link 78350 bracelet has the correct 571 end links.

The watch is fresh to market and is a fantastic deal as these are usually much higher in price. The watch does not come with box or papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Big Red Daytona reference 6263 Black Dial Unpolished w/ Box

This Rolex Daytona reference 6263 is an incredible example with a very nice black “Big Red” dial. The dial is spectacular and the lume plots are fully intact, which is rare for vintage Daytonas. The hands and dial have a nice warm lume. The registers are a nice cream color.

It features the correct Mark III screw-down pushers. It is paired on an Oyster 78350 solid link bracelet with 571 end links that appears to be original. The case appears to be unpolished and is extremely crisp with some general wear This watch has a 8.8M case serial number dating to circa 1985. It is powered by the manual-wind movement Caliber 727 made by Valjoux and is 37mm in diameter. The pushers appear to be the original Mark 3 pushers with ridges and Rolex switched to these pushers in the circa 1981/1982 timeframe for steel Daytonas with screw-down pushers.

The comes with a vintage Rolex box that is period correct and probably original to the watch. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and passed a water resistant check of 5 bar.

This is one of the nicest “Big Red” Daytonas we have ever had at a great price, so we don’t recommend waiting as I have no idea when we will find another like it at this price point.

Rolex “The Priest” Daytona Reference 6239

The Rolex Daytona reference 6239 is the original and earliest reference of the Daytona. This is an early example without the “DAYTONA” text on the dial and has been nicknamed “The Priest” in an auction in 2018 as it it is just white print and silvery-white registers on a black dial. That aforementioned example sold for 131,250 CHF at auction.

This example also has the original early “300” Mark 2 reference 6239 bezel, which differs from later examples by having 300 on the tachymeter bezel and “UNITS PER HOUR” between 12 and 2 o’clock, whereas later bezels have it by 3 o’clock. This is the second version of the steel bezel found on the reference 6239 and the earliest Mark 1 examples have a 275 and dashes rather than dots next to the numbers.

The dial and hands are original to this watch and it comes from the family of the original owner. The register hands are all original and have the flared minute and hour hands plus a thin subsidiary or running seconds hand. The bracelet is a Rolex Oyster rivet reference 7205 and slightly newer than the watch.

The case features a 1.4 million serial number dating it to circa 1966. The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch features a new TrueDome D21 crystal.

Rolex Daytona 116523 2011 GT Champion Crown Royal 24 Hour at Daytona Full Set NOS

This Rolex Daytona is a very special 116523 model won at the 2011 Rolex 24 Hour at Daytona. In January 2011, TRG and Nadeau Motorsports “filed a fifth entry for the 49th running of the Grand-Am Rolex Series presented by Crown Royal Cask No.16 Rolex 24 Hour at Daytona.” This example features a special dial for the winnings of the race. The dial is a grey/rhodium soleil finish. The case back features an engraving commemorating the 2011 GT Champion.

For those with a penchant for automotive racing and the rich history of Rolex Daytona, this is an incredible timepiece.

The watch dates to circa 2010 with a G serial. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). The watch is in new old stock condition. It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Coke GMT-Master II Reference 16710 Full Set

This Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16710 with “Coke” bezel is a super attractive watch. The black and red bezel variety is nicknamed Coke after the famous Coca-Cola soft drink!

The case has been polished in the past. The watch has an A serial, indicating production circa 1999. The watch is 40mm in diameter.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Radial Dial

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a beautiful Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 with the special “Radial Dial”. It features a 4.21M serial number dating to circa 1976. The case has been polished, but it is attractive. The bezel insert is super nice and has a beautiful faded blue with sharp red. The hands are later service hands which are noticeable under UV lighting. They match well with the luminous material on the dial.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and the corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

TUDOR Advisor Reference 7926 Like NOS

The TUDOR Advisor is an amazing and off beat model within the TUDOR catalog. The reference 7926 is an incredible model, and this is the nicest example I have ever seen. It is quite frankly a time capsule and belongs in a museum! The watch is in like NOS condition, showing very little signs of wear over the years. It measures 34.5mm in diameter (41.5,mm lug-to-lug). The case back sticker is still on the back. It is paired on its original rivet bracelet which is incredible firm. It is powered by the A. Schild calibre 1475. The watch dates to circa 1961.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Abercrombie & Fitch Alarm Watch Unpolished on Bracelet

This Abercrombie & Fitch is a super cool alarm watch. The dial features the Star of David which is incredibly cool. I have seen this on Movados, Heuers, and Abercrombie & Fitch models. The watch is powered by the A. Schilds movement. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter. It is paired on a vintage Rivet bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Grape Oyster Perpetual 36 116000 Unpolished

The Grape Oyster Perpetual 116000 is a beautiful and cool watch. This unique purple dial birthed the nickname “The Grape”. It measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. We do not know the date of production.

Heuer Carrera Tropical Reference 2447SNT Unpolished W/ Box/Papers

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s. You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

This example is a very rare reference 2447 SNT (Silver/White dial, Noir/Black Registers, and Tachymeter) dial. The subdials have developed a beautiful tropical color. The watch is powered by the manual-wind Valjoux calibre 72. The case very strong and appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a Gay Freres bracelet.

The box and papers were added by the previous owner. It was serviced by Grand Central Watch in late 2023 and is running well.

Piaget Emperador Reference 9131 C 151 Factory Diamonds In 18K YG Unpolished

This Piaget Emperador 9131 C 151 is an incredible timepiece with a diamond set dial, case and bracelet.

The watch is powered by a mechanical movement. The case is made of 18K gold. It appears to be unpolished. It measures 25mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fit a wrist as big as 6.4 inches.

Learn more about the exciting designs and movements of vintage Piaget in the article ‘Undervalued: Vintage Piaget Watches’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Piaget Emperador Reference 9131 C 505 Factory Diamonds In 18K WG Unpolished

This Piaget Emperador 9131 C 505 is an incredible timepiece with a factory diamond set dial, case and bracelet.

The watch is powered by a mechanical 9P movement. The case is made of 18K white gold. It appears to be unpolished. It measures 25mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fit a wrist as big as 6.4 inches.

Learn more about the exciting designs and movements of vintage Piaget in the article ‘Undervalued: Vintage Piaget Watches’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Lapis Lazuli Reference 8341 In 18K YG Unpolished

This OMEGA reference 8341 is such a cool and stylish timepiece. The watch is powered by a calibre 1011 movement. The case is made of 18K white gold. It appears to be unpolished. If features an incredible lapis dial and bezel. It measures 28mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fit a wrist as big as 6.5 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Aventurine Dial Integrated Bracelet Unpolished

This Audemars Piguet hexagonal case watch is an amazing timepiece with an aventurine dial and diamond hour markers. The watch is powered by a mechanical movement. The case is made of 18K yellow and white gold. It appears to be unpolished. It measures 26mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fit a wrist as big as 6.2 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Philosophique Diamond Dial 617816 Integrated Bracelet

This Audemars Piguet Philosophique is an amazing timepiece with 24-hour dial and diamond hour markers. The watch is powered by a mechanical movement. The case is made of 18K yellow and white gold. The case has been polished. It measures 26mm in diameter. The bracelet is amazing and can fit a wrist as big as 6.2 inches. It dates to the 1990s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron Constantin Patrimony 43030-206J In 18K YG

This Vacheron Constantin Patrimony 43030-206J is an incredible timepiece in 18K yellow gold. It measures 34mm in diameter. It features an ornately finished case and bracelet. The bracelet measures a wrist up to 6.9 inches. The watch dates to the 1990s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Van Cleef & Arpels 25523 18K TriGold Integrated Bracelet

This Van Cleef & Arpels reference 25523 is a special timepiece. The watch is powered by a quartz movement. The case and bracelet are made of 18K yellow, rose, and white gold. It measures 26mm in diameter. It has been polished. The bracelet is amazing and can fit a wrist as big as 6.2 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TUDOR Chronograph Reference 79180

The TUDOR “Big Block” chronograph reference 79180 is a great sports watch. This example features a B4 serial number dating to circa 1992. The case has been polished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust 16013 Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a very nice champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1983 with a 7.5M serial.

This watch does not include the box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Two-Tone Root Beer GMT-Master Reference 1675

This Rolex “Root Beer” GMT-Master reference 1675/3 is an very cool example. These have become more appreciated by collectors in recent years. The watch dates to circa 1976 with a 4.2M serial. The case has been polished in the past. The Jubilee is an original Rolex bracelet, but was modified to have gold-plated center links. The end links are from a two-tone Jubilee. The gold-plated center links on the Jubilee bracelet have some wear and are in some areas removed of the gold. It features a nice case back engraving of the initials of the previous owner.

The watch comes from the family of the original owner. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and the corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE.

Cartier Panthere WJPN0037 in 18k YG Full Set

This Cartier Panthere WJPN0037 is a very special timepiece. The case measures 27mm in diameter by 36mm in length. The case is 18k yellow gold. We believe it is unpolished. The bracelet is very nice and it features a beautiful green dial. This limited edition was made for the Middle East market at Watches & Wonders 2023.

The watch comes with box and papers.

Breguet Classique 5177 5177BB/29/9V6 in 18K White Gold Unpolished

This Breguet Classique 5177 is a very elegant dress watch.

The 18K white gold case appears to be unpolished. It measures 38mm in diameter. The watch is paired on a Breguet strap and buckle. It is powered by the calibre 777 Q. It dates to the 2020s.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. We do not know the date of production.

Movado Datron HS360 Gold Plated

This Movado Datron HS360 is a great chronograph. The gold-plated case is unpolished. It measures 37.5mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the Zenith El Primero calibre 3019 PHC.

It does not come with box and and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Autavia Reference 73363 Unpolished

This Heuer Autavia reference 73363 is a super cool example with orange accents and hands. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 7733. It is paired on a contemporary bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA De Ville Andrew Grima Inspired Model In 18K White Gold

This OMEGA De Ville is a special timepiece. These OMEGAs were inspired by the Andrew Grima designs (the Grima designed models are signed with his name). The watch is powered by a mechanical movement. The case and bracelet are made of 18K white gold. It measures 37mm in diameter by 20mm in length. It appears to be unpolished. The bracelet is amazing and can fit a wrist as big as 6.3 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA De Ville Andrew Grima Inspired Model In 18K Yellow Gold

This OMEGA De Ville is a special timepiece. These OMEGAs were inspired by the Andrew Grima designs (the Grima designed models are signed with his name). The watch is powered by a mechanical movement. The case and bracelet are made of 18K yellow gold. It measures 37mm in diameter by 20mm in length. It appears to be unpolished. The bracelet is amazing and can fit a wrist as big as 6.9 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3598 in White Gold

This Patek Philippe reference 3598 is an elegant timepiece. The case is 18k white gold. It features a “Sigma” dial. It is powered by a manual wind movement. The case measures 30mm in diameter and 35mm in length. The bracelet should fit a wrist up to 6.4 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Diehl Vulcain Cricket Waterprotected Model Unpolished

This is a cool Vulcain Cricket alarm model from the 1970s with a Diehl signed dial and case. The case appears unpolished and the diameter is 34.5mm and 39mm lug-to-lug.

The watch is powered by the calibre MSR S2. The alarm on this example is quite loud, and will surely turn some heads!

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

TUDOR Snowflake Submariner Reference 9411/0 Unpolished

The Tudor “Snowflake” Submariner Reference 9411/0 is an amazing dive watch. The case is 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1976 with an 847,XXX serial.

The caseback features a beautiful engraving “Hank, All My Love, Maggie 12-25-77”.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Movado Gilt Dial M95 Chronograph Reference 95 704 568 Unpolished on Bracelet

The Movado M95 chronographs are incredible timepieces not only for their movements, but also the cases and dials. This is a spectaculy example with a silver-gilt dial. It is paired on a Movado bracelet. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter. The case was produced by Taubert Frères (formerly François Borgel).

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TUDOR Ranger Reference 7996/0 Unpolished

The TUDOR Ranger is an incredible model with a great deal of appreciation for its similarity to the Rolex Explorer with a 6, 9 numeral layout. This reference 7996/0 is in fantastic condition with an unpolished case. It dates to circa 1968.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history. It is running well and keeping good time.

Cartier Santos Dumont XL Reference WGSA0032 18K Rose Gold Unpolished

This is a very sophisticated Cartier Santos Dumont XL Reference WGSA0032. The dial is a beautiful silver with Roman numerals. It measures 46mm x 34mm. The case is made of 18k rose gold. It appears to be unpolished. The movement is mechanical.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was produced in the 2020s.

Blancpain Fifty Fathoms Aqualung Unpolished

The Blancpain Fifty Fathoms Aqualung is a legendary dive and a model famously associated with pioneer oceanographer and French naval officer Jacques-Yves Cousteau. This watch is an attractive and large model measuring 41mm in diameter by 52mm lug to lug. This example dates to circa 1959. The case appears to be unpolished. The dial features a 1000 feet depth rating. The hands appear to have been relumed in the past.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date “Cigarette” Hands Reference 1803 on Brick Bracelet Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a spectacular example the uncommon “cigarette” hands. These are found in very few examples from the mid 1960s. The case is 18k yellow gold and measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. It has a 961,XXX serial dating to 1964. It is paired on a brick style Rolex bracelet with Florentine finish by Gay Freres dating to 1963. It is not likely the watch was born/sold on this bracelet, but it looks incredible and complements the Day-Date very well. The clasp on the bracelet is lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Breitling Pizza Navitimer Reference 1806 on Bracelet Unpolished

The Breitling Navitimer reference 1806 is one of our favorite Breitling models. The watch is a massive 48mm in diameter and was produced by EPSA. It is paired on an extremely rare Breitling mesh bracelet. The case appears to be unpolished. The dial is in fantastic condition and features very beautiful lume. The watch dates to circa 1968.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master II Reference 126710BLRO on Jubilee w/ Service Card

Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The case and bracelet clasp has been lightly polished. The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with a service card from a 2024 service by Rolex.

We do not know the date of production.

This is the best price on the planet. Let’s go earthlings!

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Zenith El Primero Reference A384 Tropical Dial Unpolished

The Zenith El Primero Reference A386 sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. It is powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019.

The watch is 38mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 45.5mm) and comes on the Gay Frères steel ladder bracelet with ZKM end links. The bracelet is missing the flip-lock clasp and has been polished. The case appears to be unpolished. The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The dial has taken on a very attractive patina.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 5970R-001 Retailed by Tiffany & Co. Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5970 is arguably the one of the most special Patek Philippe references and one of the most important perpetual calendar chronographs of all time. The reference 5970 is the successor to the Patek Philippe reference 3970. It was produced over the period of 2004-2010. It is estimated that the reference 5970 was produced in 2,800 examples. The case incorporates an aggressive lug design and features a highly legible dial. This particular example features the highly desired Tiffany & Co. double signed dial. These are extremely rare and only a few are known to the market.

The 18k rose gold case appears to be unpolished and measures 39.5mm in diameter not including the crown. It measures 46mm lug-to-lug. It is powered by the highly-praised calibre CH 27-70 Q, which is based on the Lemania calibre 2310. The reference 5970 was the last Lemania-based perpetual calendar chronograph made by Patek Philippe.

The watch comes full set with box, Certificate of Origin, pin pusher and case back. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It dates to 2005 according to the Certificate of Origin.

Patek Philippe Reference 2526 in 18K YG w/ Extract, Box & Service Box/Papers

The Patek Philippe reference 2526 is an iconic watch and this is an incredibly attractive example in 18K yellow gold. The Patek Philippe Reference 2526 is renowned as the most attractive important of Patek Philippe’s automatic references.

The watch dates to 1956 and was sold in the 1957 according to the Extract from the Archives. The case has been polished. The dial is very beautiful. The 2 o’clock hour marker is slightly askew. The case dimensions are 36mm in diameter and 41mm lug-to-lug.

It was recently serviced by Patek Philippe. It is comes with an 18mm Patek Philippe leather strap and features gold Patek Philippe buckle. It is powered by the iconic self-winding movement from Patek Philippe, the calibre 12-600. It features the 2nd series dial.

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. It does come with an Extract from the Archives, service papers and a service box. The service papers date to January 2026.

Rolex Frog Foot Mk 1 Dial Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example features an 1.73M serial dating it to circa 1967. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It features the amazing Mark 1 frog foot dial. It features “fat” style lume. It is paired on a Rolex 7026 Rolex bracelet with 58 end links dating to 1966. The bracelet has been lightly polished and was added later. For those looking for an awesome matte-dial Explorer, look no further.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Breitling AOPA Navitimer Ref 806 Unpolished

The Breitling Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) models represent an incredible chapter in Breitling’s history. This Breitling Navitimer reference 806 is an outstanding example. The dial is extremely attractive. The case is also fantastic condition. It appears to be unpolished. It is powered by the Venus calibre 178. This is one of the nicest examples of a Mk2.1 reference 806. It dates to February of 1956. It measures approximately 41mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Longines Non-Luminous Tre Tacche Reference 3864 Unpolished w/ Extract

Finding special time-only Longines watches from the 1930s-1940s is quite difficult. This Longines reference 3864 is an incredibly nice Sei Tacche watch. The steel case measures 35mm in diameter. It measrues 42mm lug to lug. It features a non-luminous dial. The case appears to be unpolished. It features fixed springbars. It is fitted with a Longines manually wound mechanical movement, caliber 12.68Z. and was invoiced on 23 December 1936 to the company Ostersetzer,, which was at that time our agent for Italy.

For those looking for a very special non-luminous Longines, this is an incredible option.

The watch does not come with box or papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Ikepod Hemipode Grand Date on Rare Bracelet Full Set Unpolished

This Ikepod Hemipode Grande Date is in new old stock condition and is COSC certified. The watch features a 43mm stainless steel case and a 4500 Dubois Depraz movement with a chronograph function. The watch features a black dial with an orange chronograph hand. It is is in outstanding condition and unpolished. The bracelet is rare and extremely comfortable on the wrist.

It comes with full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Reference 14790ST w/ White Dial

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is among the most iconic wristwatches. This example, a reference 14790ST, is a great mid-sized Royal Oak. The case measures 36mm in diameter, but wears slightly larger as it has a lug-to-lug measurement of approximately 46mm. The case has been polished.

The watch comes with a digital Extract from Audemars Piguet. These do not cite the production date. We do not know the date of production.

It does not come with a box or papers. It was serviced by Audemars Piguet in 2022 and is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-66 CB Case W/ Extract

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is a nice OMEGA Speedmaster reference 105.012. The reference 105.012 was the first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied OMEGA logo.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to the excitement of many Speedy lovers. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line from 1963 to 1966. We believe the dial is correct for the case. The case was made by CB (le Centrale Boites). It has been lightly polished. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. The bracelet will fit a wrist approximately 6.9 inches.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with an Extract from the Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Paul Newman Dial Daytona 116518LN in 18K YG on Oysterflex Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex “Paul Newman Dial” Daytona reference 116518LN is an extremely cool sports watch. The case is made of 18K yellow gold and is paired on an Oysterflex rubber strap. We believe the case is unpolished. The case measures 40mm in diameter.

The watch was sold in 2022. It comes full set with a box and papers. It was recently passed a pressure test of 5 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master II Pepsi 126710BLRO on Jubilee Full Set Unpolished

Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

This example is in great condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter. The watch was originally sold in 2019 and comes from the original owner. It comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Breitling Chronograph Ref. 777 Gilt Dial Unpolished

This Breitling chronograph reference 777 is an awesome vintage chronograph in great condition. The gilt glossy dial is highly attractive and features a gold-colored minutes/seconds track and a copper-colored outer tachymeter track. The serial number places it into the circa late 1943 to early 1944 range. The dial has an attractive and small “ANTI-CHOC” signature above 6:00 for the anti-shock capabilities of the Venus movement. It is paired on a contemporary beads of rice bracelet. The pushers appear to be original but have lost the chrome plating. The case measures approximately 37mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Ref. 16014 Linen Dial Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 16014 is so beautiful.

The 6.4M serial number dates the watch to circa 1980. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch comes on a 62510H Jubilee bracelet with an “A” clasp code, dating to circa 1979. It comes with the correct 555 end-links.

It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch is not water resistant.

Rolex No Date Submariner 114060 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. It continues as a flagship model in Rolex’s lineup to this day. The no-date reference 114060 was discontinued in 2020. The watch measures 40mm in diameter and approximately 47mm lug-to-lug. It is in excellent condition and appears to be unpolished.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. It was sold in 2017. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Chronograph 5500V/110A-B481 Full Set Unpolished

The Vacheron Constantin Overseas models have become exceedingly popular in recent years. This chronograph reference 5500V/110A-B481 is a fantastic example of why the sports watches of Vacheron Constantin are exciting. The watch features a stainless steel case. It measures 42mm in diameter. The dial is black with white sub dials. It comes with a stainless steel bracelet, and a leather strap in addition to the black rubber straps. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch was sold in 2024.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Ref. 4500V/110A-B483 Full Set Unpolished + Straps

This Vacheron Constantin Overseas reference X45AV483 is a great modern sports watch from one of the most renowned and historically significant manufacturers. The case and bracelet are made of stainless steel. The watch is in excellent condition and appears to be unpolished with minor signs of wear. The case measures 41mm in diameter. It comes with two rubber straps from Vacheron Constantin.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. The watch was originally sold in March of 202

Universal Genève Tri-Compax w/ Eric Clapton Dial w/ Box

This is a nice Universal Genève Tri-Compax with Eric Clapton dial. Clapton wore a similar model when he was part of Cream and the watch now has the moniker “Eric Clapton” in the watch community. The watch dates to the mid-late 1960s. The case has been lightly polished. The case reference and serial have been lost from the polishing, so it is not clear whether the dial was is correct for this model. The watch measures 36mm in diameter. It is powered by the calibre 281.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a Universal Genève box and hangtag. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Milgauss Ref. 116400 Black Dial Full Set Unpolished

When it comes to the antimagnetic wristwatches of the 1950s-1960s, several manufacturers produced what would go on to become iconic timepieces. Among those are the IWC Ingenieur, the Eberhard Scientigraf, the Omega Railmaster, as well as Patek Philippe’s reference 2570 / 2570-1 and 3417. However, one of the most beloved models orientated for the physicist or engineer of the day was none other than the Rolex Milgauss.

This Rolex Milgauss reference 116400 features the black dial with orange accents and iconic thunderbolt hand. The watch is in excellent and unpolished condition and is partially stickered. It measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. We do not know the full service history, but it is keeping great time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The watch dates to 2016.

Benrus Type II Class A MIL-W-50717 w/ Blue Bezel Unpolished

The Benrus Type II Class A wristwatch is one of the iconic US Military issued watches and is beginning to develop a cult following over the most recent years. It is powered by the self-winding, calibre GS1D2. The watch features fixed spring bars, and the asymmetrical case remains unpolished. It features an added vintage blue bezel - only four of these blue bezels are known and they were found in a group of vintage Benrus parts discovered by Jed Chevalier. It is sort of like a “blueberry” insert on a GMT-Master reference 1675 and a fun addition to take the watch from serious to playful.

It measures at 42.5mm in diameter and 15mm in thickness. The was issued to the US Military as part of the MIL-W-50717 contract and dated to June 1973. The watch has been in Eric’s personal collection for several years.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Longines Conquest DeLuxe Reference 9025 in 18k Rose Gold

This is an extremely attractive example of the iconic Longines Conquest. This Longines Conquest with DeLuxe dial features an incredible rose dial. The watch dates to the 1960s. It measures 35mm in diameter and is paired on an aftermarket strap and gold plated Longines buckle. The 18k rose gold case has been polished. The crown has been replaced.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Quantieme Perpetual Calendar 18k YG Ref. BA25661/002 Box & Papers Unpolished

This Audemars Piguet Quantieme Perpetual Calendar reference BA25661/002 is a fantastic complicated timepiece. APs Quantieme Perpetual models are historically important and are arguably a catalyst for models such as the Patek Philippe reference 3940. The reference 25661 was introduced in 1985 and remained in production until circa 1994. It is estimated that only 342 examples were produced (approximately 250 in yellow gold).

The watch is paired on a later leather strap with original buckle. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It appears to be unpolished with strong hallmarks. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The watch dates to circa 1991. It is powered by the beautiful automatic perpetual calendar calibre 2120/2 that can be seen through the exhibition caseback.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It comes full set with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 5070G-001 Full Set + Service Receipt

This Patek Philippe reference 5070G-001 is a fantastic chronograph designed after the classic 1940s models from the manufacturer. The case is made of 18k white gold case and is 42mm in diameter. It is powered by the manual wind calibre CH 27-70. it features a beautiful non-luminous silver dial. The case has been lightly polished.

This watch comes with box and papers and dates to 2006 (12/06) with a secret code of ATPR. It comes with service papers from Patek Philippe from 2021.

Rolex GMT-Master II 126713GRNR “Zombie” Box & Papers Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master II 126713GRNR “Zombie” is a fantastic modern sports watch. The reference number 126713GRNR denotes a steel and 18k yellow gold case and bracelet, and a black and gray rotating bezel. It captures the iconic DNA from the two-tone vintage models. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, the GMT remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch is in great condition and is unpolished. It comes as a “full set” with its original box and papers. The papers date the watch to 2023.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Patek Philippe Perpetual Calendar Black Dial Ref. 5139G w/ Extract

This Patek Philippe Perpetual Calendar reference 5139G is a beautiful 18k white gold. The case measures 39mm in diameter (approximately 45mm lug-to-lug). The watch features a black dial and is paired with an 18k white gold Patek Philippe buckle. It comes with a black hand stitched Patek Philippe leather strap as well. The case appears to be unpolished.

It comes with an Extract from the Archives. The watch was originally sold in 2011. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

A. Lange & Söhne Odysseus Ref 363.068 / LSLS3633AD in 18K WG Unpolished Box & Papers

This A. Lange & Söhne Odysseus reference 363.068 / LSLS3633AD is an incredible new product from ALS. The watch is in tremendous condition with a stunning grey guilloche dial. It features a beautiful calendar layout which is highly legible.

The case style was a very forward thinking design by Lange at the time of release. It is made of 18K white gold. The white gold case appears to be unpolished and measures 40.5mm in diameter and 11.1mm in height. It is powered by the calibre L155.1 with a 50 hour power reserve.

It is paired on an ALS rubber strap with white gold buckle. We do not know the production date, as the papers do not show this information. It was sold pre 2023.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

TUDOR Amazon Employees Watch Group Black Bay 58 79030B Full Set Unpolished

The TUDOR Black Bay 58 is one of the coolest modern watches. I love the reference 79030B - it is a classic homage to the TUDOR Snowflake. This particular example is one of only 106 examples produced for Amazon Employees Watch Group. The dial features Amazon’s mascot, Peccy, who is wearing a snorkel mask. The caseback also features Peccy, the Amazon smile logo, and the production number out of 106.

For those who love the greatness of the TUDOR Black Bay 58, this is an incredible timepiece to wear in any setting. The watch is in spectacular condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It dates to circa 2023.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Audemars Piguet Jules Audemars Metropolis 25919PT.OO.D002CR.01 Box & Papers Unpolished

This Audemars Piguet Jules Audemars Metropolis s an incredible Quantieme Perpetuel calendar with world timer indicator at 6’oclock. The platinum case measures 39mm in diameter and approximately 44.5mm lug to lug. The dial is white with silvered numerals at 2,4, 8 and 10 o’clock which are very interesting and make a visual statement. The watch features an exhibition caseback displaying the movement and engraved rotor. It comes with a platinum folding AP clasp from Audemars Piguet. The case appears to be unpolished.

It comes with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Feet First Dial Ref 5512

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is a nice example. It features a 2.79M serial dating to circa 1971. The lume features a nice warm tone. It is paired on the rare Rolex 9315 bracelet with 380 end links without date (circa 1973-1975). The case has been polished in the past.

Learn more about the iconic Submariner within Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well! It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Submariner Reference 14060M

This Rolex Submariner reference 14060M is a fantastic sports watch. It dates to circa 2004 with an F serial. The case measures 40mm in diameter. The watch is a fantastic contemporary Submariner and perfect for everyday wear. It is paired on a 93150 Rolex bracelet with 501 B end links dating to 2004 with CL date code and is original to the watch.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Heuer "Jo Siffert Colors" 2 Register Autavia Ref 73663 Unpolished

This Heuer “Jo Siffert Colors” Autavia reference 73663 is from a super cool watch that is very similar to the reference 1163 famously worn by the automotive racer. It features a Tachymeter bezel. The minute hand has been relumed. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 42mm in diameter. It is powered by the Valjoux 7736. It is paired on an annoymous strap and buckle.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron Constantin Patrimony Semainier Reference 47052 in 18K White Gold Unpolished

This Vacheron Constantin Patrimony reference 47052 is an incredible timepiece. The case is made of 18K white gold. It measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch is powered by the triple calendar calibre 889. The watch dates to the 1990s. The dial on this watch is remarkable. It is one of the best dial designs of the era.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

IWC Pilot’s Watch Performance Chronograph 41 AMG IW388305 Full Set

This is an extremely cool contemporary IWC. The IWC Pilot’s Watch Performance Chronograph 41 AMG IW388305 It is a classic interpretation of the IWC Pilot. The case is made of titanium. It is unpolished and measures 41mm in diameter. It is paired on rubber IWC strap with signed buckle. It was sold in September 2024.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

Aquastar Deepstar Big Eye Mk 4 Chronograph Mint Condition

This Aquastar Deepstar Big Eye Mk 4 chronograph is an outstanding example. The case is made in stainless steel and measures 38mm in diameter and approximately 48mm lug-to-lug. It is in mint condition. The watch features a grey dial with one white subdial at 3 o’clock displaying elapsed minutes. The watch features an engraved rotating bezel. The watch is paired with a black rubber strap and a stainless steel starfish buckle. The watch dates to circa 1965.

For those looking for one of the greatest examples of a vintage Aquastar Deepstar, this is it. The watch has been highly endorsed by the legendary Aquastar collector @10thwatch.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Edward Piguet Tourbillon 25819PT/O/0002/01 Box & Papers

This Audemars Piguet Edward Piguet Tourbillon reference 25819PT is a handsome dress watch with a tourbillon complication. It features a white dial with Breguet numerals. The platinum case features an exhibition caseback and measures 27mm by 46mm. It is paired with a leather Audemars Piguet strap and platinum folding AP clasp.

The watch comes with a box and papers. It was originally sold in 2001. We do not know the service history - it is losing a few minutes daily and will likely need a service.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5740/1G-001 Perpetual Calendar In 18k White Gold Box & Papers

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is one of the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated sports watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today.

This example is the 18k white gold Nautilus reference 5740/1G-001. It features a beautiful blue dial variant. It is powered by the self-winding perpetual calendar calibre 240 Q. The watch measures 40mm in diameter and remarkably thin at 8.43mm in height. The white gold case has been lightly polished, but is strong. For those looking for an incredible complicated Nautilus, this is a fantastic choice!

This particular model dates to 2018. The watch comes full set with the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Monaco Reference 1133B on NSA Bracelet Unpolished

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic Swiss chronograph movement featuring the iconic calibre 11. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic. The design is one of the most attractive and compelling sports chronographs.

This example is in phenomenal condition. The case appears to be unpolished. The dial is in absolutely remarkable condition, In addition, it is paired on a vintage Heuer bracelet made by NSA.

For those looking for an incredible vintage Heuer, this is an amazing watch!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Pistachio 41MM Oyster Perpetual Reference 134300 Full Set Unpolished Brand New

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 134300 is an awesome modern watch. Nicknamed “The Pistachio” - this dial is incredible and one of the most exciting 2025 releases from Rolex! People absolutely love it and are losing their minds over it! It has great proportions at 41mm in diameter. The smooth bezel gives it a slightly more dressy look. The case is in phenomenal condition and unpolished. It is brand new.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold in September 2025. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Patek Philippe 2nd Series Reference 3940J In 18k Yellow Gold w/ Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most important and elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold featuring the early 2nd series dial. The calendar/dial is in English. It is in fantastic condition. The bottom half of the 24-hour register has a beautiful slightly-beige hue from 6:00 PM to 6:00 AM. This watch features the iconic self-winding microrotor calibre 240Q.

The case is 36mm. It has been lightly polished but is very attractive. It is paired on a Patek Philippe leather strap with signed 18k yellow gold buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

The watch was come with an Extract from The Archives. It was produced in 1987 and sold in 1988.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 875 w/ Extract

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 875 is one of my favorite Memovox references. The cases are really great with fantastic proportions at 37mm in diameter (45.5mm lug-to-lug). This particular example has a very clean and beautiful dial. The case has been lightly polished, but is strong. It features a high-beat calibre 916 and the alarm sounds spectacular. For those with an appreciation for alarm watches great choice and one of my favorite recommendations to Jaeger-LeCoultre enthusiasts.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with an Extract from the Archives.

Rolex Chapter Ring PCG GMT-Master Reference 1675

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an awesome and very early pointed crown guard or “PCG” case GMT-Master reference 1675. It feautres a 623,XXX serial number dating to early circa 1960-1961. The case has been lightly polished, but it is nice. The aluminum bezel insert is extremely attractive. The gilt chapter-ring dial and luminous material are beautiful. The watch is paired on a later C&I Rolex bracelet dating to 1976.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reference 2982B Jumbo 37mm Time-Only Model Unpolished w/ Extract

We’re thrilled to offer an extremely attractive time-only Jaeger-LeCoultre reference 2982B from the early 1950s. The dial features very attractive dauphine markers similar to those seen on the Patek Philippe 2526. The watch is powered by the manual-wind Calibre P469/A.

The case is unpolished and is very large for the era. It measures 37mm in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. It features a later crown signed “JL”. It was produced in 1951

It does not come from with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with an Extract from the Archives.

Rolex Coke GMT-Master II Ref 16710 Unpolished

Originally designed for Pan-Am pilots in the 1950s, the Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most sought-after models in the brand’s lineup. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

This example, reference 16710, a stainless steel model, with a U serial dates to circa 1997. It is paired with a Rolex 78790 Oyster bracelet with a Z11 date code dating to circa 1997.

The case and bracelet are in phenomenal condition and appear to be unpolished. The watch features a black and red “Coke” bezel insert.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

A. Lange & Söhne Odysseus Ref 363.068 / LSLS3633AD in 18K WG Full Set Unpolished

This A. Lange & Söhne Odysseus reference 363.068 / LSLS3633AD is an incredible new product from ALS. The watch is in tremendous condition with a stunning grey guilloche dial. It features a beautiful calendar layout which is highly legible.

The case style was a very forward thinking design by Lange at the time of release. It is made of 18K white gold. The white gold case appears to be unpolished and measures 40.5mm in diameter and 11.1mm in height. It is powered by the calibre L155.1 with a 50 hour power reserve.

It is paired on an ALS rubber strap with white gold buckle. The watch comes with an additional leather strap.

The watch comes with box and papers. It dates to circa October 2024. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 Reference 191.039 in 18K WG Full Set Unpolished

This is an awesome A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 reference 191.039. The watch is in tremendous condition with a stunning silver dial. The case is made of 18K white gold. The white gold case appears to be unpolished and measures 38.5mm in diameter and 9.8mm in height. It is powered by the calibre L121.1.

It is paired on an ALS strap with white gold buckle. The watch comes with an additional leather strap.

The watch comes with box and papers. It dates to circa February 2023. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Ref 4500V/000R-B127 in 18K RG Full Set Unpolished

This Vacheron Constantin Overseas reference 4500V/000R-B127 is an incredible sports watch. The case is made of in 18K rose gold. The watch was sold in November 2022. It comes from the original owner. The rose gold case appears to be unpolished. The case measures 41mm in diameter. It is powered by the calibre 5100.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It comes with box or papers.

Rolex Submariner Feet First Ref 5513 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is a spectacular watch. It features a 2.90M serial dating to circa 1972. The hands feature some patina on the edges. The lume features a nice warm tone. It features the original bezel insert. It is paired on the rare Rolex “PATETED” 9315 bracelet with 280 end links dating to 1970. The case appears to be unpolished.

Learn more about the iconic Submariner within Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Blue 15510ST.OO.1320ST.06 Full Set Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the most iconic and sought after sports watches on the planet. Designed by Gerald Genta, collectors have become enamored with this important timepiece from AP.

This Audemers Piguet Royal Oak reference 15510ST.OO.1320ST.06 is an incredible stainless steel sports watch. The case measures 41mm in diameter. The dial is a very special with a beautiful blue “Grande Tapisserie” pattern. These models are among the most desired Audemars Piguet models.

The watch comes from the original owner and was sold in September 2024. The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-1 w/ Extract Unpolished

The OMEGA Speedmaster is one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This an out of this world example Speemaster reference 2998-1. It features a gorgeous dial that is turning a bit dark brown. The lume is outstanding and rich. It measures 39.5mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The original black “Base 1000” aluminum bezel is in phenomenal condition. It features the original Naiad crown.

It is powered by the manual-wind Caliber 321 (made by Lemania).

It includes an Extract of Archives from OMEGA dating to February 16, 1960. It was delivered to a US Army base in Europe.

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak C Series Ref 5402ST w/ Box

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is an icon. It is the original luxury sports watch that created and defined the category. Introduced at BaselWorld in 1972 after being designed by Gérald Genta, it turned the Swiss industry upside down and changed the fortunes of the struggling Audemars Piguet. It’s no surprise, that Patek Philippe hired Gerald Genta a few years later to introduce their own version of a steel luxury sports watch: the Nautilus.

The origin of the species is the Royal Oak reference 5402ST - nicknamed the “Jumbo”.

The dial is very attractive with a slight touch of warm patina on the surface. The watch should fit a wrist up slightly approximately 7 inches and features the original “AP” flip clasp found on C series examples. The C series models were began production in 1976 and were eventually sold unti circa 1987. This example dates to the later tail of production.

The case measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug to lug) and this is the original “Jumbo”. The case and bracelet have been lightly polished in the past like on the vast majority of these, but this case is still super attractive on the wrist.

This watch does not come with papers. It does come with a period correct box. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an attractive Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 featuring a 2.80M serial number dating to circa 1971. The case appears to be unpolished. The bezel insert is attractive with a faded red that has turned pink. There is wear near 3 o’clock. It is paired on an Oyster folded link 7836 bracelet with 280 end links dating to 1971 and is possibly original to the watch.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers. The hangtag is later. It just recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Explorer Ref 1016 Gilt Dial

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is an icon of simplicity and function. The Explorer reference 1016 was produced from 1960 until circa 1989 and it was the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" on the dial. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and I am asked for great vintage Explorer reference 1016 examples more than any other watch.

This example features an attractive gilt dial with sharp font. The hands complement the font and luminous material. It features a 1.28M serial dating it to circa 1966. The watch measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The case has been lightly polished. It is paired on a later Rolex 7206 Rivet bracelet with incorrect 80 end links.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Mark IV GMT-Master Reference 1675 Pepsi Full Set w/ Service Booklet

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an attractive Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master reference 1675. The insert is incredibly attractive and the classic blue/red synonymous with the vintage Rolex GMT-Master. The watch features a 3.24M serial number dating to circa 1973. The case appears has been lightly polished in the past. It is paired on an Oyster folded link 7836 bracelet with 380 end links without date code and is possibly original to the watch.

One neat feature of this watch is that it has a close serial to Marlon Brando’s GMT-Master reference 1675, which also had a 3.24M serial number, and shares the same Mark IV / Mark 4 dial. This run in the 3.24M range was the first use of the Mark IV dial and it did not reappear until the late 1970s.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers. It also comes with a Rolex Service booklet from the 2000s (it is not dated or filled out), It just recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Bubbleback Reference 3131 in 14k Yellow Gold

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual “Bubbleback” reference 3131 is a fascinating and early waterproof Rolex model. This example dates to circa 1946 with a 418,XXX serial. The case is made of 14k yellow gold. It has been lightly polished but is in nice condition. The dial remains unrestored which was common for many of these Bubblebacks during their peak of collecting throughout the 80s and 90s. The dial features beautiful Roman numerals and is a non-luminous variant. The crown is a later replaced Oyster crown..

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex IBM Datejust 16220 Full Set

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16220 is so beautiful. The watch dates to circa 1991 and features an X serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 78360 Rolex Oyster bracelet and 558 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition. The caseback features the former owner’s name engraved with his employer’s (IBM) logo. It was received by the employee in 1991. The case has been lightly polished.

Learn more by reading Gavin Galiardo’s Collector's Guide: The IBM Rolex Datejust.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes with box and papers.

Rolex Air-King Ref 14010 Unpolished w/ Papers & Hangtag

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch features an A serial and dates to circa 1999. The bracelet has an X9 code dating to circa 1999.

The watch does not include box. It does come with papers and a hangtag. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.
