https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Prince Imperial Black Lacquer 1585 in Steel Unpolished

This Rolex Imperial pocket watch is an incredibly cool timepiece. The Prince Imperial models were large Art-Deco pocket watches that are fascinating models. At first, we thought it was 18K white gold due to the hallmark, however it is a steel case! It measures 37mm in diameter and 41mm in length. This model is incredibly rare with a black lacquer case and black dial. The case appears to be unpolished. The case features the Geneva Key 10 indicating it was produced by Robert Meylan. The watch dates to the 1930s and this model was featured in a rare 1936 Rolex advertisement and originally retailed at 1,300 French Francs in 1936.

This watch is really a special Rolex and a connoisseurs’ choice. I would be thrilled to own something like this, and am excited to offer it for sale!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron & Constantin Chronograph Pocket Watch in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Vacheron & Constantin pocket watch is an extremely special timepiece. It is a chronograph pocket watch with a Victorin Piguet movement. Victorin Piguet is one of the most prestigious complicated movement specialist and were responsible for some of the most important complicated timepieces from Patek Philippe, Vacheron & Constantin and Audemars Piguet. This pocket is made of 18K yellow gold. We believe the watch dates to the 1910s. It appears to be unpolished. It measures 50mm in diameter. The dial is in spectacular condition. For those looking for an exceptional pocket watch, this is one of the nicest examples we have ever offered.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1601 in 18k Yellow Gold Full Set Unpolished

I absolutely love vintage Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This reference 1601 in 18k yellow gold is no exception. The watch is a very early 1601 and dates to circa 1961 and features a 680,XXX serial. It features alpha hands which are an extremely cool look on vintage Datejusts. It features an attractive champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does come with box and papers. It just passed a water test of 1 bar.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Ref 145.012-67 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional reference 145.012-67 is an exceptional example. The case appears to be unpolished. The lume is super attractive. The case measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement calibre 321. There are some scuffs on the acrylic bezel, but it is nice character.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Precision Reference 4583 On Gay Freres Bracelet Unpolished

The reference 4583 is one of my favorite time-only dress models from Rolex. In fact, it was a model I shared in a video interview with Crown & Caliber in 2017.

This watch dates to circa 1953. Its dial features a small crown and applied numeral at 12 (as well as at 6 o’clock), which is reminiscent of the Rolex “Padellone” reference 8171. The remaining hours feature applied dagger and pyramid markers also found on the more complicated Rolex. A unique distinct attribute is the leaf or “feuille” hands which are seldomly seen in Rolex models beyond this era. It is paired on a period correct Gay Freres Rivet bracelet from 1955.

The steel case is elegant on the wrist and appears to be unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter by 43mm lug to lug. It features down-turned lugs with crisp bevels. It is powered by the manual-wind calibre 700.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Montreal Chronograph Reference 110.503 Unpolished w/ Box

This Heuer Montreal chronograph reference 110.503 dates to the mid 1970s. The case is unpolished and very mint. It measures 42mm wide and 48mm in length. The watch is in outstanding condition and a great example!

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a Heuer box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera 12 Calendar Chronograph Reference 2547N Unpolished

This Heuer Calendar Chronograph Reference 2547N also known as the Carrera 12 in Heuer catalogs. The case is unpolished. It measures 36mm. It is powered by the Valjoux caliber 72C. The watch is in great condition with a very attractive dial. Of the black dial variants, we only know of 5 examples.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Seiko Golf Pocket Watch Unpolished

This Seiko Golf watches are extremely cool and uncommon models from the 1960s. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 43mm in diameter. If I saw someone playing with this on their golf bag, I would worship!

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Date Reference 15210 w/ Silver Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Date reference 15210 is a fantastic watch. It features an extremely attractive dial. The watch dates to 1997-1998 with a U serial. The case measures 34mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. For those looking for a classic Rolex Date, look no further.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447N First Execution Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 2447N is an attractive example with a 1st execution and silver script dial. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch is powered by the Valjoux calibre 72. It has a 63,XXX serial number dating to the early 1960s. The watch features a service pusher at 4 o’clock. The central chronograph hand has a nice yellow fade which is a nice and attractive contrast to the dial. I always love a great vintage Carrera and this is certainly a great option for those looking for a black dial chronograph.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

Abercrombie & Fitch For Heuer Autavia Triple Set Dash Timer

This is an extremely cool Abercrombie & Fitch for Heuer dash timer with a Hervue center clock! This dash timer dates to circa 1940. Triple sets are extremely uncommon with the original board. You usually only see two timers (or a twin board) together at maximum. These were mounted in automobiles for the purpose of racing and are absolutely incredible for those that appreciate this cross over between racing and timekeeping.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Viceroy Autavia Reference 1163V

In my opinion, Heuer "Viceroy" Autavias are some of the best buys in vintage watches. This Autavia reference 1163V is nicknamed the "Viceroy" as it was made for a promotion with Viceroy cigarette company of the United States of America in the 1970s. The case has been lightly polished. It measures 42mm in diameter.

You can read more about the history of these watches in this article by Jeff Stein.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Retailed by Freccero Reference 2500 in Rose Gold

This Patek Philippe reference 2500 is a tremendous timepiece. This example features a double-signed dial sold at the Uruguayan retailer Freccero & Cia. The reference 2500 is an interesting case design and the gold examples were produced by Wenger. The rose gold case has been lightly polished. The case measures 34mm in diameter and 39mm lug to lug. The watch dates to circa 1950.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not include the box and papers.

Heuer Jo Siffert Autavia Reference 1163

This Heuer “Jo Siffert” Autavia reference 1163 is a superb sports chronograph. The case has been lightly polished. It features an automatic movement (Caliber 11 made by Heuer-Léonidas), and is 42mm in diameter. It is paired on a Heuer buckle.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not include the box and papers.

Rolex Explorer I 214270 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Explorer I reference 214270 is an amazing modern sports watch. The case is 39mm in diameter - which they do not make anymore!!! Some have called this model the watch of Pitti Uomo Winter 2025. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 2020. As James Stacey once said when discussing the 214270, “Whether you are negotiating your day to day, or navigating uncharted territory, it is all good the Explorer way”. We agree!

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

Heuer Autavia Reference 11630 P

This Heuer Autavia reference 11630 P is an incredibly cool variant within the Autavia line - a decompression model. The watch features a diver’s decompression bezel to align with the minute hand for the diver to calculate a time at a specific depth without decompressing. These models are particularly cool with the orange accents. The case measures 42mm in diameter. It has been lightly polished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not include the box and papers.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - February 7, 2025

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Submariner PCG Reference 5512 w/ Gilt Two-Line Exclamation Point Dial

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is an awesome example with attractive pale yellow lume. It features a very well preserved gilt dial with both the chapter ring and exclamation point dial. It is a two-line dial which exists in the early reference 5512 Submariners before the introduction of the reference 5513.

The watch has a 0.819M serial dating to circa 1962. It features the original bezel insert. It is paired on a 7106 Oyster Rivet bracelet with the correct 80 end links (dating to 1963). It features the Pointed-Crown-Guard (PCG) case style which was phased out in circa 1964. The case is lightly polished but is very attractive.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Fortis Marinemaster Mk 1 Unpolished

This Fortis Marinemaster is an incredible watch. The case is unpolished and is massive on the wrist. It measures 42mm in diameter and approximately 50mm lug to lug. The dial is in fantastic condition with orange and yellow accents. These are among some of the coolest vintage dive watches and it belonged in my personal collection for years.

It does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Reference 5402ST

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is among the most iconic wristwatches and I’m thrilled to offer this exceptional and extremely early example of the origin of the species. This watch is the original Royal Oak reference 5402ST and from the A series. Only 2,000 examples were made in each A, B, and C series in steel.

This example is an extremely early A series and dates to circa 1972. It has A followed by a two-digit serial number indicating it was one of the first 100 examples produced. The dial is in excellent condition. The case and bracelet have been polished in the past, like on the vast majority of Royal Oaks, but are attractive. The watch should fit a wrist up slightly over 7 inches and features the original flip “Audemars Piguet” flip clasp with the Gay Freres “GF” logo and 1/72 date code indicating it was made in the first quarter of 1972. The case measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug to lug) and this is the original “Jumbo”.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Air-King Reference 14000 Unpolished

The reference 14000 was introduced in circa 1989 and would be produced until 1999. The case is unpolished and the dimensions are 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm. It features a E serial number dating to 1990.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Cartier Calibre de Cartier Astrotourbillon Full Set

This Cartier Astrotourbillon is a very cool complicated timepiece. The watch was a limited edition release of 100 examples. It measures 52mm in diameter and 54mm lug to lug. SOME SAY THIS IS CARTIER AT ITS BEST! SJX gave the watch a breathless review during the release in 2011 at SIHH.

“I think this is the best looking iteration of the Calibre de Cartier case yet!…The lightweight titanium gives a natural feeling of power and presence, reinforced by the exceptional wearing comfort of an ergonomic design featuring four horns that curve down to embrace the shape of the wrist…Its refined silhouette is punctuated by a satin-brushed and polished double shoulder, which protects the octagonal crown from any shocks that could interfere with the watch’s operation. The rigorous standards and creativity demonstrated by these details extend to the Roman numerals, which offer a striking reinterpretation of the Cartier codes with an extra-large XII numeral dominating the dial. Its identity is further enhanced by a succession of semi-circular finishes, which perfectly reflect the dynamics of the 21st century.”

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-6 Unpolished w/ Extract

It is no secret that I love vintage OMEGA Speedmasters. This particular reference 2998-6 is an awesome example with exceptional warm lume. We believe to be an unpolished case along with its original bezel and crown. It is extremely attractive and an example I would love to have in my personal collection and one of the nicest late 2998s I have held.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter. It comes with the original Extract of Archives from OMEGA indicating the watch was produced in 1962 and delivered to Mexico.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

Patek Philippe Reference 5110P-001 w/ Box and Service Papers

This Patek Philippe World Time reference 5110P-001 is a very attractive complicated timepiece. We believe the platinum case is unpolished. The case measures approximately 36mm in diameter (46mm lug to lug).

The watch comes with box and service papers dating to 2022.

Rolex Grey Dial Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 1601 is a superb timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). We believe the case is unpolished. It features a beautiful and uncommon grey dial. The dates to circa 1972 with a 3.11M serial.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Regatta Reference 134.500 Unpolished w/ Service Papers

This Heuer Regatta reference 134.500 is a phenomenal timepiece. These watches were used to time boat races and vintage Heuer collectors go nuts for the colorful details.

This watch does not come with box. It does come with service papers from TAG Heuer.

Tudor Submariner Reference 76100 Unpolished

This Tudor Submariner reference 76100 is an exceptional example. The watch is in great condition. We believe it is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1984.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well

OMEGA Cosmic Complete Calendar Reference 2473 in 18K Gold Unpolished

This OMEGA Cosmic calendar reference 2473 is a very cool and attractive timepiece. The 18k yellow gold case measures 34mm in diameter and 41mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447SNT Tropical Unpolished

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s. You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

This example is a very rare reference 2447 NST (Silver/White dial, Noir/Black Registers, and Tachymeter) dial. The watch is powered by the manual-wind Valjoux calibre 72. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Excelsior Park Excel-O-Graph Chronograph Unpolished

This Excelsior Park Excel-O-Graph chronograph is an amazing watch. The colorful dial is very charming. The chronograph enthusiast/collector will be extremely proud of a watch like this bearing the Excelsior Park calibre EP40.68. The case measures 42mm in diameter and 52mm lug to lug. It is a very cool competitor model to the vintage Navitimers of the period.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1002 Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 1002 is a spectacular watch. The luminous hands and dial have turned to a pale yellow color which looks brilliant on the white dial. The stainless-steel case measures 34mm in diameter (or 39.5mm lug-to-lug). The case is crisp and unpolished. This models dates to 1969 with its 2.07M serial number. The papers indicated the watch was sold to a gentleman in New York City in December of 1970. For those looking for a homerun Rolex without breaking the bank, this watch is a great option! It comes from the grandson of the original owner.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It is water resistant to 5 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/rolex-oyster-perpetual-reference-1002-full-set

Longines Reference 3164 in White Gold w/ Extract

This is a very cool and interesting vintage Longines model from the 1930s in white gold. The case has been polished. It measures 22mm in diameter and 40mm in length. The watch is powered by the calibre 20.28. The dial has been reprinted at some point in the past, but we still see it as a very cool timepiece from an incredible manufacture. We sourced the watch from a collection in Mexico a few years back.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It comes with an Extract from the Archives confirming the original invoice on 19 November 1936 to Zivy which was an agent for Longines in Mexico.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Stelline Star Dial Galaxy Reference 6098 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

We are thrilled to offer this Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 6098 “Ovettone” with the rare “Galaxy” “Stelline” dial. The Ovettone means large egg in Italian and these models are appreciated due to their larger 36mm proportions with elongated lugs. These reference 6098s in 18k yellow gold with cream-colored honeycomb dials are referred by Rolex as the “Galaxy” per their 1950s marketing material. The Galaxy models are extremely rare and some of the most special Rolex watches ever produced in our view. There are 10 examples known Galaxy models, and of the 10 examples we only know of two that are unpolished.

The case is a 928,XXX serial dating to circa 1953. This example has a beautiful honeycomb dial with eight incredible 3 dimensional star-hour markers. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a very elegant Hermès leather strap looks outstanding with the lume and central second hand. It is truly a connoisseur’s watch so it is very special to be able to offer and enjoy such a watch. Many consider these the most sophisticated time-only Rolex watches ever made.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping great time. Learn more about these special watches on the article ’In depth review Rolex: A closer look at star dials’.

Rolex GMT-Master Broad PCG Reference 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980. For Eric and myself, there are few vintage watches better to own and enjoy than a great Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675.

We are excited to offer this exceptional Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 with “Broad Pointed Crown Guards”. It is one of the nicest Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675s that I have ever held, and it is easily a grail-worthy Rolex. The case appears to be unpolished. This GMT-Master 1675 has an insanely attractive bezel insert. The gilt dial is outstanding and the lume is very warm colored. The watch has a 1.1 million serial number dating back to circa 1964 and is paired on a period correct 7206 Gay Freres Oyster Rivet bracelet with 58 end links from 1967.

It does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running great. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1013 in 18K YG Unpolished w/ Box

I’m thrilled to offer this awesome Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 1013. The reference 1013 is a rare and oversized Oyster Perpetual model which measures 36mm (43mm lug-to-lug) and is the gold offering alongside the Osyster Perpetual reference 1018. The reference 1013 and 1018s were models that were not mass-produced and it is very exciting to get them in our inventory. The reference 1013 is a very cool and less common than the steel reference 1018. These are also interesting as they have a few dial and hand variants. This example features the arrow hour markers and baton hands that are a super attractive combination. The Rolex font on this dial is very bold and it is extremely charming. The champagne soleil finish is incredibly attractive and one of the most beautiful champagne dials I’ve seen. The case appears to be unpolished. For those looking for an incredible 36mm gold Oyster Perpetual, this watch is just out of this world.

To learn more about the Rolex reference 1018, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1018’.

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980. For Eric and myself, there are few vintage watches better to own and enjoy than a great Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675.

We are excited to offer this exceptional Rolex GMT-Master 1675. The case appears to be unpolished. This GMT-Master 1675 has a very cool insert. The red has faded to a beautiful salmon color and the blue to a super attractive denim color with some fading. The watch has a 1.8 million serial number dating back to circa 1967 and is paired on a period correct C&I oyster bracelet from 1967.

It does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running great. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

I love this GMT-Master and am excited to offer it for sale!

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Black Dial Air-King Reference 14000M Unpolished

The Rolex Air-King reference 14000M is a great watch. This Air-King has a beautiful black dial and is paid nicely on an oyster bracelet. This watch features a F serial dating to 2003. The watch measures 34mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Vulcain Paul Newman Chronograph Unpolished

This Vulcain chronograph is a super cool and uncommon model. It features the rare “Paul Newman” dial variant made by Singer. The watch is a very fun one. Charlie Dunne is taking bets that when Vulcain sees it they will make a reissue like the time made an LBJ dial two weeks after his Hodinkee article! The case appears to be unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter by 44mm lug to lug.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

LIP Paul Newman Chronograph

This LIP chronograph is a super cool watch with the “Paul Newman” dial produced by Singer. The chrome plated case has been polished and measures 35mm in diameter by 42mm lug to lug.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Taylor Yachting Stopwatch

This Taylor yacht-timer stopwatch is a super cool example with a multi-colored dial. The stopwatch is deigned for timing yacht races or and nautical sports. The case is chrome plated.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Pocket Watch in Yellow Gold

This Rolex pocket watch is a very cool example dating to the 1930s. The yellow gold case measures 41mm in diameter. It is a similar case design to the later Panerai Radiomir models (which were also made by Rolex). The case has been polished in the past. The dial features very nice and bold Arabic numerals. The sword hands are very attractive and heat treated to a strong blue.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Breitling Chronomat Reference 1808

This Breitling Chronomat reference 1808 is a super cool model with the automatic calibre 11. This example features the less common silver dial variant. These models are super cool and large chronographs from the 1970s. The case measures 48mm in diameter. It has been lightly polished. It is paired on a modern Breitling mesh bracelet.

For those who are not familiar with the incredible heritage of Breiting, we encourage you to watch the linked video featuring Breitling’s Historian Fred Mandelbaum. We have become very enthusiastic about vintage Breitling watches over the past two years, and feel their catalog deserves more attention from discerning collectors.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Blue Dial Datejust Reference 16234 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

The watch dates to 1994 and features an S serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555B end links. It features a beautiful blue soilel dial.

The watch is in excellent condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 50th Anniversary Ref. 26240ST.OO.1320ST.02 Chronograph Full Set Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is easily one of the hottest watches and is desired by the most passionate watch collectors. This particular model is the “50th Anniversary” reference 26240ST.OO.1320ST.02 selfwinding chronograph. It measures 41mm in diameter. The case is unpolished and has a few scratches on the bracelet and case.

This watch comes from the original owner and includes the original box and papers.

A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia Moon Phase Reference 384.026 Full Set Like New

This A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia is a very sophisticated timepiece. It is powered by the automatic calibre L086.5. It features a 72 hour power reserve. The case is unpolished and in like new condition. It measures 40mm in diameter and is 18k white gold.

It comes full set from the original owner bought it in 2023 and wore it very seldomly.

Rolex John Bull Ladies Datejust Reference 69173

This Rolex Ladies Datejust reference 69173 is an extremely cool example. The case was hand finished / carved by master watchmaker Günter Miethe of the official Rolex retailer of The Bahamas called John Bull This was a popular request by the Bahamian retailer throughout the 1980s. It has a two-tone case. The case has been lightly polished. The watch features an 8.2M serial number dating to circa 1985. It measures 26mm in diameter. This is the first ladies John Bull Rolex I have had.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Cartier Paris Dial Vendome in White Gold Unpolished

The Cartier Vendome is a super cool vintage model. The case is 26mm in diameter. The 18k white gold case appears to be unpolished. It features a manually wound movement. The watch dates to the late 1970s/early 1980s. It comes with the original white gold deployant buckle. The dial is gorgeous and is signed “PARIS” below 6:00, which is more desirable to collectors than later models that say “SWISS MADE” in that location.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Seiko Gen 2 RAF Chronograph Unpolished

This Seiko Gen 2 RAF chronograph is a super cool model with some street cred! It is an issued watch dating to the 1990s. The P on the dial indicates the luminous material Promethium 147 which is super cool. The titanium case measures 37mm in diameter (43mm lug to lug). It is paired on a cool Pheonix nylon strap.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It recently had a battery replacement and is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16750 Box & Papers Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16750 is a very cool example. It dates to circa 1986. The case is probably unpolished with wear.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the previous reference ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Explorer II Reference 1655

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

It dates to circa 1982 (7.36 million serial number). It features the automatic calibre 1575 made by Rolex. The case has been lightly polished but is very attractive.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was just pressure tested and passed 5 bar.

LeCoultre Polaris Reference E 859 Unpolished w/ Extract

In my early days of collecting, these LeCoultre Polaris watches were a grail. Still to this day, whenever I come across a vintage Polaris I am so excited and it brings me back to the days of admiring them online. The E 859 is the ultimate JLC sports model with the iconic automatic-alarm calendar calibre 825 movement. It is one of the legendary dive watches, and it began production by Jaeger-LeCoultre in 1965. This example is really special. The dial is signed simply “LeCoultre” for the American market while European market models are signed “Jaeger-LeCoultre”. I love the American market dials, and the stylized “Automatic” script. The case is made by Ervin Piquerez (EPSA). It is super attractive and we believe it is unpolished.

This example was produced in circa 1968. It features one of my favorite dial configurations with the matte dial and luminous 6, 9, and 12. It wears so nice at 42mm in diameter and 47mm lug-to-lug. It is paired on the JB Champion bracelet which were sold in the American market on. It could likely be its original bracelet.

The watch does not come with box or papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with a Tropic strap.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this black-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1987 and features a 9.5M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 5 bar.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Automatic Time-Only Reference E 326 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Extract

I am very excited to offer this super attractive automatic time-only Jaeger-LeCoultre from the mid 1950s. The reference is designated as E 326 (E standing for Entache or waterproof). The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 812 (a bumper system which Jaeger-LeCoultre was known for during the 1950s-1960s). This example dates to 1956. It has a very warm-toned dial which is great. We believe the 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. It features a monobloc case with an outstanding angular bezel. It measures 34mm in diameter and 41mm lug-to-lug.

It does not come from with box and papers. It was just serviced and it is running well. It comes with an Extract from The Archives. For those who adore the 1950s JLC timepieces, this is an outstanding example.

Rolex King Midas Cellini Reference 4017 in White Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Midas Cellini reference 4017 is absolutely incredible. The watch is in outstanding condition. We believe the white gold case is unpolished. It measures 28mm in diameter (28mm lug-to-lug). The watch dates to circa 1974 with a 3.6M serial.

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Sea-Dweller Reference 126600 Full Set Unpolished

This Sea-Dweller reference 126600 is an awesome modern watch. The watch was sold in 2018. The case is 43mm in diameter.

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

TUDOR Chronograph Reference 79280 Unpolished

Let’s talk about that sweet spot in TUDOR’s chronograph history between the Big Block and the Prince Date (+/-Tiger) - from 1995 to 1997 there was this - the Oysterdate “Small Block”. It retained all of the Rolex markings - case and crown - packaged in a smaller, Daytona-sized stainless case. The dial at 3 o’clock still read “Oysterdate” just like the Big Block. We have seen several examples of 1995 Big Blocks and of 1997 Prince Dates. So we know there was a very short run of this model - the best of both chronographs sandwiched in a brief window.

The tachometer bezel is clearer, more modern than that of the Big Block. And the hour markers are no longer trapezoidal in shape. So it takes specific transitional design cues from the chronos that bookend it. To me, it’s the most like a Daytona that TUDOR ever made. I don’t know how many were made in this form, but I know that 1996 is the most consistent year I see on their birth certificates. This example has a B631xxx serial so we believe it is a 1995 watch.

The watch is super mint with very little signs of wear. The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch has outstanding provenance.

Rolex Cellini Reference 5240 in Platinum Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Cellini is a very cool and interesting timepiece. The watch dates to 2000 with a K serial. The platinum case measures 35mm in diameter (38mm lug-to-lug). We believe it is unpolished.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Longines Turkish Wristwatch Retailed by Nacib K. Djezvedjian & Son, Constantinople

This Longines is a very interesting and early wristwatch which was for the Turkish market. It was retailed by Nacib K. Djezvedjian & Son, Constantinople. The case is made of steel. The dial appears to have been cleaned of the luminous material and was restored.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. An Extract has been ordered.

Vulcain Cricket Sensilarm Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket is a super cool and uncommon model which was designed for the hard-of-hearing as the vibrations on the wrist were more noticeable than previous iterations. This Sensilarm is in exceptional condition and was in my personal collection for a long time. It is unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter and 42mm in length.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Aquastar Celsius Dive Gauge

This Aquastar Celsius dive gauge is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

Aquastar Celsius Black Dive Gauge

This Aquastar Celsius dive gauge is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Scubapro by Aquastar Fahrenheit Dive Gauge

This is a super cool double-signed version of Scubapro by Aquastar Fahrenheit dive gauge. It is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Scubapro Fahrenheit Dive Gauge

This Scubapro Fahrenheit dive gauge is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Aqualung U.S. Divers Fahrenheit Thermometer Dive Gauge

This Aqualung U.S. Divers Fahrenheit Thermometer Dive Gauge is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Aquastar Fahrenheit Dive Gauge

This Aquastar Fahrenheit dive gauge is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Dacor Fahrenheit Dive Gauge

This Dacor Fahrenheit dive gauge is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Scubapro Fahrenheit Dive Gauge

According to Scott Sawaya “This Scubapro Fahrenheit dive gauge is special! It is a Scubapro branded only and one of the only blue dial with steel backing I have ever seen. Most are black plastic!”

It is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Black Compass Dive Gauge

This black compass dive gauge is an awesome accessory for the vintage dive watch enthusiast. To learn more about these amazing dive gauges, read Scott Sawaya’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Vintage Dive Gauges’!

It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Submariner Starbucks 126610LV Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 126610LV is an awesome sports watch. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch is like new.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the date of production.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Rolex Submariner Feet First Reference 5512 Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is a super cool watch. It features a 3.3M serial dating to circa 1973. The hands are likely replacements and there is a small patching in the hour hand for stabilizing from a later date.

It features the original bezel insert. The case has been polished in the past. It is paired on a period correct C&I bracelet which it was likely sold on.

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It has been pressure tested and passed a 5 bar test.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Jules Jurgensen Nautical Cricket by Vulcain Unpolished

This Jules Jurgensen is a super cool and uncommon Cricket Nautical by Vulcain. The case is well worn but unpolished. It measures 42.5mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Tudor Submariner Reference 79090 Unpolished

This Tudor Submariner reference 79090 is an exceptional example. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1992. The case measures 38mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and materials. It has outstanding provenance.

Fortis Spaceleader Unpolished

This Fortis Spaceleader is a super cool and rare model. It has an amazing crosshair crystal. The PVD case is in excellent condition.

The watch does not come with box and materials. It has outstanding provenance.

By Charlie Dunne

Breitling Gilt Glossy Reference 777 Chronograph Unpolished

This Breitling chronograph reference 777 is an awesome example with an unpolished steel case. The gilt glossy dial is very attractive with a gold-colored minutes/seconds track and a copper-colored outer tachymeter track. The serial number places it into the circa late 1943 to early 1944 range. The KOS import code on the movement signifies that it was imported by Paul Buhre to the United States, which makes sense. The dial has an attractive and small “ANTI-CHOC” signature above 6:00 for the anti-shock capabilities of the Venus movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

IWC Reference R1160AD In 18K Gold Unpolished Full Set

This IWC reference R1160AD is a super nice automatic watch. It is in very mint condition. The case is unpolished. It features very beautiful perlage on the interior caseback. It measures 31mm in diameter by 39mm in length.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was serviced in the last two years and is running great.

Vacheron & Constantin Ref 6456 In 18K YG Unpolished W/ Box

This is a special Vacheron & Constantin reference 6456. It is in outstanding condition with very little wear. The watch is made of 18K yellow gold and remains unpolished. It comes from the granddaughter of the original owner. The watch dates to the late 1960s. The case was made by Edmor S.A. It is powered by the calibre 1001/1. It is 34mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Heuer Montreal White Dial Ref. 110.503 Unpolished

The Heuer Montreal was originally released in 1972. The reference 110.503 is powered by the Heuer Calibre 12. This example features a white dial with contrasting white track and subdials. It features red luminous hands and a date window at 6 o’clock. The Heuer signed NSA bracelet is unpolished. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Heuer Montreal Black Dial Ref. 110.503

The Heuer Montreal was originally released in 1972. The reference 110.503 is powered by the Heuer Calibre 12. This example features a black dial with contrasting white track and subdials. It features red luminous hands and a date window at 6 o’clock. The Heuer signed NSA bracelet is unpolished. The case has been polished.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Cartier Santos Carrée Ref. 1172961 w/ Box

This Cartier Santos Carrée is an elegant two tone watch. It measures 29 by 41mm. The case and bracelet are in 18k yellow gold and stainless steel. It is automatically wound. It features a white dial with classic Cartier Roman numerals. There is a date aperture at 3 o’clock. The case is polished.

The bracelet approximately 7 inches in circumference!

The watch does not come with papers. It comes with a Cartier box. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph Full Set “50th Year Anniversary” 26240ST Full Set

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is easily one of the most recognized watches on the market today. This particular model is the “50th Anniversary” reference 26240ST with a blue dial. It is a selfwinding chronograph with an exhibition caseback and 50th anniversary rotor. It measures 41mm in diameter. The case is unpolished and has a few scratches on the bracelet and case.

This watch comes as a “full-set” with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but the watch is running well. It was originally sold in 2022.

Rolex Submariner Bart Simpson Gilt Dial Reference 5513

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is a special example with the “Bart Simpson” coronet/dial. The Bart Simpson dials are among the final gilt versions on Submariner dials. The watch has a 1.51M serial dating to circa 1966. The lume is puffy and warm. The case has been lightly polished, but is attractive. It is paired on a firm 7206 Rolex Rivet bracelet with 80 end links dating to 1968. It features the correct long 5 bezel insert. It features the correct twin-lock crown.

Learn more about the sibling Submariner model within Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Gilt Dial Explorer I Ref. 1016

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. This model features a gilt dial. The watch features a 1.50M serial dating to circa 1966.

The case is 36mm in diameter. It has been polished. It is paired on a Rolex 7206 Oyster bracelet dating to 1965 with 80 end links which are for GMT-Masters and Submariners.

The watch does not include box or papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 1570 made by Rolex). We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Rolex Submariner Ref. 14060 w/ Hangtag Unpolished

This Rolex No Date Submariner reference 14060 dates to circa 2000 with a P serial. It is a great modern Submariner, before the switch to ceramic bezels. It measures 40mm in diameter and features a black ‘white gold surrounds’ dial with super luminova plots. It comes with a Rolex Osyter bracelet reference 93150 with an AB clasp code dating to circa 2000 as well. It is likely original to the watch. The case and bracelet are in great condition and appear to be unpolished.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch does not include a box or papers. It comes with a Rolex hangtag that features the watch’s serial number. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Heuer Carrera Ref 2447N

The only time better than now to buy a vintage Heuer was back in the 1960s! The Carrera is an iconic sports chronograph which is one of the most versatile watches to have in a collection. This is an attractive example with the black dial. It measures 36 millimeters in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. The watch is powered by the Valjoux manual-winding calibre 72. The case has been polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was serviced in November 2024, and is running well.

Rolex Submariner Matte Dial Ref 5513

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is a nice matte dial example. The watch has a 5.09 serial dating to circa 1978. The case has been lightly polished, but is attractive. It is paired on a firm Rolex C&I bracelet dating to 1978.

Learn more about the sibling Submariner model within Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Two-Tone GMT-Master Ref 16753

This Rolex two-tone GMT-Master reference 16753 is a great watch. It dates to circa 1988 with a R67 serial. The case has been polished in the past. It is paired on an Jubilee Rolex bracelet, which is very firm with K11 date code dating to 1986.

The hands have been replaced in the past.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but is it running well. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Tropical Gilt Underline Explorer I Reference 1016

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This model features a tropical gilt underline dial. The watch features a 1.04M serial and dates to circa 1964. The case is 36mm in diameter. It has been polished in the past. It is paired on a later folded link Rolex bracelet dating to 1971. This watch also features 280 end-links, which are typically associated with Submariners.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Bulova Automatic Wristwatch Unpolished

This is a very fun Bulova wristwatch. The stainless steel case measures 35mm in diameter. It features a very cool non-luminous dial. The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Shipping FedEx overnight is $100. Shipping by USPS is $10.

LeCoultre Reference 64 Eight Day World Time Travel Alarm Clock

This LeCoultre reference 64 is a very special eight-day alarm clock. The watch is produced for the American market - with the “LeCoultre” only signature dial. It features a rotating internal bezel with world time setting that can be used to reference times in other cities. The case is made of brass and measures 50mm in diameter. There is a very cool advertisement of these clocks from circa 1960.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

LeCoultre Reference 64 World Time Travel Alarm Eight Day Clock

This LeCoultre reference 64 is a very special eight-day alarm clock. The watch is produced for the American market - with the “LeCoultre” only signature dial. It features a rotating internal bezel with world time setting that can be used to reference times in other cities. The case is made of brass and measures 50mm in diameter. There is a very cool advertisement of these clocks from circa 1960.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

BVLGARI Serpenti by Vacheron Constantin in 18k Yellow, Rose & White Gold Unpolished

This is a very rare and special vintage BVLGARI (Bulgari) Serpenti model by Vacheron Constantin. These vintage Serpentis are super rare and almost mythical timepieces. Very few come to market, and this example is the most attractive configurations we have seen. The bracelet is a tri-colored gold example in rose, white, and then yellow gold into the case. The watch dates to the 1970s, per the Vacheron Constantin dial signature without the ampersand. The case measures 17mm in diameter. Other Vacheron Constatin examples have less attractive head shapes and case symmetry at the tubogas bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3970ER w/ Box

The Patek Philippe reference 3970 is part of the legendary family of Patek Philippe perpetual calendar chronographs. It is the successor to the cherished reference 2499 with its origin beginning in 1986. It would be produced over the course of the following two decades and remains one of the finest timepieces made within the recent chapters of Patek Philippe’s history.

This example features a beautiful 18K rose-gold case and has a third series dial. The movement is operated through the calibre 27-70 Q (which originates through the Lemania 2310). The pump pushers are incredibly satisfying when engaging the chronograph. This watch dates to the early 2000s. The 18k rose gold case has been lightly polished, but is very attractive.

An Extract from The Archives has been ordered. The watch comes with a box. It comes with the solid case back and pin pusher. The lug width is 19mm and it comes with a Patek Philippe pin buckle in 18k rose gold on a beautiful strap. For those looking for an incredible complicated yet elegant timepiece from Patek Philippe, the reference 3970 is one of the greatest choices!

Gallet MultiChron 12 w/ Luminous Dial

Gallet has made some of the greatest chronographs of all time and the Multichron 12 is among the most special watches from the manufacturer. The steel case has been lightly polished. It measures 38mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the Excelsior Park 40. It features a luminous dial and luminous hands. The hands are a different color, but we believe they contain the original lume.

It does not come with a box or papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Gallet Clamshell Chronograph Unpolished

This Gallet Clamshell chronograph is an awesome vintage watch from the 1940s. The cases on these chronographs are among my favorite designs and are the earliest waterprotected chronographs. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter (42mm lug to lug). It is powered by a Venus movement. The crown is a service replacement.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Salmon Gilbert Albert Dial 18845-1 in 18K Rose Gold Unpolished

This Universal Genève reference 18845-1 is a very special example in 18k rose gold. The case appears to be unpolished and features a beautiful Florentine finish. The case measures 26mm in diameter (32mm lug to lug). It is paired on a very nice Hermes leather strap.

This dial configuration is often referred to by collectors as “parachute dials” and are seen on several of the Gilbert Albert models from Patek Philippe and also seen on some watches from Swiss watch fairs during the early 1960s. The painted diagonal lines can be seen in the “Montres Golf” reference 783 designed by Gilbert Albert, as well as on a special Patek Philippe reference 3423.

The manual wind movement was just serviced and is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Gallet 24 Hour Dial Chronograph Unpolished

This Gallet chronograph is an awesome vintage watch from the 1960s. These are super attractive with the 24-hour dial. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (44mm lug to lug). It is powered by a Valjoux movement. The hands are a different color, but we believe they contain the original lume. For those looking for an awesome Gallet chronograph, this is a great option.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Breitling Tillhör Televerket Long Playing Chronograph Unpolished

This Bretling reference 810 is a super cool example that features a Tillhör Televerket dial which was given as equipment to the Swedish telecommunication company. The watch dates to the 1960s. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 38mm in diameter. The dial is phenomenal. For those looking for a rare and killer chronograph, this is a great option!

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual 36mm Reference 1018 Unpolished

We’re thrilled to offer this awesome Rolex reference 1018 which formerly belonged in Eric’s personal collection. The reference 1018 is a rare and oversized Oyster Perpetual model which measures 36mm (43mm lug-to-lug). The 1018 was a reference that was not mass-produced and as a result of its narrow font has become an exciting Oyster Perpetual model for collectors who appreciate minimal and balanced dials. Oftentimes, the reference 1018 will show signs of water ingress or feature service dials. This example features a beautiful silver soliel dial which is similar to the silver dials of Rolex Big Red Daytona 6263s. This example dates to 1966 with a 1.45M serial. The case appears to be unpolished and the watch is paired on a fantastic period correct 7206 bracelet with 58 end links dating to the 4th quarter of 1966 (which I would value at $4,000).

To learn more about the Rolex reference 1018, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1018’.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Blancpain Fifty Fathoms Bathyscaphe 5000 0130 Full Set

This Blancpain Fifty Fathoms Bathyscape is in exceptional condition. The ceramic case measures 42mm in diameter. It is a great jet-black watch for any stealthy situations or just having fun in the water.

It comes from the original owner and comes with box and papers who bought it in 2020. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional 145.012-67 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional reference 145.012-67 is a very cool example. The case appears to be unpolished and measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement calibre 321. It is paired on a newer Forstner bracelet which looks very cool. The crown is original.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was recently serviced and it is running well.

Gallet Flying Officer Chronograph Unpolished

This Gallet Clamshell chronograph is an awesome vintage watch from the 1960s. The cases on these chronographs are incredibly cool chronographs. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (45mm lug to lug).

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Enicar Mk 3 Sherpa Graph Reference 072-02-01 Unpolished

The Enicar Sherpa Graph models are extremely desirable for collectors and very difficult to find. The present watch is a fantastic example with the case made by E. Piquerez SA (also called EPSA). Enicar was way ahead of other companies with the white registers against a dark blue dial and a large 40mm diameter case with a tachymeter scale. The case appears to be unpolished.

It is a “Mark III” / “Mark 3” model with reference 072-02-01 on the case back. It features the original signed Compressor crown with the Saturn logo, while the earliest examples have no logo.

If you are looking for a large alternative cool sports chronograph , this is really is such a watch!

This watch does not include box or papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and is 40mm in diameter. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Girard-Perregaux Playboy Reference 9034 Unpolished

The Girard-Perregaux reference 9034 is a super cool model and one of the nicest examples I have seen. It is often referred to at the “Playboy” for its feature in the publication. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34.5mm in diameter.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

LeCoultre w/ Fancy Lugs in 14K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This is one of the nicest vintage American-market LeCoultre models. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 30.5mm in diameter or 39mm lug-to-lug. The case was produced by DiVincenzo & Arienti. The inlaid material within the lugs appears to be enamel. The dial is very intricate and appears to be made from Stern Freres. It is very similar to the dials found in Markowski Patek Philippe models and numerals are very similar to those found on Padellone models.

The Extract from the Archives mentions the "model [was] intended exclusively for the U.S. market" and that the production date is 1953.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Movado Ermeto Chronometer w/ Asprey London Box

The Movado Ermeto “purse watch” is one of the coolest horological accessories. These watches are super interesting and have some great variations in both material on the sterling silver cases, as well as dial variants and complications. This example is a fantastic example with Shagreen case decoration. The watch dates to the 1940s. It comes with an incredible and rare presentation box from Asprey - a prestigious London retailer.

It does not come with papers. It does come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Black Dial Air-King Reference 14000 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is a superb example with a black dial. The watch features a N serial number and dates to circa 1991. It is an early example with holes on the lug profile and T Swiss Made T dial (with later ones having “Swiss Made”. Also, the “Precision” font is slightly different. The case appears to be unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It has just recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Heuer “Derek Bell” Autavia Reference 1163 Unpolished

This Heuer Autavia reference 1163 is a great watch. It is in outstanding condition. The case appears to be unpolished. This has an MH “Minutes and Hours” bezel that is original to the watch. It is paired on an early Gay Frères beads of rice bracelet with correct early HLD end links. It has an automatic movement (Caliber 11 made by Heuer-Léonidas), and is 42mm in diameter. The serial places it in the 225,XXX range.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. This watch does not include the original box and papers.

Hublot Big Bang MECA-10 Ceramic Blue St. Barth Limited Edition Full Set

This Hublot Big Bang in blue ceramic is a super cool sports watch and a very limited edition of 25 examples for Saint Barthélemy! The watch is in exceptional condition. It measures 45mm in diameter. The retail price was $25,000, so this is the deal of a lifetime for a Hublot collector! Once your friends see you in this, they will be forced to worship you.

It comes from the original owner and comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Heuer Yacht-Timer Wrist Stopwatch

This Heuer Yacht-Timer wrist stopwatch is an insanely fun timepiece with multi-colored dial. The stopwatch is deigned for timing yacht races or and nautical sports.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Daytona Reference 6239 with Non-Luminous Dial and Hands

In the world of vintage Rolex Daytonas, finding a watch that is possibly unique is essentially unheard of - even the famed Daytonas with "Paul Newman" exotic racing dials were made in the thousands. This vintage Daytona is another level of rare as the dial never had the tritium luminous plots all other vintage Daytonas had and the hands are solid without a space for the tritium luminous material to sit for reading the time at night. Dating to circa 1966, this Daytona has a silver "Big Daytona" dial where the ROLEX / COSMOGRAPH / DAYTONA text is lined up like a pyramid, which is an aesthetically-pleasing configuration and allows for the space above the hour register by 6:00 to be clear of any text. The watch is manually wound (like all vintage Daytonas made before 1988) and approximately 37mm in diameter. This watch emerged to the market from the original Canadian in 2020. According to him, “I purchased the Rolex new when I was a teenager here in Calgary, Alberta. I worked for the Hudson Bay Company (a department store) and we received a staff discount, so I paid $375 (CAD) for it new.”

Why this watch is non-luminous is somewhat of a mystery, but it is clear that it was not a production error as the hands are notably non-luminous. Rolex made a small number of non-luminous tool / sport watches in the 1950s and 1960s, most notably the Milgauss designed for scientists and doctors working in environments such as nuclear plants. Although the watch was designed to withstand high magnetism, the original examples of the Milgauss reference 6541 also had non-luminous dials and hands as radiation-sensitive equipment such as Geiger counters could activate on watches with radioactive luminous material. Likewise, sailors on nuclear submariners required watches without luminous material for similar reasons of not wanting to trip equipment meant to detect radiation leaks. We may never know if this watch was some sort of test by Rolex to create a Daytona to be used in one of those challenging environments and that somehow ended up in a Canadian department store.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. This is a rare opportunity to find an outstanding Daytona model. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Cartier Tank Cintrée in 18K Gold Unpolished

The Cartier Tank Cintrée is one of my favorite Cartier models. The cases are very well made and among the most beautiful styles from the manufacturer.

These models have grown to become highly collectible over recent years. It is a great contrast to many sports Rolex models within a collection and can be worn in both a casual and dressed up state.

This example dates to circa 1960s. It features an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished.

The case measures 18.5mm in diameter and 34.5mm in length. The dial is very nice and without any signs of wear or crazing. Often, these have crazing.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with an extra deployant buckle and black strap.

Cartier Tank Louis Jumbo Automatique in 18K Gold Unpolished

The Cartier Tank Louis is one of the most sophisticated dress watches and these models have grown to become highly collectible over recent years. It is a great contrast to many sports Rolex models within a collection and can be worn in both a casual and dressed up state.

This example dates to circa 1970s. It features an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. These are the largest vintage Tanks. The case measures 28mm in diameter and 35mm in length. The dial is very nice and without any signs of wear or crazing. Often, these have crazing.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Tank Louis in 18K Yellow Gold

The Cartier Tank Louis is one of the most sophisticated dress watches and these models have grown to become highly collectible over recent years. It is a great contrast to many sports Rolex models within a collection and can be worn in both a casual and dressed up state.

This example dates to circa 1957. It features an 18K yellow gold case with eagle hallmark on the case back indicating it was made in France. We believe the case is unpolished. The case measures 21mm in diameter and 27mm in length. It features a European Watch Company signed movement (indicating the movement was made by Jaeger-LeCoultre) and has the very attractive octagonal crown with blue cabochon. The deployant clasp is signed with an eagle hallmark indicating it was made in France and features the EJ maker’s mark with an hourglass in the center indicating it was made by Edmond Jaeger. These original deployant clasps are highly prized. It is on a cool tan strap that is quite possibly original to the watch.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It has been recently serviced and is running well.

Rolex Datejust Black Dial Reference 126300 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 126300 is a very attractive Rolex with a black dial. It is 41mm and has a nice Oyster bracelet with smooth bezel It is unpolished. There are minimal scratches and light wear on the bracelet from being worn a few times.

The watch comes with box and papers. The watch was sold in 2023 and comes from the original owner.

Rolex Yacht-Master Reference 126655 In 18K Rose Gold New Full Set

This Rolex Yacht-Master Reference 126655 is a very attractive Rolex in 18K rose gold. It is unpolished. It is in new and unworn condition, just with handling wear from being sized and sitting in the box.

The watch comes with box and papers. The watch was purchased new in 2023 and comes from the original owner who purchased it and did not wear it.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 5726/1A-010 Full Set Unpolished

In 1976, Gerald Genta designed the Patek Philippe Nautilus which would go on to becomeone of the most iconic and desirable wristwatches on the planet. Decades later, the Nautilus has taken on many iterations such as this reference 5726/1A-010 incorporating the calendar and mooonphase.

The watch is powered by the calibre 324 QA LU 24H and dates to 2015. We believe the case is unpolished and measures 45mm in diameter.

The watch comes full set with box, papers and Certificate of Origin.

Cartier Musical Pocket Watch w/ Box

This remarkable Cartier musical pocket watch from the 1970s, crafted for the Italian market, boasts a unique charm. Adorned with green and red enamel, mirroring the Italian flag, it serenades with the delightful tune of ‘Que Sera, Sera’ which translates to ‘Whatever will be, will be. Although its alarm is more subtle compared to a Memovox, the watch, it is a beautiful melody nonetheless.

The watch does come with what we believe is the original box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Root Beer GMT-Master Reference 16753 w/ Box & Service Card

This Rolex “Root Beer” GMT-Master reference 16753 is an awesome GMT. This example dates to circa 1979 with a 6.34 million serial. The case has been polished in the past. The hands are service replacements.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

It comes with box and service card which dates to 2022.

Rolex Submariner Reference 168000 with Box

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This reference 168000 features a 9.6M serial number dating to circa 1987. The case has been polished in the past. The luminous material on the hour markers is a nice warm tone. The hands appear to be later replacements.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes with a box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Mosaic Date Reference 1501

This Rolex Date reference 1501 is a cool watch with the interesting “mosaic” or “shantung” dial. The case has been polished in the past. It dates to circa 1970 with a 2.47M serial.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
