https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Submariner Reference 1680 President José López Portillo of Mexico Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a great sports watch dating to circa 1978 with a 5.09M serial. This was the personal watch of the 51st President of Mexico (1976-1982), José Guillermo Abel López Portillo y Pacheco - and features the same personalized case back engraving which was seen on his GMT-Master reference 1675. The case appears to be unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is a great pale yellow color. It is paired on a nice oval-link Rolex bracelet made in Mexico. The watch came from a member of his family through a dealer in Mexico. For those looking for an amazing 1680 with great povenance, this is a great choice!

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

LeCoultre Deep Sea Alarm Reference E 857 Unpolished w/ Extract

The LeCoultre Deep Sea Alarm Automatic reference E 857 is one of the most exciting vintage dive watches. These cases were made by Huguenin Frères and powered by the automatic alarm Jaeger-LeCoultre calibre 815. This example has an incredibly attractive dial.

The watch was sent to JLC for a full service and JLC replaced the alarm crown (made to the specific dimensions of the original) and relumed the hour and minute hands. JLC also replaced the bezel insert with a service grey insert (with tritium), but the original insert is included with the watch. This example has an incredibly attractive dial. The dial and disc are original and unaltered. The case and case back are also unpolished and in amazing condition. This is one of my favorite watches available on the market, and an epic alternative diver. The watch dates to circa 1960.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives.

Rolex 1/4 Century The T. Eaton Co. Limited Reference 6422 in 14K Yellow Gold Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer this Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 6422 with the special dial commemorating an employee’s 25 year anniversary at The T. Eaton Company Limited. Eaton was a Canadian department store chain that would later be acquired by Sears. We love earned watches and this example is particularly nice with the dial customized by Rolex to reflect a quarter century employee. The case back is decorated with the employee George E. Page’s name and dates of employment from 1933-1958. The 14K yellow-gold case is very attractive and measures 34mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The 274,XXX serial number dates this watch to circa 1957.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a pressure test of 1 bar. For those looking for a rare and particularly amazing vintage Rolex, this is a special example!

Rolex Double Red Sea-Dweller Reference 1665 Unpolished

The Rolex Sea-Dweller reference 1665 is one of the iconic dive watches, and according to Rolex it was produced in 22,038 examples. These watches are appreciated by collectors due to their depth rating and robust cases. This Rolex Sea-Dweller reference 1665 features the highly desired and vibrant "Double Red" dial. The aluminum bezel insert has faded to a beautiful grey color. The watch features a 5.28M serial dating to circa 1978. The case appears to be unpolished. it is paired on a nice service 9315 folded link bracelet with S date stamp and 380 end links which are correct.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch was recently fitted with a True Dome crystal which looks outstanding.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished w/ Papers & Receipts

This Rolex GMT-Master is a staple Rolex model that has an incredible history. It was designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950`s to ensure that they could measure two time zones at once. This reference 1675 is a fantastic example. The case appears to be unpolished. The bezel insert is quite attractive with unique charm as it has been faded to a light pink. The dial is a “Mark 0.5” with Rolex letters featuring the “Long E”. It features the small triangle GMT hand. The watch dates to circa 1967 with a 1.62M serial. It was sold in 1968. It is paired on a nice Rolex 7206 Rivet bracelet dating to 1967 with 80 end links which are correct for the 1675. The lume on this example is super attractive and puffy.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master watch Eric Wind’s ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article which was produced with HODINKEE. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch was recently serviced and is keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The watch does not come with box, but does come with the original papers featuring the chronometer certification and receipts! For those looking for an incredible Rolex GMT-Master 1675, look no further!

Patek Philippe Reference 1560-1 in 18K Yellow Gold

This Patek Philippe reference 1560-1 is a special timepiece with great provenance. It comes from the family of the original owner. The watch was owned by Marshall Field Jr. of Marshall Field & Company or Marshall Field's. Marshall Field's was a famous retail department store which would later be acquired by Macy’s. Interestingly, Marshall Field Junior’s father was a noteworthy Patek Philippe collector. The watch is fresh to market and is an exciting find for any Patek Philippe collector!

It is a rectangular model powered by the important calibre 9’90. It features beautiful applied Breguet numerals. The watch dates to circa 1950. The case measures 22mm by 38mm. The case has been lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Feet First Red Submariner Reference 1680 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a fantastic sports watch dating to circa 1971 with a 2.6M serial. It is an incredible Red Sub! The case appears to be unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is a superb color. It is paired on a firm 9315 bracelet. For those looking for an amazing Red Sub, this is a great choice!

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Date Reference 1503 In 14K Yellow Gold

I’m thrilled to offer this Rolex Date reference 1503. The 14K yellow-gold case is very attractive and measures 34mm in diameter. It has been polished in the past. It is paired on the original 14k yellow gold Rivet bracelet which is rare and hard to find. The 2.32 serial number dates this watch to circa 1970.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Patek Philippe Reference 570 in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 570 is one of the most refined and sought-after dress watches, and an iconic model within the Calatrava family. This specific watch is a reference 570 in yellow gold. It features a non-luminous dial with applied-dauphine hour markers. The case has been lightly polished in the past. For those looking for an elegant dress watch, this is a great option.

This watch does not include the original box and paper. It is powered by the Caliber 27 SC manual-wind movement made by Patek Philippe. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Mk 1 Frog Foot Dial Explorer I Reference 1016 on Jubilee

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example features an 1.58M serial dating it to circa 1967. The case is polished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It features the original Mark 1 frog foot dial. It is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet. The lume is extremely puffy and attractive.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Monaco Chronomatic Reference 1133 Unpolished

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement, the calibre 11. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic.

This Heuer Monaco Chronomatic reference 1133 is one of the earliest examples known. It is a rare variant with the “Chronomatic” font and luminous earlier hand set.

We believe the case is unpolished. The dial has developed a unique patina with the brass dial reacting through the blue paint. The watch dates to 1969. The Heuer Monaco is actually quite a rare model and we are seeing more collectors recognize the reference 1133 as a staple within the vintage chronograph collection. For those looking for one of the earliest examples of a historically important model, this is an incredible watch.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Two-Tone Zenith Daytona Reference 16523

This is a super cool two-tone Zenith Daytona reference 16523. It has a W serial dating it to circa 1995. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. The case is polished. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Green Oyster Perpetual 126000 Unpolished Box & Papers

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual is a very collectible watch due to its green dial. For many months, this was one of the hottest, non-sport Rolex models. This watch has a steel case and comes on a steel oyster bracelet. It measures 36mm in diameter. The watch was sold in 2021. The watch is in exceptional condition.

The watch comes full set. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Dunhill Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855 W/ Papers

This is the highly elusive and rare Dunhill Memovox E 855 sold in New York during the 1960s. Very few examples are known to exist. This example dates to circa late 1960s. The case has been lightly polished. The case measures 37mm in diameter and wears excellent. The dial has a very sophisticated painted Breguet numerals and a white dial. The watch features service crowns.

The watch is powered by the calibre 825, the first automatic alarm wristwatch to incorporate the calendar complication. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement.

Along with the Atmos clocks, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. Perhaps the quintessential Memovox is the reference E 855 from the 1960s. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

To learn more about this model, read Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855.

Certina Argonaut 280 on Bracelet Unpolished

The Certina Argonaut 280 is an incredibly cool and stylish vintage watch. It presents great value for everyone and has a fantastic case design. It measures 37mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). The watch dates to the 1970s. It features a very rare red dial and is paired on an NSA bracelet signed Certina.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Rotonde de Cartier Jour et Nuit Ref 28731 CPCP Full Set

This Cartier Rotonde de Cartier Jour et Nuit reference 28731 is a sophisticated and complicated Cartier timepiece. The watch dates to circa 2008. It is powered by the calibre 9903 MC. The 18k white gold case measures 42mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447S on Gay Freres Bracelet

This Heuer Carrera reference 2447S is a cool chronograph paired on a Gay Freres bracelet for Heuer. The case has been polished. The watch is powered by the Valjoux calibre 72. It has a 77,XXX serial number dating to the mid 1960s. The dial and hands have attractive lume. The crown appears to be a service crown.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

Heuer Orange Boy Autavia Reference 1163 Unpolished

This Heuer “Orange Boy” Autavia reference 1163 is great model and the Orange Boy is one of the most exciting and attractive dial variants within the reference 1163. The watch is in great condition. The case appears to be unpolished.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 11 made by Heuer-Léonidas), and is 42mm in diameter. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Camaro AARC Reference 9220N on Gay Freres Bracelet Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive racing, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera.

This particular reference 9220N is a very special example with provenance being won at the 1967 American Road Race of Champions! The dial is in fantastic condition. The watch is paired on a Gay Freres Bracelet with HLA end links - which was added later - and is significantly hard to find. The watch is powered by the Valjoux 92.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. It does come with a catalog and ticket from the 1967 American Road Race of Champions which were added subsequently for those who appreciate the associated ephemera. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. For those who appreciate great vintage Heuer watches and the incredible examples with provenance, look no further!

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18038 Unpolished

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week. This reference 18038 is an incredibly attractive watch with a striking champagne dial. The 18K-gold case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This particular example dates to circa 1979 with a 5.95M serial.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 16013 Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a nice champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1982 with a 7.3M serial.

This watch does not include the box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Cufflinks White Brand New

We are thrilled to offer these incredible Patek Philippe Nautilus cufflinks in 18k white gold. These feature the iconic Nautilus bezel and case shape with the white dial. The cufflinks are in brand new condition. The cufflinks are in brand new condition. These cufflinks are guaranteed to elevate any formal attire! The Patek Philippe Nautilus cuff links retail for $6,720.

The cufflinks come with the Patek Philippe presentation box.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Cufflinks Blue w/ Box Brand New

We are thrilled to offer these incredible Patek Philippe Nautilus cufflinks in 18k white gold. These feature the iconic Nautilus bezel and case shape with the blue dial. The cufflinks are in brand new condition. The cufflinks are in brand new condition. These cufflinks are guaranteed to elevate any formal attire! The Patek Philippe Nautilus cuff links retail for $6,720.

The cufflinks come with the Patek Philippe presentation box.

Rolex White Daytona Reference 116520 Full Set

This Rolex Daytona Reference 116520 is a great model with white dial. The watch is powered by the in-house Rolex automatic calibre 4130. The case has been polished. It features a G serial number dating to circa 2010. It was sold in 2011.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Patek Philippe Annual Calendar Moon Phases Reference 4947R w/ Service Box

This Patek Philippe annual calendar reference 4947R is an incredible timepiece. The diamond set rose gold case is brilliant. It is powered by the calibre 26‑330 S QA LU. The case measures 38mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. These watches retailed at +$61,000 before tax!

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with a service box. It was purchased from a collector who had scratched the crystal. The watch was sent to Patek for the crystal to be replaced. A large wooden shiny Patek box can be included with the watch at no additional cost.

Patek Philippe Reference 4809J-001 w/ Certificate of Origin Unpolished

We are thrilled to offer this very exceptional Patek Philippe reference 4809. It is a stunning example and is 18k yellow gold. The case appears to be unpolished. The case is 25mm in diameter. It is an absolutely stunning timepiece.

The watch does not come with box. It does come with the certificate of origin. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Monoblocco Oyster Chronograph Reference “P.O.W.” 3525 Unpolished

This is an incredible watch for either a sophisticated Rolex or chronograph connoisseur. Often referred to as the "Monoblocco" the case body is an incredible creation from a single piece of steel. It features a fantastic luminous dial and syringe hands. These models are also synonymous and nicknamed P.O.W.s for the models which were sent to Allied prisoners in German P.O.W. camps. Hans Wilsdorf signed a letter to one prisoner stating “you must not even think of settlement during the war.”

We believe this example is unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter by 41.5mm lug to lug. The watch dates to circa 1942. The bracelet is a WAB bracelet which were British made bracelets for Rolex in the British market. One of the original inner-clasp blades is damaged, so we replaced the inner-clasp blades. The originals are included with the watch.

We understand the watch came from the original owner’s family in England and that the original owner was in the British Army.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1A-011 Full Set Brand New Unworn

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is perhaps the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated stainless steel watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today. This particular model is a reference 5990/1A-011 dating to late 2022. It features the new and highly coveted blue dial variant. It is powered by the CH 28-520 C FUS. The watch comes from the original owner. It is in brand new and unworn condition.

The watch comes with the original box and papers, as well as additional links that are original to the watch.

Rolex Mega Fat Font GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an outstanding reference 1675 featuring a 2.33M serial number dating to circa 1970. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch overall is in fantastic condition and the type of watch we would love to personally own. It is paired on a C&I bracelet. The aluminum bezel insert features the “Mega Fat Font” style and is extremely attractive with a sharp light blue and faded red.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Meters First Red Submariner Reference 1680 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa late 1969 with a 2.21M serial. It is one of the most attractive Red Subs we have ever seen. We believe the case is unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is a superb yellow. The original Patent Pending 9315 bracelet is being tightened and can be provided upon its return to the purchaser. The bezel insert has seen some action and has some scratches and a small puncture at around 9, but it is the original and I find it to be super fitting! This is how I love to find them.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. This example has an extraordinary color on the hands and luminous markers. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). We believe the case is unpolished. This example dates to circa 1975 with a 4.08M serial.

It is paired on a Rolex 7836 bracelet with 258 end links.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water test of 5 bar. This is an incredible watch that I was tempted to keep and a serious option for any Rolex lover!

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 6917 w/ Papers Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 6917 is a super cool timepiece with tremendous style. It features an original Rolex Tiger’s Eye dial, which was added later. Most stone dials are added to Rolex watches after sales. It is accompanied by the original gold/champagne dial it was born with.

This example is 18k yellow gold and we believe it to be unpolished. The case is 26mm in diameter and 33mm lug-to-lug. It is paired on the orginal bracelet

It does not come with box. It does come with the original papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Abercrombie & Fitch 1st Execution Seafarer Reference 2444 Unpolished

The Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer is the first execution reference 2444! This is the best Seafarer 2444 example I have ever seen. The minute register is designated for timing yacht races. We believe the case is unpolished and measures roughly 35mm in diameter. The watch dates to the late 1950s early 1960s.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Grey Dial Datejust Reference 1603 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this grey-dial reference 1603 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1974 and features a 3.3M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter..

The watch is in excellent condition. We believe it has an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Rolex Grey Sigma Dial Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this grey-dial reference 1601 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1972 and features a 3.08M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter..

The watch is in excellent condition. We believe it has an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Rolex Green Dial Oyster Perpetual Reference 124300 41mm Full Set

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 124300 is an awesome modern watch. It has great proportions at 41mm in diameter. The smooth bezel gives it a slightly more dressy look. The green dial is accented by very attractive. The watch is in very good condition and was worn very little by the owner.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold recently at Monaco Legends.

Rolex Roman Numerals Day-Date II Reference 218238 in Yellow Gold w/ Box

This Rolex Day-Date II Reference 218238 is a very bold timepiece. The case measures 41mm in diameter (49mm lug-to-lug). The 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. It features a very sophisticated Roman-numeral dial. We believe the case is unpolished.

We do not know when the watch was produced.

The watch comes with a box and does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 126000 Tiffany Blue Dial Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 126000 is an awesome modern watch. It features one of my favorite dial variants from the line with the “Tiffany Blue” dial. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was purchased in 2022

Rolex Spanish Day-Date Reference 18038 Unpolished

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week. This reference 18038 is an awesome watch with a very attractive gold dial. We believe the 18K-gold case is unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This particular example dates to circa 1981.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Submariner Reference 16808 in 18K Yellow Gold Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 16808 dates to circa 1981. The case has been lightly polished in the past. It features what appears to be the original bezel insert. It is overall an extremely attractive watch.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It has recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar. The original bracelet is currently being repaired and will come with the watch.

Rolex Automatic For US Market 14K Gold Filled Unpolished w/ Box, Catalog & Hang Tag

This is a super cool Rolex with an automatic calibre 1520. The case is a 14K yellow gold-filled model measuring 35mm in diameter and 41mm lug-to-lug. We believe it to be unpolished. It has a super attractive dial and alpha hands which is less often the case for American-market models.

It comes with a box, guarantee catalog, and a hang tag. It is paired on what appears to be the original strap and buckle. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Chronograph 26170ST.00.D091CR.01 Full Set

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak chronograph in steel is a great contemporary watch. The case appears to be unpolished.

This watch comes with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18239B Tridor

These Rolex Day-Date “Trader” reference 18239 models are very cool and atypical watches. The watches feature 3 different golds (rose, yellow, and white). This particular example features a very cool Roman numeral dial. The watch dates to circa 1989. The case has been polished in the past.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Bulova Devil Diver Oceanographer Snorkel on Bracelet Unpolished

This is one of the coolest vintage Bulova watches that is a tremendously fun watch to wear. This Devil Diver has a super attractive bezel insert and the applied branding on the dial is very funky. The hour markers are very distinct. We believe it is paired on its original bracelet and the case to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Mid-Size Full Set

The Overseas line has recently seen a great deal of appreciation by collectors, and I am proud to offer this fantastic example that highlights the heritage of the iconic watch model.

This Vacheron Constantin is the younger sibling of the integrated sports model, reference 222. It is the mid-size case. The watch dates to the 1990s. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Ladies Tapestry Dial Datejust Reference 69173 Unpolished w/ Box and Catalog

This Rolex Ladies Datejust reference 69173 is a super cool example. It has a two-tone case. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an R serial number dating to circa 1987-1988. It measures 26mm in diameter.

It comes with box and a catalog. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Black Dial Zenith Daytona Reference 16528 in 18K Yellow Gold

This is a super attractive black dial Zenith Daytona. The case features a U serial dating to circa 1997-1998. The case has been polished in the past. This watch does not include the original box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 4030 made by Zenith), and is 40mm in diameter.

Rolex Black Dial Daytona Reference 116520 Full Set New Old Stock Stickered

The reference 116520 was produced from the course of 2000-2016. This specific example is an incredible example with an O serial in NOS condition. The watch measures 40mm in diameter. Finding an unpolished early reference 116520 is extremely difficult, and I am thrilled to be able to offer this never worn example.

The watch comes from with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18038 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 is a super cool example with a beautiful dial. It has an an 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. The watch features an 5.4 serial number dating to circa 1978.

It does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and it is running well.

TAG Heuer Carrera Heuer 01 Chronograph Tom Brady Limited Edition CAR201R Full Set Like New

This is what happens when the greatest quarterback of all time and one of the greatest chronographs decide to team up. This TAG Heuer Carrera is the epic limited edition Tom Brady CAR201R. It is highly coveted by all. Supermodels will finally pay attention to you when you place this on your wrist. Stadiums will erupt with applause when they see what is under the sleeve. Don’t fumble this opportunity. Buy it now or live the rest of your life with regret.

This watch comes with box and papers, and is in like new condition.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Lapis Datejust Reference 16018 in 18K Yellow Gold

This Datejust reference 16018 is a superb watch with a stunning lapis lazuli dial. The watch dates to circa 1979 with a 6.41M serial. The 18K yellow gold case has been lightly polished in the past, but is still very attractive. Stone dials have become increasingly desired in recent years and when you admire beautiful examples like the present Datejust, it is easy to see why!

It does not come with box and papers. We understand it recently went through a Rolex service in 2023, but do not have the Rolex service papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 6105 in 18K Rose Gold w/ Service Papers

There is something incredible about the early Rolex Datejusts, and this is one particular example that I am amazed by. This example dates to circa 1952. It features one of the most beautiful rose gold colors. The calendar has red numerals (not roulette). The case has been polished in the past, but it is still very wonderful on the wrist. The dial has just a remarkable patina which is so nice to look down at. It is paired on the original Jubilee bracelet dating to the 4th quarter of 1952. For those who have a penchant for gold Rolex watches, this is a fantastic option.

It does not come with box and papers. It comes with Rolex Service Papers which we believe date to 2021.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5261R-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe Aquanaut is one of the most desired sports watches on the planet, and this 5261R-001 is a spectacular complicated iteration on the model. The combination of the 18K yellow gold case and dial/strap color create a vibrant and youthful look.

This example is in great condition. It was lightly worn. It comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was sold in 2022.

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph 2599.80 Unpolished Full Set

This OMEGA Seamaster chronograph is a fantastic sports watch. It has a great amount of heft and is on a very comfortable bracelet. The watch comes from the original owner who reportedly served in the US Army.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history. We do not know the date of production.

Gübelin Crystal Rock Skeleton Pocket Watch

This is a very special timepiece sold at Gübelin. The case is made from crystal rock and showcases a beautiful skeletonized movement. We believe the watch and movement were likely produced by Audemars Piguet. The crystal features platinum applied hour markers. The platinum bow is very fine and the crown features a beautiful diamond. We believe the production dates to pre 1950.

The watch does not come with box and papers. A service is recommended, as it is running intermittently.

A. Nicolet 18K Gold Minute Repeater Pocket Watch

This A. Nicolet pocket watch dates to circa 1900 and features a tremendous minute repeater movement. We believe it is based on a LeCoultre calibre. The sound is remarkable and in our view superior to many of the six-figure repeater offered by brands today. There is truly something special about the way watches were made back in the day. The case is made of 18K yellow gold and we believe it is unpolished. It features gold Louis XV hands. It features red minute demarcations.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Tiffany & Co. Vintage Ladies Wristwatch in 14K Gold Unpolished

This vintage Tiffany & Co. wristwatch is a super attractive timepiece. The movement is manual wind. It features a very nice integrated bracelet. The watch is 14K yellow gold. The dial is in exceptional condition.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Blue Dial Tank Must de Cartier Unpolished Full Set

The Cartier Tank Must is part of the legendary Tank family of watches from the famed jeweler. This reference was released after the large commercial success of the Red, Green and Blue dial Tank Musts from 2020! The quartz movement is highly accurate and according to Cartier the battery only needs to be changed every 6 years! The watch is unpolished and in excellent condition. For those looking for an elegant grab and go dress watch, it does not get better or more iconic than this blue dial Tank Must.

The watch comes with box and papers. It is in very good condition with minimal wear.

Vulcain Cricket Reference 6001 Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket reference 6001 is a super cool and funky model from the 1970s. It features an awesome multitone blue dial. It is powered by the MSR calibre. The case is chrome plated and is unpolished. It is approximately 40mm in diameter.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Geneve Retailed by Tiffany & Co. Christmas 1972 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This 18k yellow gold Universal Geneve is a spectacular timepiece. The case back features a beautiful engraving from Christmas 1972. The movement is manual wind. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 22mm in diameter 35mm lug-to-lug.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

IWC Retailed by Tiffany & Co. in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This 18k yellow gold IWC is a spectacular timepiece. The case is super interesting and a similar style to the “Top Hat” models of the era. The movement is manual wind IWC. The watch was retailed at Tiffany & Co. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 22mm in diameter 38mm lug-to-lug.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Cellini Reference 6622 Unpolished

This Rolex Cellini is a very cool and interesting timepiece. The watch dates to 1990. The 18K yellow gold case measures 33mm in diameter. We believe it is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Tropical Gilt Explorer I Reference 1016 Full Set w/ Letter of Provenance

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This model features a tropical gilt dial. The watch features a 1.15M serial and dates to circa 1965. The case is 36mm in diameter. It is in nice condition and has been lightly polished. It is paired on a period correct Rolex 7206 rivet bracelet with 58 end links dating to 1964. The hands have been color matched.

It comes with a letter of provenance from the original owner who was a Junior Officer in the British Merchant Navy. They purchased the watch at Artland Watch Co in Hong Kong while working overseas. They detail in the letter having one bad memory with the watch - an altercation in Manila where a thief tried to steal their 1016! Thankfully they held tight to the watch and had allies come to defend him.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was recently serviced in December 2025 and is running well.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE:

Longines Non-Luminous “Sei Tacche” Reference 5532 Unpolished w/ Extract

This Longines reference 5532 is an incredibly cool Sei Tacche watch. The steel case measures 33.5mm in diameter. It measrues 42mm lug to lug.

It features a non-luminous metallic grey dial that is increduble. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a Private Eyes beads of rice bracelet.

The original serial number 6'818'776 identifies a wristwatch in stainless steel bearing the reference 5532. It is fitted with a Longines manually wound mechanical movement, caliber 10.68Z and was invoiced on 23 November 1944 to the company Wirth, which was at that time our agent for Switzerland.

For those looking for a very special non-luminous Longines, this is an incredible option.

The watch does not come with box or papers. It does come with an Extract from The Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Annual Calendar 5036/1R-001 Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 5036/1R-001 annual calendar is a very special and complicated timepiece. In addition to the annual calendar, the watch displays a moonphase and power reserve indicator. The rose gold case measures approximately 37mm in diameter. The watch is powered by the Calibre 315/199, made visible with an exhibition caseback. It features an attractive white dial with luminous Roman numerals and leaf hands. It comes with a five-link rose gold bracelet. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box, papers, gold pusher and Certificate of Origin. It was originally sold in 2002. It was last serviced in 2021 by Patek Philippe.

Dunhill Time-Only Blue Dial Breguet Numerals by Jaeger-LeCoultre Unpolished

This is an extremely cool time-only Dunhill wriswatch by Jaeger-LeCoultre. It features the classic painted Breguet numeral dial found on many Dunhill JLC watches. Noteworthy, this is the same blue layout as the Jaeger-LeCoultre Dunhill Memovox E 855s. These watches are rare and extremely cool. Many seem to have been sold in Japan. The watch dates to the late 1960s. It features a stainless steel case which appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter.

It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Girard-Perregaux Deep Diver Reference 9018 FA With Papers Unpolished

This is a very rare Girard-Perregaux Deep Divery reference 9018 FA. These feature an incredibly cool roulette-style dial. It incorporates an internal rotating bezel. The case appears to be unpolished. The case production dates to circa 1970 - the year of introduction. Usually they have the Calypso hands, but this seems to be an early example and we believe the hands are original. The case is produced by EPSA. It measures 40mm in diameter (43mm lug to lug).

There is a highly popular Girard-Perregaux x Bamford collaboration watch that retailed for $15,000!

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It comes with original papers and a service receipt.

Cartier Colisee Two-Tone Wristwatch

This Cartier two-tone is made from an 18k yellow gold and stainless steel case. The case measures approximately 24mm in diameter. The dial features Roman numerals and sword hands. The case appears to have been polished in the past. The watch features an intricate bracelet design with alternating gold and steel links. It is a quartz movement watch. It will fit a small wrist of up to approximately 5.9 inches.

It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but the watch is running well.

Rolex Cosmograph Daytona Reference 126515LN on Oysterflex Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 126515LN is a great contemporary iteration of an iconic sports watch. Paired on its original Oysterflex band, it is truly suited to be worn in all active situations. The 40mm case is 18k Everose gold. The watch is in excellent condition and appears to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes “full set” with original box and papers. It was sold in 2024.

“This is hands-down, possibly the greatest Modern Rolex in Everose on Oysterflex that’s ever graced my wrist.” - Charlie Dunne

Rolex Meters First Dial Submariner 5513

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. This reference 5513 features a 1.58M serial number dating to circa 1967. It is paired with a Rolex C&I stretch rivet bracelet dating to 1966. This example features a “meters first” matte dial with extremely attractive tritium lume. It has been polished but is very attractive overall.

Learn more about the sibling model reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar. It does not come with the original box or papers.

Heuer Dash Timer Double Set “Indianapolis Speedway”

This is an extremely cool set of HEUR dash timers, the timers, in stainless steel cases, are wound and operated through monopusher winding crowns at the 12 o’clock position. They come with a plastic Heuer twinboard that holds them together.

These were mounted in automobiles for the purpose of racing and are absolutely incredible for those that appreciate this cross over between racing and timekeeping.

This example emerged from a small auction in Indiana recently. It is a slightly newer set from the 1960s and one of the coolest bits of Heuer and Indianapolis Motor Speedway memorabilia one can own!

An earlier example sold for $18,750 at Christie’s in 2017 and is now on display at the TAG Heuer museum.

We do not know the service history, but they are both running wel

Minerva Gilt Pocket Watch Unpolished

This Minerva Pocket watch in stainless steel measures approximately 47mm in diameter. The watch features a grey dial with a black gilt track that features silver roman numerals. There is a sub dial at 6 o’clock displaying small seconds. The bow features and engraved floral pattern. This is a cool pocket watch with a highly attractive dial.

We do not know the service history but the watch is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Flat 4 Kermit Submariner Reference 16610 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner “Kermit” Submariner Reference 16610 is an amazing sports watch. The case appears to be unpolished. It features the Flat 4 Bezel insert. The watch features a F0 serial dating to circa 2003-2004. It was sold in Saint Tropez which is very nice!

The service history is unknown, but it was recently put on a time graphed and is running well. The watch comes full set with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Vulcain Cricket Black Dial Mirror Track Reference S2328A Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket reference S2328A is an incredible and rare example with the black dial and mirror track. The watch is extremely attractive. It is powered by the calibre MSR S2. The case is unpolished. It features the very cool “Officer” style lugs, and an angled bezel. The case measures 35mm in diameter (41.5mm lug-to-lug).

The dial is outstanding and the outer mirror track is super cool. The hour markers are also very attractive. The “Vulcain” font is super crisp against the black dial.

For those looking for an amazing 1960s Vulcain Cricket, look no further.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Mirror Track Reference S2324A Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket reference S2324A is a fantastic and rare example with the rare grey metallic dial with a mirror track. Over the years of collecting, I have only seen two examples - one of which was in my personal collection and the other is this example! It belongs to Eric Wind and it was famously worn in the Rolex Explorer Reference Points video! It is powered by the calibre MSR S2. The case is unpolished. It features the very cool “Officer” style lugs, and an angled bezel. The case measures 35mm in diameter (41.5mm lug-to-lug). The alarm is very loud and will surely wake you up when it rings!

The dial is outstanding and the outer mirror track is super cool. The hour markers are also very attractive. The “Vulcain” font on these dials is very cool in that it has some speckling that resembles graffiti font.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Enicar Ocean Pearl Alarm Unpolished

The Enicar Ocean Pearl Alarm is a very rare watch that most people have never heard of. When you ask knowledgeable vintage watch enthusiasts to cite the manufacturers that made Alarm Dive watches - they will immediately say “The Jaeger-LeCoultre Deep Sea Alarm”, “The Polaris of course!”, or begin screaming “Vulcain Cricket Nautical, Vulcain Cricket Nautical, Vulcain Cricket Nautical!” 😱 (Yes, these conversations do occur). However, there is also an Enicar dive alarm watch - also with an EPSA case! These watches are incredibly cool, and compact dive models. It features the A. Schilds calibre. The case measures 35mm in diameter by 44mm in length. The case is unpolished. The bracelet is a later unsigned model that looks very nice on the watch. It features an Explorer dial layout and rotating bezel.

Eric has seen 2 examples (including this one). Due to the 12 people globally that know about these two watches, we will not say it is “scholarship changing”, but it is still an epic watch that is super rare!

It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe World's Columbian Exposition Chicago (World’s Fair) 1893 Pocket Watch in 18K Gold

This is a very special and important Patek Philippe pocket watch dating to 1893 produced for the World's Columbian Exposition Chicago. This was the first year Patek Philippe became a jury member of the World's Columbian Exposition.

The dial is phenomenal. It features the original glass crystal which has some scuffs. The case measures 51mm in diameter. It features “Wolf’s Teeth” gearing.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not include box and papers.

To learn more about these special timepieces, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Undervalued: Why Your Perspective Should Change About Patek Philippe Pocket Watches’.

Heuer Camaro ARR.C. Reference 9220N on Gay Freres Bracelet Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive racing, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera.

This particular reference 9220N is a very special example with provenance being won at the 1967 American Road Race of Champions! The dial is in fantastic condition. The watch is paired on a Gay Freres Bracelet with HLA end links - which was added later - and is significantly hard to find. The watch is powered by the Valjoux 92. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. It does come with a catalog and ticket from the 1967 American Road Race of Champions which were added subsequently for those who appreciate the associated ephemera. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. For those who appreciate great vintage Heuer watches and the incredible examples with provenance, look no further!

Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer Ref 2444 by Heuer Unpolished

The Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer is essentially the same watch as the Heuer Mareographe reference 2444 with a bit of retailer sauce! This example has a very attractive warm colored dial with excellent green and yellow accents in the registers. The minute register is designated for timing yacht races. The 9 o’clock register indicates tide changes. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures roughly 35mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1960s.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Big Daytona Reference 6239 w/ Letter of Provenance

The Rolex Daytona reference 6239 is the original and earliest reference of the Daytona. This example features a “Big Daytona” logo where DAYTONA is large and underneath COSMOGRAPH on the dial. I really love the way the ROLEX / COSMOGRAPH / DAYTONA text lines up in descending order like a pyramid in shape.

This example also has the original early “300” Mark 2 reference 6239 bezel, which differs from later examples by having 300 on the tachymeter bezel and “UNITS PER HOUR” between 12 and 2 o’clock, whereas later bezels have it by 3 o’clock. This is the second version of the steel bezel found on the reference 6239 and the earliest Mark 1 examples have a 275 and dashes rather than dots next to the numbers. The dial is super attractive with the silver soleil dial. The registers are turning slightly brown, but I would not necessarily call it tropical.

The case features a 1.52 million serial number dating it to circa 1967. The case has been polished in the past, but is extremely attractive.

The watch does not come with box and papers, but it does come with a letter from the daughter of the original owner. I purchased the watch from her several years ago and sold it to a client who is now selling it to help fund the purchase of a home. The watch features a new TrueDome D21 crystal. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Silver Dial Datejust Ref 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I love the steel engined turned bezel on these classic stainless steel models.

This watch features a quickest date movement. The dial is a very attractive silver dial. The reference 16030 is notable for its stainless steel engine-turned bezel. The watch features a 7.7M serial number, which dates to circa 1983.

The watch is paired with a 62510H Jubilee bracelet with an N7 date code dating to circa 1989, as well as the correct 555 end links. The minute hand has been relumed. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Audemars Piguet Ref. 14790ST Blue Dial

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is among the most iconic wristwatches. This example, a reference 14790ST, is a great compact Royal Oak. It measures 36mm in diameter, but wears larger as it has a lug to lug measurement of 46mm. The bracelet can fit a wrist of approximately 7.25 inches. The case is very attractive and appears to be unpolished.

t does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master II Ref. 16710 Coke Unpolished

Originally designed for Pan-Am pilots in the 1950s, the Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most sought-after models in the brand’s lineup. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the earlier reference ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

This example, reference 16710, a stainless steel model, with an N serial dates to circa 1991. It is paired with a reference 78790A Oyster bracelet with a P clasp code dating to circa 2001, likely original to the watch. The case and bracelet appear to be unpolished. The watch features a black and red “Coke” bezel insert.

The watch recently failed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The watch does not come with a box or papers.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447N on GF Bracelet Unpolished

The only time better than now to buy a vintage Heuer was back in the 1960s! The Carrera is an iconic sports chronograph which is one of the most versatile watches to have in a collection. This is a very attractive example with the black dial. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36 millimeters in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. The watch is powered by the Valjoux manual-winding calibre 72. It is paired on a nice Gay Freres Bracelet dating to 1969. The watch dates to circa 1968-1969.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

Rolex Fuchsia GMT-Master 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a very cool Fuchsia 1675 featuring a 1.9M serial number, dating to circa 1969. These bezels were born on watches featuring 1.8M and 1.9M serial range from circa 1968-1969.

The case appears to be unpolished. The aluminum bezel insert is very attractive. It is paired on a correct 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 80 end links dating to 1969. The hands were relumed due to previous fissures in the lume. The dial is turning a bit tropical.

It was serviced in mid 2025 and is running well. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test. The watch does not come with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Milgauss Reference 1019 Black Dial w/ Box

When it comes to the antimagnetic wristwatches of the 1950s-1960s, several manufacturers produced what would go on to become iconic timepieces. Among those are the IWC Ingenieur, the Eberhard Scientigraf, the Omega Railmaster, as well as Patek Philippe’s reference 2570 / 2570-1 and 3417. However, one of the most beloved models of orientated for the physicist or engineer of the day was none other than the Rolex Milgauss.

The Rolex Milgauss reference 1019 was the successor for the reference 6541. Like many of the Milgauss models over the years, the dial features a distinctive look that deviates from the consistent aesthetics from Rolex models.

This is the Mark 1 black dial with lume across the 3:00, 6:00, and 9:000 markers - later examples have luminous plots only on the outside of the hour markers. The case has been polished. This example features a 2.55M serial from circa 1970-1971. It is paired on a 7836 Rolex bracelet with 258 end links dating to 1970.

The watch comes with a box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Heuer Carrera Reference 3647S “Sherman Decker” Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 3647S is an extraordinary example with great provenance. It is powered by the Valjoux calibre 92. The watches belonged to the legendary driver and road racer Sherman “Sherm” Decker, and presumably was a gift from Heuer.

The patinated dial features the driver’s name SHERMAN DECKER above 6:00. We only know of two other examples of name-only Carrera dials - one being the famous JAMES GARNER example that was worn by the famous actor and that sold for over $176,000 in 2022. This same font and style of dial was used on the legendary MG Carreras.

The stainless steel case measures approximately 36mm in diameter or 45mm lug to lug. The case appears to be unpolished. This is an incredibly early example with case serial 54398 - one of the earliest known two-register Carreras. Ben Clymer has the earliest known Carrera found by Eric on eBay many years ago for him and it has case serial 53781!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
