By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 2509 Unpolished w/ Extract From The Archives

The Patek Philippe reference 2509 is one of the most attractive Calatrava models. This example dates to circa 1955 which is confirmed by the Extract from The Archives. It is powered by the manual wind calibre 12-400. The 18K yellow gold case is in great shape and is unpolished. It is 35mm in diameter and produced by François Borgel (Taubert Frères). The dial is very attractive. It features a small depression to the left of 12 o’clock which is barely noticeable. It is paired on a very expensive and well-crafted strap made by Attila Aszodi which looks incredible.

The watch comes with an Extract from The Archives. It does not come with a box. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

Patek Philippe Reference 2545 Retailed by Gübelin

The Patek Philippe reference 2545 is a very interesting and rare Calatrava model which was produced throughout the 1950s. Only a few dozen examples are known, and this is an exceptionally rare example retailed by Gübelin. The case was made by Gerlach. It is powered by the calibre 12 400. The yellow gold case has been lightly polished, but is strong.

It is signed two times with the Geneva Seal which was done on the most exceptional, important and well regulated examples during that period of Patek Philippe's history!

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running very well!

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J 2nd Series w/ Pin Buckle & Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold featuring the highly desirable 2nd series dial. It features the self-winding calibre 240Q. This example dates to circa 1988. The case is 36mm. It has been lightly polished but is strong.

The watch an Extract from The Archives confirming production and sale in 1988, and a Patek Philippe pin buckle. It does not include a box.

F.P.Journe Octa Automatique Reserve in Platinum with Brass Movement Full Set

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. Mr. Journe is one of the most significant watchmakers of the past century and the Chronomètre à Résonance is arguably his most iconic model.

This Octa Automatique Reserve in platinum measures 38mm (45mm lug-to-lug) and remains unpolished. It features a brass movement which can be found in early Journe timepieces and is highly sought after by collectors. This white gold dial has developed a sensational and shimmery patina, which is the charm of these early Journe watches.

This watch comes from the original owner and it is full set with the original box, papers and reciept. He purchased it for $18,000 from Kenjo.

Cartier Tank Cintrée Piece Unique Reference WGTA0149 Like New Full Set

I am incredibly excited to offer this stunning, unique Cartier Tank Cintrée reference WGTA0149! Inspired by 1960s designs from Cartier’s Bond Street London boutique, for many the peak of the brand’s creativity, this piece has elongated Roman numerals and removes the chemin de fer (railroad) markers. It features a vertically brushed gold dial, drawing on a piece from the Cartier Tank by Cologne book, as well as painted black Breguet hands and a black Onyx crown. This is truly a unique piece and special opportunity for lovers of Cartier watches.

The watch comes with full set with box and papers in like new condition.

Rolex Daytona Reference 6265 Full Set

This Rolex Daytona reference 6265 is a very cool example. It has a “Small Red” service dial with “Swiss only” and Luminova hands and dial. It was most likely replaced by Rolex in 2013. Originally, it would have had a “Big Red” Daytona dial with “T SWISS T” signature at 6 o’clock. This watch is from circa 1987, with a serial number in the 9.8 million range, making it one of the final manual-wind Daytonas made by Rolex!

The case is lightly polished, but is strong and very attractive. It measures 37mm in diameter. It features Mark 3 pushers, which are correct for the serial. The movement is a manual wind calibre 727 made by Valjoux for Rolex.

The watch comes with box and papers, original guarantee, and service papers from 2013. It comes from the original family. The watch is running great!

Rolex Explorer II Reference 1655

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors. This example has a lightly polished case. The hands and dial feature a nice warm lume.

It dates to circa 1972 (2.9 million serial number). It features the automatic calibre 1575 made by Rolex.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great. It just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 1655

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors. This example has a lightly polished case. The 24-hour hand appears to have turned yellow over the years from its original orange. The hands and dial feature a nice pale yellow lume.

It dates to circa 1979 (5.37 million serial number). It features the automatic calibre 1575 made by Rolex.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16220 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16220 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1991 and features a X serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

It features a case back sticker on the back. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1987 and features a 9.7M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Patek Philippe Reference 5035P Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 5035P is a very cool complicated timepiece with the clean black dial and luminous Roman numerals. The watch is powered by the calibre 315 S QA 24H. It comes from the original owner, who was a doctor and wore it for many years. The platinum case measures 37mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug) and is unpolished. As seen in the photos, the case was well worn and enjoyed by the original owner. It does not come with box and papers. Based on the movement, we believe it dates to circa 2000. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great.

TUDOR Advisor Reference 7926 Like NOS

The TUDOR Advisor is an amazing and off beat model within the TUDOR catalog. The reference 7926 is an incredible model, and this is the nicest example I have ever seen. It is quite frankly a time capsule and belongs in a museum! The watch is in like NOS condition, showing very little signs of wear over the years. It measures 34.5mm in diameter (41.5,mm lug-to-lug). The case back sticker is still on the back. It is paired on its original rivet bracelet which is incredible firm. It is powered by the A. Schild calibre 1475. The watch dates to circa 1961.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Vacheron & Constantin Reference 6562 w/ Date In Steel

This Vacheron & Constantin reference 6562 is an awesome timepiece in steel which is uncommon in the world of vintage VC. Its dial is very mint and extremely attractive. The case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter ( 43mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 w/ Papers and Booklet

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This example features a 1.72M serial number, dating to circa 1967. It is paired on a 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 58 end links. The case has been polished, but it is strong with good form. The aluminum bezel insert has taken on a very nice fade and complements the puffy lume.

The watch comes a stamped booklet and chronometer papers (which are dated to February of 1968. It does not come with a box. It was just serviced by Dr. Greg Petronzi (@truepatina) and is running great.

Rolex Submariner Meters First Reference 5513

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is very cool example featuring the “Meters First” dial which was famously worn by Steve McQueen. The watch features a 1.54M serial dating to circa 1967 (we some gilt and matte dials in this range). The watch is paired on a very nice C+I bracelet made for Rolex dating to 1974. The dial and hands have been relumed at some point in the past. The crystal has been replaced to now feature the True Dome D19. The case has been lightly polished.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not include box and papers. It was recently serviced by Dr. Gref Petronzi and is running great!

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5065/1A-010 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5065A is one of the great contemporary sports watches. At 38mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions that can appeal to both vintage and modern enthusiasts. This example has a fantastic case which remains unpolished. The hands and dial feature tritium lume which has a nice warm color. According to the Certificate of Origin, it was sold in late 2003.

It is powered by the calibre 315 SC and is on view through its exhibition caseback.

The watch comes full set with box, the Certificate of Origin, corresponding papers and a steel Aquanaut bracelet. It has recently been serviced and is performing great! For those looking for an outstanding Aquanaut, you cannot go wrong with this one!

Abercrombie & Fitch Shipmate Unpolished

This Abercrombie & Fitch Shipmate is a super cool timepiece. The watch features a very warm patina from the luminous hands and dial. The case is unpolished and measures 20mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It is a gorgeous small watch on a new strap.

Rolex Wide Boy Day-Date Reference 1803 Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a phenomenal example. The 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter It has a very dressy look with distinctly broad hour markers and hands, giving it the name “Wide Boy”. It was purchased on a U.S. Navy Exchange. It features a 2.38M serial dating to 1970 and was sold that year.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great. For those who are looking for an awesome watch [papers and box] that will make everyone respect you, this is the right choice!

Rolex Date Reference 1500 in 14K Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Date reference 1500 is a great example. The 14K yellow gold case is unpolished. The dial is brilliant. It measures 34mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). It has developed some oxidation on the surfaces of the case which are very attractive. The case back features an engraving “W.C.B. 6-24-76”. The serial is 3.81M dating to circa 1975. It is paired on a 14K yellow gold 7205 Rivet bracelet (which is also used on gold Daytonas) dating to circa 1981.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running good. It has just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

TUDOR Submariner Reference 7928 Unpolished

I’m excited to offer this extremely cool TUDOR Submariner reference 7928. The case is unpolished. It measures 39mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The original tritium lume has a warm patina on the hands and dial. The serial is a 506,XXX dating to circa 1966. It is paired on later stretch C&I bracelet dating to 1977. It features a very attractive long 5 bezel insert which is original to the watch.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history. It was recently regulated by Alkis Kotsopoulos of Swiss Watch Repair.

Rolex Non-Luminous Linen Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished

I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 1601 is so beautiful. It features a non-luminous dial with black paint in hands similar to Rolex CERN-dial Milgauss reference 1019 examples.

The watch dates to 1970 and features a 2.39M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on an American made Rolex Jubilee bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Breitling Colt Ocean Reference A17050 w/ Booklet

This is a super cool Breitling Colt Ocean reference A17050 from the early 2000s. The watch has been lightly polished, but looks very nice. The dial has very interesting and bold numerals. The case measures 38mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug).

It comes from the original owner. The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with a booklet. It was recently serviced and is running good.

Rolex Datejust Silver Dial Reference 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful. The hands and dial have a very nice warm lume.

The watch dates to 1984 and features a 8.2M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Laine G2 Full Set Unpolished

It is not everyday we get super modern cool watches like this Laine G2 with the mesmerizing purple fumé. The colorful Roman numeral dial is really outstanding and steals the show. Flip it over and you can enjoy a beautiful movement on full exhib! It also has a very interesting case which is enjoyable to look at from the profile! The watch comes with an additional strap, dial and handset which would be epic for a collector who wants to switch up the look.

The watch dates to 2021 and is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It comes from the original owner with box and papers.

Rolex Linen Datejust Reference 1603 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 1603 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1974 and features a 3.66M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 55 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Universal Geneve Space-Compax Reference 885104/01 Unpolished w/ Extract

The Universal Geneve Space-Compax was designed by the company to be their underwater dive chronograph. Not many were produced at the time and very few survive in excellent condition today. The present example is in fantastic condition with an unpolished case. This is the rare and desirable version featuring white chronograph registers. It is paired on its highly desirable bracelet made by Gay Frères.

This watch is from circa 1960, is powered by the manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and is 40mm in diameter.

The watch comes with an Extract from The Archives. It does not come with a box. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

Rolex Silver Dial Datejust Reference 16220 Unpolished

This silver-dial reference 16220 is so beautiful. The watch dates to 1991 and features a X serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555B end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Patek Philippe Reference 5227G-010 Unpolished Full Set

This Patek Philippe reference 5227G-010 is an outstanding contemporary dress watch. The watch is powered by the calibre 324 SC. The white gold case is unpolished and measures 39mm in diameter. The watch was originally sold in Texas in 2019.

It does comes with box, papers and Certificate of Origin.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1603 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 1603 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1970 and features a 2.248M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 55 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Date Reference 15037 in 14K Yellow Gold

This Rolex Date reference 15037 is a super cool watch with a stunning dial. The watch dates to circa 1981 and features a 6.7M serial. The case measures 34mm in diameter and is paired on a 14K Rolex Jubilee bracelet made in the United States. The case has been lightly polished.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time.

Rolex Domino's Air-King Reference 5500 Unpolished

The Rolex-Domino’s connection is perhaps the ultimate high-low collaboration. These watches were not made for mainstream purchase, but as a way to reward franchise owners who hit their sales targets. All were originally ordered through Schlanderer and Sons in Ann Arbor, Michigan, where Domino’s has its headquarters. In the past, these watches on the secondhand market were generally the least expensive Rolex a person could purchase as few people wanted a pizza logo like this on their dial. Dealers would buy them, swap out the Domino’s dial, and polish off the case back initials. Over the last year, however, they have gained a massive following and Christie’s recently sold a 34mm Air-King version for $20,000 including premium in an online auction. This watch has a diameter of 34mm excluding crown. It was produced circa 1987 and includes the original guarantee, plus the award letter from the Domino's CEO for reaching $20,000 in weekly sales.

To learn more about Rolex-Domino’s story, watch John P’s video with Eric Wind “Rolex Model NEVER in Stores”.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

This watch is available at the Kith Beverly Hills location at 262 N Rodeo Dr.

Rolex Black Dial Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished

I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this black-dial reference 1601 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1966 and features a 1.28M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 55 end links dating to the 4th quarter of 1965.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Daniel Roth w/ Blue Dial and Arabic Numerals Unpolished

This Daniel Roth is a super cool and attractive example from the early 2000s THG era. The watch is powered by the Zenith El Primero. The blue dial is a bit more common of the colored dials, however the Arabic numerals are extremely rare. The steel case measures 38mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug)

It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Daytona Cosmograph Reference 116500LN Brand New Stickered Full Set

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN with white dial is affectionately known as the “Panda” and is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a white dial and black Cerachrom bezel, white dial, and black registers. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. The watch comes from the original owner who purchased it in 2023.

It is in brand new condition with the original stickers remaining on the watch. It comes “full set” with original box and papers.

Rolex Oysterquartz Datejust Reference 17014 Unpolished

This Rolex Oysterquartz Datejust reference 17014 is a very nice example with a rare Roman-numeral dial. The watch features an N Serial dating to circa 1991. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug).

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Bvlgari Diagono Yellow Dial Chronograph Reference AC 38 TA Unpolished

I have a dirty little secret, and it is that I love the Diagono by Bvlgari! It is a super fun aluminum case which measures 38mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The watch wears very nice on the wrist and has a very nice yellow dial for those looking to have a bit of fun this summer!

The watch does not come with box and papers. It is running great and keeping excellent time.

LeCoultre Barracuda Reference E 558 Partial Box & Papers Unpolished

The LeCoultre reference E 558 is one of the extremely cool sports watches from Jaeger-LeCoultre. This example features a nice warm patina on the dial. The case is unpolished and measures 37mm in diameter (40mm in length). The case maker was Ervin Piquerez (EPSA). It is paired on a J.B. Champion bracelet.

It comes with a partial box and instruction/warranty papers. The watch was just serviced in March of 2023. It is running great and keeping excellent time.

Rolex Oysterdate Precision Reference 6694

This Rolex Oysterdate Precision Reference 6694 is a fantastic 34mm model from circa 1979. It is paired with its original 78350 Oyster bracelet with 557 B end links. The case is lightly polished, but is strong. It is powered by the manual-wind calibre 1225 movement. For those looking for a great value Rolex, this is a spectacular choice!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Explorer I Mk 1 Frog Foot Reference 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. This example is extremely nice and features an 2.11M serial dating it to circa 1969. It features the mark I dial with the frog foot coronet. The watch features the original Tropic 22 crystal. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive. It is paired on a Rolex 7206 rivet bracelet with correct 58 end links.

The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running very well! It just passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Transitional Reference 145.022-68 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-68 is an awesome “transitional” example with the applied OMEGA logo. The case is unpolished and measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 861 made by Lemania) and it features its original crown. The crystal is a replacement by OMEGA. It features a dot-over-90 bezel which is in phenomenal condition. These have sold for over $5,000 by themselves. The center seconds hand has been replaced.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great.

OMEGA Speedmaster reference 105.003-63 with Extract & Booklet

w

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era.

This is an awesome reference 105.003, also referred to as the “Ed White” as it was famously worn by the astronaut. The watch dates to circa 1963. It is powered by the iconic calibre 321, which was recently re-released and garnered great reception and excitement from a large portion of watch collectors. The case is lightly polished. The dimensions are 38.5mm in diameter (or 48mm lug-to-lug) with a lug width of 19mm. The bezel features the dot over 90. The crown is original.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The Omega Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box and papers or extract.

Breitling AVI Chronograph Reference 765 Unpolished

The Breitling Breitling 765 AVI was introduced in 1953 as a chronograph for pilots and is a very desirable for collectors. This example has a very attractive dial and hands with original lume. The case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter (48 lug-to-lug). It dates to circa 1961. The bezel is a later replacement example

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Enicar Digital Wristwatch Reference 119-01-01 As New

This Enicar reference 119-01-01 is an awesome wristwatch with the digital (linear) format. The movement is automatic. The watch is unpolished and in “as new” condition. For those who want an awesome alternative vintage watch, this is such a cool option!

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown. It is running good

Rolex Pink Oyster Perpetual Reference 124200 Full Set Like New

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 124200 is a super fun pink dial example. The watch was sold in 2022. It is in like new condition. The case measures 34mm in diameter.

It comes “full set” with box and papers.

Rolex Milgauss Reference 116400GV Blue Dial Green Crystal Unpolished w/ Box

When it comes to the antimagnetic wristwatches of the 1950s-1960s, several manufacturers produced what would go on to become iconic timepieces. Among those are the IWC Ingenieur, the Eberhard Scientigraf, the Omega Railmaster, as well as Patek Philippe’s reference 2570 / 2570-1 and 3417. However, one of the most beloved models of orientated for the physicist or engineer of the day was none other than the Rolex Milgauss.

This Rolex Milgauss reference 116400 features the blue dial with orange accents and thunderbolt hand. It is the model which features the green crystal. The watch is in great condition and is unpolished. It has a “random” serial dating to 2011 or later. It measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). The watch was worn only a few times.

The watch comes with box and booklet, but no warranty card. It appears to be produced in the recent years, but we do not know the exact year.

Gallet MultiChron 12 w/ Telemeter/Tachymeter Scale Unpolished

Gallet has made some of the greatest chronographs of all time and the Multichron 12 is among the most special watches from the manufacturer. This particular example features an incredible dial with both telemeter and tachymeter scales. The steel case is unpolished and measures 37mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the Excelsior Park 40. It features a beautiful non-luminous dial and luminous hands.

It does not come with a box. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 1578GM w/ Extract from the Archives Unpolished & Ephemera

This Patek Philippe reference 1578GM is a very rare and distinguished model, only made by special order in the 1950s for General Motors, and the only Patek Philippe reference named specifically to include a company’s name. As notable, this is by far the nicest example of the reference 1578GM I have ever seen.

It once belonged to one of most senior GM executives, its senior man in London and later head of GM overseas operations. These pieces were specifically commissioned by GM to honor its senior executives reaching 25+ years at the company. There are approximately 20 examples known and these are highly admired by Patek Philippe collectors.

The case measures 35.5mm in diameter (40.5mm lug to lug) and appears to be unpolished. The dial features a gilt Patek Philippe signature and seconds track with an amazing 3, 6, 9, and 12 applied Arabic numeral layout with sub seconds. It appears to be untouched.

For those looking for an outstanding vintage Patek Philippe, this is an incredible find! An astute Patek Philippe collector who came to know the original owner’s family purchased it several years ago.

It also comes with many terrific and rare materials, including photographs of the original owner with President Nixon, various letters with the GM Chairman and other documents, including a copy of the virtually never seen Henri Stern Watch Agency yellow card from New York reflecting when it was first ordered from Patek Philippe by GM. The original owner, Philip W. Copelin, is also featured in Maurice Platt’s great book ‘An Addiction to Automobiles: The occupational autobiography of an engineer and journalist’, a copy of which is also included.

It does not come with box but does include an Extract from the Archives! We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is on a matching Patek Philippe strap and buckle.

Rolex John Mayer 2.0 Daytona 126508 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished Box & Papers Brand New

When Rolex released the Land-Dweller, many of us said “Thank the Lord, they also released several Daytonas!” This was one of them! Upon the Daytona 126508’s release, collectors dubbed it the John Mayer 2.0. We love John Mayer and he loves watches!

The Rolex Daytona 126508 is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. The watch features a new dial layout that is a slightly different green and gold subdials. We love it! It features a beautiful and luxurious 18k yellow gold case that will garner respect and admiration from everyone you encounter. When people see this on your wrist, they will have no other choice but to bow down and worship you. 🙇

For those looking for the ultimate contemporary Daytona, look no further!

It is brand new.

The watch was sold in 2025. It comes “full set” with box and papers.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447N Unpolished on Gay Freres Beads of Rice Bracelet

This Heuer Carrera reference 2447N is an incredible example with the rare silver printing. The luminous hands have an extremely attractive color and pops on the contrasting black dial.

The condition is very nice, and the case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36 millimeters in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. The watch is powered by the Valjoux manual-winding calibre 72. This example dates to circa 1966. This dial and hands is the first execution with the same style of hour markers on the early Daytonas! It is a gorgeous and very class chronograph.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

For those who need a bit of context to the background of this watch, it was formerly in the collection of one of the most sophisticated collectors in the great state of Florida. Eric and Charlie worship him and his impeccable taste! He saw the watch on leather strap, and said “I think I would like to add a Gay Frères bracelet to that watch”. Eric and Charlie were amazed when he pointed to the period-correct Gay Frères beads of rice that it is currently on the watch and adds sophistication and class to an already sleek chronograph.

Rolex Fuchsia GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an incredible Fuchsia 1675 featuring a 1.92M serial number, dating to circa 1969. These bezels were born on watches featuring 1.8M and 1.9M serial range from circa 1968-1969. The case appears to be unpolished. The aluminum bezel insert is stunning - it has a small dent at 10. It is paired on a correct 7206 Rolex Rivet bracelet with 80 end links dating to 1967.

The seconds hand was color matched due to previous lume loss. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Date 15200 Unpolished

I absolutely love this Rolex Date reference 15200. The watch dates to circa 1994 with an S9 Serial. The case measures 34mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent condition. We believe the case is unpolished.

It features a V2 bracelet dating to 1996.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box or papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Linen Datejust 16014 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 16014 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to circa 1985 with a 8.64M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The watch is in very nice condition. The case appears to be unpolished.

The bracelet code features an H date code dating to circa 1983.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Wide Boy Datejust 1601 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this wide boy reference 1601 is so beautiful.

It features a non-luminous Wide Boy dial. The watch dates to circa 1974 with a 3.6M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The watch is in very nice condition. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Spillman Case 145.022 Unpolished

The Speedmaster is, of course, one of the most iconic and important watches ever made. This particular reference 145.022 is a super cool example from circa 1983. The case is unpolished and was made by C.R.S. (Charles-Rodolphe Spillmann).

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great. At this price, there are few better watches than a Spillman Speedy!

Rolex Silver Soleil Datejust Reference 16014 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 16014 is no exception. The “silver soleil” dial is one of my favorite Datejust dials, as it is so attractive and special. The watch dates to 1979 and features a 6.32M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. The dial and hands have outstanding lume!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Cartier Tank Automatique Midsize in 18K Gold Unpolished w/ Extra Deployant

The Cartier Tank is one of the most sophisticated dress watches and these models have grown to become highly collectible over recent years. It is a great contrast to many sports Rolex models within a collection and can be worn in both a casual and dressed up state.

This example features an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. These are the midsize models.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with a deployant buckle.

To learn more about the spectacular watches from Cartier, read our article My Dream Cartier Collection.

Patek Philippe Reference 1578J

Is the Patek Philippe reference 1578 the best bargain in vintage PP? It might be a contender for best entry-level Patek in general. This is a very fun reference 1578 in yellow gold. The case measures 35.5mm in diameter. The case has been polished. The timepiece dates to the 1950s. For those looking for a vintage Patek Philippe timepiece with an interesting case design with great size, this is a spectacular choice!

It is paired on a Patek strap and deployant buckle. The crown may be a service crown.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex IBM Datejust 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful. The watch dates to 1985 and features a 8.7M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 78360 Rolex Oyster bracelet and 558 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition. The caseback features the former owner’s name engraved with his employer’s (IBM) logo. It was received by the employee on December 27, 1985.

We believe the case is unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Grey Dial Datejust Reference 1603 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this grey-dial reference 1603 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1974 and features a 3.3M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The grey dial is incredibly attractive and has fantastic lume!

The watch is in excellent condition. We believe it has an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Universal Genève Desk Clock Central Seconds

This Universal Genève Desk Clock is an amazing timepiece dating to the 1960s. The case is stylized in the popular U logo for the manufacturer and features a battery powered movement. The dial features incredible dauphine and arrow hour markers. This is the perfect ephemera for hardcore Universal Genève enthusiasts.

A similar example sold at Christie’s in 2016 for $3,500.

It does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Rolex Black Dial Daytona 116500LN Unpolished Box & Papers

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a black dial and black Cerachrom bezel. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. The watch appears to be unpolished.

The watch was sold in 2022.

It comes “full set” with box and papers.

Heuer Monaco Reference 1133B Unpolished on NSA Bracelet

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement, the calibre 11. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic. This example is very nice in outstanding condition. The case appears to be unpolished, and the dial is stunning and a rare non-luminous variant.

In addition, it is paired on a vintage Heuer bracelet made by NSA.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was just serviced and is running well.

Vacheron Constantin Metiers d'Art Les Univers Infinis Angel in 18K White Gold Unpolished

This is a stunning Vacheron Constantin Metiers d'Art Les Univers Infinis Angel reference 86222/000G-9804. The watch dates to circa 2014. The case is made of white gold. It appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter. It features an automatic movement.

These were produced in very limited quantities - with a purported 20 examples made. This is highly exclusive. The dial is quite an elaborate and ornate decoration with angels and demons. The demons are covered with translucent green or blue enamel. The angels were a guilloche and hand-engraved to complete the detail of their beautiful faces.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with a photo copy of the extract. The original was lost.

By Charlie Dunne

Heuer Autavia Reference 3646 “First Execution” Unpolished

One of the most attractive Autavias is the First Execution Autavia reference 364 which features “Big Subs” (or “Big Eyes”) from the early 60s. These early Autavias are considered “Holy Grail” pieces amongst the Heuer collecting community.

The dial has a very beautiful patina throughout the hour markers with slight fading on the exterior of the 12 and 6 numerals. Often, these early Autavias get damaged within the white registers, however, this example has remained very clean. The two-register 3646 features a more symmetrical look than the 2446 and you can enjoy the attractive luminous numeral at 6:00 on the 3646.

The case is unpolished. The watch comes from the original owner and features a cool Virgin Islands engraving on the back. It is powered by the manual-wind movement (Caliber 92 made by Valjoux) and is 39mm in diameter.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012-68 w/ Extract

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012 dates to 1968 (with a -68 case production). The watch is incredibly attractive with beautiful lume! The case has been lightly polished in the past, but is nice. It measures 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 321 made by OMEGA).

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with an Extract from The Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!.

Rolex Khanjar Two-Tone Datejust Reference 1601

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 16014 is no exception. The “silver soleil” dial is one of my favorite Datejust dials, as it is so attractive and special. The watch dates to 1979 and features a 6.32M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet.

This 1601 in two tone is no exception. This example showcases an attractive “soleil sunburst” dial, yet with a very special green Khanjar logo applied. The watch dates to 1978 and features a 5.05M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet. The watch has been lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It has just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

OMEGA Railmaster Reference 2914-4 SC Unpolished

This OMEGA Ralimaster reference 2914-4 SC is one of the nicest examples I have ever seen. It has very beautiful lume. We believe the case is unpolished. The case measures 38mm by 47mm lug to lug. The watch dates to circa 1959 with a 17.3M movement number.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J with Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold. The dial is English and is strikingly beautiful.This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm. The case has been lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from the Archives. It was produced in 1990. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Paris Tank Louis in 18K White Gold Unpolished

This is a very attractive Cartier Tank Louis in 18k white gold. It measures 23mm in diameter by 31mm lug to lug. It features a very attractive Paris dial. We believe the case is unpolished. We believe it dates to the late 1970s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with a red Cartier pouch, and extra Cartier deployant and Cartier strap. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Paris Louis Tank Mini in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This is a very attractive Cartier “mini” Tank Louis in 18k yellow gold. It measures 18mm in diameter by 28mm lug to lug. It features a very attractive Paris dial. We believe the case is unpolished. It comes on a nice Cartier deployant buckle. We believe it dates to the late 1970s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 226570 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Explorer II reference 226570 is an awesome example. The case measures 42mm in diameter (and 50.5mm lug-to-lug). This example is unpolished. It was sold in 2023..

This watch comes from the original owner and is “full set” with box or papers.

Tudor Heritage Ranger Reference 79910 On Bracelet Unpolished Full Set

This Tudor Heritage Ranger reference 79910 is an awesome watch. It is paired on the original bracelet. The case appears to be unpolished

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers. It is a great value for the money. IT comes with an extra nylon strap and bund.

Patek Philippe Reference 2457 in 18K Rose Gold

This is a very attractive Patek Philippe dating to the 1950s. It is a successor to the reference 96. It is a rose gold case which is quite rare. The case is lightly polished. It measures 31mm in diameter and 39mm lug to lug. It is powered by the calibre 27 SC.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Ladies 24 Reference 4910/10A-011 w/ Service Papers

This Patek Philippe Ladies 24 reference 4910/10A-011 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch measures 24mm by 28mm. The case has been lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes from the original owner’s family with Patek service papers from 2022.

LeCoultre "Lapis" Memovox Travel Clock Reference 11015

This JLC Memovox travel alarm clock is a super cool timepiece dating to the 1960s. The case is a faux lapis shell which is very attractive. The case is gold plated. The dial is in great condition. These have very cool trapezoidal date windows - as seen on models like the Polerouter. It was produced for the American market.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Silver Soleil Datejust Reference 16014 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 16014 is no exception. The “silver soleil” dial is one of my favorite Datejust dials, as it is so attractive and special. The watch dates to 1979 and features a 6.32M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. The dial and hands have outstanding lume!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It has just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master II Pepsi Reference 126710BLRO Full Set Unpolished

Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch remains unpolished. It was sold in 2023, it was purchased from the original owner, and is “full set” with its original box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Patek Philippe Ellipse Reference 5738R-001 Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe Ellipse is a spectacular timepiece. The watch is powered by the calibre 240. The case is unpolished and made of 18K rose gold. The case is unpolished.

The watch was sold in 2018 and includes box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron & Constantin Pink Dial Pocket Watch

This Vacheron & Constantin pocket watch is an extremely cool example dating to the 1930s-1940s. The case is a two-tone stainless steel body with gold crown. The bow is very firm, and resembles designs such as the reference 600 by Patek. It measures approximately 46mm in diameter. The movement is beautifully decorated with Geneva stripes or Côtes de Genève. The dial is incredibly attractive, with a salmon or pink dial with silvered minute track and matching applied Arabic numerals.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 16570 Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Explorer II reference 16570 is a great sports watch. The case is unpolished. It is rugged and bullet proof sports watch with great versatility. The watch features a Y Serial and was sold in 2002.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

IWC Fancy Lugs in 18K Rose Gold

This rose-gold IWC is a great example. It features beautiful “pearl” minute demarcations. It measures 37.5mm in diameter and 42mm lug to lug. A great size for those looking for a sophisticated dress watch! It incorporates a fancy lug style.The case is lightly polished, but attractive. For those looking for a jumbo IWC this is a great option.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Panerai Luminor GMT 10 Days Limited Edition For Palm Beach in 18K Rose Gold Limited Edition Full Set Unpolished

This Panerai Luminor GMT has an automatic movement, featuring a 10 day power reserve. This watch measures 51.5mm in diameter and 53mm in width. It has an awesome Palm Beach case back which you can show to your enemies as they kneel before you. This limited edition watch is an amazing buy and only 10 were produced!

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. It has not been serviced, but it is running well.
