By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18038 Unpolished w/ Box

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week. The Day-Date is certainly one of the best luxury timepieces ever designed.

This reference 18038 is an incredibly attractive watch with a strikingly warm champagne dial. The 18K-gold case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This particular example dates to circa 1984 with a 8.5M serial.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Vulcain Cricket with Non-Luminous Roman Numeral Dial Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket is among the earliest styles produced just over 100 years after the La-Chaux-de-Fonds manufacturer’s inception. Like the “LBJ” dial Crickets, it features the original or “OG” style case. It measures 34mm in diameter (just under 43mm lug-to-lug).

The steel case features drilled lugs..

The dial is non-luminous and features applied-Roman numerals. The case remains unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 120, and has an exceptional voice when the alarm is engaged.

Patek Philippe Reference 3491/3 Retailed by Cartier in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Extract

This is a very special and refined timepiece by Patek Philippe retailed by Cartier. The case and bracelet are made of 18k yellow gold. The bracelet is very intricately made and special. It was produced by Jean-Pierre Ecoffey who produced many of these high-end bracelets for Patek, Audemars Pieguet, and others. The dial has a phenomenal texture that complements the woven bracelet. The case measures 26mm by 26mm. It dates to the mid-1960s-1970s. It is unpolished and one of the most beautiful Patek Philippe watches we have ever held

It does not come with box and papers. The watch was just serviced by the legendary Alkis Kotsopoulos of Swiss Watch Repair in New York City. It was serviced for the first time, reflecting the original and spectacular condition of the watch. We have ordered an Extract from the Archives from Patek Philippe for the watch.

TUDOR Snowflake Submariner Reference 9401/0

This TUDOR Snowflake Submariner reference 9401/0 is a very cool sports watch and the rare no-date variant of the model. It features a ghosted-bezel insert that was likely replaced. The bracelet is later. The case is to be well worn, and possibly unpolished.

It features an 846,XXX serial dating to circa 1976.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex IBM Datejust 16220 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16220 is so beautiful. The watch dates to circa 1991 and features an X serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 78360 Rolex Oyster bracelet and 558 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition. The caseback features the former owner’s name engraved with his employer’s (IBM) logo. It was received by the employee in 1991.

We believe the case is unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Heuer Calendar Chronograph Reference 2547S w/ Box

This vintage Heuer reference 2547S is a very cool complicated watch. It features a Valjoux 72C with calendar chronograph. The dial is silver. The steel case is 36mm in diameter. It has been polished in the past. For those looking for classic complicated Heuer, this is a great option.

The watch does not include papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service histroy, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished w/ Silver Dial

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1986 and features a 9.03M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch.

Heuer Camaro Reference 7743 NT on Gay Frères Bracelet Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive racing, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera. This particular reference 7743 NT represents why the Camaro is a watch that warrants more discussion. The dial has aged beautifully and is a dark brown. The crisp branding is emphasized in white, with highly legible subdials at 6 and 9 o’clock. The case and bracelet are unpolished. It is powered by the Valjoux 7730, a formidable bi-register chronograph that has stood the test of time.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The original Gay Frères Heuer double-grain beads of rice bracelet with correct HLA end links is worth approximately $5,000 alone.

Rolex Wide Boy Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 1601 is an awesome watch with a bold “wide boy” dial. The thick non-luminous hands and dial make for a super distinct Datejust. The case appears to be unpolished and the watch dates to circa 1972 with a 3.11M serial number. The watch measures 36mm in diameter. It is pared on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet made in the US.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Cartier Santos Carrée Two-Tone 2961 Ghost Dial

Cartier is having a moment and models like the classic manual wind Santos are very special timepieces. This example is a collectible variant known as the “Ghost”. People love them! It is made of 18k yellow gold and steel. The case measures 29mm in diameter. It dates to the 1990s. The bracelet is 7 inches!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 is spectacular example. It features the original tritium on the dial and hands. The dial very attractive. The case appears to be unpolished and measures at 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 861 made by Lemania).

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not have its original box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Zenith Chronomaster Original Limited Edition For HODINKEE Full Set Unpolished

The Zenith Chronomaster Original Limited Edition For HODINKEE is one of the coolest LEs by the Dinkers. Particularly, this dial is very attractive for a Zenith El Primero and I quite love it.

It comes with box and papers and shows very little signs of wear. It comes from the original owner.

Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master II 126710BLRO Full Set Unpolished

Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch remains unpolished. It was worn minimally over the years and has a few scratches on the buckle. Outside of that it is in exceptional condition. It was sold in 2021, it was purchased from the original owner, and is “full set” with its original box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article Eric Wind produced with HODINKEE.

Rolex GMT-Master Ref 1675 Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master is a staple Rolex model that has an incredible history. It was designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950`s to ensure that they could measure two time zones at once. This reference 1675 is a fantastic example. The case appears to be unpolished.

It features a “Mark 2” dial. The watch dates to circa 1973 with a 3.3M serial. It is paired on a non-dated Rolex 7836 folded Rivet bracelet dating to 1973-1975 with 380 end links which are correct for the 1675.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master watch Eric Wind’s ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article which was produced with HODINKEE. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - June 19th 2025

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

TUDOR Ranger Reference 7965 No Date Automatic

The TUDOR Ranger is an incredible model with a great deal of appreciation for its similarity to the Rolex Explorer with a 3, 6, 9 numeral layout. This reference 7965 is in fantastic condition with a nice case. It is a very rare no-date model with the “smiley” curved self-winding text. It measures 34mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). The dial and hands have a beautiful warm patina. It dates to circa 1962.

The watch has recently had the movement cleaned/oiled and had the crown fixed. The watch has great provenance.

It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16220 Grey Dial w/ Roman Numerals Like New

A steel Datejust is an essential for anyone who loves Rolex. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This reference 16220 features an amazing example with a grey Roman numeral dial which is quite uncommon. This Rolex features an L serial and dates to circa 1989. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is unpolished. It features the original case back sticker and is in “like new” condition.

It does not come with box and and papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1530 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust is the amazing reference 1530 which features the integrated bracelet and case design synonymous with the Oysterquartz models from the manufacturer - with the exception of an automatic movement. These watches are super enjoyable on the wrist. This case measures 36mm in diameter and remains unpolished. The dial has a beautiful warm tone. There are some light marks on the dial. The watch is running strong and keeping great time. The service history is unknown. It has recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

It does not come with box and and papers.

Rolex Thunderbird Datejust Reference 1625

The Rolex Thunderbird Datejust is a very cool watch. They are classic, but also a bit sporty with their robust signature bezels in white gold. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It has been lightly polished and features a very attractive pie-pan dial with sunken minute track. The watch recently passed a water resistance test to 3 bars.

The case features a 1.6M serial number dating the watch to circa 1966.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

This watch is available at the Kith Miami Design District location at 69 NE 41st St.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 875 Unpolished w/ Extract

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 875 is one of my favorite Memovox references. The cases are really great with fantastic proportions at 37mm in diameter (or 45.5mm lug-to-lug). This particular example has a beautiful unpolished case. It features a high-beat calibre 916. For those with an appreciation for alarm watches and Jaeger-LeCoultre this is a phenomenal option.

It does not come with box and papers. It comes with an Extract from the Archives.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16014 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 16014 is a phenomenal timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug) and is unpolished. The watch recently passed a water resistance test to 3 bars.

The case features a 8.8M serial number dating the watch to circa 1985.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

This watch is available at the Kith Miami Design District location at 69 NE 41st St.

Rolex Submariner Reference 1680 From Original Owner

This Rolex Submariner is a very fun sports watch dating to circa 1978. The watch features a service dial for a reference 1680 which is not uncommon for Rolex to add during a service. The case has been polished. For those looking to enjoy a nice Rolex Submariner that does not break the bank, this is a great option.

The watch comes from the original owner. It does not come with box and does come with serivice papers.

Rolex Submariner Maxi Mark II Dial Reference 5513

This Rolex submariner is a very cool watch. The watch features a service dial for a reference 5512 which is not uncommon for Rolex to add during a service. The case has been polished. It has been recently tested for a water resistance of 5 bar. The watch dates to circa 1973 and is a great vintage watch for the money.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 124270 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Explorer reference 124270 is a fantastic modern sports watch from the current production with great proportions. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug) and 11.5mm thick. This particular example has a few scratches on it, but it is still a beautiful unpolished case.

It comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner who purchased the watch in 2021 and has his heart set on a vintage Rolex (which I can certainly understand). For those that are eager for an excellent Rolex Explorer at a great price, this watch is for you!

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - June 2, 2023

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Date For Chris-Craft Reference 1500 Unpolished Full Set

I love corporate dial Rolex watches. This particular watch is a very exciting example customized by Rolex for Chris Craft - a prestigious American boats manufacturer. The watch appears to have been a retirement gift for the recipient with a dedication reading; “Charles H. Bell Chris-Craft Corporation 1935-1977.” The watch is in exceptional condition and the logo pairs wonderfully with the silver-tone dial. The watch dates to circa 1977.

The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes with box and papers. It was recently serviced.

Rolex Daytona John Mayer Reference 116508 Full Set Brand New & Stickered

Nicknamed for the 2nd appearance on HODINKEE Talking Watches, this Rolex Daytona reference 116508 with green anniversary dial would take on the rockstar nickname "John Mayer". Despite being a departure from the Daytona’s utility-based nature, this model has become admired amongst a range of Rolex collectors and has been spotted on many other notable wrists including Jonah Hill’s. It has recently been discontinued. The watch is in brand spanking new condition and features the original stickers, which are taken off during the sale of the watch.

The watch comes full set from the original owner. It was sold in mid 2023 and was one of the final examples sold.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 in 18K Gold

I’m thrilled to offer this awesome Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 in 18K yellow gold. The watch comes from the second owned who wore it with a service insert. We were thrilled when he shared the original insert which was kept during the service! The watch dates to circa 1978. The case is lightly polished, but very strong. It comes on what we believe to be the original Presidential bracelet dating to circa 1979. The watch can fit a wrist up to 7.1 inches.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is not known, but the watch is running great.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5066A Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 5066A is one of the great contemporary sports watches. This watch is in fantastic condition and dates to circa 1999. It comes from the original owner. At 36mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions that can appeal to both vintage and modern enthusiasts. The hands and dial feature tritium lume, and the case is unpolished.

It is powered by the calibre 330 SC and is on view through its exhibition caseback. It does come with the Extract from the Archives (one can be ordered for an additional $600).

Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 w/ Papers & Hangtag Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520 with a black dial. It has a serial dating it to circa 1997-1998. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988 until circa 2000 . This is a very nice example with an unpolished case. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). The watch retains a case back sticker.

This watch does not include a box. It does come with the hangtag and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was recently tested for water resistance up to 5 bar.

Rolex Daytona Reference 116523

This Rolex Daytona is a very cool reference 116523 in two tone. The watch dates to circa 2007-2008. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). The case has been polished in the past.

This watch does not include a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Enicar Sherpa Graph Mark 1A Unpolished

Enicar sports models are among some of the coolest and underrated vintage watches to collect. Their oversized cases are very exciting to wear. They also share history with the renowned water-resistant casemaker Ervin Piquerez (EPSA).

This example is the very rare Sherpa Graph Mark 1A. The Enicar Sherpa Graph models are extremely desirable for collectors and very difficult to find. The watch is known by collectors for its impressionable design and perhaps influencing the Rolex Daytona. Enicar was way ahead of other companies with the white registers against a black dial and a large 40mm diameter case with internal tachymeter. Only a handful of Mark 1A examples are known to the market. One way you can identify the first version of Sherpa Graph is the TACHYMETER BASE 1000 on the inner tachymeter bezel. Later versions did not say “BASE 1000”. You can find out more about this Sherpa Graph at Enicar101.org

https://enicar101.com/sherpa-graph-mkia/

The very bold case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter (50mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the calibre Valjoux 72. It is paired on an Enicar branded bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Roger Dubuis Three-Retrograde Platinum Reference H37 5799 8 5.6. Full Set

This Roger Dubuis Three-Retrograde reference H37 5799 8 5.6. in platinum is a very interesting timepiece. The dial features a retrograde scale for the minutes, hours, and calendar complication. With alternating red and black hours, it is highly legible. The blue-cathedral hands pay respect to a more refined look. For those who appreciate independent watchmaking, this platinum observatory grade wristwatch is surely something that you will not come across in the wild. it measures 37mm in diameter (43 lug-to-lug).

This watch comes with box and papers and just had a full service at Roger Dubuis that took almost 2 years complete, which cost $1,856. It appears to be unpolished. The dial appears to be an enamel dial. The watch was just serviced with Roger Dubuis.

TUDOR Oysterdate Reference 7961 Unpolished

This TUDOR Oysterdate reference 7961 is an incredibly cool vintage watch. The condition is very nice with an attractive dial and alpha hands. Also, it is complemented by the TUDOR Rose logo near 12 o’clock. The case is very nice and is unpolished. It measures 32mm in diameter (38mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on a super crisp stretch rivet bracelet by Gay Frères with 67 end links dating to the 4th quarter of 1960. The watch has a 330,xxx serial, also dating to 1960. At $1,400, this watch is an incredible buy.

This watch does not include a box and papers. It was recently serviced, and passed a 5 bar water-resistance test.

Movado M95 Retailed by Tiffany & Co. in 14K Gold Unpolished

I’m very excited to offer this exceptional vintage Movado M95 chronograph retailed by Tiffany & Co. The watch is in fantastic condition, and the case remains unpolished. On the case back is a beautiful cursive engraving by the prestigious New York retailer “Pat from Peter”. For those with a penchant for sophisticated chronographs, this is an incredible timepiece.

This watch does not include a box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running very well.

Rolex Datejust Buckley Dial Reference 16018 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

I absolutely love the Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and example is no exception! This 18K gold case is unpolished and looks so nice on the Honey Wind strap! The dial is a very attractive gold “Buckley” dial. It dates to 1987 and features a 9.8M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter

It does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great!

Rolex Explorer Reference 6610

The Rolex Explorer 6610 was produced over the course of 1955-1959 and is the predecessor to the Explorer reference 1016. This example is a very cool and accessibly priced example. It has a service tritium dial and service tritium hands. The case has been lightly polished. It features a 268,XXX serial dating to circa 1957. The watch comes from the original owner’s family.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just recently serviced by Dr. Greg Petronzi. The crystal has been replaced with a TrueDome D22.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 126000 Green Dial Full Set

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 126000 is an awesome modern watch. It features one of my favorite dial variants from the line in green. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter.

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was purchased in mid 2022.

Rolex Datejust Black Dial Reference 16234 Mint Condition

This Rolex Datejust reference 16234 is an awesome unpolished example with a very attractive black dial. It is in very mint condition and will be undoubtedly cherished by the next owner. The watch measures 36mm in diameter. and the X serial dates to circa 1991. It is paired on its original 62510H bracelet with 555 B end links. The fluted bezel is made of white gold and it maintains a case back sticker on the back.

This watch does not include a box and papers. The service history of this watch is not known, but it is running very well.

Vulcain Cricket Reference 305012 Owned By José Ferrer

This Vulcain Cricket reference 305012 is an early iteration of the waterproof cases, which would later transition into “S series” references. The watch has brilliant provenance formerly belonging to José Ferrer. In 1949, he would become the Nominee for Best Actor in a Supporting Role in Joan of Arc. In 1951, Ferrer became the first Puerto Rican and Hispanic Academy Award winner for his performance in the 1950 film Cyrano de Bergerac. He was presented his Oscar by Helen Hayes and Fred Astaire over the telephone which was documented by the Academy. For this same role, he won a Golden Globe Award for Best Actor.

Ferrer would go on be nominated by the Academy Awards again in the following year as Best Actor in a Leading Role in Moulin Rouge. During the same year at the 6th Annual Tony Awards, he would win both Best Actor-Play for The Shrike, as well as Outstanding Director for The Shrike and The Fourposter. His awards also include the following: Nominee for two Primetime Emmys, Nominee for an NYFCC Award, DGA Award, and BAFTA Film Award. In addition, he was awarded with a Star on the Walk of Fame.

This Cricket measures in at 34mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug) with a lug width of 18mm. The case is gold plated and is in phenomenal condition. The dial features a thin guilloche on the exterior of the dial which is very seldom seen on vintage Vulcain Crickets. As a passionate Cricket collector, I am thrilled to be able to offer such a fantastic timepiece.

Cartier Pasha Golf Reference 0955 in 18k Gold Unpolished

The Cartier Pasha Golf is a very cool and rare model. This reference 0955 dates to circa 1982, making it first series in the Golf Pashas (later exampled are released between 1993-1995. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 039. It features the sticker on the back. The case is unpolished

This watch does not include a box and papers. The service history of this watch is not known, but it is running very well. The two bottom pushers are stiff when pressed, and likely require a service.

Patek Philippe Reference 5226G Brand New Full Set

This Patek Philippe reference 5226G is an awesome modern wristwatch from the highly acclaimed watchmaker. The dial features a gradient texture and the lume gives off a vintage inspired aesthetic. The case is unpolished and shows minimal signs of wear. The watch dates to August of 2022.

The watch is brand new and worn twice. It comes from the original owner and is “full set” with box and papers.

Rolex Cosmograph Daytona Reference 116519LN in White Gold on Oysterflex Full Set

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116519LN with grey dial is a fantastic iteration of the iconic sports watch. Paired on its original Oysterflex band, it is truly suited to be worn in all active situations.

The watch features a grey dial, black Cerachrom bezel, black registers and the beautiful red-arched “Daytona” font. The case is in 18K white gold and is powered by the calibre 4130.

It comes “full set” with original box and papers from the original owner.

Rolex Oysterdate Reference 6494 Unpolished

This Rolex Oysterdate reference 6494 is a super cool watch with great character. The die has a nice warm patina, and hour makers have taken on a warm tone. The alpha hands have the original lume. The case is unpolished and wears very nice on the wrist. It measures 34mm in diameter (42.5mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great. The watch passed a water test up to 3 bar.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 124300 White Dial Gold Hour Markers Like New Full Set

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 124300 is an awesome modern watch. It has great proportions at 40mm in diameter. The smooth bezel gives it a slightly more dressy look. The silver dial is accented by very attractive gold hour makers. The watch is in like new condition and was worn very little by the owner.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold in December 2020.

Roger Dubuis Three-Retrograde Platinum Full Set

This Roger Dubuis Three-Retrograde platinum is a very interesting timepiece. The dial features a retrograde scale for the minutes, hours, and calendar complication. With alternating red and black hours, it is highly legible. The blue-cathedral hands pay respect to a more refined look. For those who appreciate independent watchmaking, this platinum observatory grade wristwatch is surely something that you will not come across in the wild.

This watch comes with box and papers and just had a full service at Roger Dubuis that took almost 2 years complete, which cost $1,856. It appears to be unpolished. The dial appears to be an enamel dial. The watch was just serviced with Roger Dubuis.

Porsche Design By IWC Compass Watch Reference IW3510

This Porsche Design by IWC is an insanely cool wristwatch from the 1980s. The case features an interactive element where it also serves as a compass and mirror. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 375. The watch comes from the original owner. It has a nice long bracelet which can fit a bigger wrist.

The PVD case is in fantastic condition. It measures 39mm in diameter. The service history is unknown. It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 1680

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa 1978. The case has been lightly polished, but is strong. The dial has original lume which is a very attractive warm yellow. It is paired on a period correct Rolex bracelet dating to 1979.

The watch comes from the family of the original owner. The service history is unknown. It does not come with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Oyster Royal Precision Reference 6426

This Rolex Oyster Royal Precision reference 6426 is a super cool watch with a very clean look. It comes from the original owner who purchased this in Switzerland during 1963 (the same year of production). He wore the watch during his military service in Vietnam. His name and social security number was engraved into the case back. He later removed it.

The case measures 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It has been lightly polished at some point in the past.

The service history is unknown. It does not come with box and papers.

Breitling Navitimer Reference 806 Unpolished

I love this Breitling Navitimer reference 806. It is one of the great mid-century chronographs and wears incredible on the wrist. This particular example is an AOPA model specifically branded for Breitling dating to circa 1961. The case is unpolished and in great shape. It is powered by the Venus calibre 178.

This watch just had a $1,500 service from Blake Scott of Scotchwatch. It does not come with box and papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Submariner Reference 16808 in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Blue/Teal Dial Full Set

The Rolex Submariner is certainly one of the most iconic timepieces and for great reason. The Submariner was introduced in 1954 and to this day it remains a staple in the Rolex line. This particular example is the reference 16808. It features an 8.69M serial dating to circa 1985. The blue dial has developed a teal discoloration which is very unique and attractive. The insert and dial have a Bahamian blue vibe! The 18K yellow gold case has been lightly polished in the past, but is nice. For those looking for an awesome gold Submariner, this is certainly a cool and interesting option!.

The comes with papers, and an associated and period correct box. It comes with a hangtag. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It has recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the evolution of the Rolex Submariner, see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Explorer Reference 1016 Underline Chapter Ring Dial

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989.

This example features a beautiful tropical-gilt dial with chapter ring and underline. It features an 0.901M serial dating it to circa 1963. The watch features a TrueDome crystal. The case is has been polished in the past. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive. It is paired on a Rolex 7206 bracelet with 82 end links.

The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch has just been serviced and passed a water resistance test of 5 bar..

IWC Porsche Design Ocean Bund Reference 3501 w/ Box, Extra Strap, Bracelet & Extract Unpolished

This IWC Porsche Design Ocean Bundeswehr reference 3501 is a super cool sports watch. The watch dates to 1984. These models have a cult following and are among the coolest models from IWC. The case is made of titanium and measures 42mm in diameter. The lume is insanely attractive on this example. For those who want an out of this world IWC Porsche Design wristwatch, this is a seriously cool option.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers. It comes with an Extract, an extra strap, bracelet and box.

Rolex Air-King Blue Dial Two-Tone Reference 5501 Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Air-King reference 5501 is an awesome watch with an beautiful blue dial. The steel case is 34mm. We believe the case is unpolished.. It features an R serial dating to circa 1988.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well! It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Burlwood Day-Date Reference 18038 in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Bark Texture

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 is a super cool example with a beautiful and uncommon burlwood dial. The 18K yellow gold case has been lightly polished, but is strong. It features the beautiful bark texture on the bracelet and bezel.. The watch dates to circa 1985 with an 8.7M serial.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold. The dial is in English and is strikingly beautiful. It is a second series dial. This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm. The case has been lightly polished, but is very strong.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. This is an early example that only came with a solid case back and not a sapphire exhibition case back, which started a few years later. The watch dates to 1988.

Cartier Tank Reference 1601 in Platinum Unpolished w/ Box

This Cartier Tank reference 1601 is a very special and elegant timepiece. The case is made of platinum. We believe it is unpolished. The watch measures 24mm in diameter by 30mm in length.

This watch does not include papers. It comes with a large Cartier box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It also comes with an additional black strap.

A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia Reference 216.026 in 18k White Gold Full Set

This A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia reference 216.026 is a very sophisticated dress watch. The case is made of 18k white gold. It measures approximately 36mm in diameter and 43mm lug to lug. We believe the case has been lightly polished in the past. Prices have risen in such models over recent years, and this example is a perfect entry into the brand.

The watch dates to 2015. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 3423 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Patek Philippe Reference 3423 is an exceptional timepiece. These are among my favorite references with “officer” lugs, and wear great on the wrist. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter by 43mm lug to lug. They have a very cool case with stepped bezels. The cases are made by Baumgartner. It is powered by the calibre 27SC.

It does not come with box/papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Root Beer GMT-Master Reference 16753 w/ Box Unpolished

This Rolex “Root Beer” GMT-Master reference 16753 is an awesome model which has become more appreciated by collectors in recent years. This example dates to circa 1987-1988 with a R serial. The case is unpolished. It is in remarkable condition and practically new. The bezel insert colors are very special and extremely attractive. This might be my favorite Root Beer I’ve ever offered, and the next owner will be very lucky.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

It comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from the original owner.

Cartier Octagonal Santos in 18K Yellow w/ Diamonds Dial

Cartier is having a moment and models like this Octagonal Santos are super interesting and different. The watch features beautiful diamonds on the dial, bracelet and crown. This example is made of 18k yellow gold. The case measures 32mm in diameter (38mm lug to lug). The case appears to have been lightly polished. It has an automatic movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3411 in Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 3411 is a rare and super cool dress watch,. It dates to the late 1950s - early 1960s. The 18K yellow gold case wears very nice on the wrist. The case measures 34.5mm in diameter (41mm lug to lug). We believe the case is unpolished. The case was made by Wenger. It is powered by the calibre 27SC.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Diamond Dial White Gold w/ Integrated Bracelet Unpolished

This Audemars Piguet is incredibly sophisticated. It features a beautiful diamond dial and diamond ribbon hands. It dates to the 1970s. The 18K white gold case wears very nice on the wrist. The case measures 34mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. It features an integrated bracelet made by Jean-Pierre Ecoffey.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 is one of the coolest 1675s I’ve come across. It is a beautiful example in honest condition with an unpolished case. The watch has an 3.11M serial, indicating production circa 1972. The bezel has an insanely cool blue and faded red. The watch is 40mm in diameter (46.5mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on a 7836 folded bracelet on correct 280 end links which is correct for this reference. It also comes with a True Dome D38 crystal for those who prefer a non-cyclops look.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was serviced recently by Dr. Greg Petronzi of True Patina. I love the watch and would be thrilled to own it!

Rolex Fat Lady GMT-Master II Reference 16760 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16760 also referred to as the “Fat Lady” is an incredible example. The case is unpolished. The bezel insert is superb. The lume is attractive. It dates to circa 1987. I know it is a high price, but it is the best I have ever seen. For those looking for the best Fat Lady, here is your chance!

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer Reference 1016 Gilt Dial

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989.

This example features a beautiful gilt dial. It features an 1.28M serial dating it to circa 1966. The watch features a TrueDome crystal. The case has been polished in the past. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive.

The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch has just been serviced and is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 2501 Disco Volante in Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 2501 is a super cool and unusual dress watch. The 18K yellow gold case wears very nice on the wrist. The case measures 34mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. .

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Oyster Unicorn w/ Luminous Dial Unpolished

This is an early Rolex Oyster Unicorn model from the 1930s. These are incredibly interesting timepieces that showcase some of the earliest waterproof watches. The watch is in great shape for a nearly 100 year old watch. We believe the sterling silver case is unpolished in the past. It measures 32mm by 36mm lug to lug. The dial is original and it is very rare to see one that has not been horribly reprinted.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron Constantin Les Historiques Chronograph 47101 Full Set Unpolished

This Vacheron Constantin Historiques Chronograph reference 47101 is a fantastic and romantic chronograph. These are among the most interesting and well made VC chronographs. The case is made of 18K yellow gold. We believe it is unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers.

Cartier Pasha Chronograph 18k Unpolished

This Cartier Pasha chronograph is a super cool example. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 38mm by 45mm lug to lug. It has an exhibition case back. Show your friends, and they will worship you!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Military Dial 14790ST w/ Box

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak “Military” dial reference 14790ST is a watch with a cult following. It is a great compact Royal Oak with a very interesting set of hands and radial formatted numerals. The case has been lightly polished, but very attractive. It measures 36mm in diameter, but wears larger as it has a lug to lug measurement of 46mm. The bracelet can fit a wrist a little over 7 inches.

It comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from a great American collector.

LeCoultre Central Calendar Disc in 14K Yellow Gold

This is a very cool and uncommon American-market LeCoultre watch that incorporates the rotating-central disc system also seen on Memovox models. I believe these were released alongside or briefly before the Memovox. Instead of using the alarm, the central rotating disc adjusts after 24 hours of use. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 32mm in diameter by 38mm lug to lug.

It does not come with box/papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Favre Leuba Coin Wristwatch in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

Coin watches are remarkable creations which are both extremely uncommon and very difficult to create. Made from two coins, they are an interesting blend of horology, numismatics, and history. More often than not, these coins would begin as legal tender, then would require a skilled coin maker to dissect two sides, carve an interior within each to house the timepiece, and finally reconstruct the two sides in a hinged fashion. The most sophisticated watchmakers and case makers were involved in creating these timepieces.

The case and dial are both very well preserved.

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

To learn more about vintage coin watches read Charlie Dunne’s authoritative article 'An introduction to coin watches: Where craftsmanship meets currency'!

Jaeger-LeCoultre Master Ultra Thin Perpetual 130 84 2J Full Set

This JLC Master Control ultra-thin perpetual calendar is a very beautiful dress watch. The stainless steel case measures 39.5mm by 45mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers.

Universal Geneve Tiger Eye 14K Integrated Bracelet

This Universal Geneve wristwatch is a vibe! The tiger eye dial is stunning and the case is made from 14K yellow gold. The bracelet is very well made and comfortable. It can fit a wrist up to approximately 7.25 inches. The case measures 28mm in diameter by 34mm in lenght, but wears quite big because of the bracelet.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Championship Steel and Tantalum Ref 56175TT Full Set

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Championship reference 56175TT is a great timepiece. The case is made of both steel and tantalum. Both the case and dial are very well preserved. It measures 34mm in diameter. It is a limited edition of 2,000 examples and dates to the 1990s. It has a battery-powered quartz movement and is quite thin.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers.

Vintage Hamilton Tank in Platinum Unpolished

This Vintage Hamilton Tank wristwatch is a very rare and cool timepiece. It is made of platinum and we believe the case is unpolished. It measures 21mm in diameter by 36mm lug to lug.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Grande Reverso Tribute 1931 Ultra Thin 277.3.62 In White Gold Unpolished Full Set

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso is a super attractive model. It is the classic 1931 Ultra Thin reference 277.3.62. The case is unpolished and made of 18K white gold.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box and papers.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Special Edition Grande Tribute to 1948 Ref 277.8.22 Full Set Unpolished

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Grande Tribute to 1948 is a special edition and fantastic contemporary Reverso. The clean white dial is beautiful. The case measures 27mm in diameter by 46mm in length.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers.

Panerai Luminor Marina PAM 004 OP 6052 Full Set

This is a very collectible and super cool Panerai. This Luminor Marina PAM 00004 features a PVD coated case. It measures 44mm in diameter. The lume is very attractive.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers.

Vulcain Cricket with Non-Luminous Roman Dial Unpolished

This is a spectacular cricket with a beautiful patina to the dial and hands. It features the original or “OG” style case. It measures 34mm in diameter (just under 43mm lug-to-lug). The steel case features non-drilled lugs and the dial has the distinct Roman numeral dial. The crown and pusher are original.

The early crickets had serial numbers on the case backs before they switched to reference numbers on the case backs. The case remains unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 120, and has an exceptional and loud alarm.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

IWC Perpetual Calendar Split Second Chonograph in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This IWC is a superb complicated watch. It is a perpetual calendar with split seconds chronograph. The case is made of 18K yellow gold. We believe it is unpolished. The watch measures 39mm in diameter by 48mm in length. It features very cool mobile hooded lugs

This watch does not include box/papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Jo Siffert Autavia Mk 6 Reference 1163 Unpolished “As New”

This Heuer “Jo Siffert” Autavia reference 1163 is from circa 1969. It is in outstanding condition and “as new”. The case is unpolished. It features the Mark 6 dial. It has tremendously attractive lume. I haven’t seen a nicer example.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 11 made by Heuer-Léonidas), and is 42mm in diameter. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is paired on a Heuer bracelet by Gay Freres with HLD end links.

OMEGA Constellation Quartz Chronometer in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Box

This OMEGA Constellation Quartz Chronometer is an incredibly cool watch. It measures 31mm in diameter by 41mm. We believe the case is unpolished. It is a Star Watch Case Company made in Ludington, MI. Interestingly the bracelets were made in Italy. It is a long bracelet measuring slightly over 7 inches.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Cherry Red Daytona Reference 6239 w/ Extra Bracelet

The Rolex “Cherry Red” Daytona reference 6239 a very special and rare Daytona. These Cherry Red dials predate the Big Red Daytonas by a decade. You find them in circa 1966-1967 and often the red turns to an almost pink color as seen in this example.

The case features a 1.69 million serial number dating it to circa 1967. It has been lightly polished, but is strong.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with an additional Jubilee braclelet 62510H from circa 1980.

Rolex Tapestry Dial Datejust Reference 16220 Unpolished Full Set

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This example has an outstanding Tapestry dial which is beautiful.

The watch dates to 2002 and features a Y serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter.

It features a case back sticker on the back. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does comes with papers and an associated box. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water test of 3 bar.

Vacheron & Constantin Cornes De Vache Eggly & Cie Case in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This is an extremely attractive Vacheron & Constantin dating to the 1950s. The case is very well made and was produced by Eggly & Cie. It features a very cool curved profile which is similar to knife case pocket watches produced a few decades prior. The flame or horn lugs are a very interesting detail and make the watch wear quite large. It also has the thimble crown which is incredibly cool. The case measures 36mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug) and is unpolished. For those looking for an interesting design and dressy timepiece, this is a fantastic option!

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16014 Silver Dial Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This Rolex Datejust reference 16014 is a phenomenal timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug) and is unpolished. The watch recently passed a water resistance test to 5 bar.

The case features a 8.74M serial number dating the watch to circa 1985.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Breitling Navitimer Reference 806 in 18K Yellow Gold

This Breitling Navitimer reference 806 is a super cool vintage chronograph. The dial is extremely attractive. The case is 18k yellow gold. It has been polished in the past, but is attractive. It features female spring bars. It is powered by the Venus calibre 178. These are super rare and very cool to offer!

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.
