https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Submariner Matte Dial Reference 5513 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is incredibly attractive and is one of the nicest examples I have seen. The watch features a 1.62M serial dating to circa 1967. The lume is very attractive and sits slightly above the dial surface. The case is unpolished and It is paired on its original Rolex rivet 7206 bracelet with 80 end links dating to the 4th quarter of 1967. For those looking for a spectacular Sub, this is certainly a great choice!

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

This watch does not come with box and papers. It just had a full service from Dr. Greg Petronzi (perhaps for the first time) and is good to go for years!

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Tropical Dial Reference 145.022-69 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.022-69 is absolutely incredible and I am tempted to keep it for myself. It is one of the nicest and most attractive examples in the world, so who ever purchases it can die happy!

The dial has developed a tropical brown color which is absolutely stunning. The case is unpolished and measures at 42mm with the Hippocampus case back. It is powered by a manual wind movement (an early Caliber 861 made by Lemania) and it features its original crown and crystal. The watch comes from the original owner’s family. It came head only, and I have added a period correct bracelet (clasp code 1039) with the correct 516 end links. The edge of the case features a red paint mark which would indicate the watch leaving the factory, thus showing how minimal the watch has been worn since that day.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does not come with an extract.

Cartier Santos Le Brazil Limited Edition Reference WGSA0034 Like New Full Set

This Cartier Santos Le Brazil is an amazing limited edition model released in 2020. 100 examples were produced. The platinum case measures 43.5mm x 31.4mm and is 7.3mm thick. For those looking for an outstanding contemporary timepiece, this is a great choice.

The watch comes with box and papers.

Girard-Perregaux Casquette 2.0 Like New Full Set

Originally produced from 1976 to 1978, the Girard-Perregaux Casquette left a huge impact for the iconic model. Just recently, GP re-released the Casquette 2.0. It now is cased in ceramic and Grade 5 titanium. The watch is super fun to wear and will certainly be exciting amongst watch collectors young and old.

The watch is in in new condition. It was only worn a handful of times. It comes with box and papers.

Enicar Sherpa Graph Reference 072/001 MK IIb Unpolished

Enicar sports models are among some of the coolest and underrated vintage watches to collect. Their oversized cases are very exciting to wear. They also share history with the renowned water-resistant casemaker Ervin Piquerez (EPSA).

This example is the rare Sherpa Graph reference 072/001. The Enicar Sherpa Graph models are extremely desirable for collectors and very difficult to find.

The very bold case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter (50mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the calibre Valjoux 72. It is paired on an Enicar branded bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Pulsar Pogue Reference Y563-6019 by Seiko Unpolished

Only on Wind Vintage will you find a Rolex Paul Newman alongside a sick Pulsar Seiko “Pogue”! This watch is such an incredible and cool model. It features an unpolished steel case which measures 39mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). The bezel is super attractive and the dial has an intense color. This model dates to circa 1981.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It recently had a new battery put in and is running like a champion.

Zenith El Primero Reference A384 Tropical Dial

The Zenith El Primero reference A386 sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. Powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019, the highly emphasized red chronograph hand glides over the dial in the smoothest manner.

The model’s hands and hour markers feature a pale yellow lume that immediately catches the attention of the wearer. The watch is 38mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 45.5mm) and comes on the Gay Frères steel ladder bracelet with ZKM end links. The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The case has been polished. The dial has taken on a very attractive patina. A model was recently sold at auction for over $50,000.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Rolex Air-King Reference 114200 Pink Hour Markers Unpolished Full Set

This Air-King reference 114200 is a super cool modern Rolex. It features a nice pop of color with the pink luminous hour markers. The case is 34mm in diameter and remains unpolished.

The watch comes with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It was sold in 2012.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 226570 Original Owner Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Explorer II reference 216570 is an awesome example. The case measures 42mm in diameter (and 50.5mm lug-to-lug). This example remains unpolished and was sold in November 2022. It was worn a handful of times.

This watch comes from the original owner and is “full set” with box or papers.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is in phenomenal condition with an 18K yellow gold case that is unpolished. The watch features an T serial number dating to circa 1996. It comes with a case back sticker that was removed recently.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

This watch is available at the Kith Williamsburg location at 25 Kent Ave.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished with Papers

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 1601 is no exception. The watch dates to 1971 and features a 2.84M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It was recently serviced and is water-resistant to 3 bar. It comes does not come with a Rolex box. It does come its papers.

This watch is available at the Kith Williamsburg location at 25 Kent Ave.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1005 in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Brick Bracelet Unpolished

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 1005 is incredibly attractive with oxidation developing on 18K yellow gold case. The watch is paired with a beautiful Rolex brick style bracelet with rich texture. The case measures 34mm in diameter (or 39.5mm lug-to-lug) and is is unpolished. It dates to circa 1978

The watch does not include box or papers. The watch was pressure tested and passed 3 bar. It was recently regulated and is running great.

Rolex Domino's Air-King Reference 14000 Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex-Domino’s connection is perhaps the ultimate high-low collaboration. These watches were not made for purchase by the public, but as a way to reward Domino’s franchise owners who hit their sales targets.

All these watches were originally ordered through Schlanderer and Sons in Ann Arbor, Michigan, where Domino’s has its headquarters. In the past, these watches on the secondhand market were generally the least expensive Rolex a person could purchase as few people wanted a pizza logo like this on their dial. Dealers would buy them, swap out the Domino’s dial, and polish off the case back initials. Over the last year, however, they have gained a massive following. The watch features an engraving on the back with the recipient’s initials above “T.S.M.” for Thomas S. Monaghan, the founder of Domino’s. The engravings were made in Ann Arbor, Michigan.

The watch is unpolished, and measures 34mm in diameter. The U serial number dates to circa 1997 and the dial is original featuring tritium luminous material and “T SWISS MADE T” below 6:00. The watch includes the original guarantee document.

This version features the “tilted logo” that is the last version of Dominos’s Rolex watch with the logo on the dial. One could describe this as the first version of the tilted logo dial as later versions had Super-LumiNova luminous material and signatures below 6:00 that only say “Swiss Made”.

To learn more about Rolex-Domino’s story, watch the video ”Rolex Model NEVER in Stores”.

The watch comes full set. It was bought from a gentleman who purchased it from the original owner. The service history is unknown, but we recently checked it on a timegrapher and it is running very good. It has passed 3 bar water test.

This watch is available at the Kith Williamsburg location at 25 Kent Ave.

Rolex Submariner Reference 124060 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner is certainly one of the most iconic timepieces and for great reason. The Submariner was introduced in 1954 and to this day it remains a staple in the Rolex line. This particular example is the reference 124060. It measures 41mm in diameter.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner. It was purchased in September 2020.

To learn more about the evolution of the Rolex Submariner, see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Winn-Dixie Air-King Reference 14000 10 Year Safe Driver Unpolished

Some of the most interesting Rolex watches are the special “Winn-Dixie” models which were earned by long-term employees at the supermarket franchise. This example is for a driver employee that had an impressive 10-year period in which they avoided any accidents on the job.

The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case is unpolished and dates to circa 1996.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great. It recently passed a water-resistance test.

This watch is available at the Kith Williamsburg location at 25 Kent Ave.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Non Luminous Reference 6332

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual 6332 is a very unique timepiece while being a rare and uncommon reference. The dial has developed a warm patina which looks incredible. This watch is an early post Bubbleback design. The profile of the case is brushed similar to the early Submariner reference 6204.

The case is has been lightly polished, but is strong.The dial features an elongated coronet at 12 o’clock. This watch dates to 1953 and measures 34.5mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of the watch.

OMEGA Speedmaster #SpeedyTuesday Tribute To Alaska Project III Unpolished Full Set

This OMEGA Speedmaster is an iconic watch. This example is the highly collectable #SpeedyTuesday Tribute To Alaska Project II. The watch is a limited edition of 2012. It is powered by the calibre 1861. For those looking for an an outstanding contemporary Speedy, this is a great option!

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 6503 Unpolished

For those that appreciate Oysters of all sizes, this Rolex OP reference 6503 is something to be excited over. The dial has a warm champagne tone, and the cross-hair format is very balanced. The luminous alpha hands add a bit of aggression to an otherwise dainty watch. The bezel is one of our favorites and remains in very good shape. The case is unpolished.

The watch does noIt come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running good.

OMEGA Reference 2792-1SC

Mid-century wristwatches from OMEGA represent some of best value in vintage watches. This reference 2792-1SC dates to circa 1952. It features the respected manual wind calibre 283. The case is lightly polished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It has a beautiful warm colored dial and awesome applied numerals.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1986 and features a 9M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links. It has a pair of nice warm luminous hands.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1986 and features a 9M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Underline Dial Reference 1018 Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer this early Rolex reference 1018. The reference 1018 is a rare and oversized Oyster Perpetual model which measures 36mm (43mm lug-to-lug). This example features an underline dial and alpha hands. Of the reference 1018s, only 3 or so are known with an underline dial making this extremely cool and interesting. The 1018 was a reference that was not mass-produced and as a result of its narrow font has become an exciting OP for collectors who appreciate minimal and balanced dials. Oftentimes, the reference 1018 will show signs of water ingress or feature service dials. This example features a very striking soleil dial. It dates to early 1964. The case is unpolished and the watch is paired on a period correct 6636 stretch rivet bracelet with 75 end links dating to 1964.

To learn more about the Rolex reference 1018, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1018’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch is not water resistant, the service history is not known, but it is running great!

BVLGARI Octo Finissimo Reference 103464 Full Set

The BVLGARI Octo Finissimo has been one of the highly acclaimed watch designs in recent years. It is very distinct with angled edges and impressively thin. The watch wears very big on the wrist which collectors seem to enjoy. This reference 103464 features a very attractive vertically brushed surface. We’ve priced it very well and it appears to be the lowest one in the world, so don’t miss out on this deal!

The watch comes full set. It appears to have been worn minimally.

Rolex Date Reference 15037

Rolex Date Reference 15037

The Rolex Date reference 15037 is a classic gold watch. The 14K yellow-gold case is has been polished in the past, but it is still very nice on the wrist. The 6.7M serial number dates this watch to circa 1981.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and water resistant to 3 bar.

This watch is available at the Kith Williamsburg location at 25 Kent Ave.

OMEGA Reference 121.001-64

This OMEGA is an awesome time-only wristwatch dating to circa 1964. The stainless steel case has a 34mm case diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). The dial has some signs of moisture on the edges (particularly at 3 o’clock. It is powered by the calibre 269 manual movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 214270 Full Set

Rolex Explorer I Reference 214270 Full Set

This Rolex Explorer I reference 214270 is an amazing modern sports watch. The case is 39mm in diameter and remains unpolished. The watch dates to 2015.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

Universal Genève Polerouter Date Reference 204610 Unpolished

Universal Genève Polerouter Date Reference 204610 Unpolished

The Polerouter is an iconic watch designed by Gérald Genta and remains one of the great affordable vintage watches. This Universal Genève Polerouter Date is a super cool reference 204610. The dial has developed a tropical brown.

The case is unpolished and was made by Huguenin Frères. The case measures 34mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the automatic calibre 218-2 microtor movement. The crown is a replacement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great.

Zenith Chronograph Cal. 146 HP Unpolished

This Zenith chronograph is a spectacular example. The case is unpolished and measures 37mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the highly acclaimed calibre 146 HP.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

Cartier Tank Must Reference WSTA0072 Full Set Like New

The Cartier Tank Must is part of the legendary Tank family of watches from the famed jeweler. This reference was released after the large commercial success of the Red, Green and Blue dial Tank Musts from 2020! The quartz movement is highly accurate and according to Cartier the battery only needs to be changed every 6 years! The watch is unpolished and in excellent condition. This current watch has an unworn original strap and tang buckle! It has instead been upgraded to include the new Cartier deployment clasp along with two custom double sided alligator straps from Hughes Handcrafted! For those looking for an elegant grab and go dress watch, it does not get better or more iconic than this black dial Tank Must.

The watch comes from the original owner, and has phenomenal provenance ( a great collector and individual in the watch community). It comes full set with box and papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Spillman Case Reference 145.022 Unpolished

The Speedmaster is, of course, one of the most iconic and important watches ever made. This particular reference 145.022 is a super cool example from circa 1983. The case is unpolished and was made by C.R.S. (Charles-Rodolphe Spillmann).

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can see Eric Wind’s ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

This watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great. At this price, there are few better watches than a Spillman Speedy!

Rolex Sea-Dweller Reference 1665 Full Set

This Rolex Sea-Dweller reference 1665 features a dial with beautiful yellow lume. The hour markers match the hands which have developed patina on the surfaces. The case is strong, and I believe it has been lightly detailed.

The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. The service history is unknown.

Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master II Reference 126710BLRO Full Set Brand New

Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch is in brand new condition from the original owner, and is “full set” with its original box and papers. It was sold in March 2023.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Blancpain Bathyscaphe Unpolished

The Blancpain Bathyscaphe is a very cool and historic model introduced in 1956. This particular example is a very cool model produced in the 60s featuring a glossy dial with luminous hour markers and an Arabic 12. The case is unpolished and measures 34.5mm in diameter (41.5mm lug-to-lug). It wears slightly wider due to the bezel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

Tudor Submariner Reference 79090 Full Set

This Tudor Submariner reference 79090 is an awesome sports watch. This example features the blue dial/bezel. The serial is a B377XXX dating the watch to circa 1991. The case is lightly polished but is in good condition. It measures 38mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes with box and materials. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

https://www.windvintage.com/tudor-submariner-reference-79090-full-set

Rolex Datejust Reference 16220 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16220 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1996 and features a W serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 1996 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Vulcain Cricket with Non-Luminous Roman Numerals

This Vulcain Cricket is among the earliest styles produced just over 100 years after the La-Chaux-de-Fonds manufacturer’s inception. Like the “LBJ” dial Crickets, it features the original or “OG” style case. It measures 34mm in diameter (just under 43mm lug-to-lug). It is an early example with non-drilled lugs and is paired with an 18mm Honey Wind strap made by Nick Gabarro.

The dial is non-luminous and features applied-Arabic numerals. It is a fantastic example of the iconic Cricket.

The case remains unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 120, and has an exceptional voice when the alarm is engaged.

It does not come with box and papers. The watch is running well. It has just recently been serviced!

This watch is available at the Kith Williamsburg location at 25 Kent Ave.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - June 28, 2023

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Paul Newman Tropical Daytona Reference 6239 Unpolished

This is a rare Rolex “Paul Newman” Daytona Reference 6239 with a spectacular white dial with tropical subregisters.

The tropical dial is one of the most attractive tropical dial chronograph models I have ever held. It is just out of this world!

The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on an American made oval link Rolex bracelet made by JB Champion - which adds a bit of heft to the watch on the wrist. The serial number is 2.00M dating to circa 1969. The serial is close to Paul Newman’s former watch and very close to Paul Newman’s own Paul Newman Daytona that sold for $17.8 million USD in 2018.

The watch does not come with box and papers. I purchased it from a coin store in Wisconsin who got it from the original owner’s family last year. As a proud Wisconsinite, I was thrilled such an amazing watch out of Wisconsin. I found the watch on a strap and added the Jubilee bracelet, which is unpolished and very attractive. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

This is a rare opportunity to own one of the nicest Rolex “Paul Newman” Daytonas on the planet. Is it expensive? Yes. A similar tropical Paul Newman Daytona reference 6241 sold a few weeks ago in NYC for over $900,000 and back in 2018 a similar 6239 with extremely close serial number sold for 951,000 CHF in Geneva in more worn condition. These are priced in line with those examples.

Is it the best? In my opinion, yes.

Rolex Pistachio 41MM Oyster Perpetual Reference 134300 Brand New Full Set

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 134300 is an awesome modern watch. Nicknamed “The Pistachio” - this dial is incredible and one of the most exciting 2025 releases from Rolex! People absolutely love it and are losing their minds! It has great proportions at 41mm in diameter. The smooth bezel gives it a slightly more dressy look.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold in 2025. It is in brand new condition

Rolex Day-Date Lapis/Diamond Dial Reference 18238 Full Set w/ Service Card

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week.

This reference 18238 is a very special Day-Date with the rare lapis lazuli dial with diamond hour markers. The 18K yellow gold case measures 36mm in diameter. The case and bracelet has been polished. The watch features an E serial dating to 1990.

The watch comes with box and papers. It comes with a Rolex Service Center card from 2004. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Paul Newman Daytona Reference 6239 Unpolished

This is an incredible Rolex “Paul Newman” Daytona reference 6239 with white dial.

The case and bezel appear to be unpolished and are extremely crisp. It is paired on a Rolex Oyster rivet reference 7205 bracelet with correct and very rare 71 end links dating to 1968. We added the bracelet to the watch, which we found head only on a strap. The serial number is 1.9M dating to circa 1969. The serial is close to Paul Newman’s own Paul Newman Daytona Reference 6239 that sold for $17.8 million USD in 2018.

It has good provenance and is a fantastic example. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

The watch is gorgeous - it is so difficult to find great Paul Newman Daytonas with white dials like this example. Finding an unpolished? I might see one or two maximum come to market each year. I can envision a day in the not-too-distant future where each great Paul Newman Daytona with white dial is $1 million or more and I expect whoever buys this watch will be very happy to own it as time passes.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Vulcain Cricket Reference 303001 Unpolished 5 Patents

This Vulcain Cricket reference 303001 is among the early oversized Cricket models from the 1950s. Similar to the “King Cricket” model, the case dimensions are 38mm in diameter (47mm lug to lug) with a lug-width of 20mm. It is unpolished.

These oversized or “Jumbo” Vulcain Crickets are some of the most interesting and wearable vintage Cricket models, but their interest should be far wider than that. Collectors of oversized vintage time-only and chronograph models, such as vintage Longines, Breitling or Universal Genève collectors, often have not had the chance to see one of these rare models in person, but when they do, it is not unusual to hear an audible gasp or at least eyes widening when they try it on their wrist.

This example is very interesting in that it features the 5 patents on both the case back and movement - which is incredible.

It features the beautifully applied Arabic numerals at 3, 6, 9, and 12 with corresponding applied hour markers.

The watch dates to the early to mid 1950s, and is powered by the calibre 120. As a passionate Cricket collector, I am thrilled to offer this fantastic example to the next lucky owner.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Learn more about these watches by reading Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Jumbo Vulcain Crickets: The References 301001 And 303001’

Heuer Camaro Reference 7743 NT Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive racing, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera. This particular reference 7743 NT represents why the Camaro is a watch that warrants more discussion. The dial has aged beautifully and is a dark brown. The crisp branding is emphasized in white, with highly legible subdials at 6 and 9 o’clock. It is powered by the Valjoux 7730, a formidable bi-register chronograph that has stood the test of time.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master II Black Insert Reference 16710 Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16710 is a very fun watch. This example dates to circa 2000-2001 with a K serial. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well! It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master II 126710BLRO Full Set Unpolished

Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch remains unpolished. It was worn minimally over the years and has a few scratches on the buckle. Outside of that it is in exceptional condition. It was sold in 2021, it was purchased from the original owner, and is “full set” with its original box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

Zenith Chronomaster Original Limited Edition For HODINKEE Full Set Unpolished

I’ve got a dirty little secret - I love this HODINKEE x Zenith collab! Don’t tell anyone! The Zenith Chronomaster Original Limited Edition For HODINKEE is one of the coolest LEs by HODINKEE. Particularly, this dial is very attractive for a Zenith El Primero and I quite love it.

It comes with box and papers and shows very little signs of wear. It comes from the original owner.

TUDOR Monte Carlo Reference 7159/0 Unpolished

The Monte Carlo reference 7159/0 is an awesome and rare chronograph in the TUDOR catalog. The watch is highly admired based on its vibrant orange accents and extremely cool dial design. This example dates to circa 1974 with an 826,XXX serial.

The case appears to be unpolished. The case measures 38mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The watch is paired on a Rolex 7836 folded-rivet bracelet. The bracelet is lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Patek Philippe 5196R-001 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe 5196R-001 is a classic Calatrava model. The rose gold case measures 37mm in diameter. It appears to be unpolished. The watch is paired on a Patek Philippe strap and buckle. It dates to 2016. The watch comes from the original owner.

The watch is “full set” and includes its original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Enicar Sherpa Jumbo Reference 144-59-01 Unpolished

This is a an incredible Enicar Sherpa. The case is 40mm in diameter and stainless steel. It is a super rare model and great on the wrist. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch features an automatic movement. The dial is very interesting - instead of it being a traditional sunburst finish it is radial from the 6 o’clock section. It is also non-luminous. Overall, this is just an outstanding timepiece.

This watch is from circa 1960s, does not include box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Pointed-Crown-Guard GMT-Master Reference 1675 w/ Glossy Dial

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a the quintessential GMT-Master reference 1675 featuring a 1.1M serial number dating to circa 1964-1965. It features one of the best preserved gilt dials on any 1675. It is paired on its original Rolex 7206 bracelet with the correct 80 end links dating to circa 1968.

The watch overall is in fantastic condition. The aluminum bezel insert is extremely attractive. It features the pointed crown guard or “PCG” style case. The case has been very lightly polished and is extremely attractive. It features one of the nicest glossy dials we have ever seen on a GMT.

This is a Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 I would love to personally own and consider a grail example.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Heuer First Execution Big Subs Autavia Reference 2446 Unpolished

The Heuer Autavia is recognized by collectors as one of the great sports chronographs of the 1960s and arguably the greatest Autavia wristwatches ever made were the powerful first execution “Big Subs” models that defined the genre. They were introduced by Heuer in 1962 and continued with this design through 1963, before becoming slightly more refined and less brash examples with the second and third execution models that had smaller registers, and white gold hour markers more similar to the Heuer Carrera that was introduced in 1963 and the Rolex Daytona reference 6239 also introduced in 1963.

Along with many of its contemporaries of the era, it is powered by the Valjoux 72. This example is the first generation case of the Autavia with larger subdials. The dimensions are 38mm (48mm lug-to-lug). The cases are made Huguenin Freres.

The case appears to be unpolished. The watch features a 52,XXX serial, making it among the last run of Autavias made. The hour and minute hand are color matched, which is likely due to lume cracking and loss (and is common for these models). I believe these are the original second execution hands to the watch and they are certainly correct for the serial range.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

This is probably the most attractive early Autavia I have personally held. It took my breath away when I put it on my wrist. I have held examples that have traded for over $150,000 and over $200,000 with the earlier all-lume hands and I love those, but this one really stole my heart. In 2017, I feel like this would have been worth over $150,000 retail. This example is less than a third, but I do feel like the vintage Heuer market is showing new life as more new collectors get interested in watches from motorsport’s golden age and with the new fascination with Formula 1. LVMH is the parent company of TAG Heuer and the huge F1 partnership is giving new attention to TAG Heuer. This is a grail watch and this is a great opportunity for an astute collector to “buy the dip” - otherwise I would certainly be happy keeping this piece in my collection for many years to come!

Rolex Patrizzi Dial Daytona Reference 16520 Unpolished

The adorned “Zenith Daytonas” are certainly among the highly desired neo-vintage Rolex models. Zenthi Daytonas were produced from circa 1988 until 2000. It is powered by an automatic Calibre 4030 made by Zenith. It measures 40mm in diameter (42.5 including crown) and 47mm lug to lug. The outer ring of the registers have developed a very attractive beige color. Rolex register rings on Daytonas with black dials from the early through middle 1990s would often develop some darkening to the rings. This effect is often given the nickname “Patrizzi” after the legendary auctioneer Osvaldo Patrizzi, who reportedly first identified this patina.

It features a W serial and dates to circa 1995. We believe the case is unpolished. The dial is super attractive. The bracelet has a W date code dating to 1995 and is likely original to the watch.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Linen Dial Datejust Reference 16014 Unpolished w/ Hang Tag

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 16014 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1979 and features a 6.3M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. It features a D date code dating to 1979.

The watch is in great condition. We believe the case is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great and keeping excellent time.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 16013 Champagne Dial Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a very rare champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch dates to circa 1987 with a 9.5M serial. The bracelet code is K9 dating to circa 1986.

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It recently passed a water test of 5 bar.

Rolex Air-King Ref 14010 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch features an X serial and dates to circa 1991. The bracelet has an R11 code dating to circa 1993.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Heuer Monaco Reference 1133G Unpolished

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement, the calibre 11. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic.

This is a very attractive reference 1133G with the rare all grey metallic dial. Most are grey with black registers. We believe the case is unpolished. The dial is stunning. The watch dates to the early 1970s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was just serviced and is running well.

Ikepod Silver Hemipode GMT Chronograph w/ Box, COSC, and Instructions Papers Unpolished

This Ikepod Hemipode GMT chronograph is an awesome and rare model with the silver dial and green strap. It is in fantastic condition and is unpolished.

The movement is based on an ETA 7750 and has been chronometer certified in the past.

The watch comes full set with box, instructional papers, and COSC papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Ikepod Grand Date Chronograph Orange Dial w/ Box, and Instructions Papers Unpolished

This Ikepod Grand Date Chronograph is an awesome and rare model with the orange dial. The sub registers are very sharp. It is in fantastic condition and is unpolished.

The watch comes full set with box, and instructional papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Master Control Memovox 144.2.94 In Rose Gold Full Set Unpolished

Along with the Atmos clock and Reversos, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer.

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Master Control Memovox reference 144.2.94 is a super cool model in 18k rose gold. The case measures 36mm in diameter but wears larger. It is paired on a leather strap and gold JLC deployant buckle. The case appears to be unpolished.

To learn more about earlier models, read Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time. We do not know the date of production.

Blancpain Flyback Chronograph 2185F Titanium with Papers & Service Papers

This Blancpain Flyback chronograph 2185F is a fantastic timepiece in titanium. It is paired on an aftermarket strap with the original deployant. It measures 38mm in diameter. It is in fantastic condition and is unpolished.

It does not come with box. The watch comes with booklet papers and service papers from 2025 from Blancpain. It cost $1,470 and is running great!

Panerai Luminor PAM6726 Full Set

This is a very special Panerai Luminor PAM6726. The case is stainless steel. It measures approximately 45mm in diameter. The lume is very attractive.

People are going to respect you when they see you wearing this robust Panerai. They will bow down and worship you. The world is yours when you buy this.

It is paired on an aftermarket strap and buckle. The original strap and buckle are in the box.

It was just serviced and is running well. It comes with box and papers (Teddy bear is not included). It should come with a 👑 because you are going to be the king!

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Daytona Reference 6265

This is a very cool Rolex Daytona reference 6265.. The dial is extremely attractive. This watch is from circa 1973, with a serial number in the 3.46 million range. It is a pre-Sigma dial. The case is polished, but is attractive. The central seconds is replaced. It measures 37mm in diameter. It features service pushers and a TrueDome D 31 crystal.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Blue Dial Datejust Reference 16014 w/ Box Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 16014 is no exception. The “linen” dial is one of my favorite Datejust dials, as it is so attractive and special. The watch dates to 1985 and features a 8.17M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case.

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Submariner Reference 1680

This Rolex Submariner is a very fun sports watch dating to circa 1977 with a 4.21M serial. The case has been polished, but is attractive. For those looking to enjoy a nice Rolex Submariner that does not break the bank, this is a great option. I would personally buy this watch if I didn’t have a Submariner 5512 in my collection!

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5164A-001 Unpolished Full Set

This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5164A is a spectacular complicated sports watch from the leading name in watchmaking. The travel-time complication is powered by the 26-330 S C FUS allowing the owner to simplify a second reference time while away from home. The case is in fantastic condition and remains unpolished.

This watch comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner who purchased the watch in 2023.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 5726/1A-014 Full Set Unpolished

In 1976, Gerald Genta designed the Patek Philippe Nautilus which would go on to becomeone of the most iconic and desirable wristwatches on the planet. Decades later, the Nautilus has taken on many iterations such as this reference 5726/1A-014 incorporating the calendar and mooonphase.

The watch is powered by the calibre 324 QA LU 24H. We believe the case is unpolished and measures 45mm in diameter.

The watch comes full set with box, papers and Certificate of Origin. It dates to 2024.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940G w/ Extract Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. The calendar is in English. The Patek Philippe reference 3940 is one of the ultimate in discreet complications.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm and is made of white gold. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch dates to 1999.

It comes with extract. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It does not come with box.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 69178 Unpolished

We are thrilled to offer this very exceptional Rolex Datejust reference 69178. It is a stunning example and is 18k yellow gold. We believe it to be unpolished. The case is 26mm in diameter and 33mm lug-to-lug. It is an absolutely stunning timepiece. It features an 8.8M serial dating to circa 1985.

The watch does not come with papers. It was just serviced and is running well.

Cartier Baignoire in 18K Gold Unpolished

The Cartier Baignoire is an iconic wristwatch design that has been a staple in the Cartier collection since its introduction in 1912. Baignoire is directly translate to “bathtub” in French which describes its most recognizable feature being the oval shaped case. This watch is not only a symbol of refined taste but offers a perfect blend of craftsmanship, design, and history. The watch dates to the late 1980s. It measures 22mm in diameter by 31mm in length. It is powered by a quartz movement. The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex "Four-Line Zenith” Daytona Reference 16520

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex "Four-Line Zenith” Daytona reference 16520 with a serial dating it to circa 1988 - one of the earliest automatic Daytonas ever made. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. The case has been lightly polished, but it is in very strong condition.

Four-Line Daytonas are extremely rare and simply say “Superlative Chronometer” on the dial and not “Officially Certified” as found on the predecessor and successor versions of the reference 16520. It is surmised that Rolex was finding the amount of text on the dial a bit tedious for the predecessor “porcelain dial” versions of the automatic Daytona. The white dial on this example is mint and beautiful.

This watch has good provenance and was found from the original owner. This watch also features the rare and original 200 bezel. On many of these early examples, the bezel has been swapped for a later 400 bezel during a service.

This watch includes a framed Rolex advertisement featuring the identical “Four-Line Daytona” found by our friend Nick of Ad Patina. It is neat to see this rare “Four-Line” variation in a vintage Rolex ad.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 4030 made by Zenith), and is 40mm in diameter.

This is a rare opportunity to purchase a very special early automatic Daytona.

Vulcain Cricket Reference 305000 Gold Plated

This is a very cool Vulcain Cricket. It measures 38mm in diameter (or 46mm lug-to-lug). It features one of the earliest applied winged V logo on the dial. The case is gold plated. The watch has the same case as the Jumbo references 301001 and 303001.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Moonwatch Alaska Project Full Set Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster “Alaska Project” reference 311.32.42.30.04.001 is one of the coolest iterations of the iconic Speedy. The case measures 42mm in diameter. It is powered by the calibre 1861.

It is a limited edition of 1970 examples. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It was produced in circa 2008.

The comes with box and papers We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 3970ER w/ Box & Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 3970 is part of the legendary family of Patek Philippe perpetual calendar chronographs. It is the successor to the cherished reference 2499 with its origin beginning in 1986. It would be produced over the course of the following two decades and remains one of the finest timepieces within the recent chapters of Patek Philippe’s history.

This example features a beautiful 18K rose-gold case. The case has been lightly polished at some point in the past, but it remains in strong condition. The lug width is 19mm.

The movement is operated through the calibre 27-70 Q (which originates through the Lemania 2310). The pump pushers are incredibly satisfying when engaging the chronograph. This watch dates to 1995 and was sold in October of that year. The watch comes with its original box and an extract issued in 2004. It comes with the gold case back, and the stylus. For those looking for an incredible complicated timepiece from Patek Philippe, the reference 3970 is one of the greatest choices!

Patek Philippe Reference 5146J-010 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5146J-010 is an awesome annual calendar model. The model is now discontinued. This particular example is a beautiful 18K gold watch that remains unpolished. The case measures 39mm in diameter. It features a beautiful grey dial.

The watch comes with box and certificate of origin and additional Patek Philippe paperwork. The watch dates to circa 2011.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut 5167A-001 Unpolished w/ Certificate of Origin

This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5167A-001 is a spectacular sports watch from the leading name in watchmaking. The case is in fantastic condition and remains unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

This watch comes the Certificate of Origin. It does not come with the box. It dates to 2009.

Universal Genève Compax Reference 22276 Retailed by Henrique Pfeffer in Caracas, Venezuela Unpolished

This Universal Genève is a highly attractive reference 22276 Compax model with outstanding lugs. The watch is powered by the calibre 283. The case measures 32.5mm in diameter and is unpolished. The watch features a beautiful dial with inlaid foil numerals. The dial is double signed by Henrique Pfeffer, a retailer based in Caracas, Venezuela.

This watch dates to circa 1943.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It just had a service which cost $2,000 and is running fantastic.

IWC Ceramic Fliegerchronograph Reference 3705 w/ Box and Service Guarantee

The IWC Ceramic “Fliegerchronograph” was produced for only two years (1994-1996). The 39mm case is made of ceramic zirconium oxide. It features an engraved stainless-steel case back. It is powered by an IWC modified Valjoux (ETA 7750).

The watch comes with box and papers.

Cartier Tank Normale Unpolished Box & Papers

The Tank Normale is one of the iconic models within the Cartier Tank family and the original design based on a French tank. It has been particularly special in my career as I was privileged to sell a smaller Tank Normale example that belonged to Jackie Kennedy Onassis during my time as Vice President, Senior Specialist for Christie's.

This particular example is in great condition and dates to circa 1980. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures 23mm wide by 30mm lug to lug. It remains unpolished. The octagonal sapphire cabochon crown features the flattened surface and appears to be original. The Normale has become regained a great deal of attention with its long awaited release. This example is a fantastic deal compared to the new release.

It comes with the box and papers. It comes with an additional strap.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 16570 Unpolished w/ Papers

This Rolex Explorer II reference 16570 is a fantastic sports watch. The case is unpolished. The case measures 40mm and has a case thickness of 12.2 mm. The watch features a U Serial and dates to circa 1997-1998.

The watch comes with papers, the hang tag and an extra link. It does not include a box.

By Charlie Dunne

F.P.Journe Octa Automatique Reserve De Marche Full Set

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. Mr. Journe is one of the most significant watchmakers of the past century and the Chronomètre à Résonance is arguably his most iconic model.

This F.P.Journe Octa Automatique Reserve De Marche in platinum measures 38mm (45mm lug-to-lug). We believe the case remains unpolished.

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history. It will likely need to be serviced at some point in the future. The watch dates to 2008.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 3800/1AJ w/ Extract

This Patek Philippe Nautilus is an outstanding vintage-sports watch in 18K yellow gold and stainless steel. The watch dates to the early 1990s and has been lightly polished. It measures 37mm in diameter. The dial is absolutely gorgeous, and features original lume in the hour markers and hands. It is powered by an automatic movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with an Extract from The Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 6565 in 18K Yellow Gold

This is a super cool and uncommon Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 6565 in 18k yellow gold. The watch features a 412,XXX serial dating to circa 1959. The case measures 34mm in diameter. It has been polished in the past. It features beautiful folded arrow hour markers.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

Ikepod Hemipode For Wallpaper Limited Edition NOS Full Set

This Ikepod Hemipode For Wallpaper is a sick limited edition with a superb dial. It is a rare model and in new old stock condition. The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Heuer Carrera Reference 3647S Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 3647S is a very cool example dating to circa 1965-1966. We believe the case is unpolished with heavy wear. It is powered by the Valjoux calibre 92.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 216570 "Full Set Unpolished"

This Rolex Explorer II reference 216570 is an awesome example. The case measures 43mm in diameter (and 50.5mm lug-to-lug). This example remains unpolished and dates to circa 2020

This watch comes “full set” with box or papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 16808 in 18K Yellow Gold Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 16808 dates to circa 1981. The case has been lightly polished in the past. It features what appears to be the original bezel insert. It is overall an extremely attractive watch.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It has recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

By Charlie Dunne

TUDOR Submariner Reference 94010 Unpolished

This TUDOR Submariner reference 94010 is an insanely attractive example. The case appears to be unpolished. It features an attractive black bezel insert. The watch dates to circa 1979 with a 903,XXX serial. It is paired on a period correct solid link bracelet we added to the watch.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Full Set

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a great reference 1675 featuring a 1.64M serial number, dating to circa 1967. The case has been lightly polished. The lume is very nice and puffy. The aluminum bezel insert is very attractive with one of my favorite red inserts. It is paired on a correct 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 80 end links.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 875 Unpolished on NSA Bracelet

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 875 is one of my favorite Memovox references. The cases are really great with fantastic proportions at 37mm in diameter (or 45.5mm lug-to-lug). This particular example has a beautiful unpolished case. The minute hand has a slightly lighter color. It is possible it has been relumed in the past. It is paired on the NSA bracelet specifically for these models. It features a high-beat calibre 916. For those with an appreciation for alarm watches and Jaeger-LeCoultre this is a phenomenal option.

It does not come with box and papers. An Extract from the Archives can be ordered.

Learn more about this watch in ‘A Collector’s Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 875’ by Charlie Dunne.

Cartier Ballon Bleu Diamonds Rose Gold w/ Box Unpolished

The Cartier Ballon Bleu is one of the most sophisticated timepieces and is incredibly comfortable on the wrist. This example is an 18k rose gold model with diamond bezel. The watch is powered by a quartz movement. We believe the case is unpolished and measures 28mm in diameter.

The watch comes with a box and a receipt. It does not come with papers. It dates to circa 2007 and was purchased at Cellini for approximately $21,600.

TUDOR Submariner 94010 Unpolished

This TUDOR Submariner reference 94010 is an incredibly cool example. The case appears to be unpolished. It features an attractive black bezel insert. It is paired on a black nylon strap. The watch dates to circa 1979 with a 903,XXX serial.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

OMEGA US Navy Seamaster 300 Reference CK 2913-8

This is a really spectacular OMEGA Seamaster 300 reference CK 2913. It is a rare US Navy watch with the movement number engraved on the case back. The case is lightly polished. According to the Extract, the watch dates to August 13, 1959, and was delivered to the US Sixth Fleet and US Military Sea Transportation Service in Europe.

The watch does not come with box and papers, but it does come with an Extract from the Archives.

TUDOR HODINKEE Black Bay 58 Full Set Unworn

The TUDOR Black Bay 58 is one of the coolest modern watches. This particular example is one of only 100 examples produced for HODINKEE’s Friends & Family. I love the model and have it within my personal collection! It is particularly interesting to vintage watches enthusiasts, as the design is very much inspired by the iconic Rolex “Big Crown” Submariner references 6538, 6200, 5510, and the TUDOR Submariner reference 7924. The dials have a similar texture to the matte dials with gold accents inspired by gilt dials. For those who love HODINKEE and appreciate the greatness of the TUDOR Black Bay 58, this is an incredible timepiece to wear in any setting.

I will never forget seeing my first TUDOR Black Bay 58 on the wrist of Jason Fried in Chicago many years ago and I was floored by the watch. I will never forget seeing the special HODINKEE version on the wrist of Brandon Frazin around 2019 and I was also floored by how awesome the watch was. I was honored to receive one shortly thereafter as a gift from Ben Clymer and it is a watch that really is special to me, particularly since I poured so many hours into writing for the site in its formative years and learned so much in the process. I am excited to be able to offer one for sale now and think whoever gets it will be extremely happy to own it. Phillips recently sold one for around $22,000 through its Perpetual program.

The watch is unworn. It comes from a significant and, more importantly, cool individual within the HODINKEE universe (original owner). I absolutely love my HODINKEE Black Bay 58 and believe that the dual-signed examples, like this one, are very special. Charlie Dunne says that the Black Bay 58 is his favorite modern watch. Owning a cool double-signed example from HODINKEE is an epic option for daily wear.

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

TUDOR HODINKEE Black Bay 58 Unpolished w/ Box

The TUDOR Black Bay 58 is one of the coolest modern watches. This particular example is one of only 100 examples produced for HODINKEE’s Friends & Family. I love the model and have it within my personal collection! It is particularly interesting to vintage watches enthusiasts, as the design is very much inspired by the iconic Rolex “Big Crown” Submariner references 6538, 6200, 5510, and the TUDOR Submariner reference 7924. The dials have a similar texture to the matte dials with gold accents inspired by gilt dials. For those who love HODINKEE and appreciate the greatness of the TUDOR Black Bay 58, this is an incredible timepiece to wear in any setting.

I will never forget seeing my first TUDOR Black Bay 58 on the wrist of Jason Fried in Chicago many years ago and I was floored by the watch. I will never forget seeing the special HODINKEE version on the wrist of Brandon Frazin around 2019 and I was also floored by how awesome the watch was. I was honored to receive one shortly thereafter as a gift from Ben Clymer and it is a watch that really is special to me, particularly since I poured so many hours into writing for the site in its formative years and learned so much in the process. I am excited to be able to offer one for sale now and think whoever gets it will be extremely happy to own it. Phillips recently sold one for around $22,000 through its Perpetual program.

The watch has some wear, but is unpolished. It comes from a significant and, more importantly, cool individual within the HODINKEE universe (original owner). I absolutely love my HODINKEE Black Bay 58 and believe that the dual-signed examples, like this one, are very special. Charlie Dunne says that the Black Bay 58 is his favorite modern watch. Owning a cool double-signed example from HODINKEE is an epic option for daily wear.

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers.

Audemars Piguet Blue Dial Royal Oak Reference 15500ST.OO.1220ST.01 Full Set Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the most iconic and sought after sports watches on the planet. This reference 15500ST.OO.1220ST.01 is in fantastic condition. It features a blue gradient dial. This watch is unpolished. It comes from the original owner who purchased it in 2024. The case measures 41mm in diameter and is made of stainless steel. For those looking for an incredible and sought after AP Royal Oak, this is a tremendous choice!

This watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5726/1A-014 Blue Dial Brand New Full Set

In 1976, Gerald Genta designed the Patek Philippe Nautilus which would go on to becomeone of the most iconic and desirable wristwatches on the planet. Decades later, the Nautilus has taken on many iterations such as this reference 5726/1A-014 incorporating the calendar and mooonphase. The Nautilus 5726/1A-014 is one of the most attractive models on the market with the classic blue dial.

The watch is brand new. It measures 45mm in diameter. The watch is powered by the calibre 324 QA LU 24H. For those looking for an incredible and sophisticated sports watch, this is one of the best choices.

The watch comes full set with box, papers and Certificate of Origin. It dates to 2025.

Hublot Classic Fusion Titanium Limited Edition For Hodinkee Not For Resale Full Set

The Hublot Classic Fusion Titanium Limited Edition For Hodinkee was released in 2023 and was the talk of the town. Some have cited this watch to be their favorite Hodinkee LE of all time, and it is certainly up there! The case is unpolished and the diameter is 38mm. It is made of titanium. The strap and deployant are incredibly comfortable on the wrist.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner.

For those who have been dying to sing along with the lyrics “New Watch Alert - Hublot!” this is the best watch for that special occasion!

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 16013 Unpolished w/ Champagne Dial

This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a very rare champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1987 with a 9.8M serial.

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It recently passed a water test of 3 bar.

Rolex Datejust Ref 16014 Unpolished w/ Silver Dial

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This Datejust features a silver “soleil” dial that is beautiful. This watch dates back to circa 1987 with an R2 serial and measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Patek Philippe World Time Reference 5130P-001 Full Set

This Patek Philippe reference 5130P-001 is a classic world time Calatrava. This platinum example is a fantastic complicated wristwatch. The case measures 42mm in diameter (49mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the calibre 240HU. It has been lightly polished.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was sold in 2007.

Rolex Silver Dial Air-King Reference 5500 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 5500 is an amazing watch. The steel case is 34mm. It appears to be unpolished. The watch features an L serial dating to circa 1989.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16700 w/ Papers Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16700 is a very attractive example. It dates to circa 1999 with an A serial. The case appears to be unpolished. It features an attractive bezel insert with a faded red that has turned pink.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the earlier reference ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch comes with papers. It does not come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Grey Dial Date Reference 1500

This is a super cool Rolex Date reference 15000. The watch is 34mm wide. The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The case is a 2.3M serial dating to circa 1970. The case has been polished. The dial has some crazing and scratches. t is paired on a later solid link Rolex bracelet from circa 1993.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Universal Genève Polerouter Sub 869120/02 Unpolished

This Universal Genève is a super cool Polerouter Sub 869120/02. The watch is powered by the calibre 1-69. The case measures 40mm in diameter by 48mm in length. The dial design and hand set make for a very unique sports watch. The case appears to be unpolished. There is a small hairline crack in the bezel which is often seen in these. Both the crystal and oversized crown are original and UG signed.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Breguet Classique Moonphase 3337 W/ Box & Winding Box

Is this one of the most beautiful Breguet wristwatches of all time? It very well could be! These dials have some of the most sophisticated decoration and interesting apertures for the day/date. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The bracelet is extremely comfortable and can fit a wrist up to 7.1 inches. It is powered by the automatic movement and is incredibly decorated. If you are fascinated by elegant dress watches, this is a great option and provides a lot of value relative to the price!

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time. It comes from the original owner who paid $33,000 for the bracelet alone new from Breguet! It comes with a box and winding box. Unfortunately, the original papers are misplaced.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished w/ Hang Tag

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1983 and features a 7.4M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links. The case appears to be unpolished.

It features a very nice case back engraving “Love Mom Alan B. 12-83”.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Rolex Black Dial Air-King Reference 14010M Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished and dates to circa 2000-2001.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Blue Dial Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this blue-dial reference 16030 is incredibly beautiful.

The watch dates to 1984 and features a 8.19M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

It is one of the nicest Datejusts I have seen in a long time. My crystal ball tells me that someone is going to try to purchase this watch only to be devastated that it was reserved by another collector before them. Move quickly, because Charlie Dunne is also considering buying and that will certainly ruin the hobby!

The case appears to be unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Vulcain Cricket Reference S2326B Unpolished

This is a super cool Vulcain Cricket. The massive case measures 41mm lug-to-lug, with a diameter of 38mm (excluding the crown). These models will feature the calibre MSR S2, a manual wind alarm calibre that operates in the same manner as the calibre 120. It features a vertically brushed silver dial.

To learn more about these awesome watches, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Undervalued: Oversized Vulcain Crickets’!

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Transitional Reference 16800 w/ Box & Extra Insert

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This reference 16800 is extremely cool and has a transitional dial with the non-metal surrounds. The lume is very attractive. It is 40mm in diameter. This example features a 8.32M serial number dating to circa 1984. The case has been polished. It comes with an extra bezel insert. The grey insert currently on the watch appears to have been intentionally faded.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

It does not come with papers. The watch does come with a box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Silver Datejust Reference 162200 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 2000-2001 and features a K serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555 end links.

It features a DT stamped clasp dating to circa 2002 and is likely original to the watch.

The case appears to be unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Rolex Silver Dial Air-King Reference 14000 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is a superb example. The watch features a T serial number and dates to circa 1996. The case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire. The bracelet features a Z4 clasp dating to circa 1997 and is likely original to the watch.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

TUDOR Marine National Submariner 7016/0 w/ Decommission Papers

This TUDOR Submariner reference 7016/0 is an interesting watch which was issued to the Marine Nationale. These MN TUDORs are highly admired and the most desired of the Marine Nationale watches. The watch dates to circa 1974 with a 623,XXX serial. The dial has been reprinted and relumed. The hands have been relumed. The case has been polished. The collector it comes from knew the original owner in the French Special Forces. This is how the watch was purchased from the son of the original owner after his passing. As common with many military issued watches, they are not always perfect. Regardless, the watch has a military history and is quite interesting.

It does not come with box and papers. It does come with the decommission papers dating to 1999. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Black Dial Datejust Reference 16200 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this black-dial reference 16200 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1993 and features a S serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter.

It features a T clasp dating to circa circa 1995 and is possibly original to the watch.

The case appears to be unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

Breitling Chronomat Fred Force 10 Dinard Jersey Dinard Winner Edition

This is great Breitling Chronomat model that commemorates a catamaran race from the island of Dinard to Jersey and then back to Dinard in association with the French Retailer Fred. The watch dates to circa 1993. The stainless steel case has been polished.

It comes from the original owner who was the winner piece at the Fred Force 10 Dinard Jersey Dinard long distance catamaran race. It is one of 6 examples with the blue logo.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping good time. It does not come with box and papers.
