https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Ref 783 “Montres Golf” Retailed By Gübelin Designed by Gilbert Albert w/ Pouch & Extract Unpolished

Easily one the most important visionaries to work with Patek Philippe, Gilbert Albert is renowned for his shaped watches and design-focused approach. His tenure at the manufacturer represents brought about the most imaginative chapters within the company’s history. While most associate his works with the asymmetrical wristwatch cases, this reference 783 is one of the many undiscussed relics from the Albert portfolio.

The watch is uniquely stylish and it is quite apparent when looking at certain elements, such as the segmented dial, that it was created by the designer. The watch is powered by the calibre 12-400, and the case measures 39mm in diameter and 8mm in height (including the crystal). In addition, it is a double-signed dial sold by the Lucerne-based retailer Gübelin. The case back features the Poinçon de Maître key numbered 2 indicating F. Baumgartner SA, a case specialist who produced the cases many water-resistant Calatrava wristwatches, including the legendary reference 2526. While many collectors prioritize wristwatches, this pocket watch is a treasure and an absolutely mesmerizing creation from one of the watch industry’s most talented minds.

This particular model is from the “Montres Golf” collection of three different models depicted in the advertisement (reference 782, reference 783, and reference 799) with integrated chains made for golfers to have in their pocket when playing golf. These golf watches became somewhat in vogue in the mid-20th century as golf became more popular and it became more widely-known that the impact of a swing could damage a wristwatch’s movement. The integrated chain allowed the golfer to clip it to their pocket for quick access to the time when on the course and also to ensure that it would not accidentally get lost. The “Montres Golf” watches also frequently came with a brown leather pouch, sometimes embossed with PATEK PHILIPPE in gold, but in the case of this watch embossed with GÜBELIN in gold. This reference 783 has an ornate wreath-like pattern on the chain and edge of the case that makes it particularly appealing.

This watch came from the grandson of the original owner, who was a diplomat for Iran in the 1950s and 1960s, before the Shah was deposed, and likely was a gift to him by another diplomat. To the best of our knowledge, the watch had not been opened since leaving Patek Philippe before our watchmaker opened it.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers. It comes with Extract from the Archives. It comes with a Gübelin signed pouch.

Richard Mille RM 002-V2 Tourbillon in 18K Rose Gold w/ Certificate of Authenticity & Box

Richard Mille is renowned as the ultra luxury sports watch manufacturer founded in 1999. The manufacturer is one of the most innovative manufacturers known for their use of lightweight and durable case materials most notably carbon TPT and titanium. Their distinct design language utilizes the tonneau case design with open work movements. The Richard Mille RM 002 is one of the earliest watch models from the manufacturer. This RM 002-V2 is an incredible example in 18k rose gold. It features a manual-wind tourbillon. The case measures 38mm in diameter by 45mm in length. These watches are incredibly innovative in that the crown’s button allows the wearer to operate the watch through different gears W (Winding), N (Neutral), and H (Hands/Time Setting).

The RM 002-V2 is one of the best values in Richard Mille in that these trade significantly below the debut Richard Mille RM 001 which have sold as high as $2.2M at auction despite being essentially the same watch with the RM 001’s movement being made in collaboration with APRP (Audemars Piguet Renaud & Papi).

The watch was serviced in December 2025 by Richard Mille for approximately $15,000 and is running well. It. It does come with a Certificate of Authenticity from Richard Mille and the RM box. It is the best price in the world.

F.P.Journe Octa Automatique Lune Havana 40mm Full Set As New

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. Mr. Journe is one of the most significant watchmakers of the past century and we love him!

The Octa Automatique Lune Havana edition was introduced in circa 2016 and produced until 2018. This F.P.Journe Octa Automatique Lune Havana in 18k rose gold measures approximately 40mm in diameter. The hands are a very beautiful ivory color steel. The moon disc is incredibly beautiful. The watch is in as new condition. The watch is powered by the calibre 1300.3. It features a 5-day power reserve.

This watch comes with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch dates to 2020. For those looking for an outstanding F.P. Journe, this Lune is a special timepiece.

F.P.Journe Classique Chronometre Souverain 40mm White Dial in 18K RG Unpolished Full Set

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. Mr. Journe is one of the most significant watchmakers of the past century and this Chronomètre Souverain is a very special timepiece from the catalog.

This is a beautiful example dating to 2020. It features the stunning “smooth” silver dial with radial Breguet numerals. The case is made of rose gold. It measures 40mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. It features some wear near 4 o'clock. It is paired on a green F.P.Journe strap. It is powered by the calibre 1304.

It comes full set with box and papers from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5968G-010 in 18K WG Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe Aquanaut is one of the great contemporary sports watches. This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5968G-001 is a modern classic with the green dial and strap. The strap is very long, so it will likely require the next owner to cut it down. At 42.2mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions. The case is white gold. The case is unpolished. It has some wear. The watch is powered by the CH 28-520 C and has a flyback chronograph.

It comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was purchased in 2025.

Gronefeld 1969 DeltaWorks in Stainless Steel Full Set Brand New

This Gronefeld 1969 DeltaWorks is a great ‘sports utility’ watch in stainless from the Dutch independent watchmakers. The watch is inspired by the Dutch Delta Works and designed to cope with the perils of water ingress and shocks while still upholding the elevated standards of horology and traditional craftsmanship. The case measures 45mm in diameter and is 12.5mm thick. The watch features an exhibition caseback displaying the calibre G-06 movement. This example features the ‘Khaki’ dial. The watch is in great shape and appears to be unpolished.

This watch comes with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch dates to 2026.

Heuer Carrera 1153 Blue/Grey Dial on Gay Frères Bracelet Unpolished

The Heuer Carrera reference 1153N is an awesome chronograph. It features the calibre 11 movement. It dates to circa 1969 The case appears to be unpolished. The dial is a grey tachymeter dial that has a slightly blue appearance. These are some of the most attractive dials in vintage Carreras. It is paired on a Gay Frères bracelet for Heuer.

This watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running great.

Tudor No Date Submariner Ref 7016/0 Unpolished

This Tudor Submariner is a fantastic sports. The watch was made in circa 1970 with a 741,XXX serial. The case appears to be unpolished. The luminous material at 4,7, and 8 is slightly lighter. From our experience with the shield logo Tudor 7016/0s, the hands never really match the dial lume and we are not sure why. Nonetheless, the hands are possibly relumed. It features the original MK 3 bezel insert.

It was tested to be water resistant to 5 bars.

It does not come with box and papers. It is the epitome of the classic Tudor Submariner and is a rare no-date model. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Ladies Oyster Perpetual Ref 76080 Explorer Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual is an awesome ladies model. This example features a great black Explorer dial. It measures 24mm in diameter. The watch features an A serial number dating to circa 1999. It features a X date code on the bracelet, dating to circa 1999 and is likely original to the watch. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Domino's Air-King 14000 Full Set W/ Hangtag Unpolished

The Rolex-Domino’s connection is perhaps the ultimate high-low collaboration. These watches were not made for purchase by the public, but as a way to reward Domino’s franchise owners who hit their sales targets.

All these watches were originally ordered through Schlanderer and Sons in Ann Arbor, Michigan, where Domino’s has its headquarters. In the past, these watches on the secondhand market were generally the least expensive Rolex a person could purchase as few people wanted a pizza logo like this on their dial. Dealers would buy them, swap out the Domino’s dial, and polish off the case back initials. Over the last year, however, they have gained a massive following. The watch features an engraving on the back with the recipient’s initials.

You can learn about these watches in our video Collector Conversation: Tom Monaghan - Founder of Domino's Pizza - on the Domino's Rolex.

This version features the “tilted logo” that is the last version of Dominos’s Rolex watch with the logo on the dial. .

The watch features a U^ serial dating to 1997. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box, papers and hangtag. It was originally sold in 1998. We do not know the full service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about Rolex-Domino’s story, watch the video ”Rolex Model NEVER in Stores”.

Universal Geneve Compax with MK1 Nina Rindt Dial

After a near 35 year horological hiatus, Universal Geneva finally made its long awaited return to watchmaking this month, April 2026. With all the attention surrounding the brand, it's hard to ignore the iconic Compax. This is a Universal Geneve Compax with the MK 1 Nina Rindt dial with applied U logo and thinner register hands. The reference and serial are not visible on the case back. The case has been polished. It comes from the family of the original owner.

It has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and measures 36mm in diameter, and approximately 45mm lug-to-lug.

It does not include the original box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Two-Tone Champagne Dial Datejust Ref. 16233 Unpolished

I absolutely love the Rolex Datejust. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This model 16233 has a champagne dial set with 18K gold applied coronet and index markers and sapphire crystal. It features an S5 serial dating to 1993. The bracelet features a T clasp code dating to circa 1995. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Patek Philippe Reference 5035J-001 in 18k YG Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 5035J-001 is a great complicated timepiece with a luminous cream dial and yellow gold case. The watch is powered by the calibre 315 S QA 24H. The 18k yellow gold case measures approximately 37mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug). These watches are a tremendous value for annual calendar Patek Philippe wristwatches. It is paired on a Patek Philippe leather strap and gold buckle. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes with a box and papers. It was originally sold in 2004. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Pocket Watch in 18K YG Unpolished

This Patek Philippe pocket watch is an extremely beautiful timepiece dating to the early 20th Century. The enamel dial - which is absolutely gorgeous. The case measures 47mm in diameter. It appears to be unpolished. The caseback features a beautiful Art Deco engraving of initials. The movement is signed Patek Philippe Shreve, Crump & Low - a prestigious Boston retailer of Patek Philippe.

To learn more about Patek Philippe pocket watches, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Why Your Perspective Should Change About Patek Philippe Pocket Watches’.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. We have ordered an Extract from the Archives.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Ref 3800 w/ Box & Extract

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is one of the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated sports watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today.

This Patek Philippe Nautilus is a very cool reference 3800/1 in stainless steel. The watch dates to 1990 and was sold the same year.

The case has been polished. It measures 37mm in diameter (39mm in length). The dial is very attractive. It is powered by an automatic calibre 335 S C. It will fit a wrist up to 7 inches.

The watch comes with box and an Extract from the Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well

Rolex Frog Foot Mk 1 Dial Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example features an 1.73M serial dating it to circa 1967. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It features the amazing Mark 1 frog foot dial. It features “fat” style lume. It is paired on a Rolex 7026 Rolex bracelet with 58 end links dating to 1966. The bracelet has been lightly polished and was added later. For those looking for an awesome matte-dial Explorer, look no further.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Blue Mosaic Datejust Ref 1600 Unpolished

Have you ever wondered what a Rolex Explorer reference 1016 would looked like with a blue dial? Here is your chance (but instead with a date added and with applied hour markers instead of 3, 6, and 9 luminous numerals)! It is a beautiful fiction, but a dream watch nonetheless.

This Rolex Datejust reference 1600 is a spectacular watch with at the incredible blue mosaic (or shantung) dial. The dial was added later. The watch comes with its original luminous silver dial and handset. The case appears to be unpolished and the watch dates to circa 1971 with a 2.7M serial number. The watch measures 36mm in diameter. It is pared on a Rolex Oyster 7206 bracelet with 58 end links produced in 1969. It is one of the most attractive Datejusts out there. You never find unpolished reference 1600s, so this is a very exciting watch to wear and enjoy.

It does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Green Dial 15510ST.OO.1320ST.04 Full Set Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the most iconic and sought after sports watches on the planet. Designed by Gerald Genta, collectors have become enamored with this important timepiece from AP.

This Audemers Piguet Royal Oak reference 15510ST is an incredible stainless steel sports watch. The case measures 41mm in diameter. The dial is a very special with a beautiful green “Grande Tapisserie” pattern. These models are among the most desired Audemars Piguet models.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was sold in 2025.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - May 15, 2026

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Submariner Reference 5513 w/ Gilt Dial

This Rolex Submariner is a very cool vintage example with gilt dial. It is paired on a C&I stretch Rivet bracelet dating to 1968. It features a 1.3M serial dating to circa 1966. The case and bracelet have been polished. The crown is a service replacement. The luminous plots on the dial appear original. The hands are possibly replaced.

Learn more about the sibling model reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Enicar Mk IV Sherpa Graph Unpolished

The Enicar Sherpa Graph models are extremely desirable for collectors and very difficult to find. The present watch is a fantastic example with the case made by E. Piquerez SA (also called EPSA). The case appears to be unpolished.

It is a “Mark IV” ” model. It features the original signed Compressor crown with the Saturn logo, while the earliest examples have no logo.

This watch does not include box or papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and is 40mm in diameter. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Mark 2 "Exotic Racing Dial” Reference 145.014 Unpolished

This OMEGA Speedmaster Mark 2 reference 145.014 is a phenomenal watch with an exotic-racing dial. The case is in great condition and has never been polished. It is paired on the rare 1159 OMEGA bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!

OMEGA Speedmaster Blue Metallic Dial Reference 145.012-67

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era.

This is an incredible and rare Speedmaster Professional reference 145.012-67. The watch dates to circa 1967. It features a very rare blue metallic dial which is very uncommon and special. These are very stunning! It is powered by the iconic calibre 321, which was recently re-released and garnered great reception and excitement from a large portion of watch collectors. The crown has been replaced.

The case has been lightly polished. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 19mm.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The Omega Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box and papers or extract. It was recently serviced and is running great!

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional 145.012-67

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding Omega Speedmaster reference 145.012-67 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to the excitement of many Speedy lovers.

The case was made by Huguenin Frères and appears to be lightly polished.

The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The Omega Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Air-King Reference 5500 Gilt Dial

This Rolex Air-King reference 5500 is very interesting as it features a gilt dial It features gold hour markers and alpha hands. Introduced in the mid-1940s, the Air-King is one of the great timepieces that has stood the test of time. This example has a serial number of 429XXX dating to circa 1946. The case and bracelet have been polished in the past. The watch is powered by the calibre 1530. It is paired on a Rolex Rivet bracelet dating to 1955.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water test of 5 bar.

Rolex Oyster Precision Reference 6426 Unpolished

This Rolex Oyster reference 6426 is an awesome example dating to circa 1981 with a 6.76M serial number. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The Jubilee bracelet it is paired on features an L3 date code from circa 1987.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Habring² ERWIN LAB02 Full Set

This Habring² is a very cool model designed in the Lab of Massena. It is powered by the caliber A11MS. It is a dead beat seconds movement, which we all love and admire. The case measures 38.5mm in diameter! It is unpolished. It. comes from the original owner. It is a limited edition of 50 examples. These sector dials are very attractive and very well made!

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Aventurine Dial Integrated Bracelet In 18k White Gold Unpolished

This Audemars Piguet hexagonal case watch is an amazing timepiece with an aventurine dial and diamond hour markers. The watch is powered by a mechanical movement. The case is made of 18K white gold. It appears to be unpolished. It measures 31mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fit a wrist as big as 6.8 inches.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Seamaster 300 Reference 166.024 Unpolished w/ Extract

This is a very nice OMEGA Seamaster 300 reference 166.024. The case appears to be unpolished. It was produced by Huguenin Frères. It measures 41mm in diameter. The watch is very attractive. The bezel insert has some wear but is in nice condition.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does come with an Extract from The Archives. It was produced June 25, 1969. It is paired on an Omega 1039 bracelet with 516 end links dating to 1969.

Rolex Anheuser-Busch Datejust Reference 1601

The Rolex reference 1601 is a rare variant in the Datejust with the corporate dial for the American brewing company Anheuser-Busch. The dial was reprinted in the United States during the corporate stamping where the chronometer font originally was stamped.

The stainless steel case has been polished. It is paired on a JB Champion oval link two-tone Jubilee bracelet.

The watch has a 2.06 million serial number, indicating production in 1977-1969. The watch is 36mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Reference S2328A Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket reference S2328A is a fantastic and rare example with the rare grey metallic dial with a mirror track. Over the years of collecting, I have only seen three examples - one of which is in Eric’s personal collection which he wore in the Rolex Explorer Reference Points video!

The watch has a blue “RA” stamp on the outside of “Swiss Made” at 6 o’clock. It is powered by the calibre MSR S2. The case is unpolished. It features the very cool “Officer” style lugs, and an angled bezel. The case measures 35mm in diameter (41.5mm lug-to-lug). The alarm is very loud and will surely wake you up when it rings!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Tiffany Movado Time-Only Model Unpolished

This is a very attractive time-only Movado wristwatch retailed by Tiffany & Co. The watch dates to the 1950s. It features luminous Arabic numerals. The watch is powered by a manual wind Movado calibre. The case measures 34mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Zenith El Primero Reference A384 Unpolished

The Zenith El Primero Reference A386 sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. Powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019, this watch is an iconic chronograph.

The lume is very attractive. The watch is 38mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 45.5mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 114270 W/ Box & Papers Unpolished

The Rolex Explorer reference 114270 was introduced in 2000 and remained in production until circa 2010. The watch measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the calibre 3130. This example features a K serial and dates to circa 2000-2001. It was sold in 2001. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points: A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

Universal Genève Tri-Compax "Eric Clapton" Reference 881101/01 Unpolished

This Universal Genève Tri-Compax is a very special example with a rare, non-luminous dial/hands. The case appears to be unpolished and has a 2.3 million serial number. It came from the estate of the original owner.

Eric Clapton wore a similar model when he was part of Cream and the watch now has the moniker “Eric Clapton” in the watch community.

The watch comes with an original UG-signed bracelet made by Gay Frères with the rare correct UW end links. The watch was just recently serviced and is keeping great time.

There is a small scratch above the month aperture and a little chip on the original bezel above 90 on the tachymeter scale.

The watch does not come with its original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - May 16th, 2025

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Universal Genève Film Compax Reference 22522

The Film-Compax is a legendary chronograph made by Universal Genève. There are only a few examples known. It measures 37mm in diameter and is powered by the calibre 287.

The watch was sold at START-STOP-RESET: 88 Epic Stainless Steel Chronographs for CHF 68,750 in 2016. This example is widely regarded by Universal Genève collectors as the finest example known to exist.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Special Dial 10216 in Rose Gold

This is a very cool and uncommon Universal Genève in 18k rose gold. It features an incredible dial with a yellow and white motif It measures 34.5mm in diameter or 40mm lug to lug. The case has been polished in the past. It is powered by an automatic bumper movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Universal Geneve Presidential Seal Dial Monodatic Reference 100101 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This is a very cool and special Universal Genève in 18k yellow gold. The dial is a remarkable black and gold dial likely made by Stern Frères in Switzerland for Universal Genève and features the Great Seal of the United States. The watch was recently found in Ohio and our understanding is that it may have come from the family of an American general. The watch is possibly unique and we have not seen another like it. The fact that the gold case back is engraved Universal Genève in the same style as the reference and serial numbers lends further credence to the fact that the dial was made for this case.

The reference 100101 is the Monodatic model of Universal Genève. The Monodatic was the UG competitor to the Rolex Datejust in that it had a date and was automatic.

It measures 34.5mm in diameter (42mm lug to lug). We believe the case is unpolished. It is powered by an automatic bumper movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

This is truly one of the most eye-catching Universal Genève watches we have seen and would be a great addition to any collection. It has been in my personal collection since I acquired it last year, but I am excited to offer it to our discerning clients.

Universal Genève For Vulcain Cricket Unpolished

This is an extremely rare Universal Genève Vulcain Cricket. Only a handful are known to exist, most of which are in very poor condition. It measures 34mm in diameter (42mm lug to lug). We believe the case is unpolished. It is powered by manual wind calendar alarm movement by Vulcain. It has a roulette calendar. It is paired on a modern Private Eyes bracelet.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut 5065A-001 Unpolished w/ Extra Strap

The Patek Philippe reference 5065A is one of the great contemporary sports watches. At 38mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions that can appeal to both vintage and modern enthusiasts. This example is in fantastic condition. The hands and dial feature tritium lume. We believe the case is unpolished.

It is powered by the calibre 330 SC and is on view through its exhibition caseback. The watch features the black PP rubber (tropical) strap.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Patek Philippe World's Columbian Exposition Chicago (World’s Fair) 1893 Pocket Watch in 18K Gold

This is a very special and important Patek Philippe pocket watch dating to 1983 produced for the World's Columbian Exposition Chicago. This was the first year Patek Philippe became a jury member of the World's Columbian Exposition.

The dial is phenomenal. It features the original glass crystal which has some scuffs. The case measures 51mm in diameter. It features “Wolf’s Teeth” gearing.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not include box and papers.

To learn more about these special timepieces, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘ Undervalued: Why Your Perspective Should Change About Patek Philippe Pocket Watches ’.

Rolex Submariner Reference 126610LN Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most versatile and adorned wristwatches. This particular example was purchased in 2024. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes from the original owner, and comes “full set” with box and papers.

Cartier Santos in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Box and Papers Unpolished

Cartier is having a moment and models like the classic manual wind Santos are very special timepieces. This example is made of 18k yellow gold. The case measures 27mm in diameter (36mm lug to lug). We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Ladies Tank in 18K Yellow Gold

This Cartier Tank is a super cool example in 18k yellow gold. It features what we believe to be a later guilloche service dial which is very attractive. It features the original glass crystal which has some scuffs. The case measures 20.5 in diameter and 28mm lug to lug. We believe the watch dates to the 1980s.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not include box and papers.

Abercrombie & Fitch by Heuer Pre-Carrera Reference 2447 Chronograph Unpolished

This Abercrombie & Fitch reference 2447 is a superb chronograph. The two-tone, silvered dial is stunning and is in great condition with foil-like numerals that are super attractive at different angles. The stainless steel case measures approximately 38mm in diameter. It is in outstanding condition and the case is unpolished. It is one of the most attractive Abercrombie & Fitch or Heuer chronographs we have ever seen. We believe it dates to the late 1950s. The lume appears to be original with triangular markers like seen on late examples of the Seafarer reference 2443 or early examples of the Seafarer reference 2444

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Pearlmaster Reference 81315 in 18k Rose Gold w/ Box Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust Pearlmaster reference 81315 is a very attractive and elegant sports model from Rolex. The case is made of 18k rose gold. We believe the case is unpolished.

We do not know the date of production.

The watch does not come with papers. It comes with a Rolex box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reference 2721 Calendar Model in 18K Yellow Gold on CB Tiffany Bracelet Unpolished

This Jaeger-LeCoultre calendar model is a super cool and uncommon timepiece. We believe it is the reference 2721. These are some of my favorite models from Jaeger-LeCoultre, as they are both incredible complications and wear quite large for the era due to the length of the watch. The case is 18K yellow gold and we believe it is unpolished. The dial is very attractive. It shows signs of age and the calendar track has faded. Finding unrestored dials of these Jaeger-LeCoultre calendar timepieces is very difficult. The hour markers are very special with applied Arabic 3, 6 and 12, and also beautiful pyramid markers which are very special. The watch is paired on a beautiful and period correct 14K yellow gold CB bracelet sold at Tiffany. The crown is a replacement which occurred in a previous service.

In my view, these watches on gold bracelets are a game changer. For those looking for an elegant complicated timepiece from the late 40s - early 50s, this is an outstanding option.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was just serviced and is running well.

Rolex Burlwood Dial Ladies Datejust Reference 6917 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Ladies Datejust reference 6917 is a super cool example. It has an 18k yellow gold case and beautiful “burlwood” dial. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an 6.7M serial number dating to circa 1981. It measures 26mm in diameter.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar,

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 6917 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Ladies Datejust reference 6917 is a super cool example. It has an 18k yellow gold case and beautiful champagne dial. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch features an 7.0M serial number dating to circa 1981. It measures 26mm in diameter. It is paired on a American-made bracelet for Rolex.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar,

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-66

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding OMEGA Speedmaster reference 105.012 and a classic vintage model. It could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line throughout 1963-1966.

The reference 105.012 were first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied OMEGA logo.

It has one of the most attractive dial, hand, and bezel combinations I have seen on any Speedmaster. It features the original flatter 105.012 pushers. The watch is powered by the iconic calibre 321, a calibre which was recently re-released to excitement of Speedmaster lovers.

The case was made by Huguenin Frères, and remains very crisp, showing its true form. The case is lightly polished and has its service crown. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. The reference 105.012 is highly prized and collected because it was the exact same model worn by Neil Armstrong to the Moon.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

LeCoultre Master Mariner in 14K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This is a very attractive US market LeCoultre Master Mariner. The watch dates to the 1960s and has a beautiful dial with interesting hour markers. The non-luminous dial is in spectacular condition. The case is made of 14K yellow gold. It is unpolished and has a sweet coin edge bezel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - May 17, 2024

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 785 Retailed by Beyer

To those looking for incredibly rare and spectacular Patek Philippe timepiece, Wind Vintage is here to make your dreams come true! The Patek Philippe reference 785 is a very rare pocket watch with incredibly ornate details. It features a Florentine bezel accented with a refined rope chain on both the chain and fob. The watch features the segmented dial found in many of the Patek Philippe timepieces by the highly important designer Gilbert Albert, The watch was most likely designed by Albert and dates to the 1960s. It is paired on a pinned rope and is intended to be worn as a brooch. The case is unpolished. The dial is original and unrestored. It is very similar to the reference 799 as one of the Montres Golf watches. According to Tortella and Sons, there are only 5 examples known and this appears to be the 6th example known.

The watch does not come with box and papers. An extract has been ordered from both Patek Philippe and Beyer.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18239 in White Gold w/ Papers Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18239 is a fantastic example originally sold in 1992 in Japan. It features a warm cream colored dial with painted Roman numerals. The white-gold case is in great condition and remains unpolished. The case measures 36mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box, but does come with papers.

Heuer Chronograph Reference 346 Unpolished

This Heuer reference 346 is a spectacular chronograph with great proportions. The case is 37.5mm in diameter and remains unpolished. It is a reference 346 AR, indicating a white dial and silver minute track with painted luminous hours. The hours and hands have a very nice pale yellow patina. The watch is powered by the manual-wind Valjoux 71.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Calatrava Early Reference 96 w/ Breguet Numerals

This Patek Philippe is an an early Calatrava reference 96. The dial has incurred some scratches in its lifetime, and features beautiful Breguet numerals. The spade hour hand appears to have been repaired. Nevertheless it is still a beautiful timepiece from the prestigious Genevan manufacturer and at an attractive price The case is 18K gold and is lightly polished. It measures 32mm in diameter (39mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers, but an Extract from the Archives of Patek Philippe could be ordered for approximately 500 CHF.

Rolex Submariner Reference 124060 Full Set Unworn

Rolex Submariner Reference 124060 Full Set Unworn

The Rolex Submariner is certainly one of the most iconic timepieces and for great reason. The Submariner was introduced in 1954 and to this day it remains a staple in the Rolex line. This particular example is the reference 124060. It measures 41mm in diameter.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner. It is in brand new condition and has not been worn beyond the photograph. It was purchased in March 2023.

To learn more about the evolution of the Rolex Submariner, see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Milgauss Reference 116400 White Dial Full Set NOS

When it comes to the antimagnetic wristwatches of the 1950s-1960s, several manufacturers produced what would go on to become iconic timepieces. Among those are the IWC Ingenieur, the Eberhard Scientigraf, the Omega Railmaster, as well as Patek Philippe’s reference 2570 / 2570-1 and 3417. However, one of the most beloved models of orientated for the physicist or engineer of the day was none other than the Rolex Milgauss.

This Rolex Milgauss reference 116400 features the white dial with orange accents and thunderbolt hand. The watch is in new old stock condition and features the original stickers. It has an M serial dating to circa 2007-2008. It measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Longines Chronometer Pocketwatch

This Longines pocket watch is a phenomenal timepiece. The dial appears to be porcelain and has beautiful painted Breguet numerals. The movement is a chronometer grade calibre 18.89 Z and is on display with an exhibition case back. The case measures 40.5mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. An extract has been ordered.

Rolex Milgauss Reference 116400GV Blue Dial Green Crystal Unpolished

When it comes to the antimagnetic wristwatches of the 1950s-1960s, several manufacturers produced what would go on to become iconic timepieces. Among those are the IWC Ingenieur, the Eberhard Scientigraf, the Omega Railmaster, as well as Patek Philippe’s reference 2570 / 2570-1 and 3417. However, one of the most beloved models of orientated for the physicist or engineer of the day was none other than the Rolex Milgauss.

This Rolex Milgauss reference 116400 features the blue dial with orange accents and thunderbolt hand. It is the model which features the green crystal. The watch is in great condition and is unpolished. It has a “random” serial dating to 2011 or later. It measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). It is keeping great time and is water resistant.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 6007G-001 Full Set Brand New

The Patek Philippe reference 6007G-001 is one of the recent Calatrava models with a touch of flair via the vibrant colors. This example features a homage to vintage examples with radial numerals. The center of the dial incorporates a beautiful crosshatch pattern complementing the strap. The white gold case is 40mm in diameter. The watch is powered by the calibre 26‑330 S C.

The watch is brand new and comes with box and papers. It was purchased in May 2023.

Rolex Gilt Dial Explorer I Reference 1016

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The case is lightly polished but in a very attractive state.

The gilt dial and hands have an attractive and warm patina. This example features a 1.49M serial dating to circa 1966. For context, gilt dials remained until around 1.5M-1.6M serials. It is paired on a Rolex 7206 rivet bracelet with 58 end links. The case back features a cursive engraving “E. Bramlett”.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points: A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA Constellation Reference 198.003 Retailed by Tiffany Co. Unpolished

This OMEGA Constellation reference 198.003 is an amazing timepiece retailed by the prestigious American retailer Tiffany & Co. The watch is in outstanding condition. The stainless steel case is super sharp and measures 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the electronic tuning-fork calibre 1250. The battery was recently changed and it is running great.

The watch does now come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 5575G-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5575G-001 is a highly important “World Time” watch which commemorated the manufacturer’s 175th anniversary. The unpolished white gold case remains unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug). It was limited to 1,300 examples and it features a beautiful celestial dial design. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 240 HU LU. We are proud to offer this watch at a fantastic price.

The watch comes with box and papers

Rolex Datejust Reference 16233 With Rolex Service Papers

This Rolex Datejust is a a great timepiece with a sophisticated Roman numeral dial. The watch features an R serial dating it to circa 1987-1988. The case appears to be lightly polished, but in nice condition It is paired on a 62523 bracelet with 455 B end links. The watch is running strong and keeping good time. It recently passed a water test.

The watch does not come with a box and does come with Rolex service papers.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 124270 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Explorer reference 124270 is a fantastic modern sports watch from the current production with great proportions. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug) and 11.5mm thick. This particular example has a very crisp and unpolished case.

It comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner who purchased the watch in 2021.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced.

Audemars Piguet Automatic Wristwatch Unpolished

This Audemars Piguet is a very elegant timepiece with painted Roman numerals. The watch is powered by the highly important calibre K2120 (a modification from the Jaeger-LeCoultre base movement calibre 920 used by the likes of Patek Philippe and Vacheron & Constantin). The yellow-gold case is in great condition and remains unpolished. The case measures 34mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). The movement features an import code indicating its sale in the United States.

The watch does not come with box or papers.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Reference 26400IO Full Set Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Reference 26400IO.OO.A004CA.01 is a super sporty watch with large dimensions. The case is unpolished and measures 50mm in diameter. It is powered by the automatic chronograph calibre 3126 / 3840. The watch comes full set with box or papers.

Universal Genève Chronometer Reference 10373 1 Unpolished

This Universal Genève reference 10373 1 is a very fun automatic chronometer. The 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. It shows beautiful patina on the surfaces and measures 33.5mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Audemars Piguet Jules Audemars Reference 25859ST Full Set Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Jules Audemars reference 25859ST is an awesome timepiece which is not typically seen. This example dates to circa 2004. The dial is one of the best configurations for an AP outside of the Royal Oak line. The case is unpolished and measures 38.5mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the automatic chronograph calibre 2226/2841.

On top of it being a very cool and uncommon AP, the deployant is awesome. The buckle is perhaps the second coolest I’ve ever seen, just behind a steel Ellipse deployant paired on a steel Patek Philippe Reference 1518!

The watch comes full set with box or papers and was originally sold in Japan which is always super cool.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1A-011 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is perhaps the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated stainless steel watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today. This particular model is a reference 5990/1A-011 dating to 2023.

It features the new and highly coveted blue dial variant. It is powered by the CH 28-520 C FUS. The watch comes from the original owner. The case is unpolished.

The watch comes with the original box and papers, as well as additional links that are original to the watch.

Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer Reference 2444 Unpolished

The Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer is essentially the same watch as the Heuer Mareographe reference 2444 with a bit of retailer sauce! This example has a spectacular warm cream colored dial with excellent blue accents in the registers. The minute register is designated for timing yacht races. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures roughly 35mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1960s.

The crown is likely a replacement.

The watch comes from the family of the original owner. They reside in Charlie Dunne’s hometown - How cool is that?!?!

The watch does not come with box or papers. The watch was recently serviced and it is running well!

Universal Geneve Compax Nina Rindt Reference 885103/02 Unpolished with Box & Blank Papers

A very nice example of the Universal Geneve “Nina Rindt” Compax reference 885103/02. The case appears unpolished. It is pared on a rare Gay Freres bracelet. This watch is from the mid 1960s. It is powered by the manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and is 36mm in diameter.

It comes with box or blank papers (which is common). We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Piaget Polo Reference 15562 C 701 In 18k Unpolished

This Piaget Polo reference 7661 C 701 is an incredible timepiece with a stunning dial. The lapis dial models are incredibly luxurious and rare. The watch is powered by a quartz movement. The case and bracelet is made of 18K yellow and white gold. It appears to be unpolished. The watch measures 32mm in diameter.

The Piaget Polo was the watch for stylish members of high society as well as car dealers in the 1980s. Famous actress and Bond girl Ursula Andress reportedly helped put the watch on the map when she wore it during the World Polo Cup in the 1980s outside Palm Beach in Wellington, Florida. It’s often a watch seen on the wrists of moneyed Palm Beach residents who bought them new in the 1980s or who inherited them from their parents.

Learn more about the exciting designs and movements of vintage Piaget in the article ‘Undervalued: Vintage Piaget Watches’.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Reference 16610 Box & Papers

The Rolex Submariner reference 16610 is a fantastic wristwatch. This example features an N serial and dates to 1991. The case is polished. The watch measures 40mm in diameter.

The bracelet features an R code dating to 1993.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Submariner Ref 1680 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa 1975-1976 with a 3.9M serial. It is a super nice white Sub. The case appears unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is incredibly attractive. The second hand has had the lume patched as it was falling out. It is paired on the original bracelet. The bezel has some scratches, but it remains a very raw and attractive example!

The bracelet is a folded link 9315 Rolex bracelet dating to 1976 with correct 380 end links which is likely original to the watch.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Grande GMT Q302240 in 18K RG Full Set

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Grande GMT Q302240 is an amazing model. It features two dials. The case is made of 18k rose gold. It measures 30mm by 46mm. The case appears to be unpolished. It was sold in 2009.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron Constantin 1921 Historiques Américan Reference 82035 Full Set

This Vacheron Constantin 1921 Historiques Américan is an amazing model. It is a reference 82035. The case is made of 18k rose gold. The case appears to be unpolished. It was sold in 2011. It measures 40mm by 40mm in diameter.

Josh Brolin wore one of these in ‘Wall Street: Money Never Sleeps’ and that is great! We love a good movie with a good watch! Shia wore an IWC. Perhaps Uncle Johann put it on them!

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

VC sells these new for +$40,000, so this is an amazing deal!

Panerai Radiomir California 1936 PAM 249 Full Set

This Panerai Radiomir is an amazing limited edition model. It dates to 2006 and commemorates the original Radiomir! Only 1936 examples were produced! The case is made of steel. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 47mm in diameter.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Tank Cintrée in 18K Gold w/ Service Papers

The Cartier Tank Cintrée is one of my favorite Cartier models. The cases are very well made and among the most beautiful styles from the manufacturer. These models have grown to become highly collectible over recent years. It is a great contrast to many sports Rolex models within a collection and can be worn in both a casual and dressed up state.

This example dates to circa 1978. It features an 18K yellow gold case. It features a service dial. The case appears to be unpolished.

The case measures 20mm in diameter and 36mm in length.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was serviced by Cartier in 2024 and is running well.

To learn more about the spectacular watches from Cartier, read our article My Dream Cartier Collection.

Patek Philippe World Time Reference 5110J-001 w/ Box

This Patek Philippe World Time reference 5110J-001 is a very attractive complicated timepiece. The case is 18k yellow gold. The case measures approximately 36mm in diameter (46mm lug to lug). We do not know the date of production.

We do not know the date of production.

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Ref 1680 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a fantastic sports watch dating to circa 1975 with a 3.93M serial. The case appears to be unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is attractive.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

IWC Rattrapante Chronograph IW3712 in RG Full Set

This IWC Rattrapante Chronograph IW3712 is very cool! It measures 41mm. It features the coolest split seconds chronograph! The case is 18k rose gold, but looks yellow to us! Supposedly it was only in rose!

It appears to be unpolished. It features a beautiful dial!

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

These boxes are messy!

Rolex Blue Datejust Reference 1603

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this blue-dial reference 1603 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1974 and features a 3.33M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet. It has been polished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 1 bar.

OMEGA White Dial Ranchero Reference 2990 w/ Extract

This is a very cool OMEGA Ranchero reference 2990 with white dial. The white dial variants are quite uncommon and collectible. The watch was produced in late 1960 and delivered to Sweden. The case measures 36mm in diameter. It has been polished. The luminous material is very warm. It is paired on a contemporary OMEGA Strap and buckle.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers. It does come with an Extract.

Vulcain Cricket Black Textured Dial Unpolished

This is a rare Vulcain Cricket with black textured dial. It dates to the late 1950s and is one of the coolest models of the early Crickets. The watch is chrome plated and measures 34mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Polerouter Silver Dial Ref 20368-2 Unpolished

This is a very fun Universal Genève Polerouter reference 20368-2. It measures 34mm in diameter. The case appears unpolished. It features a luminous dial. It features an automatic movement. The watch is paired on a vintage Universal Genève strap and buckle which are possibly original to the watch. The strap is bent and may need replacing.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Universal Genève Polerouter Retailed Freccero Reference 10376-1 18k RG Unpolished

This is a very cool Universal Genève Polerouter reference 10376-1 in 18k rose gold. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter (39mm lug to lug). The watch is paired on a JB Champion strap and Universal Genève buckle which are possibly original to the watch. It features an automatic movement. The dial is double signed by Freccero!

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers.

Universal Genève Ladies Polerouter 825603/07 Unpolished

This is the cutest Universal Genève Polerouter on the planet. It is a reference 825603/07. It measures 26mm in diameter (32mm lug to lug). The watch is very attractive with nice luminous plots. It dates to the early 1960s.

And guess what…. It’s unpolished!.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers

Universal Genève Polerouter Black Dial S20217-4

This is a very cool Universal Genève Polerouter reference S20217-4 with black dial. The case has been lightly polished. It measures 35mm in diameter (44mm lug to lug). The watch is paired on a Universal Genève strap and buckle which are possibly original to the watch. It features a bumper automatic movement.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers.

Universal Genève Polerouter Reference 20360-2

This is a super interesting Universal Genève Polerouter reference 20360-2. It measures 35mm in diameter (44mm lug to lug). The case has been lightly polished. It features silver dial.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers

Universal Geneve Polerouter Date Non-Luminous Ref 204503-2 Unpolished

This is a very cool Universal Genève Polerouter Date reference 204503-2 with black non-luminous dial. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 35mm in diameter. The watch is paired on a Universal Genève strap and buckle which are possibly original to the watch.

It features a microrotor automatic movement.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Audemars Piguet Reference 5072ST

The time-only vintage Audemars Piguet watches with VZSS movements based upon a Valjoux ebauche are some of the most beautiful and desirable time-only watches ever made. Among those models, the 5072 is arguably the most desirable because of the large size and water-resistant case with antimagnetic dust cover.

For the reference 5072s, most are in yellow gold (BA) and a few are in rose gold, but only a mythical few are known in steel (ST). In fact, far fewer than 10 are known to the market in steel. This is the third one I have owned and I can say this reference in steel is my all-time favorite time-only Audemars Piguet. The cases were made by Charles-Rodolphe Spillman (C.R.Spillman). Spillman also made cases for Universal Geneve chronographs that are extremely desirable, early Rolex Oyster cases, and later made the Rolex Daytona cases for the 1960s to the 1980s. In addition, CRS made cases for OMEGA Speedmasters in the 1980s and it is regarded as one of the finest case makers in the world.

This example is the only one I have seen with a wider Audemars Piguet font. All the rest I have seen in steel have an extremely tiny Audemars Piguet font.

The case measures 37mm and is fitted to a 19mm strap, a common size for the Spillmann cases. The case has been lightly polished and has just been serviced. The crown has been replaced. We believe it came from the family of the original owner. The hour markers and hands were previously painted for legibility for the original owner, but our watchmaker has expertly removed the paint without harming the integrity of the dial (no other dealer would disclose this). This is truly a special and uncommon opportunity to purchase this grail watch. An unpolished reference 5072ST example has traded for more than double what we are asking for this one!

Heuer Jo Siffert Autavia Mk II Reference 1163 Unpolished

This Heuer “Jo Siffert” Autavia reference 1163 is from circa 1969. It is in outstanding condition. We believe the case is unpolished. It features the Mark 2 Flat V dial.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 11 made by Heuer-Léonidas), and is 42mm in diameter. It was just recently serviced. It is paired on a Heuer buckle.

Rolex Red Submariner Reference 1680 w/ Meters First Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa late 1969 with a 2.15M serial. It is an incredibly attractive Red Sub and has been one I have tracked for a number of years. We believe the case is unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is a superb yellow. It is paired on a 7206 rivet bracelet from a few years earlier (1967). For those looking for an outstanding

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 1655 Unpolished

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

It dates to circa 1984 (8.2 million serial number). It features the automatic calibre 1575 made by Rolex. It comes from the family of the original owner. It is paired on what we believe to be the original Jubilee bracelet. We believe this example is unpolished.

Charlie Dunne loves this watch and is hoping to buy it before you do!

It does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced for the first time.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940P-011 Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. The calendar is in English.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm and is made of platinum. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch has a solid caseback.

It comes with a Patek Philippe strap and a deployant buckle. It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Reference 16618 in 18K Gold w/ Blue/Purple Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 16618 is an outstanding Submariner dating to circa 1991. The 18k yellow fold case is in fantastic condition and is unpolished. The case has developed beautiful patina. We occasionally see blue dial 16618 from 1990-1991 have a purple phenomenon which is extremely exciting to witness on the wrist at different angles.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex White Matte Dial Datejust Reference 1601 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 1601 is no exception. The white “matte” dial is incredibly attractive and uncommon. It is also a “sigma dial” featuring the Greek letters flanking Swiss below 6 o’clock. Rolex only used Sigma dials, indicating the hands and hour markers are made of white gold, in the 1970s. Most white Datejust dials from the 1970s are reprinted.

The watch dates to circa 1973 and features a 3.27M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter.

The watch is in excellent condition. We believe the case is unpolished. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut 5167A-001 Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5167A-001 is a spectacular sports watch from the leading name in watchmaking. The case is in fantastic condition. We believe it is unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

This watch comes with box and papers.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Perpetual Ultra-Thin Reference 4300V/120G-B945 Full Set Unpolished

Vacheron Constantin is 1/3 of The Holy Trinity, and renowned as one of the best complicated watch specialists. This VC Overseas Perpetual Ultra-Thin Reference 4300V/120G-B945 is one of the best models within their current production. The dial is outstanding with a piercing blue. The white gold case is unpolished and measures 41.5mm in diameter.

BEST PRICE ON THE MARKET!

The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold in 2021. It comes with an alligator strap and rubber strap! The bracelet is in excellent condition and has only been worn a few times.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Carolina Bucci Ref. 77350CE.OO.1266CE.02.A Full Set

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the coolest iterations of the iconic model. It was designed by the prestigious Italian jewelry designer Carolina Bucci in 2022. The ceramic case is incredibly light and comfortable. It measures 34mm in diameter. The rainbow sapphire dial is out of this world and one of the most exciting releases from AP in recent years.

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Diver Ref 15710ST.OO.A027CA.01 Full Set

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Diver is a fantastic and tough dive watch. The stainless steel case measures 42mm in diameter and is 14mm thick. The watch comes on a classic Offshore Diver bracelet with additional straps. The dial features the distinct blue “Méga Tapisserie” pattern. For those looking for a great sports watch, look no further.

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Tank Louis in 18K Yellow Gold

This Cartier Tank Louis is a very nice dress model. There are scratches near the left side of the dial. We believe it dates to the 1980s. The 18k yellow gold case has been polished in the past. The case measures 23mm by 30mm.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Tank Chinoise in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This is a very attractive Cartier Tank Chinoise. We believe it dates to the 1980s. The 18k yellow gold and we believe it is unpolished. The case measures 22mm by 30mm. It is powered by a quartz movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced by Cartier for approximately $500.
