By Charlie Dunne

Happy Thanksgiving to all those reading this! We are hoping you are enjoying the holiday with your loved ones. As a small bonus between football and turkey, we have curated an incredible group of timepieces for this week’s What’s Selling Here! These include a beautiful unpolished Cartier Santos or Massif in 18K yellow gold, another remarkable Cartier in 18K yellow gold, a super attractive TUDOR Ranger reference 7995/0, a pair of very cool TAG Heuer limited editions for HODINKEE, a remarkable and unpolished GMT-Master reference 1675, a very attractive PCG Submariner reference 5513 with underline dial,a full set Zenith El Primero Revival, a full set Rolex Daytona reference 116528, a super interesting TRILOBE Nuit Fantastique in steel, an unrivaled deal on an OMEGA Seamaster 300 limited edition for James Bond Spectre, Panerai 1950 3 Days Ceramica GMT Pam00441, a beautiful Rolex GMT-Master II 16713 with serti dial, a very cool Heuer Carrera reference 1553, a Laurent Ferrier Sport Auto Blue, a H. Moser & Cie. Pioneer Centre Seconds Funky Blue limited edition reference 3200-0500 for Govberg, a Jaeger-LeCoultre reference 11012 retailed by Hermès, and much more!

Cartier Santos Or Massif in 18k Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Cartier Santos Or Massif is a beautiful 18K yellow gold timepiece. The case measures 29mm in diameter (41mm lug to lug). The case is unpolished. For those looking for an elegant and amazing gold timepiece, this is a great choice.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TUDOR Ranger Reference 7995/0

This TUDOR Ranger reference 7995/0 is a spectacular vintage watch. I love these rare no-date models with the “smiley” automatic font. It features a beautiful TUDOR “Rose” logo which collectors go crazy for. The case is lightly polished, but it is strong. The watch measures 34mm in diameter. It dates to circa 1968. If you love the Ranger, do yourself a favor and get this sweet example for the holidays…. You deserve it!

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TAG Heuer Skipper HODINKEE Limited Edition Unpolished Full Set

The Skipper is one of the many incredible Heuer watches to feature a touch of vibrant design. Drawing inspiration from the iconic reference 7754, the Skipperara would re-emerge in June 2017 when HODINKEE introduced this limited edition to great fanfare. The watch swiftly sold out and since its release, the watch remains one of the most successful limited editions from both the media company and watchmaker.

The Skipper is a tremendous watch for the summer, capturing a high-spirited look on just about any strap paring. The watch is powered by the TAG Heuer caliber 18, and is on view through the sapphire exhibition caseback. The dimensions are 39mm in diameter, 11mm tall, and the lug-width is 19mm.

The watch comes "full set" with box and papers. It comes from the original owner.

TAG Heuer Carrera 'Dato' Limited Edition For HODINKEE Unpolished Full Set

This TAG Heuer Carrera 'Dato' is a very attractive limited edition release for HODINKEE. The watch is super clean with the black and white dial. It is 39mm in diameter and unpolished. It features the Calibre Heuer 02.

The watch comes "full set" with box and papers. It comes from the original owner.

Rolex Cocktail Watch Unpolished

This is a very special and ornate Rolex. The dial is a rare textured pattern with alpha hands and applied 3,6, and 12 numerals. In a very attractive and unique manner, the coronet is the same size as the numerals. When the dial is capped, the hood resembles a beautiful seashell. The case is unpolished.

It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a very attractive 1675 featuring a 2.9M serial number, dating to circa 1972. It is very similar to my first reference 1675, except even better! The aluminum bezel insert features a petrol blue and faded red. The MK II dial has super attractive lume which has developed into a warm yellow.

I find these early 1970s examples with the faded red and yellow lume to be extremely nice options for those looking to get a lifetime reference 1675!

The case is unpolished and in fantastic condition. It is paired on a Rolex Oyster folded link 7836 bracelet with 380 end links which are correct.

We do not know the service history. This 1675 was just regulated and was tested to be water resistant by the great Alkis Kotsopoulos of Swiss Watch Repair in New York City. It does not come with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel ’.

Rolex Sky-Dweller Reference 326935 in 18K Rose Gold Unpolished Full Set

This Sky-Dweller is a very special watch. The watch is in fantastic condition with very minimal wear. The case measures 42mm in diameter. It has minimal wear.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner. It was purchased in 2014.

Rolex Submariner Reference 16800 Mint Condition

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This reference 16800 features a 9.1M serial number dating to circa 1986. The watch is in very mint condition.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona Reference 116528 Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Daytona is a very cool reference 116528. The watch dates to circa 2005-2006. The watch has been barely worn and is in spectacular condition.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 16570

This Rolex Explorer II reference 16570 is a fantastic sports watch. The case is lightly polished. The case measures 40mm and has a case thickness of 12.2 mm. The watch features a T serial and dates to circa 1996.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Zenith El Primero Revival Unpolished Full Set

The Zenith El Primero Revival is a very attractive timepiece. Particularly, this dial is very attractive for a Zenith El Primero and I quite love it.

It comes with box and papers and shows very little signs of wear. It was sold in 2020.

Rolex Submariner PCG Underline Dial Reference 5513

The Rolex Submariner is an iconic sports watch that is beloved by countless watch enthusiasts. This reference 5513 is a very attractive example dating to circa 1963. The case features the pointed crown guard. It has been polished. It features a very nice gilt dial with the desirable underline dial.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TRILOBE Nuit Fantastique 38mm With Dune Dial In Steel

TRILOBE is a very interesting and unique manufacturer. This Nuit Fantastique features the rare steel case which measures 38mm in diameter. Usually, these are found in white gold. The dial is very beautiful with the “Dune” variant. The case is in excellent condition and is unpolished.

The watch comes from the original owner. It has box and papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 16610 Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 16610 was originally sold at American military base in Europe. It features a service dial and hands and service clasp on bracelet. The case is lightly polished. The watch measures 40mm in diameter. It features an T serial and dates to 1996.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Panerai 1950 3 Days Ceramica GMT Pam00441

This is a fantastic opportunity to own the highly coveted 1950 3 Days Ceramica GMT Pam00441! When you show to your friends this watch, they will bow down and worship you for your extremely good taste.

You will sleep well at night knowing you got a fantastic deal. Best price on the planet!

The watch is in great condition. It does not come with box and papers.

OMEGA James Bond Spectre Seamaster 300 Unpolished Full Set

This OMEGA Seamaster 300 limited edition for James Bond Spectre us a very attractive timepiece. It is a must have for Seamaster enthusiasts or James Bond fans!

You will sleep well at night knowing you got a fantastic deal. Best price on the planet!

The watch is in great condition. It comes with box and papers.

Rolex GMT-Master II Reference 16713 Serti Dial w/ Diamonds and Rubies w/ Box

This Rolex GMT-Master II Reference 16713 is a very beautiful sports watch with the serti dial featuring diamonds and rubies. The watch dates to circa 2001-2002. The case has been lightly polished.

The watch is in very nice condition with a stunning dial. It comes with a box. It does not come with papers.

Heuer Carrera Reference 1553

This Heuer Carrera reference 1553 is such a funky and cool sports chronograph. It dates to the 1970s. The case has been lightly polished. The watch is in very nice condition with a stunning dial.

It does not come with box or papers.

Cartier in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Cartier is a remarkable timepiece in 18K yellow gold. The watch features a radial texture throughout the bezel which looks great when the light hits it. The dial features 3, 6, 9 and 12 applied numerals. It is powered by a manual-wind LeCoultre calibre. It features a beautiful engraving on the case back. 26mm in diameter. 34mm lug-to-lug. It was made in Switzerland by JLC for sale at Cartier New York. These are very uncommon with central seconds

The watch does not with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Quartz Diamond Dial 18K Yellow Gold Reference 6303/789 Unpolished

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is very attractive. The case is made of 18K yellow gold and is unpolished. The dial is very nice with diamond hour markers. The case measures 33mm in diameter.

The watch does not with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

H. Moser & Cie. Pioneer Centre Seconds Funky Blue Limited Edition Reference 3200-0500 Full Set

This H. Moser & Cie. Pioneer Centre Seconds Funky Blue is a super attractive limited edition model for Govberg. The dial is incredibly legible with the signature blue Fumé dial. The case is made of titanium and measures 40mm in diameter. It is a limited edition of 100 examples.

It is currently the best price on the planet!

The watch comes "full set" with box and papers. It comes from the original owner.

Laurent Ferrier Sport Auto Blue LCF040.T1.C1GC5 Full Set

The Laurent Ferrier Sport Auto in blue is one of the most attractive models from the LF catalog. The dial is incredibly legible with alpha hands, and the gradient blue dial. Both the case and bezel are super distinct with very unique DNA. The case is made of Grade 5 titanium and measures 41.5mm in diameter.

It is currently the best price on the planet!

The watch comes "full set" with box and papers. It comes from the original owner.

Zenith Chronomaster Original Limited Edition For HODINKEE Unpolished Full Set

The Zenith Chronomaster Original Limited Edition For HODINKEE is one of the coolest LEs by the Dinkers. Particularly, this dial is very attractive for a Zenith El Primero and I quite love it.

It comes with box and papers and shows very little signs of wear.

Heuer Camaro Reference 7220NT Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive raving, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera.

The reference 7220N is powered by the manual wind calibre Valjoux 72. The N in the reference designates the black dial, and T for Tachymeter. The case measures 37mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It dates to circa 1970.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It can be paired on rare and very attractive Heuer bracelet by GF for an additional charge.

Heuer Carrera Inverse Panda Reference 2447NS

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s. You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

This example is the reference 2447 NS (Noir/Black dial, and Silver/White Registers) “inverse panda” dial. The watch is powered by the manual-wind Valjoux calibre 72. The case lightly polished, but very strong.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It can be paired on really nice Heuer bracelet by GF for an additional amount.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reference 11012 Travel Clock Retailed By Hermès

We absolutely love Jaeger-LeCoultre’s clocks. Their catalog is spectacular and this reference 11012 travel clock is one of the coolest styles from the era. It is particularly special because it was sold by Hermès - a retailer with a wonderful relationship with JLC. The case is gold plated, and features a tortoise shell body. The dial is spectacular and has an asymmetrical date window which is very attractive. It has a manual-wind alarm calendar.

For those that enjoy decorating their desk or office with incredible timepieces, this is a seriously cool option.

This watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Tiffany & Co. Gold Bracelet Watch for Bunny Mellon

This yellow gold Tiffany & Co. ladies watch is a delicate and gorgeous timepiece. The design of the bracelet is extremely stylish and stunning. The gold hour markers and hands compliment the white dial exquisitely. This watch is powered by a C.H. Meylan movement with a case made in 14k gold from Cress Arrow.

The engraving on the case back reads R.L.M. We understand this watch was originally owned by Rachel Lambert Mellon - known as "“Bunny Mellon” - and from a relative of hers. Bunny famously had a collection of Rolex retailed by Tiffany & Co. watches, including a group of Explorer reference 1016s, that Sotheby’s sold when liquidating her estate. These watches are highly priced by collectors and an example is shown in the HODINKEE Reference Points on the Rolex Explorer that we organized.

This watch does not come with box or papers. The bracelet measures 6.5 inches in length.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 in 18K Yellow Gold Full Set

The Daytona reference 6263 in gold with a black dial and black acrylic bezel is one of the most desirable vintage Rolex models. This example features an 18k yellow gold case. It has been polished in the past, but is very attractive. The watch measures approximately 37mm in diameter.

These are among the least common 4-digit Rolex Daytonas, so finding a great example in gold is quite the hunt. The bracelet is a Rolex reference 7205 with 71 end links which are correct. This bracelet would be valued at around $20,000-$25,000 with these end links and are extremely hard to find.

The black acrylic bezel appears to be original and is in great condition while the crown and pushers appear to be the original 18k gold and the pushers are the correct Mark 2 pushers for this serial range.

The watch dates to circa 1979 with a 5.9M serial. It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It features a D21 TrueDome crystal which looks fantastic.

Universal Genève Monodatic Dioramic 100112-1 Eastern Arabic Bezel Pink on Pink Unpolished

This is a very cool and rare Universal Genève Monodatic also known as the Dioramic. It measures 34.5mm in diameter and 41mm lug to lug. It features a pink on pink dial case which are both in outstanding condition and very attractive. We believe the case is unpolished. It features an incredibly cool Eastern-Arabic bezel. It is the only example we know of in this configuration. The pyramid hour markers and alpha hands are also very special. The watch features female spring bars.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

TUDOR Hodinkee Black Bay 58 Full Set Unpolished

The TUDOR Black Bay 58 is one of the coolest modern watches. This particular example is one of the 100 examples produced for HODINKEE’s Friends & Family. I love the model and have it within my personal collection! It is particularly interesting to vintage watches enthusiasts, as the design is very much inspired by the iconic Rolex Big Crown Submariner 6538, 6200, 5510, as well as the TUDOR Submariner 7924. The dials have a similar texture to the matte dials with gold accents inspired by gilt dials. For those who love HODINKEE and appreciate the greatness of the TUDOR Black Bay 58, this is an incredible timepiece to wear in any setting.

The watch has some light wear and scratches, but we believe it is unpolished. It comes from an important (and most importantly cool) individual within the Hodinkee universe.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Jaeger-LeCoultre "Bruce Wayne" Reverso Grande Date 8 Days Unpolished Full Set

Move aside Rolex GMT-Master II, because this is the true “Bruce Wayne” watch. Worn by Hollywood superstar (and perhaps the best Batman) Christian Bale, this watch had a cameo in Batman Begins. To some, it was the star of the movie. Way better than the Breguet La Tradition worn by the always tired Ben Affleck (@watchAbbie hates that watch by the way).

The Jaeger-LeCoultreReverso Grande Date 8 Days reference 240.8.15 measures 46.5mm in length, 30mm in diameter and has a height of 12mm. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Zenith El Primero Ref A386 Unpolished

Introduced in 1969, the Zenith El Primero Reference A386 sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. Powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019, the highly emphasized red chronograph hand glides over the dial in the smoothest manner.

The model’s hands and hour markers feature a pale yellow tritium that is very attractive. Competing with the tri-colored subdials, the attributes of this watch are really a beautiful testament to the sports watches of yesteryear. The chronograph seconds hand appears to be slightly newer, but is correct for the reference.

The watch is 38mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 45.5mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm. We believe the case remains unpolished showing its original finishing and bevels.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box or papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 3423 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Box and Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 3423 is a very cool model that is very nice to wear. They are not overly delicate or dressy watches and can be enjoyed in casual settings. These are among my favorite references with “officer” lugs, and wear great on the wrist. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter by 43mm lug to lug. They have a very cool case with stepped bezels. The cases are made by Baumgartner. It is powered by the calibre 27SC.

These references have several dial configurations, hour markers, and are sometimes seen with retailer dials. This example features a super cool dial variant with segmented lines ( confirmed and described in the Extract as “painted diagonal hour markers”) which can be seen in the “Montres Golf” reference 783 designed by Gilbert Albert. This dial configuration is seen on several of the Gilbert Albert models from Patek Philippe and also seen on some watches from Swiss watch fairs during the early 1960s.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with papers. It does come with a small Patek Philippe box from the period. It does come with an Extract from The Archives citing the date of production in 1960 and sale in July 7th, 1961.

Universal Genève Tri-Compax Reference 22539 Non-Luminous Unpolished

I’m very excited to offer this outstanding Universal Genève Tri-Compax reference 22539. The watch is in fantastic condition and the case is unpolished. It measures 36.5mm in diameter and 44mm lug to lug. This case design is one of my favorite cases from all UG models.

The watch is powered by UG’s column-wheel caliber 287. For those that love vintage Universal Genève timepieces, this one is a really special example. Furthermore, it features a rare non-luminous dial with incredible Art-Deco numerals which has developed a nice warm tone.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Four Line Black Dial Zenith Daytona Reference 16528 in 18K YG Unpolished

This is a super attractive black dial Zenith Daytona in 18k yellow gold. The case features an L serial dating to circa 1989. It features an awesome “Four Line” dial which are seen on the earliest Zenith Daytonas.

Four-Line Daytonas are extremely rare and simply say “Superlative Chronometer” on the dial and not “Officially Certified” as found on the predecessor and successor versions of the reference 16520. It is surmised that Rolex was finding the amount of text on the dial a bit tedious for the predecessor “porcelain dial” versions of the automatic Daytona.

We believe the case is unpolished. It is powered by the automatic movement (Caliber 4030 made by Zenith), and is 40mm in diameter. It features the early bracelet clasp without the flip lock, which is quite interesting.

The watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vulcain Cricket Breguet Dial Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket is a very early waterproof/waterprotected model. It predates the reference S2329A but features nearly the same case. These dials are incredibly cool and feature the rare “waterproof” font with applied Breguet numerals. The case is unpolished. It measures 34mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the Calibre 120’s successor the MSR S2. I have only seen three examples of this variant - this being a fourth.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it running well.

Gallet Clamshell Non-Luminous Chronograph Unpolished

This Gallet Clamshell chronograph is an awesome vintage watch from the 1940s. The cases on these chronographs are among my favorite designs and are the earliest waterprotected chronographs. The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter (42mm lug to lug). It is powered by a Venus 150 movement. it features a very attractive multi-tone dial. The crown is a replacement one which was done in the past.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Tribute to 1931 Unpolished Full Set

The Jaeger-LeCoultre “Tribute to 1931” Reverso models are among many collector’s favorite models of the iconic timepiece. For enthusiasts of JLC or simply those who prefer the Reverso, this is one of the Grail Rervso models. These models harken back to the original sterile or anonymous dials with simply “Reverso” font. This example features the very cool syringe hands. The case is unpolished.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Seiko Pogue Automatic Chronograph Reference 6139-6001 Unpolished

The Seiko reference 6139-6001 is an incredible vintage Seiko known as the “Pogue”. These watches are are some of the most fun and exciting vintage Seiko watches. The dial and bezel combination on this watch is iconic and this example is in fantastic condition. The watch is approximately 40mm. We believe the case is unpolished. It has the original H-link bracelet which is great on the wrist. The watch dates to circa 1970.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Vacheron & Constantin Model 97342 in 18K YG Full Set

This Vacheron & Constantin Model 97342 is a very sophisticated timepiece in 18k yellow gold. The case measures 24mm in diameter and 31mm in length. The case is possibly unpolished. It dates to the late 1960s.

The watch is powered by a manual wind movement. The case back features screws in the back side of the lugs.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Glashutte Original Senator Perpetual Calendar 100-02-25-05-05 18K Rose Gold Full Set

The Glashutte Original Senator Perpetual Calendar wristwatch is a very sophisticated dress watch. The 18k rose gold case measures 42mm in diameter and 48.5mm in length. We believe the watch is unpolished. It is a very nice combination of rose gold and black dial.

The watch is powered by an automatic movement. It features an exhibition case back so you can show your friends the movement. They will worship you.

It comes with box and papers from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Black Gilt Dial Memovox Reference E 855

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 855 is one of my favorite watches of all time. This example dates to circa mid 1960s. The case measures 37mm in diameter and wears excellent. The case has been lightly polished but is very nice. It features the rare black gilt dial.

The watch is powered by the calibre 825, the first automatic alarm wristwatch to incorporate the calendar complication. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement.

Along with the Atmos clocks, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. Perhaps the quintessential Memovox is the reference E 855 from the 1960s. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

To learn more about this model, read Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855.

Benrus Type II Class A Unpolished

The Benrus Type II Class A wristwatch is one of the iconic US Military issued watches and is beginning to develop a cult following over the most recent years. It is powered by the self-winding, Benrus caliber GS1D2 / Modified ETA 2620. The watch features fixed spring bars - the top fixed spring bar was replaced with a standard spring bar. The asymmetrical case remains unpolished (albeit a few scratches from its time in action). It measures at 43mm in diameter and 15mm in thickness. These watches were issued to elite forces like the Navy SEALS, UDT, and other special operations forces. Rarely were these watches not damaged during active duty in the field. It’s in excellent original condition.

It was issued to the US Military as part of the MIL-W-50717 contract. The watch dates to November 1978. It comes from a very good and affable collector.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Ref 69178 Unpolished

We are thrilled to offer this very exceptional Rolex Datejust reference 69178. It is a stunning example and is 18k yellow gold. We believe it to be unpolished. The case is 26mm in diameter and 33mm lug-to-lug. It is an absolutely stunning timepiece. It features an 8.1M serial dating to circa 1984.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera Tropical Reference 2447SNT Unpolished

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s. You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

This example is a very rare reference 2447 SNT (Silver/White dial, Noir/Black Registers, and Tachymeter) dial. The subdials have developed a beautiful tropical color. The watch is powered by the manual-wind Valjoux calibre 72. The crown is a service replacement. The case very strong and we believe it is unpolished. Overall, it is an incredible sports

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Retailed by Tiffany & Co. Reference 4819 w/ Box

We are thrilled to offer this very exceptional Patek Philippe reference 4819 retailed by Tiffany & Co. It is a stunning example and is 18k yellow gold. We believe it to be unpolished. The case is 25mm in diameter. It is a quartz powered watch. It is an absolutely stunning timepiece. .

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 in 18K YG

as originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch is paired on a gold 7206 bracelet with aftermarket end links. The case has been polished in the past. The hands have been relumed and color matched.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE. You can also read about the iconic watch in Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Tiffany & Co Nautilus Reference 5711/1A Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is perhaps the most desired watch on the planet. In addition, it is signed by the most prestigious New York retailer Tiffany & Co. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated stainless steel watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today. The watch is unpolished and in exceptional condition and dates to 2008.

The watch comes with the original box and papers.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Tiffany & Co 5164A-001 Full Set Unpolished

This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5164A is a spectacular complicated sports watch from the leading name in watchmaking. In addition, it is signed by the prestigious New York retailer Tiffany & Co. The travel-time complication is powered by the 324 S C FUS allowing the owner to simplify a second reference time while away from home. The case is in fantastic condition and remains unpolished.

This watch comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner who purchased the watch in 2016.

Rolex Daytona John Mayer Reference 116508 Full Set Unpolished

Nicknamed for the 2nd appearance on HODINKEE Talking Watches, this Rolex Daytona reference 116508 with green anniversary dial would take on the rockstar nickname "John Mayer". Despite being a departure from the Daytona’s utility-based nature, this model has become admired amongst a range of Rolex collectors and has been spotted on many other notable wrists including Jonah Hill’s. It has recently been discontinued. The watch is in fantastic condition and is unpolished. It was worn a couple of times.

The watch comes full set. It was sold in 2022.

Breitling Dive Watch Unpolished

This is a very attractive Breitling dive watch. The dial is super cool with luminous 12, 6 and 9 layout with a date function. The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Benrus Ultra Deep Unpolished

This is a very attractive Benrus Ultra Deep. The dial is super cool with luminous hour markers. The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Breitling Sprint Chronograph Reference 2010 Unpolished

This is a very attractive Breitling Sprint chronograph reference 2010. The case is large and has a bold presence on the wrist. It has a nice pop of color on the dial with red accents. The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 6542 in 18K Yellow Gold

This Rolex GMT-Master Reference 6542 in 18K yellow gold is a very cool and uncommon model. The watch dates to circa 1959 with a 412,XXX serial. The bezel is a red and blue bakelite, which is obviously seen on steel models, whereas the gold models had an all-burgundy Bakelite bezel insert. The watch comes from the family of the original owner and the bezel was presumably or swapped added at their request.

The case has been lightly polished in the past, but looks honest and is not recut like a cyborg. It was just fully serviced and is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

It does not come from with box and papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

In this week’s What’s Selling Here we have a number of amazing timepieces. The star of the show is most certainly the Paul Newman 6241. These Newmans have moved very quickly each time we’ve listed them over the past few months, so reach out fast if you are needing one! I am personally thrilled about the Memovox E 855 - my first vintage watch! It is a spectacular unpolished example with a great deal of patina on the dial and thick branding at 12 o’clock. On top of that, we have a super attractive “Claude Brasseur” Memovox Reference 875. This watch was worn by the actor in a French espionage film ‘il faut vivre dangereusement’. I watched the movie, and cannot tell you a thing about the plot - as I was unable to find a version with subtitles. If anyone wants to fill me in on what I missed, please reach out to me! We also have a beautiful Patek Philippe reference 3940 in yellow gold with box and papers. Eric and I have discussed this reference countless times at the office (along with the reference 3970) and I think these are among of my favorite complicated timepieces by Patek. On the affordable side, we have a super clean Damas that someone needs to pick up swiftly. It has a remarkable dial and super fresh case. It is a bit small, but packs a mighty punch! Two very interesting watches to mention are the OMEGA calibre 33.3 chronographs - both of which are 38mm in diameter. Take a moment to check these out as I don’t anticipate we will have many of these in the future.

Rolex Paul Newman Daytona Reference 6241 with Box

The vintage Rolex Daytonas garnering the nickname "Paul Newman" due to their exotic dials made by Singer with small boxes in the registers are some of the most iconic and desirable vintage watches that exist.

The present watch is one of the most desirable versions, a reference 6241 featuring a white dial with Mark 1 dial with oversized T SWISS T signature below 6:00. It features the original “Mark 1” acrylic bezel. There is a slight crack in bezel by 90.

The watch dates to circa 1968 and features a 1.76M serial number. The dial is in remarkable condition. The case is probably very lightly polished, but is in remarkable condition. The bracelet is probably original to the watch with the highly-desirable 71 end links. It is dated 1969, indicating the watch probably left the Rolex factory in 1969

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J Unpolished with Box & Papers

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold featuring the later series dial. The dial is English and has beautiful serifs for the month and day abbreviati∂ons in the registers.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm. The case is unpolished.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox “Claude Brasseur” Reference 875.42.204 Unpolished

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 875 is one of my favorite Memovox references. The cases are really great with fantastic proportions at 37mm in diameter (or 45.5mm lug-to-lug). The case is unpolished. This example features the blue and white dial variant nicknamed by French Jaeger-LeCoultre collectors “Claude Brasseur” for its cameo on the actor’s wrist in ‘il faut vivre dangereusement’. The dial is in phenomenal condition and is super attractive.

The bottom right lug has a slight dent, but is still very attractive.

It features a high-beat calibre 916. For those with an appreciation for alarm watches and Jaeger-LeCoultre this is a phenomenal option.

It does not come with box and papers. An Extract from the Archives can be ordered.

Learn more about this watch in ‘A Collector’s Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 875’ by Charlie Dunne.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855 Unpolished

Along with the Atmos clocks, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. Perhaps the quintessential Memovox is the reference E 855 from the 1960s. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

This example dates to circa the 1960s. The case is unpolished. The case measures 37mm in diameter and wears excellent. The dial has a beautiful patina, which I find very attractive.

The watch is powered by the calibre 825, the first automatic alarm wristwatch to incorporate the calendar complication. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement.

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Damas Wristwatch Unpolished

This Damas is a super cool wristwatch. The dial is very attractive with a blue outer scale. The case is super crisp and unpolished. The watch is not a chronograph, but instead has continuous running center seconds, which can be stopped using the pusher at 2 o'clock and reset with the pusher at 4 o'clock.

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-6

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This is a super cool reference 2998-6 featuring warm luminous hour markers. The case has been polished and it is paired on a 7912 bracelet with 6 end links. The crown is a later example which has been replaced. The bezel has some wear. The lume is incredibly attractive and is super dreamy!

This watch does not include box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

OMEGA Calibre 33.3 Chronograph 38mm In Steel

This is am incredible and uncommon chronograph dating to the 1940s with an enhanced case. The case measures a massive 38mm in diameter and features a beautiful dial with applied hour markers. It is powered by the legendary OMEGA calibre 33.3. For those looking to add an outstanding timepiece to their collection, this is a great option. The case is lightly polished, but is very nice.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Chronograph 38mm In Steel

This is a very attractive and uncommon chronograph dating to the 1940s with an enhanced case. The case measures a massive 38mm in diameter and features a beautiful multi-scaled silvered dial. It is powered by the legendary OMEGA calibre 33.3. For those looking to add an incredible timepiece to their collection, this is a great option. The case is lightly polished, but is very nice.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Trilogy Enamel Dial 1/9 In 18k White Gold, 18k Rose Gold, And 18k Yellow Gold

This is a rare opportunity to acquire three important timepieces from the OMEGA Speedmaster trilogy. These watches feature the enamel dials and are in 18K cases of white, rose and yellow gold. The cases are unpolished.

The watches do not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but they are running well.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article)

Tissot Visodate Unpolished

This Tissot Visodate is a super cool timepiece. It features a gold capped case in rose. The condition is very nice. It measures 35.5mm in diameter. It appears to be paired on the original strap and buckle.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm. The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Concord Saratoga Royale in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished w/ Papers

This Concord Saratoga Royale is a super cool timepiece. The bracelet is phenomenal. Some may call it blasphemous, but I would argue it is superior to many of the top names’ offerings. The case is 18K yellow gold, It is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter. It has a very nice and high quality bracelet.

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers from 1996. The watch comes from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Milgauss Reference 6541 Unpolished

The Rolex Milgauss reference 6541 is an incredible timepiece produced for scientists and doctors who worked in highly-magnetic environments. Only a few hundred examples of the Milgauss reference 6541 were produced at most and they remain mythical for collectors of rare sport Rolex watches. In 2023, a similar Rolex Milgauss sold at auction for approximately $2.5 Million. These watches were very special featuring honeycomb dials, a lightning seconds hand and hour markers unique for the Milgauss model. Along with the interesting design, the case is very robust on the wrist.

This example dates to circa 1958-1959 with a 412,XXX serial, like all the other reference 6541 examples. We believe the case is unpolished, with some wear. The watch is paired on a Rolex “Big Logo” stretch bracelet with 65 end links dating to the 2nd quarter of 1959, which was added to the watch. The luminous dauphine hour and minute hands have tritium and are possibly replaced. The original non-luminous dial is in beautiful condition. The watch features the original and rare non-luminous bezel insert and rotating bezel. Many Milgauss reference 6541 examples had smooth non-rotating bezels for the American market, but the rotating bezel examples are extremely rare. The case back has an engraved “ROLEX OYSTER CASE” along the edge, which is a very neat and unusual detail. The red MILGAUSS text has faded to a beautiful pink. The watch is fresh to market and came from the nephew of the original owner in Canada.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was recently serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

This Rolex Milgauss reference 6541 is truly a dream and a special opportunity for a connoisseur to get one of the rarest Rolex sports models from the 1950s.

Rolex Prince Royal Pocket Watch Reference 1649 in 14K Rose Gold Unpolished

This Rolex pocket watch is an incredibly cool timepiece. The Prince Royal models were larger chronometer pocket watches. The rose gold case measures 37mm in diameter and 41mm in length. We believe the case is unpolished. The case features the Geneva Key 12 indicating it was produced by Genex SA. The case engraving cites a production date in circa 1944. The interior case features an engraving “To Theodore Marconette from Employees of Hotel Cleveland May 1944”, as well as the initials hand engraved on the case back. It features a“Pink-on-Pink” dial and case with applied Roman and pyramid numerals. This watch is really a special Rolex and a connoisseurs’ choice. I would be thrilled to own something like this, and am excited to offer it for sale!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Pocket Watch Reference 11743 in 18k Rose Gold

This Universal Genève pocket watch is a very special timepiece. The rose gold case measures 45mm in diameter. It has been lightly polished in the past. The case features the Geneva Key 23 indicating it was produced by Eggy & Cie.

It features a“Pink-on-Pink” dial and case. The case shape and bow are identical to the Patek Philippe reference 650 pocket watch also made by Eggly & Cie.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3442/1 In 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Patek Philippe reference 3442/1 is a very cool and uncommon dress watch. It features painted Breguet numerals. It is powered by the calibre 23-300. The case measures 28mm in diameter (34mm lug to lug). The case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J-014 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold. The dial is in English and is strikingly beautiful.. This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case measures 36mm. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The Certificate of Origin cites the date of purchase to August 2001.

TUDOR Submariner Reference 7016/0

The TUDOR Submariner is a great sports watch. The watch features a 0.62M serial dating to circa 1968. The case has been polished in the past, but is attractive. The bracelet is a Rolex C&I stretch Rivet bracelet dating to circa 1968. The dial has nice warm luminous plots. The hands appear to be replaced or relumed at some point. The insert appears to be a few years later. For those looking for an affordably priced vintage TUDOR Submariner, this is a great choice.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Blue Applied Roman Dial Datejust Reference 16030

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This blue-dial reference 16030 is a super cool watch with a rare dial configuration with applied Roman numerals. The watch dates to 1987 and features a R2 serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and has been lightly polished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

TUDOR Ranger Reference 7995

This TUDOR Ranger reference 7995 is a super cool vintage watch. I love these rare no-date models with the “smiley” automatic font. It features a beautiful TUDOR “Rose” logo which collectors go crazy for. The case is lightly polished. The watch measures 34mm in diameter. It dates to circa 1966 with a 0.541M serial. It is paired on a period correct bracelet dating to 1970. The hands are possibly relumed and glow a tad bit longer under UV.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Heuer Super Autavia 2nd Generation Reference 16.8433 Dashtimer

This Heuer dashboard super Autavia is an amazing watch dating to the late 1960s. It features tritium lume and is a the very attractive 2nd generation or execution of the prestigious dashtimer. It is powered by the Valjoux 5. Learn more about these amazing timepieces on Jeff Stein’s On The Dash article!

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running great.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - November 8, 2024

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 5013 in Platinum Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5013 is one of the most impressive complicated timepieces produced. It was introduced in 1992 and was offered until circa 2010. These watches are particularly rare, and among the most technically advanced timepieces by the manufacturer. It is estimated that only 45 were produced in platinum (and 101 accross all metals). The platinum case measures 37mm diameter, 46.5 lug-to-lug, and 12mm in height. It appears to be unpolished. Listening to the repeater complication, these models are among the nicest contemporary minute repeating timepieces we have heard. The model dates to circa 2011.

The watch comes with box and Certificate of Origin. It was recently serviced by Patek Philippe Geneve for over $4,400 in 2022.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-4 Unpolished

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This an absolutely incredible reference 2998-4. It has great luminous hour markers and matching hands. The case is unpolished. It is paired on the original bracelet . The crown is original. It features the original dot over 90 bezel.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of the watch, but it is running well. It came from the family of the original owner in Ohio.

Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 Black Dial Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520 with a black dial. It has a U serial dating it to circa 1997. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988 until circa 2000. This is example features a crisp unpolished case and unpolished bracelet. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch does not include a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona Reference 6263 in 14k Gold

The Daytona reference 6263 in gold with a black dial and black acrylic bezel is one of the most desirable vintage Rolex models, made even more special when it is in the rare and rosy 14k gold for the American market. This watch is in outstanding condition and the gold has developed a bit of oxidation making it even more of a rich rose gold color. The tritium luminous plots are in excellent condition and match the hands perfectly. The black acrylic bezel appears to be original and is in outstanding condition while the crown and pushers appear to be the original 14k gold and the pushers are the correct Mark 2 pushers for this serial range. The bracelet is also a Rolex reference 7205 and made of 14k gold.

The watch measures approximately 37mm in diameter. The serial number is 5.4 million, dating to circa 1978-1979. It does not have the original box and papers, but does have excellent provenance.

This is a grail watch and any collector would be thrilled to own and wear this example.

Cartier Basculante Reference 2390 Unpolished

This Cartier Basculante is a very nice timepiece. It is a reference 2390 with the manual wind calibre. The watch is unpolished. The stainless steel case measures 25.5mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well. It comes with an extra black strap with a Cartier buckle.

Zenith El Primero Reference A385 Unpolished

The Zenith El Primero sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. Powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019, the highly emphasized red chronograph hand glides over the dial in the smoothest manner.

The watch is 37mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 46.5mm). The lug width is 19mm The case remains unpolished showing its original finishing and bevels.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running great.

TAG Heuer Skipper HODINKEE Limited Edition Unpolished Full Set

The Skipper is one of the many incredible Heuer watches to feature a touch of vibrant design. Drawing inspiration from the iconic reference 7754, the Skipperara would re-emerge in June 2017 when HODINKEE introduced this limited edition to great fanfare. The watch swiftly sold out and since its release, the watch remains one of the most successful limited editions from both the media company and watchmaker.

The Skipper is a tremendous watch for the summer, capturing a high-spirited look on just about any strap paring. The watch is powered by the TAG Heuer caliber 18, and is on view through the sapphire exhibition caseback. The dimensions are 39mm in diameter, 11mm tall, and the lug-width is 19mm.

The watch comes "full set" with box and papers.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5165A-001 Unpolished Full Set

This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5165A-001 is a spectacular sports watch and one of the most beloved models from PP. The stainless steel case is in fantastic condition and remains unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

This watch comes full set with the Certificate of Origin and box. The watch was sold in 2010.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16750 Full Set

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16750 is a very attractive example. It dates to circa 1986. The case has been polished, but it is nice. The hour and minute hand appear to have been replaced in a past service. The lume in the dial is very attractive and has a warm patina.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the previous reference ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Sea-Dweller Reference 126600 Unpolished Full Set

This Sea-Dweller reference 126600 is an awesome modern watch. The watch was sold in 2019. The case is 43mm in diameter.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

Rolex Oyster Reference 6082 Unpolished

This Rolex Oyster reference 6082 is an awesome vintage watch which I am very excited to offer. The watch is a manual wind movement. The case measures 34.5mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug) and is unpolished. The crystal appears to be original to the watch. The watch dates to circa 1960. It is paired on a Gay Freres Rivet bracelet.

The dial is by Fabrique de Cadran Stern Frères and features incredible numerals at 12 and 6 o’clock which are the same as those found on the Rolex Precision reference 4583, Rolex “Padellone” reference 8171 and even on some Movado timepieces.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

NAOYA HIDA & Co. TYPE 1D Full Set

NAOYA HIDA is one of the hottest independents in recent years. It plays on the classic designs from early Calatrava timepieces. This example is in wonderful condition. The dial made of German silver, and features Breguet Arabic indexes which were hand-engraved. The watch is powered by the calibre 3019SS. The case is 37mm in diameter (44mm lug to lug).

The watch comes with box and papers and was sold in 2023.

Rolex Submariner Cermit Reference 126610LV Full Set Brand New

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most versatile and adorned wristwatches. This particular model has never been worn outside of the photo in this listing. The watch was purchased in June 2021.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes from the original owner, and comes “full set” with box and papers. It is in brand new condition

Rolex Submariner Reference 14060 Unpolished w/ Service Card

This Rolex Submariner reference 14060 dates to 1997-1998 with an U serial. The case is unpolished and measures 40mm in diameter.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers It comes with a service card from 2023.

Rolex Submariner Reference 116610LN Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 116610LN is an awesome sports watch. The case is well worn but it remains unpolished. The watch comes full set with box and papers, and service papers. It dates to 2018.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Rolex Daytona Cosmograph Reference 116500LN Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN with white dial is affectionately known as the “Panda” and is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a white dial and black Cerachrom bezel, white dial, and black registers. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. The watch comes from the original owner who purchased it in 2018.

It is great condition and comes from the original owner. It is unpolished. It comes “full set” with original box and papers.

Jaeger-LeCoultre 18K Gold Memovox Reference E853 Unpolished w/ Extract

Along with the Atmos clocks, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

This is one of the best Memovox E 853 examples we have ever seen, The watch is powered by the calibre 815, automatic alarm wristwatch. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement.

The dial and hands feature radium lume. The 18K yellow gold case is unpolished, and in remarkable condition. The watch measures approximately 37mm in diameter. I cannot imagine finding a better example in the forthcoming years! The watch comes with an Extract from the Archives.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Chronograph Reference 5500V/110A-B148 Full Set Brand New

The Vacheron Constantin Overseas models have become very popular in recent years. This chronograph reference 5500V/110A-B148 is a fantastic example of why the sports watches of VC are exciting.

The watch comes full set. It is in like new condition and was worn only a few times since its purchase in January 2023.

Rolex Air-King Reference 14000 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is a superb example. The watch features a N serial number and dates to circa 1991. The case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5065J-001 Extra Strap Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5065J-001 is one of the great contemporary sports watches. At 38mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions that can appeal to both vintage and modern enthusiasts. This example has a fantastic case which remains unpolished. The hands and dial feature tritium lume which has a nice warm color. According to the Certificate of Origin, it was sold in 2001.

It is powered by the calibre 315 SC and is on view through its exhibition caseback.

The watch comes full set with box, the Certificate of Origin, corresponding papers and an extra black rubber strap. For those looking for an outstanding Aquanaut, you cannot go wrong with this one!

Heuer Camaro Reference 7220NT Unpolished

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive raving, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera.

The reference 7220N is powered by the manual wind calibre Valjoux 72. The N in the reference designates the black dial, and T for Tachymeter. The case is unpolished and the watch is paired on a very nice and rare GF bracelet. The case measures 37mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It dates to circa 1970.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1005 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 1005 is a great watch It has an 18K yellow gold case. The watch is paired with a beautiful Oyster bracelet. The case measures 34mm in diameter (or 39.5mm lug-to-lug) and is is unpolished. It dates to late circa 1974

The watch does not include box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Casio G-Shock MRGB5000D-1 KIWAMI MR-G Full Set

This “MR-G” Casio G-Shock is one of the coolest and most interesting G-Shock watches.

These breakthrough timepieces are a study in metal craftsmanship. The case and bezel are made of Ti64-based super-hard titanium alloy, while the band is made of another special titanium alloy, DAT55G, that is both three times harder than pure titanium and highly workable. The bezel top is crafted in COBARION®, a cobalt-chrome alloy four times harder than pure titanium.

The bezel alone consists of 25 different components, each individually polished for a stunning detailed texture. The state-of-the-art Multi-guard Structure incorporates flat springs and silicone buffers to the multi-component bezel, ensuring outstanding shock absorption to protect the dedicated MR-G module with gold-plated retainer plate.

MRGB5000D with contrasting hairline and mirror finishes and a titanium carbide coating for exquisitely polished silver. Both watch faces feature an iconic brick pattern and red line in a nod to the original design

It comes with box and papers. It comes from the original owner who wore it sparingly.
