https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Ref 5970G-001 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5970 is an iconic model. This perpetual calendar chronograph was the successor to the Patek Philippe reference 3970. The reference 5970 was produced over the period of 2004-2010. It incorporates an aggressive lug design and highly legible dial which collectors love.

The reference 5970R is powered by the calibre CH 27-70 Q which is a highly praised movement in Patek Philippe circles originating through the Lemania calibre 2310.

This example is in white gold. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 39.5mm in diameter not including the crown. It is 46mm lug-to-lug. The watch dates to 2007 per Certificate of Origin.

The watch comes full set with box and the Certificate of Origin and white gold case back. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Mk 0 Great White Sea-Dweller Reference 1665

This Rolex “Great White” Sea-Dweller reference 1665 is a very attractive watch with the earliest MK 0 dial. The MK 0 is the rarest of Great Whites and is highly collectible for those that appreciate the nuance in vintage Sea-Dwellers. These dials feature the “Sea-Dweller” font with a slightly longer line of depth rating in addition to a closed 6.

It features very attractive warm colored lume.

The case and bracelet have been polished. The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. It features the Mk I dial.

It dates to circa 1976 with a 4.13M serial. It is paired on an folded Oyster bracelet with VD date code dating to circa 1979.

The watch does not come box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Daytona Double Swiss Underline Reference 6239

This extremely early Rolex Daytona reference 6239 is a very cool example with a rare “Double Swiss Underline” dial. It was part of the earliest crop of Daytona watches made by Rolex It has the rare and early “Mark 1” 275 bezel with dashes next to the numbers. It is a very honest example which came from the son of the original owner.. The case has been lightly polished, but is in strong shape. The case has a 923,XXX serial and dates to circa 1963.

It is paired on a Gay Freres 7205 bracelet with 57 end links dating to 1967.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

A. Lange & Söhne 1815 Annual Calendar Reference 238.026 18K WG Full Set

This A. Lange & Söhne 1815 Annual Calendar is calculated to remain accurate for nearly 123 years provided it is kept wound. The white gold case measures 40mm in diameter. The watch also features a solid silver dial that displays a moon phase, date, day of the week and month. It’s powered by a manually wound movement, the L051.3.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It comes as a “full set” with box and papers.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16753

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16753 is an awesome model which has become more appreciated by collectors in recent years. This example dates to circa 1986 with a 9.3M serial. The case has been polished. The dial is extremely attractive. It has a relumed 5 o’clock marker which is odd, but our colleague Gavin says “It’s 5 o’clock somewhere!” which we love! Perhaps that was the inspiration!.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Cartier Tortue Monopoussoir Chronograph W1546551 in Platinum Full Set

This Cartier Tortue Monopoussoir is an elegant chronograph operated by (as the name suggests in French) a singular pusher built into the winding crown. The white gold “tortue” case measures about 38mm in diameter at the widest point. The lug-to-lug is approximately 48mm. The watch features an exhibition caseback and sapphire cabochon crown. The movement was a collaboration between Vianney Halter, Fançois-Paul Journe, and Denis Flageollet!

The watch comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch is a Cartier Collection Privée Cartier Paris watch. (CPCP).

The date of sale is not mentioned in the papers.

Rolex Skydweller Two-Tone Ref. 326933 Full Set

The Rolex Sky-Dweller was introduced in 2012, bringing “The Crown” back into the conversation of complicated timepieces. While the Sky-Dweller is the most complicated Rolex, the beauty lies in Rolex’s ability to tone down the complication into a simple and clear dial layout. The first impression is a highly legible calendar watch, which is an annual calendar with GMT functionality.

The watch features a rotating 18k gold bezel that changes the crown function. It features a two-tone stainless and 18k yellow gold Jubilee bracelet and “champagne” dial. The watch measures 42mm in diameter.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers, and was originally sold in 2021. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Day-Date Ref. 128235 Rose Gold Green Aventurine Dial Full Set

This Everose gold Day-Date, in outstanding condition, is a great watch from the current classic catalogue. It features a “Green Aventurine” dial with Roman numeral indexes set with diamonds. The watch is powered by the Rolex Calibre 3255, with a 70-hour power reserve and a double quick-set day and date function. The case measures 36mm in diameter and approximately 43mm lug-to-lug.

The case and bracelet appear to be unpolished and are in excellent condition. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers. It was sold in 2023.

Rolex Day-Date Green Dial Ref. 228239 Full Set Unpolished New Condition

This Rolex Day-Date reference 228239 is a super attractive modern watch in white gold. The watch has an extremely beautiful green dial. It has a 40mm case and is approximately 47.5mm lug to lug. The watch is in new condition.

It comes as a “full set” with its original box and papers. It was sold in 2022. We do not know the full service history, but the watch is keeping great time. It recently passed a 5-bar water resistance test.

Rolex Datejust Blue Dial Reference 126300 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 126300 is a very attractive watch. It is 41mm in diameter. It is unpolished. This example features a blue dial and a stainless steel Oyster bracelet. The case measures 41mm in diameter.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers, and was originally sold in 2018. It comes from the original owner!

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Oysterquartz Day-Date Reference 19018 CPO Box and Papers

This is very cool, 18k yellow gold Rolex Oysterquartz Day-Date reference 19018. The watch dates to 1979 with a serial number in the 6.2 million range. The integrated gold bracelet is likely original. Both the case and bracelet have been polished in the past. The watch was previously part of Rolex’s Certified Pre-Owned program (Rolex CPO) and sold in 2024.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well and recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes with a box and papers.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore 15720ST.OO.A027CA.01 Full Set

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Diver is a tough and robust dive watch. The case measures approximately 42mm in diameter, 14mm thick, and is stainless steel. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch features a rotating inner bezel. The watch comes on a classic Offshore Diver rubber strap. The dial features the distinct blue “Méga Tapisserie” pattern. For those looking for a great sports watch, look no further.

The watch comes as a “Full Set” with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was sold in September 2023.

Rolex Daytona Reference 6239 w/ Black Dial Full Set

This extremely early Rolex Daytona reference 6239 is a very attractive watch. It has the rare and early “Mark 2” 300 bezel. The case has been lightly polished, but is in strong shape. The case has a 1.59M serial and dates to circa 1967. It is paired on a Rolex 7835 folded link bracelet with 60N end links dating to 1968 which is possibly original to the watch

The watch comes with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Zenith Daytona Tropical Dial 16520 Full Set

The adorned “Zenith Daytonas” are certainly among the highly desired neo-vintage Rolex models. Zenthi Daytonas were produced from circa 1988 until 2000. It is powered by an automatic Calibre 4030 made by Zenith. The case has been polished. It measures 40mm in diameter (42.5 including crown) and 47mm lug to lug. The outer ring of the registers have developed a very attractive tropical color similar to the “Patrizzi” dials but this is slightly later that these models. It features a U8 serial and dates to circa 1997-1009.

The watch comes with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Porsche Design Compass/Ocean 2000

This IWC Porsche Design Compass is a cool sports watch in Titanium. The watch features an integrated bracelet and rotating bezel with a crown at 4 o’clock. The case measures approximately 40mm in diameter and is approximately 11mm thick. It appears to be unpolished.

It was recently serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test. It does not come with a box or papers.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Reference 4100BA In 18K YG

The Audemars Piguet Royal is among the most iconic wristwatch models ever produced. This reference 4100BA is made in an 18k yellow gold case and bracelet. The case measures approximately 35mm in diameter placing it in the category of “midsized” Royal Oaks. The case and bracelet are lightly polished but remain attractive. The dial is a very attractive champagne color and is well preserved.

We do not know the full service history but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Hodinkee O31132403006001 Full Set Like New

This Omega Speedmaster was made in collaboration with Hodinkee, celebrating the watch-media company’s 10th anniversary in 2018. The watch pays homage to the reference 2998 Speedmaster of the past, with the pre-professional case design. The color scheme is appropriated from the Speedmaster “Day-Date” of 1996. 500 examples of this model were produced by Omega.

The case measures 39.7mm in diameter, 47mm lug-to-lug, and is powered by the manually wound Omega Calibre 1861. This example is in like-new condition. It comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster “Ed White” 105.003-65 Unpolished

It is no secret that I love vintage OMEGA Speedmasters. You can see the HODINKEE Reference Points video I did on the subject with Ben Clymer.

This is an amazing reference 105.003, also referred to as the “Ed White” as it was famously worn by the astronaut. The watch dates to circa 1965. It is powered by the iconic calibre 321, which was recently re-released and garnered great reception and excitement from a large portion of watch collectors. The dimensions are 38.5mm in diameter (or 48mm lug-to-lug) with a lug width of 19mm. The case appears to be unpolished. The dial features tritium lume that has turned warm in color.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The Omega Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers and Extract. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master Ref. 16753 on Jubilee

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16753 is an awesome model which has become more appreciated by collectors in recent years. With an R serial, this example dates to circa 1988. The bracelet is in great condition and dates to circa 1986 with an K date code.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Air-King 116900 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 116900 is a great modern watch. The case is unpolished a measures 40mm in diameter. The dial features pronounced numerals at 3, 6 and 9 and features the highly attractive green “Rolex” print. It was sold in 2018.

The watch comes with with box and papers.

Rolex Explorer II Ref 1655.

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

This example is an attractive Explorer II reference 1655. It dates to circa late 1979 (6.34 million serial number). The case has been polished but it attractive.

The watch is paired on 78360 Oyster bracelet with correct 580 end links with a D date code dating to circa 1979 and is likely original to the watch.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

TUDOR Monte Carlo Ref 7159/0 Unpolished

The Monte Carlo reference 7159/0 is an awesome and rare chronograph in the TUDOR catalog. The watch is highly admired based on its vibrant orange accents and extremely cool dial design. This example dates to circa 1972 with an 775,XXX serial.

The case appears to be unpolished. The case measures 38mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The watch is paired on a Rolex 7836 folded-link bracelet with G date code G date code. The bracelet is lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Double Red Sea-Dweller MK 4 Reference 1665

This Rolex Sea-Dweller reference 1665 features the highly desired and vibrant "Double Red" dial. The earliest Sea-Dwellers used cases like on the Rolex Submariner reference 5512, but incorporated a helium escape valve (HEV) added to the left side of the case.

This watch features a 5.2 million serial dating to circa 1977-1978. The case is polished, but is attractive. The watch measures 40mm in diameter.

It is paired on an solid Oyster bracelet with OP3 date code dating to circa 2006.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Submariner Ref. 1680

The Rolex Submariner is one of the greatest sports watches and for great reason. This reference 1680 dates to circa 1978 with a 5.25M serial. The case has been polished in the past. The lume and hands are attractive. It is paired on a solid-link Rolex bracelet with VC date code dating to circa 1978.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Mk 3 Double Red Sea-Dweller 1665 Full Set

This Rolex Sea-Dweller reference 1665 features the highly desired and vibrant "Double Red" dial. The earliest Sea-Dwellers used cases like on the Rolex Submariner reference 5512, but incorporated a helium escape valve (HEV) added to the left side of the case.

It features a MK 3 dial. This watch features a 4.19 million serial dating to circa 1976. The case is polished. The watch measures 40mm in diameter.

It is paired on an folded-link Rolex Oyster bracelet with A date code dating to circa 1976.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Vintage Hamilton Tank in Platinum Unpolished

This Vintage Hamilton Tank wristwatch is a very rare and cool timepiece. It is made of platinum and we believe the case is unpolished. It measures 21mm in diameter by 36mm lug to lug. It has the very cool fancy numerals, sort of an Americanized version of Breguet numerals

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Movado Breguet Numerals M95 Chronograph Unpolished

This Movado chronograph features a stunning dial with Breguet numerals. The case measures approximately 35mm in diameter and 43mm lug to lug. The case appears to be unpolished. It features very attractive and uncommon applied-Breguet numerals. It is powered by the legendary calibre M95!

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

Blancpain Fifty Fathoms Mil-Spec Limited Edition Hodinkee Full Set New

This Blancapin Fifty Fathoms reference 5008-11B30 was made in 250 in collaboration with Hodinkee. At 40.7mm in diameter, this no-date dive watch pays homage to the watches made for the US Navy in the past. It features a unidirectional sapphire crystal bezel and a gloss-like dial. It also features an orange moisture indicator.

This example is in like-new condition. It comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Reference 270.3.36 In White Gold Unpolished

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso is a super attractive complicated model. The watch dates to circa 2003. The case appears to be unpolished and made of 18K gold. It measures approximately 26mm x 42mm.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not include box and papers.

TUDOR HODINKEE Unique Piece Black Bay 58 Ref 79030N Full Set Unpolished

This is a very special TUDOR HODINKEE Black Bay 58 piece unique with a HODINKEE case-back engraving. The watch was presented in 2023 for a charitable raffle supporting the Horological Society of New York. HODINKEE raised a great deal of money for the charity and it was very inspiring.

It features signs of light wear and scratches, but it is unpolished.

“You might recognize this watch from the cover of the Hodinkee Magazine Vol. 10, and you might just be able to get it for yourself! In celebration of our second annual 10/10, we’re raffling off this unique Tudor Black Bay 58. Back in 2019, we worked with Tudor to create a Tudor Black Bay 58 with a Hodinkee-signed dial. These pieces were made specifically for Hodinkee employees and were never made available for public sale. Among the already few Hodinkee-signed Black Bay 58s, this one watch, in particular, was crafted to be truly one-of-a-kind, complete with a custom caseback engraving to celebrate Volume 10 of Hodinkee Magazine. Now is your chance to own a truly special Tudor, while also contributing to an amazing organization. A portion of the raffle proceeds will be donated to The Horological Society of New York. Tickets will be available for purchase until 3 AM EST/12 AM PT. The winner will be announced on the following day 10/11 after 12 PM ET.”

I will never forget seeing my first TUDOR Black Bay 58 on the wrist of Jason Fried in Chicago many years ago, and I was floored by the watch.

The TUDOR Black Bay 58 is one of the coolest modern watches. The design is inspired by the iconic Rolex “Big Crown” Submariner references 6538, 6200, 5510, and the TUDOR Submariner reference 7924. The dials have a similar texture to the matte dials with gold accents inspired by gilt dials. For those who love HODINKEE and appreciate the greatness of the TUDOR Black Bay 58, this is an incredible timepiece to wear in any setting.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. It comes from the original owner. It comes with the H10 Magazine.

TUDOR “Snowflake” Submariner Ref. 7021/0

The TUDOR “Snowflake” Submariner Reference 7021/0 is an amazing dive watch. The case measures approximately 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The case has been polished but is attractive. The watch dates to circa 1969 with a 738,XXX serial number. It is paired with a reference 9315 Oyster bracelet with 580 end-links. The dial has a very attractive blue color and nice lume.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Submariner 5513 Meters First Dial

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. This reference 5513 features a 1.98M serial number dating to circa 1969. This example features a “meters first” matte dial with very attractive tritium lume. The hands have been relumed. It has been polished. It is paired on a rubber strap (one side is Everest and the other is Rubber B). The bezel insert is later.

Learn more about the sibling model reference 5512 and 5513 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with box or papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional “Snoopy” Limited Edition Full Set

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic wristwatches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This example, made in a limited edition, features Snoopy on the seconds sub dial and on the caseback. The motif is accompanied by the quote “Eyes on the Stars”.

The watch measures 42mm in diameter, approximately 48mm lug-to-lug, and is powered by the manual Omega Calibre 1861 movement. It features an acrylic crystal. The stainless steel case and bracelet appear to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It comes with box and papers.

Rolex 36mm Oyster Perpetual Ref 1018

The reference 1018 is a rare and oversized Oyster Perpetual model which measures 36mm (43mm lug-to-lug). The 1018 was a reference that was not mass-produced and as a result of its narrow font has become an exciting OP for collectors who appreciate minimal and balanced dials. This example features a grey soleil dials which remains in a fantastic state. Oftentimes, the reference 1018 will show signs of water ingress or feature service dials.

The case has been lightly polished and the watch is paired on a folded link Rolex bracelet dating to 1972. The watch features a 2.09M serial dating to circa 1969.

To learn more about the Rolex reference 1018, read our Director of Content and Research Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1018’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is not known, but it is running great! It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Submariner Meters First Ref 5513 Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. This reference 5513 features a 1.58M serial number dating to circa 1967. It is paired with a C&I stretch rivet bracelet dating to 1966. This example features a “meters first” matte dial with very attractive lume. The case appears to be unpolished. It features the original long 5 insert.

Learn more about the sibling model reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with the original box or papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Cartier Tank Solo Yellow Gold Ref. 2742

This is an elegant Cartier tank in 18k yellow gold. The case measures 27.5x35mm. It features a Cartier classic Roman numerals dial with blue sword hands. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch comes with a Cartier brown leather strap and a gold Cartier pin buckle. We do not know the date of production.

No offers. It is dirt cheap.

The watch does not include a box or papers. It just had a battery replaced and is running well;

Rolex Day-Date Champagne Diamond Dial Ref. 18238 in 18K YG

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is an exceptional example. It has an 18K yellow gold case. The watch features an E serial number dating to circa 1990. This has an uncommon factory dial that features a champagne dial with diamond set track that contains gold Arabic numerals. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Grey Diamond Dial Ref. 18238 in 18K YG

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is an exceptional example. It has an 18K yellow gold case. The case appears to be polished. The watch features an E serial number dating to circa 1990.

This has an rare factory dial that features a brushed grey/silver dial with a pavé diamond set track that contains gold Roman numerals as hour markers.

It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

TUDOR Submariner Reference 7928/0 Nylon Unpolished

I’m excited to offer this excellent Tudor Submariner reference 7928. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The serial is 596,XXX, dating to circa 1967. The lume on the hands and dial is very attractive. It is paired with a green nylon strap.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. The watch does not come with a box or papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Patek Philippe Reference 570 in 18K WG w/ Box & Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 570 is one of the most refined and sought-after dress watches. It is an iconic model within the Calatrava family. This specific watch is a reference 570 is a very attractive example. It features applied-dauphine hour markers which is one of the most attractive configurations. The white gold case is lightly polished.

It comes with the Patek Philippe Extract from the Archives and was produced and sold in 1969. It comes with a period correct Patek Philippe box.

This watch does not include the original papers. It is powered by the Caliber 27 SC manual-wind movement made by Patek Philippe.

Patek Philippe Calatrava Reference 2482 In 18K YG W/ Extract

This Patek Philippe reference 2482 is an amazing timepiece in 18K yellow gold. The case has been lightly polished and has a great size at 36mm in diameter and 44mm lug-to-lug. The lug design on this model are very attractive from both straight forward and the profile. The dial is made by Fabrique de Cadran Stern Frères and is a beautiful two-tone silver dial. This example has my personal favorite dial configuration with similar numerals found in the Rolex "Padellone", Rolex Oyster reference 6426, Rolex Oyster reference 6082, Rolex Precision reference 4583, certain Movado M95 chronographs and St. Christopher coin watches.

The watch was produced in 1950. It comes with an Extract from The Archives.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 3940J 2nd Series w/ Extract Unpolished

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. This particular example is in yellow gold featuring the highly desirable 2nd series dial. The dial is English and has beautiful serifs for the month and day abbreviati∂ons in the registers.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. This example dates to circa 1988. The case is 36mm. The case is unpolished. It never had a display back, as seen in the early examples of the reference 3940.

The watch an Extract from the Archives confirming production and sale in 1988, its original Patek Philippe pin buckle in 18k yellow gold, and a Patek Philippe stylus with accents in yellow gold. It does not include a box.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Grande Reverso Ultra Thin Tribute to 1931 Reference 277.8.62 Unpolished

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Grande Reverso Ultra Thin Tribute to 1931 is one of my personal favorite Reverso models. The dial layout and absence of branding is taken from the early Reversos of the 1930s and is a very pure design. Additionally, the sword hands make for a very handsome look. The watch is paired on an aftermarket strap (made to look like Casa Fagliano’s designs). It comes with a signed 20mm JLC buckle. It was produced in a limited numbers in 2011 to for the 80th anniversary of the Reverso.

I love this watch, and the Reverso overall. Learn more about my thoughts on these timepieces by reading My Top 10 Watches of All Time article.

It does not include box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

IWC Mark XVIII Limited Edition HODINKEE Full Set

Originally released in the first quarter of 2019, the Mark XVIII was arguably one of the best received limited editions from HODINKEE to date. The highlight of the watch is most certainly the dial. While fauxtina can certainly have a polarizing effect, the custard superluminova hands were executed in a very naturalistic fashion.

Loosely based upon the 1948 Mark II and the 3705 Fliegerchronograph from the early 2000s, HODINKEE sought to create a modern IWC that would attract the vintage audience and ultimately be sentimental in the eyes of true enthusiasts.

The case is made of Ceratanium (a combination of ceramic and titanium). It measures in at 39mm (46mm lug-to-lug). The watch is in mint condition and comes with its original box and papers.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16014 Linen Dial Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 16014 is no exception. The “linen” dial is one of my favorite Datejust dials, as it is so attractive and special. The watch dates to 1983 and features a 7.4M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Sea-Dweller Reference 116600 Full Set

This Sea-Dweller reference 116600 is an awesome modern watch. The case is unpolished. The watch was sold in September of 2015.

The watch comes full set with box and papers from the original owner.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 w/ Service Papers

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The case is lightly polished. This example dates to circa 1974. It is paired on a Rolex 7836 bracelet with 258 end links.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points: A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does comes with service papers.

Rolex Bluesy Two-Tone Submariner Reference 126613LB Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 126613LB is an very fun sports watch in two tone. The case is well worn but it remains unpolished. The watch comes full set with box and papers, and service papers. It dates to November 2020.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch comes full set with box and papers.

Cartier Tank A Vis Reference 2485 in Platinum w/ Box

This Cartier Tank A Vis reference 2485 is part of the Collection Privée Cartier Paris. It is designed with inspiration from waterproof models from the 1930s. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 27mm by 31mm

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well

Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 Black Dial Unpolished w/ Service Card

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520 with a black dial. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988 until circa 2000. This is example features an unpolished case and unpolished bracelet. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). It features a P Serial dating 2000 making it one of the last Zenith Daytonas.

This watch does not include a box or papers. It comes with a service card dating to 2023.

Rolex Submariner Reference 126610LN Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most versatile and adorned wristwatches. This particular example was purchased in 2022.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes from the original owner, and comes “full set” with box and papers.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012-67 w/ Extract

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012 dates to circa 1968 (with a -67 case production). The watch is good condition. The case has been polished in the past, but is nice. It measures 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 321 made by OMEGA).

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see Eric Wind’s ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video produced with HODINKEE.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes with an Extract from The Archives. The watch was serviced last year and is running well.

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Reference 145.005-67

This OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Reference 145.005 dates to circa 1967. We love the watch. It has a very attractive dial. The case has been polished in the past, but is nice. It measures 35mm in diameter. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 321 made by OMEGA).

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great.

By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 5396/1G-001 On Bracelet Full Set Unpolished

This is a very special Patek Philippe reference 5396G. The watch is powered by the calibre 324 S QA LU 24H, an automatic annual calendar. This dial variant is among my personal favorites featuring a blue soleil finish with the classic hour markers. It is paired on a very nice white gold bracelet by Patek.

The watch In fantastic condition and is unpolished. The white-gold case wears incredible on the wrist. It measures 38.5mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug).

The watch comes full set with box and papers. It was sold in 2013 in Asia.

Cartier Santos Or Massif “Gordon Gekko” in 18k YG Unpolished

model worn by Gordon Gekko in the 1987 film, Wall Street. The case measures 29mm in diameter (41mm lug to lug). We believe the case is unpolished. Often these have shiny screw heads, but we have examined it closely and it appears the bracelet is also unpolished. For those looking for an elegant and amazing gold timepiece, this is a great choice.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Zenith Chronograph Reference A277 Unpolished

The Zenith Chronograph reference A277 is an awesome watch. The cases have very cool bevelled lugs. This example has a very attractive faded bezel insert. We believe the steel case remains unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter. It is paired on the iconic “ladder” Zenith bracelet.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Batman GMT-Master II Reference 126710BLNR w/ Box

Rolex “Batman” Rolex Batman GMT-Master II reference 126710BLNR is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not knot the date of production. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced.

Rolex Wide Boy Datejust Reference 1601 Non-Luminous Unpolished

This is a super cool Rolex “Wide Boy” Datejust reference 1601 with a the uncommon non-luminous dial! We believe the case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The watch has a 3.11M serial number dating the watch to circa 1972.

It was recently serviced and is running great. The watch comes does not come with box and papers

Rolex Date Reference 1500 in 18K Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Date reference 1500 is a great example. We believe the 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. The dial is very attractive and it features the uncommon alpha hands. This handset would be phased out in circa 1964. It measures 34mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The serial is 0.93M serial dating to circa 1964.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

A. Lange & Söhne Little Lange 1 Reference 181.038 Full Set Unpolished

This A. Lange & Söhne Little Lange 1 Reference 181.038 is a super cool watch. It is a great contemporary dress watch from the leading German watch manufacturers. We believe the white gold case is unpolished. It measures 37mm in diameter. The watch is quite slim, with a height of 9.5mm. It comes on a Veblenist strap and steel buckle.

The watch full set with box and papers. It does not have the small calendar pusher. It was sold in 2020.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 2526 in 18K YG Unpolished w/ Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 2526 is an iconic watch and this is an incredibly attractive example in 18K yellow gold. The Patek Philippe Reference 2526 is renowned as the most attractive important of Patek Philippe’s automatic references. The watch dates to 1953 and was sold in the same year according to the Extract from the Archives. The case appears to be unpolshed.

The dial is extremely beautiful. The case dimensions are 36mm in diameter and 41mm lug-to-lug. The case appears to be unpolished.

This Patek Philippe 2526 is an early example and was likely the 190th calibre 12-600 movement made based on the serial number. This example was made and sold in the first year of the 2526’s production: 1953.

Finding an unpolished 2526 is nearly impossible, we have only held far fewer than 10 unpolished examples of the 2526.

It is comes with an 18mm Patek Philippe leather strap and features gold Patek Philippe buckle made by Wenger. It is powered by the iconic self-winding movement from Patek Philippe, the calibre 12-600.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from the Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 3700/1A

The Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 3700/1A is one of the most iconic designs from Gérald Charles Genta. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated stainless steel watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today.

The watch dates to the late 1970s. The dial is a service replacement dial without accent - although is extremely attractive. The case and bracelet have been lightly polished, but are strong. The watch will fit a wrist approximately 7 inches. It measures 42mm in diameter.

The watch comes from a prominent collection of vintage sports watches and incredible independents. For those looking for an incredible Nautilus 3700, look no further!

Patek Philippe created a spare 3700/1 case for the watch for the owner during an expensive service that is unpolished and is on the watch. The original 3700/1 case comes with the watch. A copy of the service documents is included.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 2552 in 18K YG w/ Extract

This Patek Philippe reference 2552 is an incredibly attractive example in 18K yellow gold. The Patek Philippe Reference 2552 is certainly one of the most attractive automatic Patek Philippe models, and this is very much due to the great dial layout and case design. The watch dates to 1957 and was sold in 1958. The case is has been lightly polished, but is quite attractive. The dial is beautiful. The case dimensions are 36mm in diameter and 41mm lug-to-lug. It is paired on an 18mm Patek Philippe leather strap and features gold Patek Philippe buckle made by Wenger. It is powered by the iconic self-winding movement from Patek Philippe, the calibre 12-600.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from the Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Reference 5402BA in 18K YG

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is an icon. It is the original luxury sports watch that created and defined the category. Introduced at BaselWorld in 1972 after being designed by Gérald Genta, it turned the Swiss industry upside down and changed the fortunes of the struggling Audemars Piguet. It’s no surprise that Patek Philippe hired Gerald Genta a few years later to introduce their own version of a steel luxury sports watch: the Nautilus.

The origin of the species is the Royal Oak reference 5402ST - nicknamed the “Jumbo”. This example is a reference 5402BA and features an 18k yellow gold case and bracelet. The gold iteration was initially released in 1977, and approximately 736 examples of the reference were sold until 1990.

We are ecstatic to offer this fantastic gold example that dates to the late 1970s. The case measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug to lug), and this is the original “Jumbo” size. The case and bracelet have been polished in the past, like on the vast majority of these watches. The yellow gold has a very warm tone and is extremely attractive. The bracelet fits a wrist of approximately 6.9 inches.

This watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona Reference 6265 Unpolished

We are thrilled to offer this amazing Rolex Daytona reference 6265. It has one of the most attractive silver soilel finish dials I have ever seen. We believe the case is unpolished. The bracelet has been lightly polished. This watch dates to circa 1973, with a serial number in the 3.55 million range. It features the original Mark 2 pushers and measures 37mm in diameter. The movement is a manual wind calibre 727 made by Valjoux.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 1 bar.

Audemars Piguet Automatic Tourbillon 25643BA in 18K YG

This Audemars Piguet Automatic reference 25643BA is an interesting tourbillon wristwatch in 18k yellow gold. The watch dates to circa 1988 and measures 29x33mm. It is powered by the calibre 2870. It features a guilloché dial layout, with apertures for the tourbillon and automatic oscillating rotor. It is paired on a leather strap, with an 18k yellow gold Audemars Piguet signed buckle. The caseback engraving is numbered 208.

The watch was serviced by Audemars Piguet in 2022 and is keeping great time. It does not come with a box or papers.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 15300ST.OO.1220ST.02 Full Set

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the most iconic and sought-after sports watches on the planet. This reference 15300ST.OO.1220ST.02 is in a 39mm “Jumbo” size. It features a blue “tapisserie” dial. The case and bracelet are stainless steel. For those looking for an incredible and sought-after modern Royal Oak, this is a great watch.

We do not know the full service history, but the watch is running well. This watch comes “full set” with a box and papers. It was originally sold in 2010.

Cartier Trinity Ring Wristwatch in 18K WG, RG & YG

This Cartier wristwatch is a very elegant timepiece. It features the signature Trinity ring design with diamond set bezel in rose gold, white gold and yellow gold. It measures approximately 27mm in diameter. It is paired on a deployant Cartier buckle and leather strap. The case is unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

Rolex Submariner ref. 5513

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. This reference 5513 features a 7.5M serial number dating to circa 1983. It is paired with a reference 93150 Oyster with an H clasp code bracelet dating to 1983. It has the correct 580 end-links. This example features an attractive matte dial with tritium lume. It has been polished in the past but is attractive. The second hand appears to have been re-lumed.

Learn more about the sibling model reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with the original box or papers.

Rolex GMT-Master Ref 1675 on Jubilee

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

The case has been lightly polsihed. It is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet dating to 1970. This is a very attractive 1675 featuring a 1.8M serial number, dating to circa 1968.

The hands have been relumed. The bezel insert is extremely attractive.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

OMEGA Moonshine Speedmaster 18K Gold Full Set New

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This brand new “Moonshine” model is a very attractive iteration of the Speedy. It is powered by the calibre 3861. The watch is in brand new condition. It is an incredible deal compared to the $36,500 MSRP before taxes! Incredible value!

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

Rolex Batman GMT-Master II Reference 116710BLNR Unpolished Full Set

Rolex “Batman” Rolex Batman GMT-Master II reference 116710BLNR is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The 116710BLNR is now discontinued on the Oyster bracelet and is highly admired.

The watch is unpolished. It comes from the original owner who is a friend of Charlie Dunne! Great Provenance! The watch comes full set with box and papers. It was sold in 2016! It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 6542

The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 6542 marked the first reference in what would go on to become one of the most celebrated timepieces across all manufacturers. The timepiece became an icon within the James Bond movie Goldfinger while being worn by Honor Blackman for her portrayal of "Pussy Galore".

This example features 482,XXX serial number dating to circa 1959. The case has been polished. The watch features a service bezel from a later time period. The watch features a service dial and service hands.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced.

Rolex Zenith Daytona 16520 White Dial Mint Condition Full Set Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520. It has a n A serial dating it to circa 1999. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. We believe the case is unpolished. It is in mint condition and unpolished with stickers. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). The white dial variants are seriously attractive watches and are among Charlie’s favorite Daytonas.

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

For those looking for a perfect Zenith Daytona with box and papers, this is an amazing watch.

OMEGA Apollo 8 Dark Side of the Moon 311.92.44.30.01.001 Full Set New

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era.

This is a modern “Dark Side of the Moon” Speedmaster reference 311.92.44.30.01.001 which pays homage to NASA’s Apollo 8 mission of 1968.

The watch is in like-new condition. It is paired with a rubber strap strap. The black ceramic case measures 44.25mm in diameter with a lug width of 21mm. It features a sapphire crystal. It dates to 2023.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

OMEGA Speedmaster Dark Side of the Moon Ref. 311.92.44.51.01.005 Full Set New

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era.

The watch is in like-new condition. It is paired with a nylon strap. The black ceramic case measures 44.25mm in diameter with a lug width of 21mm. It features a sapphire crystal. We do not know when it was sold.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Ref 42042J in 18K YG Full Set

The Overseas line has recently seen a great deal of appreciation by collectors, and I am proud to offer this fantastic example that highlights the heritage of the iconic watch model.

This Vacheron Constantin is the younger sibling of the integrated sports model, reference 222. The yellow gold case measures approximately 36.5mm in diameter. The watch was originally sold in 2002. The 18k yellow gold case appears to be unpolished.

The watch comes as a full set with a box and papers. The Vacheron was serviced by VC in August 2025 and received a new rotor along with the movement overhaul. It was sent to VC through Shreve Crump and Low in Boston.

Longines Conquest Reference 9001-1 on Wittnaur Bracelet Unpolished

This is an extremely attractive example of the iconic Longines Conquest line. The dial features a two-tone non-luminous silver dial which is out of this world. The case is unpolished. The watch dates to the late 1950s. It measures 35mm in diameter and 43mm lug-to-lug. It is paired on a very rare Wittnaur Nautilus bracelet made by JB Champion that is the same style these were sold on.

The watch does not include papers. It does come with a box. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 2403 In 18k Rose Gold Salmon Dial

This is a special Patek Philippe reference 2403 semi-circular hooded lug design in 18k rose gold? The rectangular case measures approximately 37x21mm. The watch features a highly attractive salmon dial with applied hour markers and Arabic numerals at 12 o’clock. There is a small second hand just above 6 o’clock. The case has been polished in the past.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

Longines Black Non-Luminous Time-Only Model Unpolished

This is a cool time-only Longines with non-luminous gilt dial. I features Arabic numerals. The case is in stainless steel and measures 35mm in diameter and approximately 43mm lug-to-lug. It is powered by a manual wind movement. The case appears to be unpolished. It appears to have the original mineral crystal. It appears to be a reference 7117 - 4.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

OMEGA Roman Numerals Pocket Watch in 18k YG Unpolished

This is an elegant Omega pocket watch with applied Roman numerals and an 18k yellow gold case. he watch measures approximately 46mm in diameter. It features a small seconds sub dial at around 6 o’clock and leaf hands.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

OMEGA Chronometre Pocket Watch in Stainless Steel Unpolished

This is a nice Omega pocket watch with applied Arabic numerals and a stainless steel case. The watch measures 45mm in diameter. It features a small seconds sub dial at around 6 o’clock and leaf hands. The case appears to be unpolished

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

OMEGA Full Arabic Numerals Pocket Watch in 18k YG Unpolished

This is an elegant Omega pocket watch with applied Arabic numerals and an 18k yellow gold case. The watch measures approximately 46mm in diameter. It features a small seconds sub dial at around 6 o’clock and leaf hands. The watch features a caseback inscription that reads “DMD”.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

Sinn Ref. 556 Emerald Green Full Set

This Sinn ref. 556 is a great stainless steel sports watch that measures 38mm in diameter by 45.5 mm lug-to-lug. This watch is part of a series that paid homage to various gemstones and is one of 400 pieces to feature this Emerald Green color. The dial features hand-applied indexes, and the watch is powered by the automatic SW200-1 movement. It dates to 2022.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It comes with box and papers.

Heuer Camaro Ref. 73343T

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive racing, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings, such as the Autavia and Carrera. This example has a very attractive dial layout with orange chronograph and minute hands. It features the 7743 case with the calibre 7733 movement. The case has been polished in the past.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the full service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Autavia GMT 2nd Execution Ref. 1163

In my opinion, the Heuer Autavias are some of the best buys in vintage watches. They feature very large cases, which are very enjoyable on the wrist. This reference features a stainless steel case which measures 43mm in diameter (approximately 47mm lug-to-lug). It has been polished. The dial is in great condition, and the bezel insert displays an attractive faded patina. The watch is paired with a black rally strap.

It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

Heuer Jo Siffert Autavia Reference 1163T w/ Box

This Heuer “Jo Siffert” Autavia reference 1163T is an awesome watch. It features the Tachymeter bezel. The case has been polished in the past. It as an automatic movement (Caliber 11 made by Heuer-Léonidas), and is 42mm in diameter.

This watch does not include papers. It comes with a vintage Heuer box. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Audemars Piguet Tank in 18K RG

This Audemars Piguet is a beautiful timepiece dating to the 1930s-1940s. It measures 22mm in diameter by 33mm in length. The 18k rose gold case has been polished. It is an elegant manual wind timepiece which appears to be similar to the calibre 9’90. It features a beautiful rose dial.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is paired on a strap and unsigned buckle.

Vulcain Cricket Reference S2321B Lavender Dial Unpolished

This Vulcain Cricket reference S2321B is an awesome example with a gorgeous lavender dial. It is in spectacular condition with an unpolished case measuring 34mm in diameter and 39mm lug-to-lug.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Reference 3940G w/ Box

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. The calendar is in English. The Patek Philippe reference 3940 is one of the ultimate in discreet complications.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm and is made of white gold. The case has been lightly polished.

The watch dates to circa 1999-2000.

It comes with box. It does not come with papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera 1153N 2nd Execution

The Heuer Carrera reference 1153N is an incredible 1970s chronograph. It features the calibre 11 movement. The case has been polished. The dial is a 2nd execution with the rare decimal inner track. It has turned tropical and has a champagne color instead of the original blue.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is paired on a strap and unsigned buckle.

Rolex Submariner Reference 5513 w/ Box & Receipt

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is a very cool example. The watch features a 1.815M serial dating to circa 1968. The case has been lightly polished but is strong. It is paired on a C&I Rolex Rivet bracelet dating to 1970, which was possibly sold on the watch. The lume appears to be original under UV light.

Learn more about these watches within Charlie Dunne’s article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

This watch comes with box and the original receipt. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Roger W. Smith Series 2 Open Dial in Platinum Unpolished Full Set

Roger Smith is one of the most prestigious independent watchmakers in the world. He was the apprentice of George Daniels who is the widely considered greatest watchmaker of the last century. Smith has been instrumental in the continued legacy of fine British watchmaking. This Series 2 dates to pre 2017. It is numbered 00 and was the first Open Dial Watch of this dial design.

The case is made of platinum and measures 40mm in diameter, 46mm lug to lug, and a height of 14.5mm. The case is unpolished. It comes from a collector who wore it only occasionally and carefully over the years of owning it.

The watch comes with original Linley wooden box and commissioned paper from Roger Smith. It is paired on the original Roger Smith leather strap and platinum buckle. The watch has never been serviced, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Pink on Pink Two-Tone Dial Pocket Watch Reference 703 in Rose Gold Unpolished Full Set

This Patek Philippe reference 703 is one of the most beautiful pocket watches I’ve come across. It has a pink-on-pink tone dial with applied-Roman numerals. The case measures 48mm in diameter and is powered by the calibre 17. It was produced in circa 1947. The case is unpolished and the bow is very stiff. For those looking for one of the most incredible time only Patek Philippe timepieces, this is a no brainer.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Sapphire/Diamond Dial Bark Bezel Day-Date Reference 18078 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18078 is a super cool and uncommon model. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. It features a bark bezel and bracelet, with a very stylish sapphire and diamond dial. It features an English day wheel. The watch features an 7.44 serial number dating to circa 1983.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This is one of my favorite Explorers I have ever seen. This example features an 8.5M serial dating it to circa 1984. We believe the case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive and has a warm yellow tone. The hands are slightly darker. It is paired on a Rolex 78360 bracelet with 580 end links with the bracelet clasp code indicating it was born on the watch.

This is an awesome Explorer I reference 1016. I was considering buying this example for my personal collection (like a few of the Zenith Daytonas, reference 1016s and 1655s over the past few months). Ultimately, I decided I would hold tight until 2025 as I am on a bit of a roll. For those looking for a great vintage Rolex Explorer, here is your chance!

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great. It features a TrueDome D22 crystal. It has just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5740/1G-001 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is perhaps the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated sports watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today. This particular model is a reference 5740/1G dating to 2018. It features a beautiful blue dial variant. It is powered by the calibre 240 Q. The watch comes from the original owner. The white gold case is unpolished. For those looking for an incredible complicated Nautilus, this is a fantastic choice!

The watch comes with the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1A-001 Full Set Unpolished

The Patek Philippe Nautilus is perhaps the most desired watch on the planet. The introduction of the model marked the first true sports watch from the manufacturer in 1976. Along with the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, it ushered in the world of integrated stainless steel watches which are adored by watch enthusiasts today. This particular model is a reference 5990/1A-011 dating to 2020. It features the grey dial variant. It is powered by the CH 28-520 C FUS. The watch comes from the original owner. The case is unpolished. For those looking for an incredible complicated Nautilus, this is a fantastic choice!

The watch comes with the original box and papers.

Rolex Confetti Dial Day-Date Reference 1803 in White Gold

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a very cool example in white gold on the rare Presidential bracelet. It features a black dial in very nice condition which is often classified as the “confetti dial” for the character these can take on. The watch features a 2.75M serial and dates to circa 1971. The white gold case and bracelet have been polished in the past.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Sprite Destro GMT-Master II Reference 126720VTNR Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex “Destro” or “Sprite” GMT-Master reference 126720VTNR is a watch that took the world by storm. We believe the watch is unpolished.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE.

It comes from the original owner with box and papers. It dates to 2022.

Heuer Bundeswehr Flyback Chronograph

This Heuer flyback chronograph is a super cool example for the Bundeswehr (West German Air Force). We believe the watch is unpolished. The case is measures 42mm in diameter. The watch dates to the 1970s.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Carrera Reference 2447S 2nd Execution Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 2447S is a very great example with a 2nd execution dial. The case is unpolished. The watch is powered by the Valjoux calibre 72. It has a 92,2XX serial number dating to circa 1966-1967. The dial and hands have outstanding lume which is super attractive with this strap. I love this Carrera and am excited to see someone score an awesome Heuer.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

Rolex Oysterquartz Datejust Reference 17013 w/ Roman Numerals Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Oysterquartz Datejust reference 17013 is a very nice example. The model was supposedly designed by Gérald Genta. The watch features an A serial dating to circa 1999. The watch is in excellent condition. We believe the case is unpolished. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It features a very modernist style bracelet. The Roman numeral dial is very cool and interesting that it is a non-luminous variant.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Heuer Autavia “Jo Siffert Colors” 3 Register Reference 73663 Full Set Unpolished

This Heuer “Jo Siffert Colors” Autavia reference 73663 is from a super cool watch that is very similar to the reference 1163 famously worn by the automotive racer. It is in outstanding condition. The case is unpolished. It has super attractive lume.

This watch comes with box and papers (which are not filled out and very typical with vintage Heuer). We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is paired on a Heuer bracelet by Gay Freres with HLF end links.

Cartier Paris Tank Louis 78086 in 18K Yellow Gold

This is an attractive Cartier Tank Louis in 18k yellow gold. It measures 23mm in diameter by 31mm lug to lug. It features a very attractive Paris dial. The case has been lightly polished. We believe it dates to the 1970s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It domes with a signed Cartier buckle. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer for American Express Gold Plated Unpolished

These Heuer watches are very interesting and uncommon models made for American Express clients in circa 1980. The case is gold plated and measures 37mm in length, 24.5mm in diameter. It is a very cool Tank styled watch with a beautiful dial.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great.

Rolex Wide Boy Day-Date Ref 1803 in 18K YG Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a phenomenal gold watch. We believe the case is unpolished. The dial is outstanding as you can see in the photos below. It has a very dressy look with distinctly broad hour markers and hands, giving it the name “Wide Boy”. The watch features a 2.9M serial dating to circa 1972.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Heuer Super Autavia 3rd Generation Dashtimer

This Heuer dashboard super Autavia is an amazing watch dating to the late 1960s. It features tritium lume and is a the very attractive 3rd generation or execution of the prestigious dashtimer. It is powered by the Valjoux 5. Learn more about these amazing timepieces on Jeff Stein’s On The Dash article!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great.

Heuer Master-Time 8 Days Dashtimer

This Heuer dashboard Master-Time 8 Days is an amazing dashtimer dating to the late 1960s. It features tritium lume and is a the very attractive model from the most prestigious dashtimer manufacturer.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 3940P

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. The calendar is in English.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm and is made of platinum. The case appears to be lightly polished, but strong. The watch has a solid caseback.

It is paired on what appears to be the original Patek Philippe strap and a deployant buckle. It does not include a box or papers. An Extract from The Archives has been ordered.

Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer Reference 2444

The Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer is essentially the same watch as the Heuer Mareographe reference 2444 with a bit of retailer sauce! This example has a spectacular warm cream colored dial with excellent blue accents in the registers. The minute register is designated for timing yacht races. The case is lightly polished and measures roughly 35mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1960s.

The watch does not come with box or papers. The watch was recently serviced and it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Maxi Mark 1 Reference 5512

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is an awesome example with attractive pale yellow lume. It features a Maxi Mk I dial. The watch has a 5.4M serial dating to circa 1978. It features the original bezel insert. The case is lightly polished but is attractive on the wrist. It is paired on a period correct 93150 bracelet with the correct 580 endlinks.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It has been pressure tested and passed a 5 bar test.

Rolex Milgauss Reference 116400 Black Dial w/ Box and Service Card

When it comes to the antimagnetic wristwatches of the 1950s-1960s, several manufacturers produced what would go on to become iconic timepieces. Among those are the IWC Ingenieur, the Eberhard Scientigraf, the Omega Railmaster, as well as Patek Philippe’s reference 2570 / 2570-1 and 3417. However, one of the most beloved models of orientated for the physicist or engineer of the day was none other than the Rolex Milgauss.

This Rolex Milgauss reference 116400 features the black dial with orange accents and thunderbolt hand. The watch has been polished, but is nice. It has an M serial dating to circa 2007-2008. It measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug).

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes with a box and does not come with original papers. It comes with a service card from 2021.

Movado M95 Chronograph on Bracelet Unpolished

The Movado M95 chronographs are incredible timepieces not only for their movements, but also the cases and dials. This is very attractive and nice example with a tachymeter dial. It is paired on a period correct Movado bracelet. The case is unpolished and measures 35mm in diameter. The case was produced by Taubert Frères (formerly François Borgel).

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 16013 Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Datejust is very attractive timepiece with a beautiful white dial often described as porcelain. The watch dates to 1986 with a 8.99M serial number. The steel case and gold bezel are unpolished and measure 36mm in diameter. The watch is paired on 62523H bracelet. The bracelet is in good condition and has a little play. It is not very stretched.

The watch was just serviced. It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Abercrombie & Fitch Solunar Unpolished

This Abercrombie & Fitch Solunar is a super nice example has a spectacular colors in the dial. The minute register is designated for tide changes. The case is unpolished and measures roughly 36mm in diameter. The watch dates to the early 1950s.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Piaget Polo Reference 365843

This Piaget Polo is an incredible timepiece with a stunning dial. There is a good chance the brand will re-release a model like this within the next year. When this occurs, expect to see examples in this condition be extremely hot and go up in value. Charlie Dunne personally feels this watch is very good look, as he was recently pulled over for egregiously speeding through West Palm Beach wearing a similar model and managed to not get a horrendous ticket! Perhaps the officer thought so too???

The watch is powered by quartz. The case is made of 18K yellow and white gold. It has been lightly polished, but is very strong. The watch measures 34mm in diameter. The bracelet is in awesome condition and can fir a wrist as big as 7.4 inches.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. This example features a 5.5M serial dating it to circa 1978. The case is lightly polished and the bezel is unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive on this dial. It is paired on a Rolex 78360 bracelet with 558 end links.

The 1016 is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Air-King Reference 14000 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is a superb example. The watch features a X serial number and dates to circa 1991. The case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers.
