By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Reference 96 In Platinum W/ Diamond Dial W/ Extract

The Patek Philippe reference 96 is not only among the iconic wristwatches of Patek Philippe, but also is recognized as leaving a monumental impact on the traditional wristwatch. While the Cartier Tank shaped the way we recognize rectangular timepieces, the same could be said about the reference 96. Although it was not the first round wristwatch, it set the standard with its attention to proportion and dimension.

Along with the venerable calibre 9-90, the reference 96 was among the first creations under the Stern family which focused the direction of the company towards wristwatches over pocket watches. The reference 96 would lay the foundation for what we know today as the “Calatrava” (both in relation to Patek Philippe and other manufacturers).

This particular reference 96 is an exceptional outlier from the conventional models due to the fact that it features a dial with diamond hour markers and platinum case. In addition to the early long signature (Patek, Philippe & Cie). These small details can be easily overlooked due to the emphatically darker dial, yet like many watches from Patek Philippe, the understated subtleties are truly what make the models special.

The platinum case has been polished in the past, but is attractive. The width is 30.5mm in diameter. It is powered by the calibre 12-120. The watch accompanies an Extract from the Archives. The watch dates to 1948 and was sold July 8th, 1948. It also comes with a platinum Patek Philippe buckle, made by Wenger, as well as a light blue Jean Rousseau strap..

Patek Philippe Reference 3940P-011 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 perpetual calendar is one of the most elegant complicated timepieces one can find from any manufacturer. The calendar is in English.

This watch features the thin self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor. The case is 36mm and is made of platinum. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch has a solid caseback. It features a 4th series dial. The papers indicate it was sold in 2001.

It comes with a Patek Philippe strap and a deployant buckle. It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is keeping good time. The amplitude is a little low at 195, so it may require a service in the future. It comes from the original owner.

The Patek Philippe reference 3940 is one of the ultimate in discreet complications and finding an unpolished full set example like this one in platinum is extremely difficult. I have no idea when we will next find another one like it.

Rolex Submariner Reference 5513 Unpolished

This is a very nice Rolex Submariner reference 5513 dating to circa 1972. We believe the case is well worn, but unpolished. It is paired on a 9315 folded link Rolex Oyster bracelet. The dial is very attractive with warm patina and what is likely the original tritium pearl. The bezel assembly appears to be unpolished and the original Mark 3 aluminum bezel insert has a nice fade to it.

Learn more about the iconic Submariner within Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article “ Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512 ’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Overall, this is an excellent example of a gorgeous vintage Submariner.

Rolex Tiffany & Co. Explorer II Reference 1655

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

This example is a very special Explorer II featuring the highly coveted double-signed dial by Tiffany & Co. It dates to circa 1978 with a 5.35 million serial number. The case has been polished but is attractive.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was just pressure tested and passed 3 bar.

Rolex Gilt Dial GMT-Master Reference 1675

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a cool gilt-dial 1675 featuring a 1.19M serial number, dating to circa 1965. The case has been polished. The aluminum bezel insert is very attractive. It is paired on a period-correct 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 80 end links dating to circa 1968. The hour, minute, and GMT triangle hands were relumed in the past.

It is running well and was serviced by Steve at Grand Central Watch in 2023. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test. The watch does not come with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

OMEGA Broad Arrow Speedmaster Reference 2915-2 Unpolished w/ Extract

The Speedmaster is, of course, one of the most iconic and important watches ever made. This particular example is a OMEGA Speedmaster reference 2915-3 with “Broad Arrow”hands. We believe the case is unpolished. It has its original “Base 1000” bezel. The lume on the dial appears to be original and is in good shape for one of these early examples. The watch measures 39mm in diameter.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video and read the corresponding article.

This watch does not come with original box and papers, but does include an Extract from OMEGA confirming production on February 24, 1959 and that it was delivered to Germany.

Rolex Silver Tapestry Day-Date Reference 18038 Unpolished

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week. This reference 18038 is an awesome watch with a very silver Tapestry dial. We believe the 18K-gold case is unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This particular example dates to circa 1986 with a 9.1M serial.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 69178 Unpolished w/ Papers

We are thrilled to offer this very exceptional Rolex Datejust reference 69178. It is a stunning example and is 18k yellow gold. We believe it to be unpolished. The case is 26mm in diameter and 33mm lug-to-lug. It is an absolutely stunning timepiece. It features an 9.5M serial dating to circa 1987.

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water test of 3 bar.

A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 Reference 101.032 in 18K Rose Gold w/ Box & Extract

This A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 reference 101.032 is a fantastic timepiece. It has a beautiful silver dial which looks so great with the black font. The 18k pink/rose gold case has been lightly polished and measures 38.5mm in diameter. One thing that is worth highlighting in these Lange watches are the very nice numerals and hands. The watch is powered by the calibre L 901.0. The applied-Roman numerals are very refined and something to admire while looking down at your wrist.

It does come with box and an Extract or Certificate of Origin. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is paired on an aftermarket strap. It is paired on an original ALS tang buckle.

IWC Limited Edition Portugieser Tourbillon F.A. Jones Reference 5447-02: in 18K Rose Gold Full Set

Large and in charge. This IWC Limited Edition Portugieser Tourbillon shows everyone in the room who is boss. It is a massive 43mm rose gold case. The case has been lightly polished. It is a limited edition of 50 examples. It features a Tourbillion, which is incredible!

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from the family of the original owner. It was sold in 2013.

Rolex Green Dial Oyster Perpetual Reference 124300 41mm Full Set

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 124300 is an awesome modern watch. It has great proportions at 41mm in diameter. The smooth bezel gives it a slightly more dressy look. The green dial is accented by very attractive. The watch is in very good condition and was worn very little by the owner.

The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold recently at Monaco Legends.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-1 Unpolished w/ Extract

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This museum-grade example reference 2998-1 features a gorgeous tropical dial. The case remains unpolished in my view. The original black “Base 1000” aluminum bezel is the best condition one I have ever seen. It includes an Extract of Archives from OMEGA dating to February 16, 1960, and delivered to a US Army base in Europe. This watch does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter. The watch was recently serviced by Alkis of Swiss Watch Repair. The watch comes with an Extract from The Archives.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

Heuer Carrera Reference 3647T Unpolished

This Heuer Carrera reference 3647T is a very cool example dating to circa 1966. The watch features an unpolished case, and is powered by the Valjoux calibre 92.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote back in 2013.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-66

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding OMEGA Speedmaster reference 105.012 and a classic vintage model. It could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line throughout 1963-1966.

The reference 105.012 were first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied OMEGA logo.

It has one of the most attractive dial, hand, and bezel combinations I have seen on any Speedmaster. It features the original flatter 105.012 pushers. The watch is powered by the iconic calibre 321, a calibre which was recently re-released to excitement of Speedmaster lovers.

The case was made by Huguenin Frères, and remains very crisp, showing its true form. The case is lightly polished and has its service crown. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. The reference 105.012 is highly prized and collected because it was the exact same model worn by Neil Armstrong to the Moon.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. I purchased the watch from the son of the original owner, who was an amateur race car driver and glider pilot. He loved speed and this was a faithful companion on his wrist! The son was selling it to help fund his daughter’s wedding.

Patek Philippe Ladies Aquanaut Diamond Bezel 5067A-001 Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5067A-001 is a very beautiful timepiece. The steel case measures 35mm in diameter. It features a very attractive diamond-set bezel. The watch was originally sold in 2016. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. It recently had the battery replaced. The watch comes from the family of the original owner.

OMEGA Reference 2383-5 Unpolished

This OMEGA reference 2383-5 is a very cool watch. The case is 35mm in diameter by 42mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished. It dates to the 1940s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Honeycomb Dial Oyster Precision Reference 6422

This Oyster Precision reference 6422 features a spectacular honeycomb dial. The watch dates to circa 1955. The case measures 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). It has been lightly polished, but is a strong timepiece. It is a manual-wind movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 6917 Unpolished w/ Papers

We are thrilled to offer this very exceptional Rolex Datejust reference 6917. It is a stunning example and is 18k yellow gold. We believe it to be unpolished. The case is 26mm in diameter and 33mm lug-to-lug. It is an absolutely stunning timepiece. It features an 6.23M serial dating to circa 1979. It was sold in 1981

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water test of 3 bar.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 16013 w/ Champagne Dial Unpolished

This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a very nice champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1987 with a 9.5M serial.

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It recently passed a water test of 1 bar.

Rolex Silver Dial Date Reference 1500 Unpolished

This Rolex Date is a super attractive example with a silver dial. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch has a 2.205M serial number dating to circa 1969.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great. The watch just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 16013 Unpolished w/ Papers

This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a very nice champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1987 with a 9.6M serial.

This watch does not include the original box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It recently passed a water test of 3 bar.

Rolex Straight Hand Explorer II Reference 1655

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

It dates to circa 1971 (2.73 million serial number).The case has been polished but is attractive. It features a very orange 24-hour hand, and warmer lume.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was just pressure tested and passed 3 bar.

IWC Big Pilot's Watch TOP GUN Miramar IW501902 Full Set

This IWC Big Pilot's Watch TOP GUN Miramar IW501902 is a super cool watch from IWC. The ceramic case measures 48mm in diameter and is highly legible! For all you Mavericks out there looking for a Charlie Blackwood, this might just break the ice.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was sold in 2014, and comes from the family of the original owner.

Glashutte Original Senator Moon Phase Skeletonised Edition 1-49-13-15-15-04 Full Set

This Glashutte Original is a very sophisticated timepiece. The 18k rose gold case measures 42mm in diameter. It is beautifully decorated on the movement. The MSRP was +$41,0000

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was sold in 2016, and comes from the family of the original owner.

Glashutte Original The Star Golden Dragon Rose Gold 1-65-01-60-60-04 W/ Papers & Hardshell Box

This Glashutte Original is a very sophisticated timepiece. The 18k rose gold case measures approximately 39mm in diameter. It is beautifully decorated with rubies and diamonds.

The watch comes with papers. It does not come with a wooden box, but has the hardshell cardboard box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from the family of the original owner.

Glashutte Original Star Autumn Leaf 1-90-00-11-11-04 W/ Full Set

This Glashutte Original is a very sophisticated timepiece. The 18k rose gold case measures approximately 39mm in diameter. It is beautifully decorated with diamonds and has a fantastic Mother of Pearl dial.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from the family of the original owner.

Corum Coin Watch

This is a very cool Corum coin watch. It is made of 18K gold. It is quartz powered. it measures approximately 32mm in diameter.

It does not come with box and papers. The quartz battery was just replaced.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Submariner Gilt Four Line 5512 PCG Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. This reference 5512 features a 693,XXX serial number dating to circa 1962. This example features the early PCG or Pointed Crown Guards case style. It features an incredible gilt dial with four lines of text in both gilt and silver font. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a 7206 Rivet bracelet with 80 end links dating to 1963. The bracelet has been polished. The luminous material is very attractive and original to the watch.

According to Rolex, the Submariner reference 5512’s production consisted of only 17,338 examples. The Submariner reference 5513’s production consisted of a much larger amount, 151,449 examples!

Both Eric and I were quite astonished at the number of 5512 Submariners produced being a mere 17,338, as we assumed it was much larger - in the tens of thousands. In simplified terms, there was only just about one 5512 produced for every ten 5513s produced (or 11.4% specifically).

Finding well preserved gilt Submariner models is incredibly difficult. This is truly one of the most special and attractive gilt Submariner reference 5512s we have come across.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with box or papers.

Learn more about reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Rolex Submariner “Tiffany & Co.” Reference 1680

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is an incredible watch with the special and highly sought after double-signed dial by Tiffany & Co.

The watch features a MK III dial with luminous tritium plots that into a very warm dial color. The case has been lightly polished but is very attractive. The watch features a 5.1M serial number and dates to circa 1979. It is is paired with a reference 93150 Oyster bracelet with 580 end links and a VD clasp code, also dating to crica 1979. The bracelet appears to be original to the watch.

Finding a special Tiffany & Co. signed Rolex sports watch is very difficult and we are extremely excited to share this watch!

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Big Crown Submariner Reference 5510 Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner reference 5510 is the final iteration of the “Big Crown” Submariner with the oversized 8mm crown. The model was introduced in 1958 remains one of the iconic and highly-coveted sports watches. It also marked the first Sub to utilize the calibre 1530.

The particular example is in outstanding condition with what appears to be worn, but unpolished case. It measures in at approximately 38mm in diameter. It is paired on a stretch rivet bracelet with the “Big Logo” dating to 1959. The bracelet has possibly later 80 end links.

To learn more about the evolution of the Rolex Submariner, see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article on HODINKEE.

Rolex MilSub Submariner Reference 5513 Royal Navy

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches ever produced and they have a cult like following. Many collectors cherish the earliest designs of the 1950s, while some prefer the quintessential design to be Submariners with crown guards which was introduced with the reference 5512/5513 case references. Of the most desired Submariners, models such as the “James Bond” or “Big Crown” come to mind, alongside the elusive “MilSub” models.

We are thrilled to offer this Rolex MilSub reference 5513 issued to the Royal Navy. The case has been lightly polished, but is very attractive. The watch features a 3.9M serial dating to circa 1975 and features an issued engraving on the case back dating to 1975. It is paired on a nylon strap, with fixed springbars. It features the original rare “sword” hands for military specifications. There are approximately 1,110 MilSub examples produced across three references (5513, the double reference 5513/5517, and 5517) of which there are approximately 300 known.

The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Learn more about these watches within Charlie Dunne’s article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Overall, this is one of the most attractive MilSubs I have ever seen and these are arguably the coolest Submariners ever made!

Universal Geneve Compax ‘Nina Rindt’ Reference 885103/02 Unpolished

After a near 35 year horological hiatus, Universal Geneve is expected to make its long awaited return to watch making in the fall of 2026. With all the attention surrounding the brand, it's hard to ignore the iconic Compax. This is a fantastic example of the Universal Geneve “Nina Rindt” Compax. The case appears to be unpolished. It has a blue central seconds hand.

It is a mark 1 example, with unpainted hour and minute hands, thin subdial hands, and an applied U. It also features the original “dot over 90” bezel. It has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and is 36mm in diameter.

This watch has a serial number dating to circa 1965.

It does not include the original box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Ref 3319/7 in 18K YG Unpolished

This Patek Philippe in 18k yellow gold is a very elegant ladies’ wristwatch. The watch features a beautiful mesh bracelet that measures just shy of 19mm at the widest point and tapers down to approximately 15mm near the clasp. The bracelet also features intricate finishing that plays with the light. It is a manual winding watch. It appears to be unpolished. It will fit a wrist approximately 6 inches in circumference.

This watch was worn by Danielle Fishel on Dancing with The Stars!

An extract from the Patek Philippe archives was ordered in September 2025. The watch was recently serviced and is keeping good time. It does not come with a box or papers.

Tiffany Movado Guilloche Dial Ref 48461 in 14K YG Unpolished

This is a very attractive time-only Movado wristwatch retailed by Tiffany & Co. The watch dates to the 1950s. It features luminous Arabic numerals. The watch is powered by automatic movement. The case measures 34mm in diameter and 40mm lug-to-lug. The case is 14k gold and appears to be unpolished. The dial features a stunning guilloche motif, with pointed applied indices and alpha hands.

Originally gifted to Mr. Rea A. Axline, a brilliant chemist who discovered and patented anti-corrosion coatings for machinery, he amassed a large fortune during the Second World War in which his discovery was vital to the Allied Power’s victory.

After his death, he and his wife, an artist, donated $30 million dollars to the San Diego Art Museum and San Diego MOCA.

This Movado timepiece was gifted to Mr. Axline for his time as the mayor of Lake Success, a tiny village on the border of Queens and Long Island, and a fact that was otherwise lost to time.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with the original box or papers.

Rolex Rail Dial Mark 2 Great White Sea-Dweller Ref. 1665

This Rolex “Great White” Sea-Dweller reference 1665 is a very attractive watch with great lume.

The case and bracelet have been polished. The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. It features the Mk II Rail Dial.

It dates to circa 1978-1979 with a 5.7371M serial. It is paired on an Oyster bracelet with H date code dating to circa 1983.

The watch does not come box and papers. It features a TrueDome D39 crystal. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Batgirl GMT-Master II Reference 126710BLNR Full Set Unworn

Rolex “Batgirl” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLNR is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

This reference 126710BLRO is paired with its steel Jubilee bracelet. It is “full set” with its original box and papers.

It comes from the original owner. The was purchased in 2019.

Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master II 126710BLRO on Jubilee w/ Box & Papers Unpolished New Condition

Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch is in brand new condition. It is partially stickered.

The watch comes with box and papers. It dates to 2019 and comes from the original owner.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex Black Dial Daytona 116500LN Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a black dial and black Cerachrom bezel. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past.

The watch was sold in 2019. It comes “full set” with original box and papers from the original owner.

It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Mk I Great White Sea-Dweller Reference 1665

This Rolex “Great White” Sea-Dweller reference 1665 is a very attractive watch with great lume.

The case and bracelet have been polished. The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. It features the Mk I dial.

It dates to circa 1978 with a 5.38M serial. It is paired on an folded Oyster bracelet with C date code dating to circa 1978.

The watch does not come box and papers. It features a TrueDome D39 crystal. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Kermit Flat 4 Submariner Ref 16610 Full Set

This Rolex Submariner “Kermit” Submariner Reference 16610 is an awesome sports watch. It features the “Flat 4” insert which collectors love. This Submariner is a cult classic. The watch was sold in 2004. It features an F serial dating to circa 2003-2004. The case measures 40mm in diameter. It has been polished in the past.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Rolex Red Submariner Ref 1680

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa 1974 with a 3.78M serial. The case has been polished The dial and hands have been relumed.

It is paired on a Rolex folded Oyster bracelet dating to circa 1988.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

Eberhard Automatic Textured Dial

This Eberhard is a cool time-only wristwatch that dates to the 1950s. The stainless steel case measures 36mm in diameter and approximately 43mm lug-to-lug. The watch features a pinstripe dial and applied alternating Arabic numeral and arrow-shaped indices. The watch features dauphine hands. The case appears unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

Rolex GMT-Master Pepsi Ref 1675

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

This example features a 5.4M serial number, dating to circa 1978. The C&I bracelet dates to 1978.

The case has been lightly polished, but is nice. The lume is very attractive on the dial. The seconds hand has been relumed. The insert is a later service insert.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Heuer Silver Print Carrera Ref 2447N Unpolished

The only time better than now to buy a vintage Heuer was back in the 1960s! The Carrera is an iconic sports chronograph which is one of the most versatile watches to have in a collection. This is a very attractive example with the black dial. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36 millimeters in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. The watch is powered by the Valjoux manual-winding calibre 72. The watch dates to the late 1960s.

It comes on an unsigned buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TUDOR Submariner Ref 7928

This TUDOR Submariner reference 7928 is a very cool sports watch. The case has been polished. It measures 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The lume on the hands and dial have been relumed. The serial is a 505,XXX dating to circa 1966. The watch is an attractive and fair price for a vintage Submariner. If you are looking for an affordable vintage TUDOR this is a very cool option.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It passed a pressure test for 5 bar. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

TUDOR Monte Carlo Reference 7159/0 Unpolished

The Monte Carlo reference 7159/0 is an awesome and rare chronograph in the TUDOR catalog. The watch is highly admired based on its vibrant orange accents and extremely cool dial design. This example dates to circa 1974 with an 826,XXX serial.

The case appears to be unpolished. The case measures 38mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). The watch is paired on a Rolex 7836 folded-rivet bracelet. The bracelet is lightly polished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. The watch just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Ref 145.012-67

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is a cool OMEGA Speedmaster reference 145.012-67 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to the excitement of many Speedy lovers.

The case has been polished. It is paired on a later 1171 OMEGA bracelet dating to 1972.

The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The Omega Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article).

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Reference 5508 Glossy Dial

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic sports watches that was introduced in 1953. It is a model that represents the greatest attributes of the manufacturer. The reference 5508 began production in circa 1958 and lasted to 1962. It is also the last of the no-crown-guard Submariners. This is an insanely attractive example that dates to the tail end of the reference’s production in circa 1962. The glossy dial is very beautiful with warm-yellow lume. It also features the "exclamation point" at six o'clock. The case measures 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). It has been lightly polished, but is strong.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch recently passed a water resistance text of 5 bar. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

For those looking for a special vintage Submariner, this is a an incredible option!

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex Ladies Datejust Reference 69038 with Pleiades Dial in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Ladies Datejust reference 69038 is an exceptional watch. It features a rare pleiades dial with diamonds. The bezel is set with diamonds and sapphires and the lugs are set with diamonds. The watch is paired on a firm presidential bracelet. Both the watch and bracelet are made of 18k yellow gold. The watch measures approximately 26mm in diameter and 32.5mm lug-to-lug. These watches were a fortune back in the day at retail. The serial is a 9.5M dating to circa 1988.

We do not know the service history but it is running well. The watch does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Floating Cosmograph Zenith Daytona Black Dial Two-Tone 16523

The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. The case has been lightly polished but is very attractive. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This two-tone Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16523 dates to circa 1988. It has a R9 serial number, which denotes the earliest Zenith Daytonas and it incredibly rare. It features a very valuable Floating Cosmograph Daytona dial. These dials are very special and many dealers would purchase these watches to add the dial and handset into early gold Zenith Daytonas!

It is paired on a 78360 two-tone Oyster bracelet with correct 403 end links. It features an R8 bracelet code dating to 1993.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

For those looking for an amazing two-tone Zenith Daytona, this is a fantastic watch.

Urwerk UR-106 ‘Only Watch’ Full Set Mint Condition

This Urwerk reference UR-106 was made as a Piece Unique for Only Watch. The watch features an aggressive case shape which is crafted from titanium with a black PVD coating. The dimensions measure approximately 50 x 35mm. The watch features satellite hours indication which gives the appearance of analog and digital time. It is powered by the automatic calibre UR-6.01. For those looking for something eclectic, and highly interesting this is a great opportunity.

Despite our passion leaning towards vintage watches, there are certainly a number of modern watches that are undeniably awesome. Urwerk watches represent forward thinking watchmaking and get just about everyone excited - whether you are simply curious about watches or are amazed at the linear format on the timepiece. This is truly a fun watch to wear.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of the watch but it is running well. It was previously sold at Phillips for the 2015 “Only Watch” charity auction in favour of research on Duchenne muscular dystrophy, and belonged to an incredible collector! It comes with the 2 year International Warranty paperwork from Urwerk dating to 2020.

Rolex Submariner Two-Tone Ref 16613 Full Set Unpolished As New

The Rolex Submariner is an icon in the world of dive watches, and perhaps the most recognizable sports watch in the world.

The reference 16613 is a two-tone watch with a case and bracelet in stainless steel and 18k yellow gold. It features a black unidirectional aluminum bezel and an Oyster bracelet. The watch measures 40mm in diameter. It features an A serial number which is period correct. The watch is in immaculate and As New condition. It is incredibly cool and uncommon to find a watch like this.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers and was originally sold in 1999, in Hawaii. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

Rolex Submariner 5512 Meters First Dial

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. This reference 5512 features a 1.83M serial number dating to circa 1968. This example features a “meters first” matte dial with very attractive tritium lume. The case has been polished, but it is a very attractive Sub.

Learn more about reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with box or papers.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Master Ultra-Thin Perpetual Calendar Q130842J Full Set Unpolished

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Master Ultra-Thin Perpetual Calendar watch is a great and elegant complicated dress watch in stainless steel. The case measures 39mm in diameter. It appears unpolished. It is 9.2mm thick. It features a perpetual calendar complication with subdials for the day, date, and month as well as apertures for the year and moonphase. It is powered by the automatic Jaeger-LeCoultre calibre 868.

The watch comes as a “full-set” with the box and papers. It was sold in 2021. It has an 8 year warranty from JLC and is running well.

Rolex “Zenith” Daytona Two-Tone Champagne Dial Ref. 16523

The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This “Zenith” Daytona features an S serial number dating the watch to circa 1994. The reference 16523 is a two-tone watch with an 18k yellow gold and stainless steel case and Oyster bracelet. This example features a champagne dial with black subdial tracks. The watch appears to be lightly polished but is attractive.

It is paired on a 78393 two-tone Oyster bracelet with correct 403 end links. It features an T8 bracelet code dating to 1995.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

For those looking for an amazing two-tone Zenith Daytona, this is a fantastic watch.

By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe Retailed by Tiffany & Co Reference 410P Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer this extraordinary Patek Philippe reference 410 in platinum perfect for a connoisseur of the manufacturer. The watch features a beautiful retailer signature of Tiffany & Co. It has applied-Arabic numerals with feuille (leaf) hands. The platinum case is unpolished. It measured 22mm in diameter (36mm lug-to-lug). The watch is powered by the highly important calibre 9-90. The watch dates to the mid 1930s and features a spectacular case back engraving.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. An Extract from the Archives has been ordered.

Heuer Autavia Reference 2446 First Execution Big Subs Unpolished

The Heuer Autavia is recognized by collectors as one of the great sports chronographs of the 1960s. Along with many of its contemporaries of the era, it is powered by the Valjoux 72. This example dates to circa 1962. It is the first execution case of the Autavia with larger subdials. The dimensions are 38mm (48mm lug-to-lug). The case is unpolished. It is paired on a very nice Gay Fréres bracelet for Heuer.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex GMT-Master MK 2 Matte Dial Reference 1675 Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a spectacular 1675 featuring a 3.3M serial number, dating to circa 1973. The case is unpolished and in fantastic condition. It is paired on a Rolex Oyster folded link 7836 bracelet with 380 end links which are correct. The aluminum bezel insert is extremely attractive with a faded blue and faded red. The MK II dial has super attractive lume.

We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced.

OMEGA Dynamic Reference 166.107 w/ Integrated Bracelet

This OMEGA Dynamic is a very cool model with an integrated bracelet which is very long. The case design is very nice on the wrist and wears 41mm in diameter. It appears to be a reference 166.107 with an automatic calibre. The case has been polished in the past.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Reference 145.006-67 in 18K Gold

The OMEGA Seamaster reference 145.006-67 is a very nice hefty chronograph. The case is 37mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). This example has a nice patina developing on the side of the case. The case has been polished in the past.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Reference 2505-21

This OMEGA Reference 2505-21 features a super attractive dial with leaf hands. The case is a jumbo and measures 37mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). The watch wears so nice on the wrist. It is powered by the manual wind calibre 268. The watch dates to circa 1959. The case has been polished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Reference 2374/3 Unpolished

This OMEGA Reference 2374/3 is a very attractive mid-century timepiece. The case measures 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). The watch wears very well on the wrist and has spectacular lugs. The dial is very attractive with luminous Roman numerals. It is powered by a bumper automatic calibre. The watch dates to circa 1944. The case is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Reference 2791-1 Unpolished

This OMEGA Reference 2791-1 is a very attractive and rare mid-century timepiece. The case measures 35mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The watch wears very well on the wrist and has nice twisted lugs. The dial is very attractive dial with applied-Arabic numerals. The watch dates to circa 1954. The case is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

This is a very cool and ornately decorated Waltham pocket watch dating to circa 1907. The 14K yellow gold case has some great floral patterns. The dial is in very nice shape. It measures 34mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

Rolex Root Beer GMT-Master Reference 1675/3

This Rolex “Root Beer” reference 1675/3 is a very fun GMT. Between the lugs it is stamped 1675, but because it is a two tone is designated as a 1675/3. This example dates to circa 1974. The case is lightly polished, but strong. It appears the hands are slightly newer with SuperLuminova.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Cream Dial Reference 16234 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 16234 is an awesome unpolished example with a very attractive creamy dial. It is in very mint condition and will be undoubtedly cherished by the next owner. The watch measures 36mm in diameter. and the W serial dates to circa 1995. It is paired on its original 62510H bracelet with 555 B end links. It will fit a wrist above 7.5 inches. The fluted bezel is made of white gold and it maintains a case back sticker on the back.

This watch does not include a box and papers. The service history of this watch is not known, but it is running very well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Datejust Reference 68274 Unpolished

This Rolex ladies’ Datejust reference 68274 is an awesome unpolished example. The case measures 30mm in diameter. It is paired on a nice Jubilee bracelet.

This watch does not include a box and papers. The service history of this watch is not known, but it is running very well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Polaris Reference Q9068670 Full Set Unpolished

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Polaris is an incredible and sharp looking sports watch. This Q9068670 was sold in 2022. It measures 42mm in diameter. The watch is paired on the original rubber strap which is very comfortable and sturdy. It is powered by the in-house automatic calibre 899A/1 . It features a 38 hour power reserve. These movements are very much performance oriented, and have undergone 1,000 hours of testing before leaving the JLC factory. The Polaris and the Reversos are among my favorite offerings in the Jaeger-LeCoultre catalog.

This watch comes with box and papers. The service history of this watch is not known, but it is running very well.

IWC Ingenieur Sport Chronograph IW380901 Full Set Unpolished

The IWC Ingenieur is one of those sports watches you weren’t sure you needed until right now! The Sport Chronograph reference IW380901 which was produced in only 500 examples. The case is made of titanium and measures 44.3mm in diameter. It’s a true big boy!

The watch is paired on the original strap which is very comfortable and sturdy. It has a 68 hour power reserve, so don't worry if you set it down and wear your Big Pilot!

This watch comes with box and papers. This example was sold in 2017. The service history of this watch is not known, but it is running very well.

Rolex Air-King Black Dial Reference 14000 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is an awesome example with a rare and beautiful black dial. The watch features a NB serial number and dates to circa 1991. The case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Ladies Oyster Perpetual Reference 67230 Unpolished

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual is a super attractive model. The case is unpolished and it has a super crisp bezel. It measures 25mm in diameter and 32mm in length.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Early Wristwatch From Circa 1917

This antique wristwatch is a very cool and unusual timepiece by Heuer. The cushion case measures 33mm in diameter. It features a nice dial with luminous painted numerals.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Reference OT 2884 In 18K Rose Gold

This OMEGA Seamaster reference OT 2884 is a very special chronograph. It features an 18k rose gold case which has been lightly polished in the past. It has a spectacular dial. The case is 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the important calibre 321.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

OMEGA Time-Only Wristwatch w/ Fancy Lugs in 18K Gold

This OMEGA is a very rare and interesting timepiece. It features very eccentric lugs, and beautiful “foil” numerals. The case is 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is a beautiful center seconds time-only model dating to the 1950s.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

OMEGA De Ville Chronograph Reference 105.005-65 In 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This OMEGA De Ville reference 105.005 is an awesome chronograph. It features an 18k yellow gold case which is unpolished. It has a super attractive dial. The watch dates to 1965. The case is 35mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the important calibre 321.

The watch does not come with box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Blue Dial Reference 126300 Full Set Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 126300 is a very attractive rolex with a blue dial. It is 41mm and has a nice Oyster bracelet. It is unpolished.

The watch comes with box and papers. The watch was sold in 2021.

By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Champagne Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is a super nice watch in outstanding condition. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. It features an incredibly attractive champagne dial. It features an X serial, and dates to circa 1991.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Zenith Daytona 16520 White Dial Unpolished Box & Papers

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520 with a white dial. It has a W serial dating it to circa late 1995. It was sold in 1996. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988 until circa 2000. We believe the case is unpolished and it has an unpolished bracelet. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Hulk Submariner Ref 116610LV Unpolished Full Set & Service Papers

This Rolex Submariner reference 116610LV is an awesome sports watch. We believe the case is unpolished. For those looking for a sick Submariner, the Hulk is a great option. It is an epic green dial and green bezel Rolex that screams “MONEY!” ‘Money’ by Pink Floyd is a fantastic song with a great bass line.

The watch comes full set with box and papers. It dates to 2015. The watch comes with service papers from 2023 (they mention they did not polish the case). It recently passed water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

Enicar Sherpa Super Jet 146/003 Unpolished

The Enicar Sherpa Super Jet models are extremely cool and uncommon watches. This example is in very fun with a silvery champagne dial. The case made by E. Piquerez SA (also called EPSA). Enicar was way ahead of other companies with the large dimensions. The case appears to be unpolished and is approximately 40mm in diameter.

This watch does not include box or papers.. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Heuer Autavia Tropical Reference 1163 Unpolished

Heuer Autavias are some of coolest vintage watches. This example is one of the nicest Heuers with a tropical brown color and very nice colorful hands and registers. The watch is paired on a Heuer bracelet made by Gay Frères. They feature very large cases which are very enjoyable on the wrist. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 43mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). It has one of the most attractive bezel inserts on any GMT I’ve ever seen and I would love to own this watch in my personal collection.

This example dates to circa 1970. It was recently serviced and is running great. It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex GMT-Master II Ref 126711CHNR Box & Papers Unpolished Brand New Partially Stickered

Rolex “Root Beer” GMT-Master II reference 126711CHNR is a fantastic modern sports watch. It captures the iconic DNA from the two-tone vintage models. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, the GMT remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

We believe the watch is unpolished. It comes “full set” with its original box and papers. The papers date the watch to 2019.

The case is partially stickered. It is brand new.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Heuer Autavia Reference 1163 on GF Bracelet w/ Box

This Heuer Viceroy Autavia Reference 1163 is a very cool sports watch. This is the non-Viceroy style - which features the tachymeter bezel. The lume is very attractive. The watch is a robust automatic chronograph and a great value in vintage Heuer. It 204,XXX serial.

It features an automatic Heuer-Léonidas-made movement, the Caliber 11. The case measures 42mm in diameter (approximately 47.5mm lug to lug). It is paired with a Gay Frères Swiss-made stainless steel Heur Bracelet.

The case appears to be unpolished but the case back is polished.

The hands are replaced with Monaco hands.

The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a box We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Enicar Sherpa Super Dive 144-35-02 Unpolished

The Enicar Sherpa Super Dive models are extremely cool and uncommon watches. This example is in very fun with a matte black dial. The case made by E. Piquerez SA (also called EPSA). Enicar was way ahead of other companies with the large dimensions. The case appears to be unpolished and is approximately 40mm in diameter.

This watch does not include box or papers.. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Black Dial Daytona Reference 116500LN Full Set Unpolished Brand New

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a black dial and black Cerachrom bezel. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. The watch is brand new..

The watch was sold in 2022. It comes “full set” with original box and papers.

Rolex White Dial Daytona Reference 116520 CPO Pouch & Service Card

The Rolex Daytona reference 116520 is a fantastic automatic sports watch.. The case has been polished. It features a D serial dating to circa 2005-2006. The dial is slightly creamy and very attractive.

The watch comes with the CPO box, pouch and and service card. It was serviced by Rolex in 2023. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Air-King Ref 14010 Silver Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished. It features an N serial and dates to circa 1991.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Air-King Ref 14010M with Pink Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished and has a D serial dating to circa 2005-2006.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Neat Fonts Submariner Reference 5512

This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is an awesome example with attractive orange lume. The watch has a 1.6M serial dating to circa 1967. It features the original bezel insert. The case has been lightly polished, but is attractive. It is paired on a C&I Rolex bracelet dating to 1968. The lume and dial are extremely attractive.

It features a replaced Triplock crown.

Learn more about the iconic Submariner within Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article “Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It just passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Heuer Camaro 7220NT 3rd Execution

Heuer is renowned for its rich history with automotive raving, and one of the great models from the golden age of the brand is the Camaro. The Camaro line has always been a sleeper when compared to the more discussed siblings such as the Autavia and Carrera.

The watch is powered by the manual wind calibre Valjoux 72. The N in the reference designates the black dial, and T for Tachymeter. The case measures 37mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It has been polished in the past.

The watch comes does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Small Crown Submariner Reference 6536

The Rolex “Small Crown” Submariner reference 6536 is a classic reference. The dial on this example is a matte service dial, which I believe dates to the 1980s. The hands appear to be service replacement hands from the same period as the dial, while the second hand is later. The case has been polished and measures 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). It features the original bezel which has a nice blue color. It is paired on a nylon strap. The watch dates to circa 1957 with a 0.229M serial.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Heuer Calculator 110.633N on Bracelet

Back in the day, Calculator watches were looked at as super nerdy. NOT TODAY! Today, they are considered ALPHA! We’re optimistic about pocket protectors coming back! We just curated a collection of incredible Heuer Calculator watches. It could take a lifetime to find these watches in this condition. Also, they will garner respect from every woman you encounter. A lot of people reached out to us about the OMEGA Equinoxe, but this is a way cooler watch if we’re being honest.

This Calculator reference 110.633N is an epic example paired on a very rare bracelet. The case measures 45mm in diameter. These bezels are extremely cool and fun to use. The colors are incredible.

Adrian van Hooydonk, BMW Design Chief wears one of these.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Adrian van Hooydonk, BMW Design Chief wears one of these.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Tropical PCG GMT-Master Reference 1675 Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is an out of this world GMT-Master reference 1675 featuring a 0.504M serial number dating to circa 1960. The watch was sold in 1961. It is one of the earliest reference 1675s and features a beautiful tropical dial. This watch was sold in The Bahamas, and the dial faded to this beautiful color from exposure to the tropical sun.

The watch overall is in fantastic condition. The aluminum bezel insert is extremely attractive and it features the early “coke bottle top” bezel with sharp knurling. It features the pointed crown guard or “PCG” style case which is seen until circa 1963-1964. We believe the case is unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes with box and papers. This is a Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 I would love to personally own and consider a grail example. Of the earliest “Chapter Ring, Pointed-Crown-Guard” models, I have only held two that I believe to be unpolished - one formerly belonging to The Great Charlie Dunne, and the other being this one! It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5065J-001 Unpolished Extra Straps Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5065J-001 is one of the great contemporary sports watches. At 38mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions that can appeal to both vintage and modern enthusiasts. This example is super mint. We believe the case is unpolished. The hands and dial feature tritium lume which has a nice warm color. According to the Certificate of Origin, it was sold in 1999 - the first year of production. It is powered by the calibre 315 SC and is on view through its exhibition caseback.

The watch comes full set with box, the Certificate of Origin, corresponding papers and an extra black rubber strap. For those looking for an incredible gold Aquanaut, you cannot go wrong with this one!

Rolex Concorde GMT-Master 1675 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The present watch is an exceptionally preserved and unpolished example of a GMT-Master in 18k gold with an extremely rare straight hour and minute hands nicknamed the "Concorde" due to its appearance in a Rolex advertisement about Concorde pilots from the late 1960s.

I believe one special characteristic those watches that are original have versus those where people have swapped the hour and minute hands recently is that the minute hand is extremely thin on the real ones and is also very thin in the original Rolex advertisement, whereas many I see today have thick minute hands.

I have only sold a handful of Rolex Concordes, and this is the nicest one I have sold. It is featured in the Reference Points video. I sold another amazing example on bracelet years back, but that one was not on the gold 7206 bracelet like this one (which is nearly impossible to find). This bracelet was added to the watch.

We believe the case is unpolished. The beautiful bevels on the edge of the case and perfectly faded bezel insert get me excited each time I see this watch.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE. You can also read about the iconic watch in Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Submariner Gilt Dial Reference 5513

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic sports watches and a model that represents the greatest attributes of the manufacturer. I am very excited about this example, as it has an outstanding gilt dial! The puffy yellow lume and gilt font make for an outstanding impression each time you look down at the watch on your wrist. The case is lightly polished, but it is very strong and crisp.

This example has a 1.3 million serial number dating the watch to circa 1966. The watch is paired on a tight and crisp Rolex 7206 Swiss-made Oyster rivet bracelet with 80 end links that is in phenomenal condition!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Learn more about these watches within Charlie Dunne’s article “ Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512 ’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-4 Unpolished

The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. This an absolutely incredible reference 2998-4. It has great luminous hour markers and matching hands. The case is unpolished. It is paired on the original bracelet . The crown is original. It features the original dot over 90 bezel.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The OMEGA Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of the watch, but it is running well. It came from the family of the original owner in Ohio.

Rolex Explorer I Ref 214270 Full Set

This Rolex Explorer I reference 214270 is an amazing modern sports watch. The case is 39mm in diameter - which they do not make anymore!!! The case appears lightly polished. We believe the watch dates to 2012. It is the Mk1 short hand version. As James Stacey once said when discussing the 214270, “Whether you are negotiating your day to day, or navigating uncharted territory, it is all good the Explorer way”. We agree!

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

OMEGA Speedmaster Moonwatch Alaska Project Unpolished Full Set

This OMEGA Speedmaster “Alaska Project” reference 311.32.42.30.04.001 is one of the coolest iterations of the iconic Speedy. The case measures 42mm in diameter. It is powered by the calibre 1861.

It is a limited edition of 1970 examples. The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It was produced in circa 2008.

The comes with box and papers. It is paired on an aftermarket JB Champion style bracelet, but the original white straps and bracelet come with the watch and are seen below. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Gallet MultiChron Calendar Chronograph Valjoux 72C in 14K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Gallet triple date chronograph is an outstanding timepiece, The case is 14K yellow gold. We believe it is unpolished. It measures approximately 40mm in diameter. It features female springbars.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Two Tone Submariner Bluesy Reference 16613 Full Set

This Rolex Submariner reference 16613 with a U serial dating to circa 1998. The case is in has been polished.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Ceinture in 18K Unpolished

This Cartier Ceinture is a very elegant timepiece in 18k yellow gold. The case measures approximately 27mm in diameter. We believe it is unpolished. It is paired on an aftermarket lizard strap. It has an original Cartier buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012-67

This OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 145.012 dates to 1967 (with a -67 case production). The watch is incredibly attractive with beautiful lume! The case has been lightly polished in the past, but is nice. It measures 42mm. It is powered by a manual wind movement (Caliber 321 made by OMEGA). It is paired on a period correct 1039 bracelet dating to circa 1970.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, see my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!.

Cartier Santos Or Massif “Gordon Gekko” in 18k Yellow Gold Full Set

This Cartier Santos Or Massif is a beautiful 18K yellow gold timepiece. This is the same model worn by Gordon Gekko in the 1987 film, Wall Street. The case measures 29mm in diameter (41mm lug to lug). The case is lightly polished. For those looking for an elegant and amazing gold timepiece, this is a great choice.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Date Reference 6827 Unpolished Two-Tone with Radial Dial

This Rolex Date reference 6827 is an awesome watch. It features a steel and 14k yellow gold case and original two-tone solid-link Oyster bracelet. The 14k gold bezel, crown, and center links have developed a nice rosy color / patina and indicates the watch was originally destined for the American market. The watch measures 31mm in diameter (37mm lug to lug). The case is unpolished and it has a rare and beautiful “radial” dial.

It comes from the grandson of the original owner, who was a prominent lady in Michigan. The grandson has had this watch for over 20 years. He would occasionally wind it up, set the time and date, and then lock it back up, but has decided to part with it to help fund the purchase of a Rolex in a larger size that he can wear.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar. Vintage Rolex watches in 31mm are extremely uncommon and have become extremely popular, probably partially due to the fact that @watchabbie wears a 31mm vintage Rolex!
