By Charlie Dunne

Cartier in 14K Rose Gold on Tiffany & Co. C+B Bracelet Unpolished

This Cartier is a very sophisticated timepiece in 14K rose gold. It features the Cartier NY style signature on the dial. The case measures 21mm in width (36mm lug-to-lug). The movement is produced by Girard-Perregaux and features a US import code. It is paired on a very rare C+B bracelet with the retailer Tiffany & Co enscribed which was added and looks phenomenal. The case is unpolished. The dial is extremely beautiful, and features painted-Roman numerals. Interestingly, the 10 is painted XI instead of 11!

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers. The watch comes from the family of the original family a few years ago.

Rolex Midas Cellini Reference 4126 NOS Full Set

This Rolex Midas Cellini reference 4126 is absolutely incredible. The watch is in new old stock condition. It measures 28mm in diameter (28mm lug-to-lug).

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It comes full set with box or papers.

Breguet Two-Tone Wristwatch From 1970 w/ Papers

Breguet is having a moment, and this is the perfect watch for a Breguet connoisseur. It is an incredibly rare model dating to circa 1970. The case is made of yellow and white gold and remains unpolished. It measures 26mm in diameter, 38mm lug-to-lug). It features a manual wing movement and applied-Roman numerals.

The watch does not come with box. It does come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

IWC Pilot Mark XII Reference IW3241 Unpolished Full Set

This IWC Mark XII reference IW3241 is a fantastic watch. It is paired on a very cool IWC bracelet which is very high quality. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (44mm lug-to-lug).

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

This comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great and keeping good time! It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Vacheron Constantin Overseas Chronometer Reference 42050

This Vacheron Constantin Overseas is a very cool watch with a great design. The case is strong. We believe it has been lightly polished. It measures 37mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is powered by the calibre 1310. We do not know the year of production.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

OMEGA Reference 14159 Unpolished

This OMEGA reference 14159 is a fantastic timepiece with an unusual design. The dial is extremely attractive. The case is unpolished. It measures 36.5mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug), and feels quite large on the wrist.

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

Rolex Matte Dial Submariner Reference 5513 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is a superb example. The watch dates to circa 1982. The lume is very attractive has a nice warm yellow tone. The case is unpolished and It is paired on the Rolex Oyster 93150 bracelet with 580 end links. For those looking for a spectacular Sub, this is certainly a great choice!

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great and keeping good time!

Rolex GMT-Master II Coke Reference 16710

This Rolex “Coke” GMT-Master reference 16710 is a very fun watch. This example dates to circa 2001-2002. The case has been polished in the past. It measures 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug).

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well!

Tudor Marine Nationale Submariner Reference 9401/0 w/ Copy of Provenance Ledger

This Tudor Submariner reference 9401/0 is a very cool and attractive watch which was issued to the Marine Nationale. The watch dates to circa 1975. The case has been lightly polished. It features a replacement bezel, which is incredibly attractive.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with a copy of the Marine Nationale ledger.

Rolex Date Reference 69160 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 69160 is a very nice timepiece. It measures 26mm in diameter (32mm lug-to-lug). The case is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1994.

It does not come with box and papers. The watch was just serviced and is running well.

Rolex Roman Dial Day-Date Reference 118208

This Rolex Day-Date reference 118208 is a very fun timepiece. It measures 36mm in diameter. The 18k yellow gold case has been polished in the past. It dates to circa 2000.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Heuer Monaco Reference 1133B Unpolished Full Set w/ Racing Provenance

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement, the calibre 11. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic. This example is in phenomenal condition. The case is unpolished, and the dial is stunning. In addition, it is paired on the original Heuer strap with buckle.

The watch comes with box and papers and has racing provenance. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Zenith El Primero Chronomaster Gold Reference 18.3200.3600/69.C901 Unpolished Full Set

The Zenith El Primero is one of the legendary chronographs. This reference 18.3200.3600/69.C901 is a fantastic interpretation of the sports chronograph. The rose gold case is very crisp and measures 37.5mm in diameter (45.5mm lug-to-lug).

It comes box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

H. Moser & Cie Streamliner Tourbillon Rainbow Reference 6804-1201 Full Set New

This H. Moser & Cie Streamliner is a very special timepiece with the “rainbow” tourbillon. The watch is new. It was sold in December 2022. It is powered by the in-house calibre HMC-804. It features 60 baguette-cut rainbow-coloured sapphires. The case measures 40mm in diameter.

It comes with box and papers from the original owner.

Citizen Windsurfing D120

This Citizen Windsurfing D120 is a super cool wristwatch. It is an awesome throwback to those who grew up wearing the Casios and Citizens wristwatches in middle school and perfect if you have a desire to revisit the past nostalgia. The D120 supports dual time zones and/or time + date in one display. All functions work as they should and the watch is keeping accurate time.

Casio Yacht Timer TRW-31

This Casio Yacht Timer TRW-31 is a fantastic multi-purpose timepiece. In addtion to the chronograph, the top of the dial incorporates a yacht timer. It is an awesome throwback to those who grew up wearing the Casio wristwatches in middle school and perfect if you have a desire to revisit the past nostalgia of the brand.

Patek Philippe Tourbillon 5101P-010 Unpolished Full Set

The Patek Philippe reference 5101P-101 is a juggernaut of a timepiece. These references were introduced in 2003. This example was sold in 2005. The watch is powered by the calibre TO 28-20 REC 10J PS - a chronometer movement with 10-day power reserve. These are some of these are some of the highest-grade movements within Patek Philippe’s recent catalog. The platinum case is unpolished and measures 30mm in diameter (51.5mm lug-to-lug). It is estimated that 500 examples were produced in platinum.

It comes with certificate of origin, box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18038 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 is a super cool example with a beautiful dial. It has an an 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. The bracelet has a little play, but is not overly stretched. The watch features an 5.3 serial number dating to circa 1978.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Blue Mosaic Datejust Reference 1603 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this mosaic-dial reference 1603 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1971 and features a 2.84M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a US Made Rolex Jubilee bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

F.P.Journe Chronomètre à Résonance 38mm in Platinum w/ Box, Extract & Receipt

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. Mr. Journe is one of the most significant watchmakers of the past century and the Chronomètre à Résonance is arguably his most iconic model.

This 38mm F.P.Journe Chronomètre à Résonance in platinum with a rose gold movement. This white gold dial is sensational. The case is unpolished.

This watch comes from the original owner who purchased new in 2006 at Cellini. The watch comes with the box, Extract and receipt.

Tudor Marine Nationale Submariner Reference 9401/0 w/ Copy of Provenance Ledger

This Tudor Submariner reference 9401/0 is a very cool and attractive watch which was issued to the Marine Nationale. The watch dates to circa 1975. The case has been lightly polished. It features a service bezel insert, which is incredibly attractive.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with a copy of the Marine Nationale ledger.

Rolex Silver Dial Datejust Reference 16200 Unpolished w/ Hangtag

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16200 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1991 and features a X serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555B end links.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Movado Calendomatic Unpolished

This Movado is a very interesting complicated model. It measures 33.5mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). It has a super cool stepped case with drilled lugs.

The watch is in very nice condition with an unpolished case. We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

OMEGA Reference 2639-2

This OMEGA Reference 2639-2 is a super cool timepiece. The dial is very attractive. The case appears lightly polished, but strong. It measures 35mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug).

We do not know the service history, but it is running great. It does not come with box or papers.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Daytona Reference 6265 w/ Tropical Dial

This is an incredibly attractive Rolex Daytona reference 6265 with tropical subdials. The case and bracelet have been polished, but it is incredibly attractive. This watch dates to circa 1971 with a 2.87M serial number. It measures 37mm in diameter and features period correct MK 2 pushers. They were recently replaced back to period correct Mark 2 pushers.

The movement is a manual wind calibre 727 made by Valjoux. The minute register hand appears to be replaced.

The watch is paired on a correct style C&I stretch rivet bracelet which was recently added. It is in great condition and dates to 1977 The watch comes from the son of the original owner, who has now become a friend.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It features a new TrueDome D21 crystal that is gorgeous.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 Box & Papers Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a great reference 1675 featuring a 2.82M serial number, dating to circa 1971.

The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on a period correct C&I Rolex rivet bracelet dating to 1970.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box and papers. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read Charlie Dunne’s comprehensive article ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex Sigma Dial Floating Big Red Daytona Reference 6263 Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex “Floating Big Red” Daytona reference 6263 is an extremely rare variant with the “Sigma” dial. This is one of the earliest Big Reds made and it is the first silver Floating Big Red Sigma Daytona I have offered for sale. The Greek Sigma letters next to T SWISS T below 6:00 refers to the hour markers being made of white gold.

The dial is in great condition with bold red “Daytona” font and original tritium lume on the dial and in the hands. The white registers are extremely clean and beautiful.

The case appears to be unpolished. The bracelet has been lightly polished. This Daytona features its original rare “Mark 1” acrylic bezel (with “hooked 2” in 200).

The central seconds hand appears to be a replacement.

This watch has a 4.13 million case serial number dating to circa 1976. It measures 37mm in diameter. The Rolex Oyster solid link 78350 bracelet has the correct 571 end links.

The watch is fresh to market. The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold in Australia at Lindsays in 1980. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Pepsi GMT-Master II Ref 16710 w/ Box Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master II reference 16710 with “Pepsi” bezel is unpolished. The watch has an P serial, indicating production circa 2000. P Serials are some of our absolute favorites! The watch is 40mm. This is a beautiful example with a great insert. The case appears to be unpolished.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It comes with box. It does not come with papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16700 w/ Papers Unpolished

This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16700 is a very attractive example. It dates to circa 1999 with an A serial. The case appears to be unpolished. The bracelet features an X date code and is likely original to the watch.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the earlier reference ‘Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Submariner Ref 5513 Maxi 4 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is an awesome example. The case appears to be unpolished. The bracelet is lightly polished. It features a spectacular warm lume on the dial which is a “Maxi 4” dial. The watch features a 7.3M serial dating to circa 1982. The bracelet features a VE date code from. circa 1980 which is period correct.

Learn more about the sibling model reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Datejust Mint Green Dial 126234 Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Datejust reference 126234 is a great example from the modern catalog. The steel case is 36mm appears unpolished. It features a beautiful green dial which looks great in any setting.

The watch dates to December 2024. The watch features a white gold fluted bezel and its original Jubilee bracelet.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with a “full set” including box and papers.

Rolex Air-King Black Dial Ref 14000M Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000M is an awesome example with an uncommon and beautiful black dial. The watch features a F2 serial number and dates to circa 2003-2004. We believe the case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire. It measures 34mm in diameter.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Datejust 16014 Linen Dial Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’.

The variety seen in vintage Datejust models over the years is incredible. I believe the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches any person can own. This reference 16014 with a linen dial is so beautiful.

The 5.3M serial number dates the watch to circa 1978. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent and unpolished condition. The watch comes on a 62510H Jubilee bracelet with an “A” clasp code, dating to circa 1976. It comes with the correct 555 end-links.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex 31MM Datejust Reference 6827 in 18k YG w/ Black Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 6827 is an incredible watch. It is made of 18k yellow gold and is paired on an 18k yellow gold jubilee Rolex bracelet. The watch dates to circa 1979. The watch measures 31mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. If features a beautiful black dial - it is likely a bit newer, but is extremely attractive.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Vintage 31mm Rolex watches are extremely uncommon and have become very popular by connoisseurs. We believe this is largely due to the fact that @watchabbie wears a 31mm vintage Rolex! Everyone is obsessed with her and that is great!

Patek Philippe Reference 3923A

The Patek Philippe reference 3923A is a rare contemporary Calatrava. The reference 3923 in stainless steel, like this example, was only made in 450 pieces. These watches were delivered for retail in Japan. It shares a very similar case design to the Patek Philippe 3940, although it measures 31.5mm in diameter, and approximately 37mm lug to lug.

The watch dates to circa 1989. We believe the case has probably been lightly polished but is attractive. It features the original pin buckle. It has been purported that there are only 50 of this silver dial variant.

It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Zenith El Primero Ref. A3817 Unpolished

The Zenith El Primero is one of the most iconic chronographs in history, and one of the first chronographs to feature an automatic movement with calendar. This ref. A3817 features overlapping sub dials in different colors, silver, gray, and blue, and attractive tritium luminous hands and indices. It is paired with a Gay Frères steel ladder bracelet dating to 1970.

We believe the case is unpolished. The bracelet is polished and a bit stretched, but is a good candidate for restoration by Michael Young in Hong Kong.

It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Yacht-Master ref. 226659 Unpolished Box & Papers

The Yach-Master is a refined take on the Rolex dive watch, and this example in 18k white gold blends classic sportiness with a luxurious touch. The watch measures 42mm in case diameter and features an “Oysterflex” bracelet with an 18k white gold clasp. It is powered by the Rolex Calibre 3235. It has approximately 70 hours of power reserve.

The watch is in great condition and appears to be unpolished. It comes as a “full set” with its original box and papers.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

This Vulcain Cricket features an incredibly rare dial and is one of the most beautiful and interesting Cricket dials we have ever seen! We’ve pretty much seen them all, but this was a surprise! There are semicircular Guilloché designs carved into the dial, along with leaf life or folded arrowhead indices at 3, 6, 9, and 12 o’clock. The watch features original dauphine hands with luminous stripes. The case appears to be unpolished and is gold plated.

It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Tank Americaine 2505 in 18K RG Unpolished w/ Box

The Americaine reference 2505 is a very elegant watch. This watch is in 18k rose gold and measures approximately 26mm in diameter by 45mm in length. It features an automatic movement with a date. The watch features the classic Cartier blue sword style hands, a painted Roman Numeral dial, and a blue sapphire ‘cabochon’ crown. The rose gold case appears to be unpolished. The watch comes with a brown Cartier leather strap and rose gold buckle.

We do not know the date of production.

We do not have the full service history, but the watch is running well. The watch comes with a Cartier box. It does not come with papers.

We do not know the date of production.

Patek Philippe Ref. 3418 with Box

This Patek Philippe reference 3418 is a very cool and uncomon timepiece in stainless steel and was initially launched in 1958. It features a “convertible case” that allows for additional lugs to be swapped for the Milanese belt-buckle integrated bracelet. It features the original Unroc bracelet by Cornu & Cie. The watch features a silver dial with patina and a subsidiary seconds dial. The case measures approximately 34.5mm in diameter. It has been lightly polished.

It does not come with papers. It does come with a box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex GMT-Master II 126713GRNR “Zombie” Box & Papers Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master II 126713GRNR “Zombie” is a fantastic modern sports watch. The reference number 126713GRNR denotes a steel and 18k yellow gold case and bracelet, and a black and gray rotating bezel. It captures the iconic DNA from the two-tone vintage models. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, the GMT remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day.

The watch is in great condition and is unpolished. It comes as a “full set” with its original box and papers. The papers date the watch to June 2023.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

Rolex Great White Mk I Sea-Dweller Reference 1665 Unpolished

This Rolex “Great White” Sea-Dweller reference 1665 is a very attractive watch with warm colored lume. It is a fantastic example. The case and bracelet appear to be unpolished. The lug-to-lug length is 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. It features the Mk I dial.

It dates to circa 1978 with a 5.4M serial. It is paired on an solid Oyster bracelet with VC date code dating to circa 1978 and is likely original to the watch.

The watch does not come box and papers. It features a TrueDome D39 crystal. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

IWC Rose Gold Chronograph Ref. IW388309 with Box & Papers Unpolished

This Rose Gold IWC Pilot’s Watch Performance Chronograph measures in at 41mm in diameter, approximately 51.5mm lug to lug. It features a black lacquer dial. It is a self-winding movement with a power reserve of 46 hours. The current retail price is $27,900. This is a great opportunity to own a like-new sports chronograph in a luxury metal. It was sold in 2025. The case is unpolished and is 18k rose gold.

It has a solid 5N deployant clasp which was an add-on to the standard pin/buckle.

The watch comes as a “full set” with its original box and papers.

TUDOR PCG Submariner Reference 7928

This TUDOR Submariner reference 7928 is a very attractive Submariner. The case production dates to circa 1962 with a 364,xxx serial. It features the PCG (Pointed-Crown-Guards) case design. The dial and lume is very attractive. It features the correct “Long 5” bezel insert and twinlock crown. It is 40mm in diameter and measures 47mm lug to lug. It is paired on a C&I Rolex Rivet bracelet dating to 1961.

It passed a 5 bar water test just recently.

The watch does not have box or papers.. It features a True Dome D19 crystal

TUDOR Pelagos FXD M25717N-0001 Full Set

The TUDOR Pelagos is a great dive watch. The case is made of titanium and measures 42mm in diameter. The dial and bezel are very attractive. The watch comes with interchangeable straps. WORSHIP! It truly is an amazing product and TUDOR is a remarkable company. These are some of the highly collectible modern TUDOR models, and are very cool.

The watch recently passed a water resistance text of 5 bar. The watch comes full set with box and papers. It was purchased in 2025

Rolex Oysterquartz Day-Date Reference 19018 Black Dial in 18K YG

This is a very cool black dial Rolex Oysterquartz Day-Date reference 19018. The 18k yellow gold case bracelet dates to 1984 with a serial number in the 7.5 million range. The integrated gold bracelet has a D clasp code and dates to circa 1979, but we believe it to be original. Both the case and bracelet have been polished in the past.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well and recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with a box or papers.

Cartier Santos Dumont Reference WSSA0046 / 4298 Black Enamel Unpolished Box & Papers

This is a very regal Cartier Santos Dumont reference WSSA0046 / 4298. The dial is a beautiful silver with Roman numerals. It measures 31.5mm by 43.5mm (lug to lug). The case is made of Stainless Steel with black enamel inlay. It is unpolished. The watch features a mechanical movement. It was sold in 2025.

The watch comes with the original box and papers.

TUDOR Pink Black Bay Chrono M79360N-0019 Full Set Unpolished

This Tudor Black Bay Chronograph ref. 79360N features an “Inter Miami” Pink dial. The watch is 41mm in diameter, 50mm lug to lug, and approximately 14.5mm thick. The automatic chronograph has a 70-hour power reserve. It comes with the original 5-link stainless steel bracelet and is chronometer certified.

The watch comes with the original box and papers, and it recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It dates to 2025.

TUDOR M.N.21 Pelagos FXD Reference M25707B-0001 Full Set Unworn

The TUDOR Pelagos is a great dive watch. The case is made of titanium and measures 42mm in diameter. The dial and bezel is a nice blue color. The watch comes with interchangeable straps. It truly is an amazing product and TUDOR is a remarkable company.

This is the first release with "M.N.21" engraved on the back. There are apparently only 150 or so of these, because it came out in November 2021 and they went straight to M.N.22.

Several years back Charlie Dunne went over to talk with a group of collectors. One particular collector who he had never met before that day was wearing the Pelagos FXD. He was rude and made fun of Charlie for no reason in front of a bunch of people! It was unjustified, but now everyone is on good terms - and we still love the watch!

The watch is unworn and brand new.

The watch recently passed a water resistance text of 5 bar. The watch comes full set with box and papers. It was originally purchased in 2021.

Bamford London X Dover Street Market Peanuts GMT Full Set

This collaboration between Bamford London and Dover Street Market produced a Snoopy GMT in a limited edition series of 100 pieces. This example is number 60. The movement is the automatic Sellita SW330-2. Upon its initial release, this watch retailed for around $2,195. Both Bamford and Dover Street Market have signed the dial.

The watch comes with its original box and papers.

Unimatic x Hodinkee Black Dial Ref U1-HGMT with Box & Papers

WORSHIP!

This collaboration with Hodinkee marks Unimatic’s first-ever GMT reference! The watch is stainless steel and measures 41mm in diameter and approximately 49mm lug to lug. It has a unidirectional rotating 24-hour bezel and a black-luminous dial. The watch is powered by the automatic Swiss movement, the Sellita SW330-2. The watch was produced along with two other references, the U2-H and the U1-H, each of which was only produced in 500 examples.

The watch has recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The watch comes with its original box. It does not come with box.

Unimatic x Hodinkee Gray Dial Ref U1-H with Box & Papers

This dive watch was made in a limited series collaboration with Hodinkee of 1,500 pieces. The watch was produced along with two other references, the U2-H and the U1-HGMT, each of which was only produced in 500 examples.

The watch is made in stainless steel and measures 41mm in diameter and approximately 49mm lug to lug. It has a unidirectional graduated 60-minute bezel and a signature grey-luminous dial. The watch is powered by an automatic Swiss movement, the Sellita SW200-1.

The watch comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers.

Ikepod Hemipode “Rosso” Limited Edition with Original Box NOS

This Ikepod is a time capsule dating back to circa 2002, when the company produced a 33-piece limited edition series in tribute to Alfa Romeo’s Tipo 33 race car. This rare Hemipode Rosso has a black PVD case and a “Thunderbird Tire” strap. The watch features a chronograph complication and a second time zone at 6 o’clock. The watch is running well, and it is in like-new condition.

It comes with the original box and instruction booklet. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Zenith Covergirl El Primero Ref A3818 Unpolished

This Zenith “Covergirl” El Primero reference A3818 is such a sweetie! Collectors can attest to the fact that the Zenith El Primero reference A3818 is one of the most desirable vintage Zenith models. Its nickname "Covergirl" derives from its feature on the cover of the Zenith book by Manfred Rössler.

The case appears to be unpolished. The dial is very attractive. It dates to circa 1972.

The watch does not come box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Eberhard Scafograf 200 Unpolished w/ Box

The Eberhard Scafograf 200 is a unique and rare vintage dive watch. It features large circular and triangular luminous hour markers. It comes on an original Eberhard bracelet made by Gay Freres. It will fit a wrist approximately 7 inches. The endlinks are aftermarket. The bracelet is period correct.

The dial layout and handset are incredibly cool and different from every manufacturer. The combination of gilt lettering, warm lume and gold surrounds makes for a stunning aesthetic.

The watch measures 39mm in diameter and 47mm lug-to-lug. The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a box which was added later.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Eberhard Scafograf 100 Unpolished w/ Box

The Eberhard Scafograf 200 is a unique and rare vintage dive watch. It features large circular and triangular luminous hour markers. It comes on an original Eberhard bracelet made by Gay Freres. It will fit a wrist approximately 7 inches.

The dial layout and handset are incredibly cool and different from every manufacturer. The combination of gilt lettering, warm lume and gold surrounds makes for a stunning aesthetic.

The watch measures 35mm in diameter and 45mm lug-to-lug. The watch does not come with papers. It does come with a box which was added later.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Otsuka Lotec No. 6 Full Set

This watch from the Japanese brand Otsuka Lotec is notable for winning the Challenge Award at Grand Prix d'Horlogerie de Genève. The watch was designed by Japanese watchmaker Jiro Katayama, who is known for selling his watches exclusively in the Japanese market. The watch is powered by a Miyota movement with an in-house module that is responsible for the retrograde minute and hour hands. The watch features an unconventional crystal shape with apertures in the dial for a date wheel and seconds track. The case features exposed screws around the bezel and wire lugs. It is in like-new condition. It measures 42.6mm in diameter and 11.8mm thick.

It comes as a “full set” with original box and papers. It was sold in 2025.

Porsche Design Chronograph By Orfina

This is a very cool Porsche Design chronograph By Orfina. It is powered by a Valjoux calibre 7750. The PVD version of these watches were worn by Tom Cruise in Top Gun. The case has been polished. It measures 40mm in diameter. It is paired on the original NSA bracelet.

It does not come with a box or papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well.

Heuer Jo Siffert Autavia Mk 2 Reference 1163 Unpolished

This Heuer “Jo Siffert” Autavia reference 1163 is from circa 1968-69. It is in outstanding condition. The case appears to be unpolished. The case back seems to be lightly polished to remove an engraving. It features the Mark 2 dial and the original Tachymeter bezel. This variant has the enlarged blue hour markers and ridged hour markers and metal hour and minute hands. It features the Flat V logo/branding. It features a 141XXX serial just after the Chronomatic Siffert range.

This watch does not include the original box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 11 made by Heuer-Léonidas), and is 42mm in diameter.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. We are including a Heuer bracelet by Gay Freres with HLD end links.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Abercrombie & Fitch Seafarer Reference 346 Unpolished

Before Abercrombie & Fitch was the “middle of the mall store” as Jeff Stein coined it, the company was the preeminent destination for outdoor sporting apparel. Among the high-quality goods you would expect to find were the incredible sports watches by Heuer. The most desirable models from the retailer would be the Seafarer.

It is estimated that a total of 10-15 examples of the reference 346 were produced. It features a 2nd execution dial variant with the radium-filled Arabic numerals 11,12, and 1, with round hour markers. This particular example is in pristine condition.

The watch features a Valjoux 71 movement which was modified to incorporate a tide complication. The case is unpolished. It measures in at 37.5mm wide (44mm lug-to-lug). The lug width is 20mm and the height is 14.5mm (including the crystal). The watch is paired on a Gay Freres Rivet bracelet.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This is one of my favorite Explorers I have ever seen. This example features an 9.0M serial dating it to circa 1986. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is super attractive and has a warm yellow tone. It is paired on a Rolex 78360 bracelet with 580 end links.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great. It features the original crystal. It has just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Antimagnetic Reference 3529 Chronograph Unpolished

We are thrilled to offer this very rare and beautiful Rolex antimagnetic chronograph reference 3529. The watch features a Valjoux 69 movement. The case is unpolished and measures 25.5mm in width (36.5mm lug-to-lug). The watch is paired on a beautiful leather strap.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great. The watch comes from the family of the original owner.

Rolex Blue Dial Datejust Reference 16014 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 16014 is a phenomenal timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is unpolished. It features a beautiful and uncommon blue dial. The dates to circa 1985.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Black Dial Datejust Reference 16014 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 16014 is a phenomenal timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). It is unpolished. It features a beautiful and uncommon black dial. The dates to circa 1987.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Tide Dial Datejust Reference 1601

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 1601 is so beautiful. However, this example is very special in that if features a corporate logo for the company Tide. Tide was a chemical and fertilizer company associated with growing cotton. Their tagline was “Symbol of Quality”.

The watch dates to 1968. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a Jubilee bracelet.

The watch is in great condition with a lightly polished case. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date II Reference 218238 in Yellow Gold Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date II Reference 218238 is a very bold timepiece. The case measures 41mm in diameter (49mm lug-to-lug). The 18K yellow gold case is unpolished. It features a very sophisticated Roman-numeral dial. The has a G serial and dates to circa 2010 .

The watch does not come with box and papers. It comes from the family of the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Spider Dial Sea-Dweller Reference 16660

The Rolex Sea-Dweller reference 16600 is a super fun sports watch. The watch measures 40mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). The case is lightly polished. The watch dates to circa 1984. The dial has developed some cracks, also referred to as a “spider” dial. It is more discernible under a loupe or at specific angles. The lume is a warm tone.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time. The watch has recently passed a pressure test of 5 bar.

Rolex Air-King Reference 14010 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case is unpolished and dates to circa 1991.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running great.

Rolex Mark 4 Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This is an incredible Explorer 1016. This example dates to circa 1976. The case is unpolished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive and has a warm yellow tone. It is paired on a Rolex 7836 bracelet with 258 end links.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great. It features the original crystal. It has just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Blue Mosaic Oyster Perpetual Reference 1002 Unpolished Full Set

This is a spectacular Rolex Oyster Perpetual ref 1002. The watch is in mint condition, with very little signs of wear. This example dates to circa 1971. It features a blue Mosaic dial. The case is unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter. The lume is very attractive. It is paired on an oval link Jubilee bracelet made in the US. For those who are looking for a spectacular vintage OP, this is a great choice!

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great. It has just passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Benrus Type 1 Unpolished

The Benrus Type 1 Class A wristwatch is one of the iconic US Military issued watches and is beginning to develop a cult following over the most recent years. It is powered by the self-winding, calibre GS1D2. The watch features fixed spring bars, and the asymmetrical case remains unpolished (albeit a few scratches from its time in action). It measures at 42.5mm in diameter and 15mm in thickness.

It was issued to the US Military as part of the MIL-W-50717 contract. The watch dates to November 1973 and features the serial number 238. It features the original crystal.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Reference 1680 Unpolished

This Rolex Submariner reference 1680 is a very cool sports watch dating to circa 1970. It is one of the most attractive white Subs we have ever seen. The case is unpolished. The dial, minute and hour hand all have the original lume which is an incredibly attractive warm yellow. The second hand has had the lume patched as it was falling out. It is paired on the original bracelet. The bezel has some scratches, but it remains a very raw and attractive example!

The watch comes from the family of the original owner. It was recently serviced by Dr. Greg Petronzi. It does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Ghost Dial Day-Date Reference 1803 in White Gold Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week. This reference 1803 is very nice and rare example in white gold. The 18K white gold case is very strong and possibly unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This particular example dates to circa 1973.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Darth Vader's Cartier Tank Louis Xl Jumbo 17002 Unpolished

The Cartier Tank Louis is one of the most sophisticated dress watches and these models have grown to become highly collectible over recent years. It is a great contrast to many sports Rolex models within a collection and can be worn in both a casual and dressed up state.

This example dates to circa 1970s. It features an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. These are the largest vintage Tanks. The case measures 28mm in diameter and 35mm in length. The dial is spectacular condition and without any signs of wear or crazing. Often, these have crazing.

We are thrilled to offer this particular was, as it was owned by David Prowse the famous actor who played Darth Vader in the Stars Wars Trilogy. This watch is mentioned in detail in his autobiography ‘Straight From The Forces Mouth’ which is incredible.

“Somehow, we've moved on to Dallas but although it's a new city the routine's the same and there's another hectic schedule lying in wait.

Five more interviews later, I managed to squeeze in a sunbathe and a swim in the hotel pool whilst I was waiting to be collected to fly to our next stop which was St. Louis. Met a hairdresser from Champagne Illinois, who was in Dallas looking for a job. The temperature today was 97°F so I dived into the pool to accompany her on a few lengths. I hadn't swum more than about 20 yards when I suddenly realised that I had three thousand dollars' worth of Cartier watch on a present from Prince Khalid of Saudi Arabia) and the watch wasn't waterproof. I beat a hasty exit out of the pool and got the hotel concierge to get my watch to a repairer so that it could be cleaned out before the innards went rusty. I was later informed that I had only just made it in time. Another few seconds in the pool and my precious Cartier would have ticked its last.”

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer II Reference 1655 Unpolished

Rolex introduced the Explorer II reference 1655 for use by spelunkers (cave diving) and speleologists (cave researchers) where the use of a 24-hour hand is important for the wearer to be able to differentiate between night and day. These watches also have the nickname ‘Freccione’ meaning ‘big arrow’ in Italian and are sometimes falsely called the “Steve McQueen” despite no evidence he ever owned or wore one. A slow seller when initially released, the reference 1655 has gained a cult following due its unusual and distinctive design. The reference 1655 is one of the references I am asked most often to find for collectors.

This example is outstanding. It dates to circa 1975 (3.9 million serial number). It features the automatic calibre 1575 made by Rolex. We believe this example is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex L Serial Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example is one of the last Explorer 1016s produced. It dates to circa 1989 with a L serial. It was sold in 1990. It features a pale white lume on the hands and luminous markers. The case is 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). We believe the case is unpolished. It is in mint condition and one of the best I have seen. It is paired on a bracelet with a flip lock clasp which is seen on the final 1016s.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It come full set with box and papers.

Rolex Fuchsia GMT-Master Reference 1675 Full Set

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a very cool Fuchsia 1675 featuring a 1.82M serial number, dating to circa 1968. These bezels were born on watches featuring 1.8M and 1.9M serial range from circa 1968-1969. The case is lightly polished and worn well. The aluminum bezel insert is very attractive. It is paired on a correct 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 80 end links. The hour, minute, and GMT triangle hands were relumed in the past.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test. The watch comes with box and papers and comes from a Vietnam veteran.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

Rolex “Blue” Daytona Reference 6262

This Rolex Daytona reference 6262 is a super cool watch. The Daytona font on these examples appears to have a light blue color in certain lights (similar to secondary stamped Submariner 5512’s SCOC font). This occurs as the font was stamped subsequent to the Rolex Cosmograph font on the dial. The dial is attractive with clean subdials. The case has been polished. The watch measures approximately 36mm in diameter (39mm including the crown). It features a 2.52M serial number dating to circa late 1970. The central chronograph hand is slightly later. It is paired on a period correct bracelet dating to the 4th quarter of 1970.

It was recently serviced and is running well. It does not come with box and papers.

Movado Explorer Dial Kingmatic S Unpolished

This Movado Kingmatic is an awesome watch. The case measures approximately 35mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). We believe the case is unpolished. It features a beautiful and uncommon glossy Explorer dial similar to the Rolex Explorer 1016. The dates to the 1960s.

Fingers crossed Rich Fordon finds this watch worthy of including in his weekly column!

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Grey Dial Datejust Ref 1601

This Rolex Datejust reference 1601 is a great timepiece. The case measures 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). The case has been polished, the bezel appears to be unpolished. It features a beautiful and uncommon grey dial. The the hands are luminous and the dial is a non-luminous one. The hands were possibly replaced. The dates to circa 1972 with a 3.04M serial.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16014 Unpolished w/ Silver Dial

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

This Datejust features a silver “soleil” dial that has is beautiful. This watch dates back to circa 1986 with an 8.93M serial and measures 36mm in diameter.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Breitling AOPA Reference 765 Unpolished w/ Box & Booklet

The Breitling Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) models represent an incredible chapter in Breitling history. This example is a great entry to vintage Breitling models and is an overall awesome chronograph! It has an unpolished case. It measures 38mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug). The dial is in great condition with warm patina on the numerals. The watch is powered by the Venus calibre 178.

The watch comes with box and a Breitling booklet. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève Compur Reference 52403 in 18K Yellow Gold

This Universal Genève Compur reference 52403 is a very cool chronograph model dating to circa 1945. The yellow gold case measures 38mm in diameter. It has been polished in the past. The case was made by Spillman. The dial is attractive, and in nice condition for this era. The watch is paired on a strap made from a repurposed Hermes bag, and a Universal Genève gold buckle.

It does not come with box/papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Date Black Dial Reference 15210 Unpolished

This Rolex Date reference 15210 is a fantastic watch with a beautiful black dial. The watch dates to circa 2000 with a P serial. The case 34mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Cosmograph Daytona White Gold Reference 116519LN On Oysterflex Full Set

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116519LN with grey dial is a fantastic iteration of the iconic sports watch. Paired on its original Oysterflex band, it is truly suited to be worn in all active situations. It dates to 2022. It has some light wear. We believe the case is unpolished. It is partially stickered.

The watch features a grey dial, black Cerachrom bezel, black registers and the beautiful red-arched “Daytona” font. The case is in 18K white gold and is powered by the calibre 4130.

It comes “full set” with original box and papers.

Rolex Datejust Ref 16014 w/ Linen Dial Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 16014 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to circa 1978 with a 5.46M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar

Rolex Daytona Cosmograph Reference 116500LN w/ Black Dial Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch. The watch features a black dial and black Cerachrom bezel. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch dates to 2023. It comes “full set” with original box and papers.

Patek Philippe Ellipse Reference 3749/1 In White Gold Unpolished

This Patek Philippe Ellipse is a spectacular timepiece. ]The case is unpolished and made of 18K white gold. We believe the case is unpolished and it features a fantastic bracelet. It dates to the 1970s. It is a manual wind movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Reference 168000 Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. The reference 168000 is a rare watch produced for approximately one year. The lume is very attractive. It is 40mm in diameter. This reference 168000 features a 9.6M serial number dating to circa 1987. We believe the case is unpolished.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

It does not come with box/papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Universal Genève White Gold Pocket Watch Reference 71726 For Saudi Arabian Market Watch w/ Box

Universal Genève pocket watch reference 71726 is a very sophisticated timepiece. The white gold case measures 47mm in diameter. We believe it is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1956. It is powered by the calibre 274. The dial is very special with a palm tree and swords seen in UG watches for the Saudi Arabian market. Some say these watches were possibly a gift from a member of the royal family to diplomats.

It does not come with papers. It comes with a beautiful green presentation box with matching logo. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold w/ Black Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is a super nice watch in outstanding condition. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. It features an incredibly attractive black dial. It features an W serial, and dates to circa 1995.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar

Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Reference 1601 Full Set

This Rolex Datejust reference 1601 is a very cool watch. It dates to circa 1971 with a 2.52M serial number. The steel case and gold bezel have been polished. It measure 36mm in diameter.

It comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Wide Boy Day-Date 1803 In 18K YG

This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a very cool wristwatch with the uncommon Wide Boy dial in silver. The 18K yellow gold case has been polished. It has a 2.03M serial dating to circa 1969.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar

Rolex Speedking with Gilt Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Speedking is a super cool watch with gilt dial. The steel case measures approximately 30mm in diameter (36mm lug to lug). We believe the case is unpolished. It features a 1.48M serial dating to circa 1966.

It does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Ebel Perpetual Calendar Chronograph Ref 3136901 in White Gold on Bracelet Unpolished

Ebel wristwatches have become very interesting for collectors in recent years. Particularly with their association to the Zentith El Primero calibre and interesting designs. This Ebel has a very cool white gold case and bracelet. It wears great at 43mm in diameter. We believe the case is unpolished. It features a perpetual calendar and chronograph complication. Ebel was the second company (after Patek Philippe) to introduce a perpetual chronograph. This watch dates to the 1980s. For those looking for a complicated chronograph on bracelet, this is unbeatable value.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Cartier Tank Normale Reference 78092 Unpolished

The Tank Normale is one of the iconic models within the Cartier Tank family and the original design based on a French tank. It has been particularly special in my career as I was privileged to sell a smaller Tank Normale example that belonged to Jackie Kennedy Onassis during my time as Vice President, Senior Specialist for Christie's.

This particular example is in great condition and dates to the 1980s. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures 23mm wide by 30mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished. The octagonal sapphire cabochon crown features the flattened surface and appears to be original. The Normale has become regained a great deal of attention with its long awaited release. This example is a fantastic deal compared to the new release. This is a MK 1 example, of which only approximately 800 were produced.

It does not come with the box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-65 Unpolished

The Omega Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding Omega Speedmaster reference 105.012-65 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

The reference 105.012 were first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied Omega logo. It is in phenomenal condition, with a slightly warm tone developing throughout the perimeter, and is beginning to show signs of becoming tropical.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to excitement of many Speedy lovers. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line throughout 1963-1966.

The case was made by Huguenin Frères, and remains very crisp, showing its true form. The watch appears to be unpolished and has its original crown, which is uncommon as most were replaced during service. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm. The reference 105.012-65 is highly prized and collected because it was the exact same model worn by Neil Armstrong to the Moon. The watch was just serviced and I am thrilled to offer this great watch to the next lucky owner.

Seiko Blue Pogue 6139 Unpolished

The Seiko “Pogue” chronograph is one of the most interesting and distinctive watches from the 1970s. They have earned the nickname the “Pogue” since Colonel William R. Pogue wore a similar watch during a NASA Skylab mission in 1973, making it the first automatic chronograph to be worn in space. These are wonderful watches on the wrist and very easy to wear. This Blue Pogue is a very attractive example. The case is unpolished.

This watch does not include box or papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 6139A made by Seiko), and is 41mm in diameter. It was recently serviced and is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 1601 w/ White Matte Dial

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 1601 is no exception. The white “matte” dial is incredibly attractive and uncommon. It is also a “sigma dial” featuring the Greek letters flanking Swiss below 6 o’clock. Rolex only used Sigma dials, indicating the hands and hour markers are made of white gold, in the 1970s. Most white Datejust dials from the 1970s are reprinted.

The watch dates to circa 1972 and features a 3.05M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter.The watch is in excellent condition. The case has been lightly polished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box and papers. It has just passed a pressure test of 3 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Queen Midas Reference 4313 in 18k YG

The King & Queen Midas were among the most expensive Rolex by far at retail in the 1960s and 1970s. Starting in the 1970s, Rolex changed the dial to have Cellini instead of Midas in Greek on the dial and also introduced the smaller Queen Midas model. The King Midas has been closely associated with Elvis Presley, who is photographed holding one, and more recently with Rihanna, who wore a customized model for her pregnancy announcement.

It is said that Gérald Genta designed these watches and they certainly seemed to be part of the evolution of integrated watch design that led to the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, IWC Ingenieur 1832, and Patek Philippe Nautilus.

The integrated bracelets and deployant buckle is extremely firm. The 18k yellow gold case has been lightly polished. It measures approximately 24mm at the widest point, and the length of the case is also 24mm. It measures a wrist up to 6 inches.

The timepiece is powered by a calibre 650.

The watches do not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but they are running well.

Rolex Tiffany & Co. Datejust Ref 16013 Unpolished

This Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is a beautiful two-tone example with a champagne dial. This watch was sold by the prestigious New York retailer Tiffany & Co. This watch has a 6.2 million serial number and dates to circa 1979. The watch is paired with a Jubilee service bracelet from circa 2003 and 455 end-links.

The case is in strong condition and has been lightly polished in the past. It has an automatic movement (Caliber 3035 made by Rolex), and measures 36mm in diameter, or approximately 44mm lug to lug.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with a box or papers.

Chopard L.U.C 1860 In Lucent Steel Full Set Unpolished

The Chopard L.U.C 1860 In Lucent Steel took the world by storm in 2023, and for good reason. It is probably among the best releases from the manufacturer. It has a beautiful salmon guilloche dial, and the steel case is great. The watch measures 36.5mm case that measures 8.2mm in thickness. The case is unpolished.

It comes with box and papers. This is a great value and considerably lower than the $27,000 MSRP!

Rolex Oyster Perpetual Bubbleback Reference 3372 in 18K

This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 3372 is a great example of a non-luminous dial "Bubbleback" with a 443,XXX serial dating to circa 1946. These early Oyster cases would help play a role in ‘How Rolex Became Rolex: The Oyster Case Story’

The case has been polished in the past. It features an automatic calibre, a yellow gold case, and measures 32.5mm in diameter (39.5mm lug-to-lug). The dial is incredible and it is so rare to find unrestored dials in Bubblebacks.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Ref E 853 Unpolished

The Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox reference E 853 is one of my favorite watches of all time. This example dates to the late 1950s. The case measures 37mm in diameter and wears excellent. We believe the case is unpolished. I have found the reference E 853 to be quite hard to model to locate nice examples. The E 855 is also tricky to find nice models, but the no-date variant is just a bit more tricky.

The watch is powered by the calibre 815, automatic alarm wristwatch. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

Along with the Atmos clock and Reversos, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer.

To learn more about the subsequent model, read Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 855.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

OMEGA Seamaster Chronograph Ref 105.001 in 14k YG

The iconic Omega Seamaster remains one of the brand's flagship watches to date. This example, reference 105.001 in 14k gold, is a tasteful take on the Omega chronograph. The case is has been lightly polished. The watch features the legendary Omega 321 movement, renowned for powering many of the Speedmasters throughout the late 1950s and 1960s. It measures 35.5mm in diameter and approximately 42.5mm lug-to-lug.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

OMEGA Seamaster Automatic 18k Rose Gold Ref 14701 Unpolished

This watch is an elegant take on the iconic Omega Seamaster line. The case is in 18k rose gold and appears to be unpolished. The watch features an automatic movement, the Omega 562, and has a date window at the 3 o’clock index. It measures 34mm in diameter and approximately 42mm lug-to-lug.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

Rolex Submariner Reference 114060 Full Set

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This reference 114060 was originally sold in 2016. The case appears to be unpolished.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Cartier Paris Tank Normale 78092 in 18K Gold Unpolished

I’m thrilled to offer this Cartier Tank Normale. The watch is in outstanding condition and features the original Paris dial. The case is approximately 23mm in diameter by 30mm lug to lug. It comes on a Cartier deployant. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It dates to late 1970s. The case appears to be unpolished.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running well.

Universal Genève Polerouter Tropical 869111/01 on GF Bracelet

The Polerouter is an iconic watch designed by Gérald Genta, and this example, reference 86911 with date, features a stunning tropical dial. The case appears to be unpolished and was made by Huguenin Frères. The case measures 36mm in diameter and approximately 44.5 mm lug-to-lug. The watch is paired with a stainless steel Universal Genève bracelet made by Gay Frères, dating to 1966.

The watches do not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but they are running well.

A. Lange & Söhne Meter Dial Datograph Reference 403.035 Full Set

This A. Lange & Söhne Datograph Reference 403.035 is an exceptional chronograph from the German manufacturer. This is an early example with a “Meter dial”. The dial reads “Base 1000 Meter" by three o’clock, whereas later examples say “Meters” instead of “Meter”. The case is Platinum, and it appears to be unpolished. It measures 39mm in diameter, approximately 47.5mm lug-to-lug. We do not know the date of production.

The watch was serviced in 2020 by ALS and it is running well. The watch comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers. The pushers on boxes on these tend to be difficult. We recommend not closing the watch and papers in the box, as they can get stuck.

Ikepod Hemipode Grand Date Special Edition with Box & Papers Unpolished

This Ikepod Hemipode Grande Date is in new old stock condition and is COSC certified. The watch features a 43mm stainless steel case and a 4500 Dubois Depraz movement with a chronograph function. The watch features a Yellow dial, with black subsidiary dials and an orange chronograph hand. It is a special edition piece in like-new condition.

It comes with the original box, instruction booklet, and COSC certificate.

Nivada Dato Reference 4368 Unpolished

This Nivada Dato is a classic racing chronograph in great condition. The case appears to be unpolished. This example features a white dial with black sub-dials and orange accents. The watch features a date window at 6 ‘o’clock.The watch looks great paired with a honey Wind watch strap. The case measures approximately 35.5mm in diameter and 43.5mm lug-to-lug.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with the original box or papers.

Movado Tempomatic Ref. 16151 Breguet Numerals Unpolished

This Movado Tempomatic is a great watch. The case measures approximately 33mm in diameter (41mm lug-to-lug). The stainless steel case appears to be unpolished. The dial features gold Breguet-style numerals and gold alpha hands. It features an FB (Taubert Freres) case.

The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Nivada Grenchen Datomaster 3423 Unpolished

This Nivada Grenchen Datomaster is a robust racing chronograph with a sharp case that appears to be unpolished. This example features a grey dial with white sub-dials and orange accents. The watch features a date window at 12 o’clock. The watch looks great paired with a honey Wind watch strap. The case measures approximately 35.5mm in diameter and 43.5mm lug-to-lug.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with the original box or papers.

TUDOR Ranger Ref 7996

The TUDOR Ranger is an incredible model with a great deal of appreciation for its similarity to the Rolex Explorer with a 12, 6, and 9 numeral layout. This example has a 540 thousand serial number, dating to circa 1966. It is paired on a 7835 Rolex bracelet dating to circa 1972 with 357 end-links. This example features a matte dial with warm tritium lume patina. The hands have been relumed. The case measures 36mm in diameter and approximately 43mm lug-to-lug.

The watch has been polished in the past. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with the original box or papers.

Rolex Day-Date Ref. 128235 Rose Gold Diamond Dial Full Set

This Everose gold Day-Date, in outstanding condition, is a great watch from the current classic catalogue. It features a “rosé” dial set with diamonds as hour indexes. The watch is powered by the Rolex Calibre 3255, with a 70-hour power reserve and a double quick-set day and date function. The case measures 36mm in diameter and approximately 43mm lug-to-lug.

The case and bracelet appear to be unpolished and are in excellent condition. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes as a “full set” with the original box and papers. It was sold in 2022.

Rolex Air-King Ref 14010 Pink Dial Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14010 is a very attractive timepiece. The watch is slightly over 34mm in diameter (with a lug-to-lug length of 43mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is slightly lower at around 11.5mm including the crystal. The case appears to be unpolished and has a A serial dating to circa 1999.

The watch does not include box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

OMEGA Seamaster Gilt Dial Gold Plated Reference 14704

This Rose Gold IWC Pilot’s Watch Performance Chronograph measures in at 41mm in diameter, approximately 51.5mm lug to lug. It features a black lacquer dial. It is a self-winding movement with a power reserve of 46 hours. The current retail price is $27,900. This is a great opportunity to own a like-new sports chronograph in a luxury metal. It was sold in 2025. The case is unpolished and is 18k rose gold.

It has a solid 5N deployant clasp which was an add-on to the standard pin/buckle.

The watch comes as a “full set” with its original box and papers.

Yema Yachtingraf Unpolished w/ Box

This Yema Yachtingraf is a very cool watch, designed as a chronograph regatta timer. The watch measures approximately 39mm in diameter and 44.5mm lug-to-lug. The stainless-steel case appears to be unpolished. It features a bi-directional rotating bezel and a contrast sub dial to time the 5-minute countdown of a regatta race. This watch is powered by the Valjoux 7733 movement.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. The watch comes with the original box.

Heuer Skipper Autavia Ref 7764 HM on GF Bracelet Unpolished w/ Box

This Heuer Autavia reference 7764HM is a great regatta-timer chronograph. It features a bi-directional rotating bezel and a blue, red, and white sub-dial to time the 5-minute countdown of a regatta race. The stainless-steel case appears to be unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. The watch comes with the original box.

Rolex Submariner Meters First 5513

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. This reference 5513 features a 1.7M serial number dating to circa 1968. It is paired with a Gay Frères 7206 rivet bracelet dating to 1966. It has the correct 80 end-links. This example features a “meters first” matte dial with very attractive tritium lume. It has been polished in the past, but is attractive.

Learn more about the sibling model reference 5512 within Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512’.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with the original box or papers.

Rolex Submariner Ref 16800 Unpolished

The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This transitional reference 16800 features a 8.4M serial number dating to circa 1984. It is paired with the original reference 93150 Oyster bracelet, with an I6 date code, from circa 1984. It has the correct 593 end-links. This example is in incredible condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It has the original case-back sticker. The dial features tritium indices with white metal surrounds that have developed a very attractive warm patina.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and read the corresponding article.

We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It does not come with the original box or papers.
