https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe "Dorè" Dial Reference 3940J Unpolished Full Set Certificate of Origin & Extract

This Patek Philippe reference 3940 in yellow gold is a rare and uncommon model from the early period of the amazing reference 3940. Whereas the earliest examples of the Patek Philippe reference 3940 only had a solid case back. This example for sale dates to 1985. Starting in the early 1990s, Patek Philippe made the 3940 models with both exhibition and solid case backs except for the platinum model, which only had a solid case back for the entirety of its run.

The references 3940 is arguably the most elegant Patek Philippe watches made over the last 40 years. The watch is powered by the self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor, which when released was the thinnest automatic perpetual calendar movement available. The case is 36mm, and it comes on tan strap. We believe the case remains unpolished. The watch shows little wear and features crisp hallmarks, which is extremely rare for reference 3940s. The dial is a very beautiful "dorè" dial. It is confirmed by the Extract stating “gilded dial”

The watch comes with an Extract from the Archives indicating a production of 1987. It comes full set with box papers and certificate of origin. It comes with the original gold buckle.

Rolex GMT-Master 1675 on Jubilee in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The present watch is an exceptionally preserved GMT-Master reference 1675 in 18k gold.

We believe the case is unpolished. The beautiful bevels on the edge of the case and perfectly faded bezel insert makes for an incredibly attractive watch. The watch features a 3.6M serial dating to circa 1974.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE. You can also read about the iconic watch in Charlie Dunne’s Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16528 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished

The watch features its original dial with gorgeous creamy tritium lume. The case features a S serial dating to circa 1994. The case is very crisp and we believe to be unpolished, which is insanely hard to find. This is one of my favorite vintage gold automatic Daytonas I have ever been able to offer. .

This watch does not box and papers, has an automatic movement (Caliber 4030 made by Zenith), and is 40mm in diameter. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Rolex Fuchsia GMT-Master Reference 1675 Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980.

This is a very cool Fuchsia 1675 featuring a 1.9M serial number, dating to circa 1969. These bezels were born on watches featuring 1.8M and 1.9M serial range from circa 1968-1969. We believe the case is unpolished. The aluminum bezel insert is very attractive. It is paired on a correct 7206 Rolex rivet bracelet with 80 end links.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test. The watch comes with box and papers.

To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel’.

TUDOR Ranger Reference 7996/0 Unpolished

The TUDOR Ranger is an incredible model with a great deal of appreciation for its similarity to the Rolex Explorer with a 6, 9 numeral layout. This reference 7996/0 is in fantastic condition with an unpolished case. It dates to circa 1968.

It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history. It is running well and keeping good time.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 3800/1 w/ Extract from The Archives

This Patek Philippe Nautilus is remarkable reference 3800/1 in stainless steel. The watch dates to circa 1982. The case is lightly polished, but attractive. It measures 37mm in diameter (39mm in length). The dial is super attractive, and features original lume in the hour markers and hands. It is powered by an automatic movement.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It does come with an Extract from the Archives. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The bracelet can fit a approximately 7 inches.

Tudor Oyster Prince Reference 7995/0 Unpolished

This Tudor Oyster Prince is a very awesome wristwatch with a beautiful black dial. The hour markers are very appealing on this example as they stand very tall and are distinguished against the black background. The watch is an automatic, and the case is in fabulous shape appearing to be unpolished. This example dates to circa 1968. It measures 34mm in diameter.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

TUDOR Ranger Reference 7965 Unpolished

The TUDOR Ranger is an incredible model with a great deal of appreciation for its similarity to the Rolex Explorer with a 3, 6, 9 numeral layout. This reference 7965 is in fantastic condition with a nice case. It is a very rare no-date model with the “smiley” curved self-winding text. It measures 34mm in diameter (40mm lug-to-lug). The dial and hands have a beautiful warm patina. It dates to circa 1962.

The watch has recently had the movement cleaned/oiled and had the crown fixed. The watch has great provenance.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

F.P.Journe Chronomètre Bleu Full Set

F.P.Journe has become one of the most sought after and beloved independent watchmakers. To many, the of the adoration for the brand will begin in the iconic Chronomètre Bleu. The tantalum case and buckle feature light scratches.

This watch was originally purchased new in 2019. It comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It comes from the original owner.

Jaeger-LeCoultre US Edition Grande Reverso Ultra Thin 1931 Tribute 1931 Unpolished

This Jaeger-LeCoultre Grande Reverso Ultra Thin Tribute to 1931 is one of my personal favorite Reverso models. The dial layout and absence of branding is taken from the early Reversos of the 1930s and is a very pure design. Additionally, the syringe hands make for a very handsome look.

I love this watch, and the Reverso overall. Learn more about my thoughts on these timepieces by reading My Top 10 Watches of All Time article.

It does not include box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Gallet MultiChron 12 Silver Luminous Dial Unpolished

This Gallet Multichron 12 chronograph is a fantastic example. The lume is very attractive and the dial is in spectacular condition. The case is 37mm in diameter by 43mm lug to lug. We believe the case is unpolished. For those looking for a brilliant Gallet chronograph, this is a really great choice.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch does not come with box and papers.

Rolex Date Reference 15000 Unpolished

This is a super cool Rolex Date reference 15000. The watch is 34mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 42mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The case is an R7 serial dating to 1988. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but is it running well.

Breguet Type XX Chronograph

The Breguet Type XX chronograph is one of the most interesting issued chronographs. These watches were issued to the French Air Force. This watch was issued in 1954 per the case back engraving. The case has been polished in the past. The dial appears to have been reprinted or perhaps a service replacement which is common for these. The hands appear to be relumed and differ to the lume on the dial and do not glow, which is commonly seen on these models as well.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona White Dial Reference 116500LN Full Set Unpolished

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 116500LN is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a white “Panda” dial and black Cerachrom bezel. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. We believe the case is unpolished.

The watch was sold in 2021. It comes “full set” with original box and papers.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Day Date Moonphase 25594ST

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Day Date Moonphase 25594ST is a very attractive Royal Oak. The case measures approximately 36mm in diameter.

The deployant is a service one. It comes with an extra link and deployant which we believe it the original.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Daytona Cosmograph Reference 126500LN Unpolished Full Set

The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 126500LN with white dial is affectionately known as the “Panda” and is one of the most sought-after watches in the world. It is an excellent contemporary timepiece easily suitable for any collector to wear as an everyday watch.

The watch features a white dial and black Cerachrom bezel, white dial, and black registers. It beautifully pays homage to the red arched “Daytona” font from the past. The watch was purchased it in 2024.

It is in great condition. It comes “full set” with original box and papers.

Heuer Monaco Reference 1133B On NSA Bracelet Unpolished

The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement, the calibre 11. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic. This example is in phenomenal condition. We believe the case is unpolished, and the dial is stunning. In addition, it is paired on the Heuer bracelet made by NSA. It dates to circa 1970.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Submariner Reference 14060

This Rolex Submariner reference 14060 dates to circa 1997 with an U7 serial. The case has been polished. It measures 40mm in diameter.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ video and corresponding article.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Small Crown Submariner Reference 6536

The Rolex “Small Crown” Submariner reference 6536 is a classic reference. The dial on this example is a matte service dial, which I believe dates to the 1980s. The hands appear to be service replacement hands from the same period as the dial, while the second hand is later. The case has been polished and measures 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). It features the original bezel which has a nice blue color. It is paired on a nylon strap from Double RL (RRL). The watch dates to circa 1957 with a 0.229M serial.

To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner’ article and corresponding video.

The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Paul Newman Daytona Reference 6239 with Box, Letter of Provenance and Service Receipt

This is a rare Rolex “Paul Newman” Daytona Reference 6239 with “Mark 1” black dial. It is from the earliest run of “Paul Newman” or “Exotic” dial Cosmograph Daytonas.

The Mark 1 Paul Newman Daytonas feature a larger “T SWISS T” below 6:00, the 300 Units Per Hour bezel, and feature a thin seconds register hand plus flared minute and hour register hands.

The is very nice and attractive example. The rare 300 bezel appears unpolished while the case appears to be lightly polished. The serial number is 1.59 million dating to circa 1967.

The watch comes with box, a letter of provenance, and service papers. It previously came from the original owner who purchased the watch new in Mexico. The Jubilee bracelet is an “Hecho en Mexico” bracelet. This is a rare opportunity to own a Rolex “Paul Newman” Daytona with black dial.

Rolex Paul Newman Daytona Reference 6241 Full Set

The vintage Rolex Daytonas garnering the nickname "Paul Newman" due to their exotic dials made by Singer with small boxes in the registers are some of the most iconic and desirable vintage watches that exist. The present watch is one of the most desirable versions, a reference 6241 featuring a white dial and the original “Mark 1” acrylic bezel. The watch dates to circa 1968 and features a 1.76M serial number. The dial is in spectacular condition. The case has been lightly polished, but is quite strong.

This watch came from the family of the original owner, and I found it for a client several years ago. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch comes with box and papers.

Vulcain Efemerid Reference 383001 Complete Calendar w/ Moonphase Unpolished

This Vulcain Efemerid is a very special and rare model. The watch features a spectacular dial by Stern Freres. If you were to buy a Rolex or Patek Philippe with this dial type, you would be paying north of six figures. It features an unpolished stainless steel case which measures 34mm in diameter ( 42mm lug-to-lug).

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

A. Lange & Söhne Reference 381.029 Saxonia Outsize Date Unpolished Full Set

This A. Lange & Söhne Reference 381.029 Saxonia Outsize Date is a very attractive timepiece. The white gold case is remains unpolished. It shows very little signs of wear, as the owner rarely wore it.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers from 2020. It comes from the original owner.

Jaeger-LeCoultre Memovox Reference E 853

Along with the Atmos clocks, Jaeger-LeCoultre’s advancements in alarm timepieces throughout the 20th century are certainly one of the most iconic genres from the Le Sentier manufacturer. This Memovox is the reference E 853 from the late 1950s. The reference’s “E” stands for Etanche, which indicates the case is a water-resistant model.

The watch is powered by the calibre 815. The movement is a bumper-automatic, which means it does not rotate a full 360 degrees. Instead, the oscillating weight will bounce back and forth between two spring sides within the movement. This example was produced in the late 1950s.

It features a very attractive and warm toned dial with an uncommon dial layout of 3, 6, 9, and 12 numerals. It also features the very attractive dauphine hour markers seen on many JLC dials from the 1950s. The case has been polished in the past. It is 37mm in diameter. The case back is later with a serial for the reference E 855 from the 1960s which was replaced during a service. It features the correct unsigned crowns. Among the vintage automatic Memovox references, the E 853, E 855, E 875 are some of my personal favorites. They are some of the most classic Jaeger-LeCoultre models and wear very nice on the wrist.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Datejust Reference 16014 Linen Dial Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This 16014 is no exception. The “linen” dial is one of my favorite Datejust dials, as it is so attractive and special. The watch dates to 1985 and features a 8.17M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on its original bracelet.

The watch is in excellent condition with an unpolished case.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Patek Philippe Tiffany Signed Reference 3820 w/ Breguet Numerals Unpolished

This Patek Philipe reference 3820 is a very refined timepiece. The dial is very exceptional for a 1980s model and is double signed by the prestigious American retailer Tiffany & Co. The case measures 32mm in diameter. It is powered by the calibre 177.

The case is unpolished. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016

Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch.

This example features an 2.16M serial dating it to circa 1969. The case is polished and measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on a Rolex 7836 bracelet with 258 end links. The bracelet has a bit of stretch and is original to the watch.

To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history and is running great. It features the original crystal. It is offered at very attractive price and comes from the son of the original owner.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Reference 5712/1R-001 Unpolished Full Set

This Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 5712/1R-001 is a very attractive complicated sports model. The 18K rose / pink gold case is remains unpolished. It does show signs of wear and scratches.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers from December 2022. It comes from the original owner.

Rolex Linen Dial Datejust Reference 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this linen-dial reference 16030 is so beautiful.

The watch dates to 1984 and features a 7.5M serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter.

The watch is in great condition with an unpolished case. It does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great and keeping excellent time. It just passed a water resistance test of 3 bar.

Tudor Heritage Ranger Reference 79910 On Bracelet Unpolished Full Set

This Tudor Heritage Ranger reference 79910 is an awesome watch. It is paired on a bracelet.

The watch comes “full set” with box and papers.

Heuer Skipper Autavia Reference 73463 Unpolished

Heuer Autavias are some of the best buys in vintage watches. Particularly exciting are models such as this “Skipper” reference 73463 example with a vibrant dial design. The case is unpolished. The model is very bold on the wrist and has great proportions. For those looking for a stylish wristwatch that can also fly under the radar, I certainly recommend models such as these.

The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running great.

Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 White Dial Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520 with a white dial. It has a U86 serial dating it to circa 1997. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988 until circa 2000 . This is a very nice example with an unpolished case. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch does not include a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Rolex Honda Date Ref 15000 Unpolished

We’re incredibly excited to offer the fourth known Rolex Honda Date reference 15000 example. This example comes from the family of the original owner. The engraving on the back features the text “Honda Bowl Winner ‘85”. These watches were earned in sales competition from Honda dealers. The watch is 34mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 42mm). The lug width is 19mm and the height is 12.5mm including the crystal. The watch is powered by the calibre 3035, and features a quickset (indicative of the 5-digit reference). The case serial number is 7.5 million dating to circa 1984. It features an I bracelet code from late 1984.

The watch does not come with the box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Black Dial Two-Tone Zenith Daytona Reference 16523 with Papers Unpolished P Serial

This two-tone Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16523 dates to circa 2000. It has a P serial number, which denotes the final run of the reference 16523 and is very uncommon. There were also some P serial reference 116523 examples made with the in-house Rolex automatic calibre 4130.

The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug). One of the end links is an incorrect end link, but matches well.

The two-tone oyster bracelet reference 78393 has a V9 serial and dates to circa 1996 and is likely original to the watch.

This watch comes with box and papers.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well.

There is a guy who has been asking us about P serial Daytonas for several years. We are very confident he will not buy this when we send it to him!

Patek Philippe Reference 1589 In 18K YG

This Patek Philippe reference 1589 is a very cool and uncommon model. The case is 18k yellow gold and measures approximately 36mm in diameter. The case has been polished in the past. The hallmarks are very strong. The dial is a later service dial. The case back features a beautiful engraving reading “Meyer S Slotkin”. It features a Patek Philippe gold buckle.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Silver Datejust Ref 16030 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

The watch dates to circa 1987 and features an 9.88 serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. It features a beautiful silver soilel dial. This watch features a quick-set date movement.

The watch is paired with a 62510h Jubilee bracelet with a K11 date code dating to circa 1986 and 555 end links.

The watch is in excellent condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Silver Datejust Reference 16234 Unpolished

I absolutely love steel Rolex Datejusts. I recently wrote about them in our blog ‘Don't Sleep On This: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have.

The watch dates to circa 1988 and features an R8 serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 62510H Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 555B end links. It features a beautiful silver soilel dial. This watch features a quick-set date movement.

The watch is in excellent condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Heuer Silverstone Reference 110.313B Unpolished

The Heuer Silverstone is one of the coolest and funkiest chronographs of the era. It features the calibre 12 movement. This reference 110.313B is in great condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It has a very cool and attractive blue dial.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Rolex Silver Air-King Ref. 14000 Unpolished

This Rolex Air-King reference 14000 is a superb example. The watch features a A3 serial number and dates to circa 1999. The case is unpolished. This Air-King is an awesome wristwatch for both casual and formal attire.

We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time It does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test.

Rolex Date Reference 15200 Unpolished w/ Papers Pinkish/Silver Dial

I absolutely love this Rolex Date reference 15200 The watch has a U7 serial code and. dates to circa 1997. The case measures 34mm in diameter. The watch is in excellent condition. We believe the case is unpolished.

We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with box. It does come with papers.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Zenith Daytona White Dial Reference 16520 White Dial Unpolished Box & Papers

This is an outstanding example of a Rolex Daytona reference 16520 with a white dial. It has a W serial dating it to circa late 1995. It was sold in 1996. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988 until circa 2000. This is example features a crisp unpolished case and unpolished bracelet. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

Rolex Oyster Perpetual 36mm Reference 1018 Unpolished

We’re thrilled to offer this awesome Rolex reference 1018 which formerly belonged in Eric’s personal collection. The reference 1018 is a rare and oversized Oyster Perpetual model which measures 36mm (43mm lug-to-lug). The 1018 was a reference that was not mass-produced and as a result of its narrow font has become an exciting Oyster Perpetual model for collectors who appreciate minimal and balanced dials. Oftentimes, the reference 1018 will show signs of water ingress or feature service dials. This example features a beautiful silver soliel dial which is similar to the silver dials of Rolex Big Red Daytona 6263s. This example dates to 1966 with a 1.45M serial. The case appears to be unpolished and the watch is paired on a fantastic period correct 7206 bracelet with 58 end links dating to the 4th quarter of 1966 (which I would value at $4,000).

To learn more about the Rolex reference 1018, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘Collector's Guide: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 1018’.

It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well and keeping good time.

Universal Genève Polerouter De Luxe 10234-3 in 18K Unpolished

This is an incredible Universal Genève Polerouter De Luxe reference 10234-3. The 18K yellow gold case appears unpolished, It measures 36mm in diameter by 43mm lug to lug. The dial is an incredible gilt example with the gold exterior. It is non-luminous and extremely elegant on the wrist. It is paired on an incredible period correct Gay Freres bracelet that is out of this world.

Buy this and every Polerouter collector on the planet will kneel before you and worship.

It comes with a box and does not come with papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

Enicar Sherpa Super Jet 146/003 Unpolished

The Enicar Sherpa Super Jet models are extremely cool and uncommon watches. This example is in very fun with a silvery champagne dial. The case made by E. Piquerez SA (also called EPSA). Enicar was way ahead of other companies with the large dimensions. The case appears to be unpolished and is approximately 40mm in diameter.

This watch does not include box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.title: What's Selling Here - September 5, 2025

https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.author: charlie dunne
https://www.windvintage.com/blog/whats-s.body_text:
By Charlie Dunne

Patek Philippe "Dorè" Dial Reference 3940J Unpolished & Extract

This Patek Philippe reference 3940 in yellow gold is a rare and uncommon model from the early period of the amazing reference 3940. Whereas the earliest examples of the Patek Philippe reference 3940 only had a solid case back. This example for sale dates to 1985. Starting in the early 1990s, Patek Philippe made the 3940 models with both exhibition and solid case backs except for the platinum model, which only had a solid case back for the entirety of its run.

The references 3940 is arguably the most elegant Patek Philippe watches made over the last 40 years. The watch is powered by the self-winding calibre 240Q with microrotor, which when released was the thinnest automatic perpetual calendar movement available. The case is 36mm, and it comes on tan strap. We believe the case remains unpolished. The watch shows little wear and features crisp hallmarks, which is extremely rare for reference 3940s. The dial is a very beautiful "dorè" dial.

The watch comes with an Extract from the Archives indicating a production of 1985, as well as a Patek Philippe winding box.

OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 2998-2 Tropical Dial Unpolished

It is no secret that I love vintage OMEGA Speedmasters. This particular reference 2998-2 is one of the nicest 2998s I have seen. It came from the family of the original owner. We believe the case is unpolished. It features the original bezel and crown.

It is powered by the manual-wind movement (Caliber 321 made by Lemania), and is 39.5mm in diameter. This is a watch that should be in the OMEGA museum and would be a great addition to any serious collection.

To learn more about the Omega Speedmaster, you can read my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Omega Speedmaster’ article and corresponding video with HODINKEE.

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is a super attractive example that I would be elated to have in my personal collection.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Reference 5402ST A Series Unpolished

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is an icon. It is the original luxury sports watch that created and defined the category. Introduced at BaselWorld in 1972 after being designed by Gérald Genta, it turned the Swiss industry upside down and changed the fortunes of the struggling Audemars Piguet. It’s no surprise, that Patek Philippe hired Gerald Genta a few years later to introduce their own version of a steel luxury sports watch: the Nautilus.

The origin of the species is the Royal Oak reference 5402ST - nicknamed the “Jumbo”. It is no surprise that the A series examples are hotly hunted and desired by collectors around the world.

The bluish-grey tapisserie dial is in excellent condition, which is uncommon for these. Often these early dials can have an uneven and sometimes unattractive patina. The watch should fit a wrist up slightly over 7 inches and features the original flip “Audemars Piguet” flip clasp (often replaced with the AP flip clasp) with the Gay Freres “GF” logo. The case measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug to lug) and this is the original “Jumbo”. We believe the case and bracelet are unpolished.

This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. For those hunting for a piece of history, this is your chance.

Rolex Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 W/ Black Dial Unpolished

This is an outstanding example of a black dial Rolex Daytona reference 16520. It has a U9 serial dating it to circa 1998. The “Zenith Daytonas” have appreciated significantly the last few years as people respect the modified Zenith El Primero movements that Rolex used for their first automatic Daytonas starting in 1988. We believe the case is unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter (46mm lug-to-lug).

This watch does not include the original box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

Rolex Day-Date "Spanish" Reference 18238 in 18K Yellow Gold Unpolished Full Set

This Rolex Day-Date reference 18238 is a super nice watch in outstanding condition. It has an an 18K yellow gold case. We believe the case is unpolished. It features a Spanish calendar and an incredibly attractive champagne dial. It features an E serial, made in 1990 and originally sold in Spain in 1992.

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well.

Rolex Day-Date 40 Diamond Bezel Baguette Dial 228348RBR Full Set Like New

When you wear a Rolex, everyone respects you. When you wear a Day-Date, everyone remembers you. When you wear the Rolex Day-Date 40 reference 228348RBR with diamond bezel and baguette set indices, everyone worships you.

Diamond-set Rolex models are incredibly beautiful and the nicest stones/settings in any watch manufacturer. This 228348RBR is a very attractive and elegant model which is amazing on the wrist. The black dial and contrasting 18K yellow gold case make for a very luxurious look. The case measures 40mm in diameter.

The watch comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was worn 3-4 times. and is in outstanding condition. When your enemies notice this watch on your wrist, they will bow before you.

OMEGA Speedmaster Professional Reference 105.012-66 CB Case Unpolished Full Set

The OMEGA Speedmaster was introduced in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing. However, it would reach iconic status by eventually reaching spaceflight qualification by NASA amongst several of the highest grade chronographs of its era. This is an outstanding OMEGA Speedmaster reference 105.012-65 is a classic vintage model and could be a perfect timepiece for just about any watch collector.

The reference 105.012 were first Speedmaster with crown and pusher guards, garnering the “Professional” name on the dial for the case upgrade. It features a stepped dial, and an applied OMEGA logo.

It is powered by the highly admired calibre 321, which was recently re-released to excitement of many Speedy lovers. The reference 105.012 was the first asymmetrical case to be released in the Speedmaster line throughout 1963-1966.

The case was made by CB (le Centrale Boites), and it is unpolished with its original crown, which is uncommon as most were replaced during service. The watch phenomenal condition and remains unpolished. The dimensions are 42mm in diameter with a lug width of 20mm.

To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster’ video I made with Ben Clymer in 2015 (and corresponding article)

The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great!

It comes with a copy of the Extract from the Archives as the original was misplaced.

Heuer Carrera "Panda" Reference 2447SN on Gay Freres Bracelet Unpolished

The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s, the present watch is notable for its unpolished case and white clean dial. Finding Carreras from the 1960s with great condition is extremely difficult.

This watch is heralded by collector Jeff Stein as the "Holy Grail" of the Carreras. The second execution of the Carreras introduced a white dial with black registers in the reference 2447SN circa 1968/69. It is paired on a “double grain” bracelet by Gay Frères. The watch is powered by the manual calibre 72 made by Valjoux.

You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote for HODINKEE back in 2013:

The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.
